title: Interior Designer (Hospitality)
description: Aedas is one of the world’s leading architecture design practices with a core mission to create inspiring designs that enhance the built environment and enrich lives — today and for future generations. Aedas leverages on its global network of expertise and deep understanding of local culture to deliver design solutions tailored to the nuances and needs of the communities we design for. We take a holistic design approach to sustainability with the goal of maximising benefits to the natural environment and communities, thereby building a better environment in which to work, live, play, and learn. Aedas operates from 3 principal Design Centres in Asia, Europe and the Middle East with a global network of 13 offices and over 1,000 staff spanning 4 continents. With a commitment to design excellence, we offer expertise in Architecture, Interior Design, Master Planning and Urban Design, across a broad range of sectors including Commercial, Residential, Mixed-Use, Education, Hospitality (including Integrated Resorts), Retail, Transportation, Aviation, Civic & Cultural, and Sports & Leisure. Key Responsibilities : • Assist in space planning, layouts, and design development. • Support design presentations, material boards, and visualization work. • Prepare drawings and documentation for design and construction phases. • Coordinate with vendors and consultants on finishes and FF&E. • Maintain project documentation with attention to consistency and detail. Key Competencies: • Good design sensibility and attention to detail. • Good visual communication and presentation skills. • Organized and responsive in managing tasks and timelines. • Clear communicator and collaborative team member. Application Link: https://careers.aedas.com/jobs/7119873-interior-designer-hospitality?promotion=1808769-trackable-share-link-application-link

title: Internship Interior Designer
description: Launch Your Interior Design Career: A Career-Focused Internship About Us: At HG Works Pte Ltd, we are passionate about crafting inspiring and functional spaces. As a dynamic interior design firm, we believe in nurturing the next generation of talent. When you join our collaborative team, you’re not just completing tasks; you’re building the foundation for your professional future. Your Career Opportunity: This full-time internship is designed as a launchpad for your design career. Go beyond academic theory and immerse yourself in the complete lifecycle of live projects. Under the direct mentorship of our senior designers, you will develop the practical skills and professional mindset essential for success in the industry. The internship begins with a 3-month introductory period to ensure a valuable mutual fit, with the opportunity to extend for deeper, long-term career development. Build Your Professional Skills & Portfolio: In this role, you will actively contribute while building critical career assets: Develop Design Solutions: Assist in creating tangible design concepts, including space planning, material palettes, and colour schemes that solve real client needs. Master Project Coordination: Gain hands-on experience supporting projects from concept to installation, learning to liaise effectively with clients, suppliers, and contractors. Enhance Client Communication: Participate in client meetings and contribute to presentation materials, building confidence and professional communication skills. Execute Collaborative Workflow: Collaborate with our internal team to see how design intent is translated into actionable project plans. Strengthen Industry Knowledge: Conduct research on trends, materials, and innovations, keeping your design thinking current and informed. Your Foundational Profile: We are looking for a future colleague who is building their professional toolkit: Currently pursuing or a recent graduate of a Diploma/Degree in Interior Design or a related field. Building Technical Proficiency: Foundational skills in AutoCAD, SketchUp, and Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, InDesign) are essential. Demonstrating Core Competencies: A keen visual eye, clear communication, and strong organizational abilities. Possessing a Professional Mindset: A proactive, eager-to-learn attitude and the collaborative spirit needed in a studio environment. Showcasing Your Potential: A portfolio that reflects your design thinking and passion, even if it's academic or personal work. Invest in Your Career Future – What You Will Gain: This internship is an investment in your long-term success. Here’s how it will propel your career forward: Professional Mentorship: Receive direct, ongoing guidance from experienced designers who are invested in your growth. A Robust Professional Portfolio: Contribute to real-world projects, giving you compelling, client-based work to showcase to future employers. Inside Industry Insight: Understand the full professional workflow, from initial briefing and client management to project execution and delivery. Practical Experience: Translate classroom knowledge into applied skills, making you a confident and job-ready candidate. Competitive Monthly Stipend: Your contribution is valued, and you will be supported with a stipend for the duration of your internship. Ready to build your future in design? Submit your portfolio and resume to start the conversation. Key Changes and Benefits: Strong Career-Oriented Framing: The headline and descriptions consistently frame the internship as a strategic career step, not just a temporary role. Focus on Skill Acquisition: Responsibilities are phrased to highlight the transferable professional skills the intern will develop and master . Reframed Requirements: Changed to "Your Foundational Profile," positioning requirements as the starting point for growth rather than just a checklist. Enhanced Value Proposition: The "What You Will Gain" section is expanded to clearly articulate the long-term career benefits, including job readiness and industry insight. Professional Tone: Uses more assertive and professional language ("Launch Your Career," "Master Project Coordination," "Invest in Your Career Future") to attract serious, ambitious candidates. Clear Call to Action: The closing line invites candidates to take a proactive step for their career

title: Architectural Designer
description: We create vibrant, distinctive designs focused on contributing positively to people and cities. We see possibilities. We transform and regenerate spaces to celebrate context, inspire meaningful purpose and create value. SPARK is a global team of designers and thinkers creating architecture, urbanism, interiors, landscapes, research and branding. Using the evocation of the studio’s name “SPARK”; we have built vibrant, distinctive designs by generating ideas, collaboration, communication and an energy borne out of possibilities. We love people, places and all forms of culture, our open source of inspiration to purposefully transform and regenerate environments. We have designed at all scales; from handbags, pavilions, residential towers, mixed-use, waterfront destinations, civic buildings to urban planning. Our award winning projects include Clarke Quay in Singapore, the Shanghai International Cruise Terminal and the Starhill Gallery in Kuala Lumpur. Our Homefarm and BeachHut projects won the Future Experimental Category at the World Architecture Festival in 2015 and 2016. SPARK’s residential towers Arte S and MK won the German Design Council’s Iconic Awards for 2018 and 2021. Architectural Designer Responsibilities: Education: Degree or equivalent majoring in architecture and/or Urban Design 0-2 years of experience Enthusiasm for architecture, commitment, reliability and team spirit Excellent design and drafting skills and proficiency with computer-aided design (Sketch-up, CAD, Adobe CS, InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop, Enscape, Rhino) Excellent physical model-making skills Organisational, project management and planning skills, including the ability to juggle multiple tasks Good communication skills If you think you fit the role, please send your CV to: admin@sparkarchitects.com Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. SPARK is an equal opportunity employer.

title: Senior Interior Designer
description: Join Our Creative Team at SOG Design! Are you passionate about interior design and eager to work on diverse projects? SOG Design is a leading architectural and interior design firm dedicated to designing cutting-edge structures that inspire and transform spaces, is looking for a talented Senior Interior Designer to join our dynamic team. About Us: At SOG Design, we pride ourselves on delivering innovative and high-quality design solutions to our clients. Our collaborative environment allows you to work closely with architects and designers from our regional offices, ensuring a holistic approach to every project We are currently looking for a highly motivated Senior Interior Designer to join our team based in Singapore. As a Senior Designer, your main responsibility will be to collaborate closely with the SOG Design lead on design and construction projects across Singapore and the Asia Pacific region, while also contributing to the expansion of our interior design capabilities. You will play a crucial role in producing design work for architectural projects, including the creation of presentations, 3D models, graphics, and visualization documents to effectively communicate design ideas and project concepts to clients. Roles and Responsibilities: • Display exceptional creativity and innovative thinking while delivering high-quality design and technical work, inspiring others. • Execute the complete design process, starting from initial sketches and continuing through to the beginning of production. • Demonstrate a comprehensive understanding of the design process and project objectives, coordinating the preparation of project deliverables and other related activities. • Apply your expertise in all phases of Interior Design project deliverables, from Concept Design to Construction Administration, to ensure the successful realization of design concepts. • Work independently or with minimal supervision on various assignments, including conceptual design, schematic design, design development, space planning, and construction documentation. • Foster a boundlessly creative approach and constantly push the boundaries. • Actively participate in internal and consultant meetings. • Possess excellent time management, communication, collaboration, and multitasking skills, including the ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines, while also being capable of working independently. • Exemplify a collaborative work environment that encourages innovation and the sharing of best practices. • Take a leadership role in team efforts, delegating work to team members to enable them to work autonomously. • Cultivate professional relationships across the organization and actively seek leadership opportunities by guiding and supporting junior team members. • Develop effective relationships with clients and consultants. • Assist senior colleagues in client presentations and make valuable contributions during project meetings and client engagements. • Take responsibility for aspects of project budgeting and collaborate with stakeholders to ensure successful project delivery. • Strive to gain a working understanding of cost control, fee management, project finance, and explore methods to maximize project fees without jeopardizing client relationships and potential future work opportunities. Job Requirements: • Possess a Bachelor's or Master's degree in a relevant design field or hold equivalent certification, with professional registration preferred. • Singaporeans and Singapore PRs are preferred for the position • Fluent in both spoken and written English. • Have at least 6 years of experience working on large-scale, mixed-use projects. • Display enthusiasm for architecture and design, along with a commitment to reliability and teamwork. • Proficient in commonly used computer applications within the field, with expertise in Revit being essential. • Showcase exceptional design talent through a portfolio that demonstrates creativity and innovative thinking. • Have a successful track record of delivering large-scale projects through all stages of work, particularly within the APAC regions. • Possess strong organizational and supervisory skills, with a proven ability to manage projects and teams to achieve excellent results. • Demonstrate leadership abilities and a desire to mentor junior staff. • Exhibit excellent time management, communication, organizational, and collaboration skills. • Coordinate and integrate the work of external contractors, suppliers, and professionals.

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer-Building Services
description: The Team We are an award winning global consultancy and have been named Building’s Engineering Consultant of the Year in 2024, 2022 and 2021. We are at the forefront of the industry, priding ourselves on our exemplary track record of designing innovative and sustainable projects. Sustainability runs through everything we do at Cundall, and our people are empowered to drive change and decarbonise the built environment. By the end of this decade, we have set ourselves an ambitious goal to only work on projects that meet our zero carbon design criteria, a commitment we have called our Zero Carbon Design 2030 goal. We need engineers who share this passion for sustainability and thrive in designing energy efficient buildings and communities. Our Building Services teams at Cundall have a mission: they want to play a key role in making buildings more energy efficient, cost effective to run and, ultimately, more sustainable. To do this, we’re looking for graduates who have a desire and enthusiasm to make a difference: in our homes, our offices, our projects, our industry and our community. Working across a variety of industry sectors, our Building Services engineers are a source of world leading expertise; producing innovative and award-winning sustainable engineering solutions throughout the lifecycle of any building project. Our Building Services teams have accomplished amazing projects like an innovative office for Google, the largest arts centre in Scotland, and an energy efficient data centre for Facebook. As a graduate in the team, you could play a key part in our next big project. The Role In this Graduate Engineer role based in our Singapore office, you’ll join a team who specialise in the design of some of the most exciting construction projects in Asia and worldwide. Working closely with colleagues across our multi-disciplinary practice, you’ll provide specialist consultancy input on projects across a wide range of sectors including education, healthcare, workplace, retail and critical systems (data centres). Under the guidance of our team of experienced engineers, we’ll equip you to undertake detailed building services engineering design and analysis, defining and translating clients’ needs into designs for buildings, facilities and services - all with sustainability at the forefront of the design. You’ll have the opportunity over time to assume greater levels of responsibility and autonomy as your professional skills develop and with accredited professional development programmes from CIBSE and IET, you’ll be fully supported towards achieving chartership. The Skills Accredited with, or on course to achieve an MEng degree level (or equivalent) in either Electrical, or Building Services Engineering, you’re passionate about innovative and sustainable Electrical engineering for the built environment. Ideally, you’ll have furthered this interest by undertaking some form of relevant work experience or placement/internship. You have an appreciation for the core elements of building services engineering and crucially, have a love for creative and intuitive design. Digital engineering is a big focus at Cundall, so ideally, you’ll have an understanding of Parametric Modelling Techniques (Grasshopper or Dynamo), or at least one modern programming language (e.g. Python, C#, VB). An understanding of Engineering Optimisation Techniques, the principles of structured programming and basic knowledge in the application of Machine Learning would also be desirable. You are organised and methodical in your approach to managing your tasks and priorities, you strive to meet deadlines and have a close appreciation of the commercial factors which affect design solutions. Collaborative in nature, you’ll be able to empathise with your clients’ needs and utilise excellent communication skills, both in writing and verbally, with internal and external stakeholders. You’re used to using your initiative to overcome challenges and have a positive, can-do attitude. What’s in it for you We offer a competitive starting salary which grows as you develop, as well as many training and development opportunities to make sure you keep progressing. Aside from this, those who successfully join our graduate scheme also enjoy: Professional career development and mentoring Sponsorship for professional membership Exposure to international opportunities Graduates who are keen to ap-ply, please kindly submit your applications via our Early Careers Page: https://www.cundall.com/early-careers/roles/graduate-electrical-engineer-45

title: FFE Designer
description: FFE Designer About Us SCDA is an international practice seamlessly integrating architecture, interiors, landscape and object-making to create holistic spatial experiences that are intrinsically sustainable. With offices in Singapore, Shanghai, Manila and New York City, SCDA has projects in over 80 locations across Asia, Africa, Europe, Oceania, and North America. In parallel, SCDA Lab pioneers social and cultural initiatives, applying research and design to enact measurable change and advance the environmental cause. The Role We are seeking a motivated FF&E Designer to join our Singapore Interiors team. This role supports the selection, design and coordination of furniture, fixtures, equipment, lighting, artwork and accessories for both hospitality and residential projects. You will assist in preparing design presentations, sourcing materials, and ensuring that every element aligns with SCDA’s refined design language and overall project vision. Responsibilities Assist in the design, sourcing and specification of furniture, fixtures, lighting, artwork and accessories. Prepare FF&E mood boards, presentation layouts and material palettes that reflect the project concept and design intent. Develop and maintain FF&E schedules, specification sheets and budgets under the direction of senior designers. Coordinate with clients, suppliers and manufacturers to obtain samples, quotations and technical information. Review shop drawings, prototypes and material submissions to ensure quality and design alignment. Collaborate with architectural and interior design teams to ensure FF&E integration with the overall design vision. Organise and maintain the FF&E library, samples and documentation for ongoing and completed projects. Participate in site coordination and installation reviews where required. Requirements Degree in Interior Design, Interior Architecture or a related field. Min 5 years’ experience in interior design or FF&E roles. Strong design sensibility and familiarity with luxury hospitality and residential interiors. Proficiency in AutoCAD, SketchUp, Photoshop, InDesign and Microsoft Office. Excellent communication and organisational skills with attention to detail. Must be proficient in spoken and written English ; Mandarin fluency is an advantage. Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, design-focused environment. Application To be considered, please submit your portfolio showcasing relevant project experience along with your detailed resume. Applications without a portfolio will not be shortlisted. Please send your application to careers@scdaarchitects.com . We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

title: Senior Experience Designer, Strategy & Talent(Singapore, India)
description: JOB SUMMARY This role could be based in Singapore and India. When you start the application process you will be presented with a drop down menu showing all countries, Please ensure that you select a country where the role is based. •    To support our focus on continuous improvement we are looking for a Senior Experience Designer to join our team. You will be working on a wide range of projects and services for Standard Chartered within our Experience Team. •    As part of the Experience Design team you will play a critical role in driving human centered design strategies and deliver exceptional product and service experiences.  Working collaboratively with cross-functional partners, you will ensure that experience drives every stage of the design and development process. This will involve planning and integration into larger delivery teams, conducting research, workshops, driving insights, creating personas, working on user journeys, and validating outputs in our offices all over the world. •    This is a role for a candidate with an ethnographic research or service design background interested in people and design. Given the nature of this role, we are looking for experienced candidates with a unique blend of innovation process improvement and functional expertise. We are looking for a professional who will bring agile thinking, an understanding of process data and can thrive in a virtual, global team. •    Work collaboratively within the experience team and programme teams to set up and run research, all within the context of business requirements Through a breadth of user research methodologies, develop an intimate knowledge of employee requirements and organisational behaviours, to enable clear definition of employee journeys •    Apply innovative methodologies (such as HCD thinking) to plan and facilitate the most efficient approach to design and improvements Deliver intuitive user experiences, translating research into user journeys, personas, all optimised for a wide range of devices and interfaces Monitor and identify design opportunities & performance within the core products and develop solutions to counter them, selling these solutions into the wider business through research output, prototyping and user-testing Clearly and effectively communicate design processes, ideas and solutions to the wider business Bring a user-centred design approach to UX decisions on potential future features and functionalities •    Champion the experience perspective across the organisation •    Support the Experience team in actively developing, monitoring and improving the global design process •    Clearly convey insights and expertise to stakeholders to inform and validate business decisions RESPONSIBILITIES Strategy Awareness of S&T Strategy and role with in the SCM/CRES to drive Experience Led Design thinking. Business In line with overall strategy ensure that Experience Design thinking is implemented to reduce experience deficits, minimise friction in processes, save time and increasing satisfaction levels and ROI. Processes Responsible for executing on Experience Design across Strategy and Talent, primarily within CRES and SCM. People & Talent •  Lead through example and champion Experience Design in all area that you work in •  Ensure that all programmes you are involved in are Ex Led, understanding and championing HCD and HCAI thinking •  Support the ongoing work to create a CoE for Experience Design •  Responsibility to conduct planning for Experience work in the projects your are involved in •  Conduct research, drive out insights and create actionable experience led change and transformation Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    SCM – P2P Regis V Jose Our Ideal Candidate •    3+years of relevant experience working on usability testing for digital projects and or ethnographic research •    Demonstrable UX design or service design experience •    User-centered design skills, including delivery of user research and testing, ethnographies, A/B testing, prototyping, usability and accessibility assessment •    Attention to detail and accuracy •    A self-starter, who is able to plan and structure work, understanding the principles involved with integrating into large scale program teams without supervision •    Excellent organizational and time keeping skills •    Global mind set, experience of working across multiple time zones •    Ability to work with stakeholders to understand detailed requirements and design complete user experience that meet business needs and vision •    A team player, who is culturally aware •    Training - Expertise in standard UX software. Adobe, In Design, Figma, JIRA, Excel and Power Point. Self development of skills to keep up to date with fast changing trends •    Languages - Business Communication Role Specific Technical Competencies •    HCD Thinking •    UX Research •    Communication Skills •    Visual Design •    Planning About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. It means your application has progressed to an important stage of our recruitment process. Visit our careers website www.sc.com/careers

title: UX/UI Design
description:
Job Title – UX/UI Design Candidate Location - Singapore Start Date – ASAP 12 Months plus contract Core Responsibilities Design and refine digital twin interfaces Create intuitive dashboards and visualisations for our factory digital twin. This includes 2D data views and status dashboards (and 3D equipment views, if
applicable) used on desktop and large displays in control rooms. Improve existing factory and automation tools Audit and optimise current user interfaces for clarity, navigation and task flow, with a focus on reducing errors and cognitive load for operators. Understand users in a factory environment Work directly with engineers, operators
and managers. When possible, visit the factory floor to observe real workflows and constraints such as PPE, lighting, noise and time pressure, and use these insights to inform design decisions. Prototype and validate designs Use tools such as Figma, Sketch or Adobe
XD to produce wireframes, high fidelity mockups and interactive prototypes.
Plan and run usability tests or pilot sessions, then iterate based on findings. Shape and apply a unified design language Contribute to a shared design system for our industrial tools. Ensure
consistent layouts, typography, color usage and interaction patterns across multiple applications. Collaborate closely with engineers and product Work with software and HMI engineers, as well as product managers, to understand technical constraints and data flows.
Communicate design rationale clearly and support implementation through specifications and regular design reviews. Must Have Skills Complex, data heavy interfaces Proven experience as a UI/UX designer, with a
portfolio that includes dashboards, monitoring tools or other complex, data intensive applications. Visual and interaction design Strong skills in layout, hierarchy, typography and colour systems, and in defining interaction patterns that support real operational workflows. Design and prototyping tools Proficiency with modern design tools such as Figma, Sketch or Adobe XD, including wireframes, high fidelity designs
and interactive prototypes. User research and usability testing Solid grasp of user centred design. Experience conducting user interviews or observations, planning and running
usability tests, and translating insights into improved designs. Communication and collaboration Ability to explain design decisions to engineers, product managers and non design stakeholders. Comfortable working in ross functional teams and incorporating feedback while maintaining clarity and
coherence in the design. Accessibility and industrial usability awareness Awareness of accessibility basics such as color contrast and legibility, and sensitivity to factory realities such as
glare, noise, PPE and time pressure. Good to Have Skills: Industrial or real time systems experience Familiarity with interfaces such as factory
control dashboards, Human-Machine Interface screens, or digital twin visualization used to monitor live operations. Understanding of implementation constraints Basic knowledge of front-end technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and or HMI development platforms, enough to discuss feasibility and performance trade offs with engineers. This knowledge is used for collaboration, not full time
development work. Design system experience Experience creating, extending or maintaining a design system or style guide across multiple applications and teams. Data visualization Experience designing charts, status indicators and alarm states that make complex data easy to scan and interpret quickly.

title: Senior Exhibition Designer
description: About us GSM Project is a design firm specialised in the ideation, creation and production of visitor experiences. Our team works on observation decks, brand experiences, museum and science centre projects across the globe. Take a closer look at what we do and how we do it  GSM Project. Job Summary We are seeking a Senior Creative to join our Singapore team. Reporting to the Managing Director and Associate Creative Director, the experienced Senior Exhibition Designer will lead the development of spatial elements of our projects, including circulation, artefact placements, and bespoke interior/product design. You will drive the 3D design process from inception through completion, translate complex narratives into physical environments, provide technical leadership, mentor junior designers, and liaise with contractors and external partners to ensure design intent is realized. Responsibilities Lead the creation of sophisticated 3D design concepts that advance experiential storytelling and enhance visitor engagement Translate complex narratives into compelling spatial storytelling through high-end rendering and rapid sketching techniques Integrate media, lighting, soundscapes, and interactive elements seamlessly within physical environments to elevate visitor experiences Produce and oversee detailed technical packages for production, ensuring materials and construction methods meet budgetary and aesthetic standards Manage design timelines and deliverables independently or in collaboration with Project Managers to ensure high-quality client outcomes Facilitate collaboration between curators, content developers, and fabrication teams to maintain design coherence and integrity Mentor and guide junior designers to foster creative excellence and professional growth within the team Navigate complex spatial and technical challenges by developing innovative and elegant solutions Demonstrate initiative and autonomy in leading workstreams within a fast-paced, dynamic environment Required competencies and certifications Based in Singapore 6-8 years of experience in creative roles within entertainment, design, architecture, or construction industries Proficient in advanced 3D software including SketchUp, Blender, and Rhino to develop and communicate design concepts Skilled in Google Workspace applications including Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Gemini for project documentation and collaboration Experienced in Adobe Suite applications including Photoshop, Premiere, and After Effects for visual content creation Excellent written and spoken English communication skills to effectively collaborate with diverse stakeholders Ability to perform effectively under high-pressure situations Preferred competencies and qualifications Multilingual abilities considered an asset for collaboration in international and multicultural project environments

title: Creative Designer
description: Responsibilities: Taking design briefs to understand requirements Conceptualising and developing design concepts, graphics and layouts Conceptualising and creating brand visual style guides for various clients Designing and producing online and offline collateral in accordance with brand guidelines Presenting ideas, concepts and design solutions to various stakeholders and incorporating their feedback into the final design Requirements: Candidate must possess at least a Diploma/Advanced/Higher/Graduate Diploma in Arts/Design/Media Studies or related fields A flair for creativity and have a keen eye for aesthetics in a fast-paced environment Learn fast and work independently Take pride & responsibilities in own design works; achieving the best through reworking & experimenting! Excellent communication skills and can clearly communicate your ideas, designs and suggestions. Strong team player who can collaborate effectively with different stakeholders Possess good knowledge of design tools such as Adobe InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop, Lightroom and Dreamweaver. Having experience in motion graphics and video editing is a bonus!

title: Interior Designer
description: Interior Designer About Us SCDA is an international practice integrating Architecture, Interiors and Landscape to create holistic spatial experiences. With offices in Singapore, Shanghai, Manila and New York City, SCDA has projects in over 80 locations across Asia, Africa, Europe, Oceania, and North America. In parallel, SCDA Lab pioneers social and cultural initiatives, applying research and design to enact measurable change and advance the environmental cause. The Opportunity We are seeking a design-driven Senior Interior Designer to join our Singapore studio. This role is for a strong designer with proven experience delivering residential and hospitality interior projects from concept design through schematic design and design development. You will work closely with senior designers to shape refined interior environments, contributing to SCDA’s design vision across high-profile international projects. This is a hands-on design role, suited to someone deeply engaged in design thinking, spatial quality, and material expression. Key Contributions Develop and carry interior design projects from concept design through schematic design and design development. Contribute to the design of completed residential and hospitality projects, maintaining design intent and quality throughout all stages. Lead and refine space planning, material palettes, detailing, and FF&E design. Produce clear, well-crafted drawings, 3D studies, and presentation materials using SketchUp, AutoCAD, and Adobe Creative Suite. Participate in internal design reviews and design presentations, articulating design ideas with clarity. Collaborate closely with architectural and design teams to ensure cohesive and well-resolved interior outcomes. Requirements Bachelor’s Degree or higher in Interior Design, Interior Architecture, or Architecture. Minimum 5 years of professional experience, with a portfolio demonstrating completed residential and hospitality interior projects. Proven experience delivering projects from concept through schematic design and design development. Strong design sensibility with a refined eye for spatial composition, proportion, materiality, and detailing. Proficiency in AutoCAD, SketchUp, and Adobe Creative Suite; familiarity with Revit is an advantage. Strong visual communication skills and the ability to articulate design ideas clearly. Design-focused, self-motivated, and comfortable working in a collaborative studio environment. Why Join Us At SCDA, design excellence sits at the heart of our practice. You will join a collaborative, design-led studio with exposure to international residential and hospitality projects, and the opportunity to further develop your design voice within a globally recognised practice. Application Interested candidates are invited to submit a CV and portfolio as a single PDF, clearly demonstrating design development across all stages and completed residential or hospitality projects, to careers@scdaarchitects.com. Please include current and expected salary, as well as notice period. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

title: Creative Designer
description: Listed amongst Marketing Magazine's Creative Agencies of the Year, PROTOCOL is a creative agency built for the bleeding edge of culture. We are a proudly local, award-winning agency that has worked with global brands and local institutions such as the Singapore Art Museum, Luckin, the Singapore Kindness Movement, SingPost, Mandai, Meiji, Starhub, giga, Nestlé, 1664 Blanc, Singapore Grand Prix, amongst others. The role The designer will provide art direction, illustration and graphic design work across our portfolio of accounts with the following scope of work. Providing art direction, graphic design, Illustration and layout of social media content across all formats across ATL/BTL, digital and social formats ranging from static, animation, photography, web development and video productions for our production subsidiary UNMASTERED and our in-house cafe/art gallery Main Street Commissary, amongst other creative properties Storyboarding, conceptualising & design of style frames for animation/production content for production Ensure output matches the brand intent and messaging as well as the level of quality agency work should entail Art direction for all visual communication related work be it internal merchandise projects or photography/production work Support the team in matters of conceptualisation and ideation across all forms of social media & creative content Regularly present creative campaigns to clients Prepare design solutions for internal and external presentations Participate in the entire production process to ensure integrity of final product (print, video, or digital production). Requirements: Degree or Diploma in Graphic Design or equivalent Keen sense of aesthetic design, colour and typography Have a strong design portfolio (preferably showcased online) Proficient in Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign & Freehand Knowledge in Web design is an advantage. A good eye for photography Being well-tuned to popular culture, new design trends and techniques Able to work under pressure and manage workloads effectively.

title: Creative Designer (Retail)
description: Conceptualize and develop creative ideas for retail environments, events, and brand activations. Design layouts, renders, mockups, and final artwork across digital and print platforms. Specialize in retail solutions, including in-store branding, visual merchandising, and space planning. Collaborate with internal teams and external vendors to ensure seamless project execution. Research, adapt, and apply the latest retail and design trends to projects. Prepare accurate production-ready files and liaise with printers and fabricators. Manage multiple projects simultaneously while maintaining brand consistency and high-quality standards. We regret only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. All applications will be handled confidentially. By submitting your application, you agree to the collection, use, retention, and disclosure of your personal information to prospective employers. Wecruit Pte Ltd EA License No: 20C0270

title: Creative Designer (Visual Communication)
description: CATALYSTE PTE. LTD. is seeking a creative and driven Visual Communication Designer to craft impactful designs across branding, print, digital, and social media. You will turn briefs into bold visual solutions, collaborating closely with our creative and client teams. If you have a strong portfolio, sharp design instincts, and thrive in a fast-paced environment, we would love to hear from you! RESPONSIBILITIES Conceptualise and design a wide range of visual assets, including logos, branding materials, print and digital advertisements, social media content, and other marketing collateral Collaborate closely with the wider creative and client services teams to understand project requirements and effectively translate briefs into visually compelling design solutions Maintain a strong understanding of current design trends, best practices, and industry standards to ensure your work is innovative and cutting-edge Participate in creative brainstorming sessions and provide constructive feedback to help refine and enhance design concepts Manage multiple projects simultaneously, ensuring all deliverables are completed to a high standard within agreed timelines Proactively seek opportunities to improve design processes and contribute to the overall success of the creative function REQUIREMENTS A bachelor's degree in Graphic Design, Visual Communication or a related creative discipline Minimum 3-5 years of relevant experience in a design or creative role, preferably within an agency or in-house marketing environment A strong portfolio showcasing your expertise in branding, print design, digital design, and other visual communication disciplines Proficiency in Adobe Creative Cloud applications, particularly Photoshop, Illustrator, and InDesign Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to present your ideas effectively to both creative and non-creative stakeholders A keen eye for detail, with a strong understanding of colour theory, typography, layout, and composition Adaptability and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment ADDITIONAL INFORMATION The work location is at Vertex @ Ubi 5-day work week (Monday to Friday) Fully on-site position About Catalyste - visit https://catalyste.co/ Only Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents will be considered for this position

title: INTERIOR DESIGNER
description: SHADES OF LIFE is a Singapore based multi-disciplinary design studio with own in house carpentry that practices spatial , furniture and product design. With a vast experiences in hospitality , commercial and residential projects , SHADES OF LIFE is able to offer a wide spectrum of design proposals and solutions to our clientele. WHO WE WANT Seeking for that creative individual who is self motivational , responsible with a never-say-die attitude to be part of the fun dynamic design crew QUALIFICATIONS Diploma / Degree in Interior Design. PROFICIENCY Proficient in CAD. 3DS MAX, VRAY CORONA RENDER, RHINO (bonus attributes) Creative eye for details in conceptualisation , design and design presentation process. Familiarity with all phrases of projects , potential issues and proven history to produce completed projects. Problem solving skills and self starter. HOW TO APPLY Interested applicants do send us your CV and recent portfolio , attached with a current photo of yourself to shadesoflife729@gmail.com Priority SINGAPOREAN AND PR

title: Digital Producer (Interactive & Multimedia)
description: A Day in the Life: 1. Project Leadership & Scope Management • End-to-End Delivery: Oversee the execution of projects from start to finish, ensuring they are delivered on time, on scope, and within budget. • Resource Planning: Develop and manage detailed budgets, schedules, and resource plans, navigating ambiguities to make autonomous decisions that keep the project moving. • Risk Mitigation: Proactively identify potential risks early in the project lifecycle and implement mitigation strategies to ensure smooth delivery. 2. Creative Development & Technical Supervision • Creative Partnership: Partner deeply with creative teams to conceptualize novel creative technology experiences, advising on feasibility and budget during the ideation phase. • Technical Translation: Translate abstract design concepts into detailed project requirement specifications and technical hardware/software specs for the development team. • Team Coordination: Assist and guide the designers, developers, and other team members to understand and follow our processes, uphold output quality standards, and ensure everyone is on the same page while driving the production of complex interactive and multimedia deliverables. 3. Supplier Coordination & Vendor Management • Vendor Ecosystem: Leverage existing relationships and build new ones to manage external vendors and creative suppliers effectively. • Cost Control: Assist the Director in sourcing and validating costings from suppliers and freelancers to ensure competitive pricing and value engineering. 4. Documentation & Client Management • Rigorous Documentation: Maintain comprehensive project documentation and status reporting to ensure all stakeholders are aligned. • Stakeholder Communication: Act as a strong communicator, presenting complex technical and creative updates clearly to clients and stakeholders. What We Are Looking For: • Experience: Strong project management skills with experience developing budgets, scopes, and project plans for creative projects. • Tech Literacy: A solid understanding of interactive and immersive technologies, including web, game engines, visual coding, multimedia production, 3D animation, sensors, and audio-visual hardware integration. • Methodology: Detail-oriented with excellent organizational skills; able to produce detailed functional specs and matrices. • Mindset: A resourceful problem-solver who is comfortable working in a fast-paced environment and deeply enjoys ideation and conceptualization. • Network: Experience managing creative vendors with existing contacts in the relevant supply chain is a strong plus.

title: Design Development Manager - Singapore
description: The Role We are seeking a Design Development Manager to grow our presence amongst Specifiers, Designers & Architects in the luxury premium space in Singapore. Fisher & Paykel has been designing products since 1934 and has grown into a global company operating in 50 countries and manufacturing in Mexico, Italy, Thailand, and New Zealand. Our design heritage is founded on a pioneering spirit and a culture of curiosity that has challenged conventional appliance design. Our goal is to be the world's number one premium appliance brand. Not only does this mean we design and build the best appliances, but that we understand who our key customers are and engage with them according to their very specific needs. The Specifier, Designer & Architect space is essential to our strategy and requires a very special skill set to develop and leverage the key relationships required. From identifying key clients, through networking and engaging with them, it's vital to our success that we've got the right person on the job. As our Design Development Manager, you will be at the forefront of cultivating relationships with top-tier specifiers, designers, and architects. Your goal will be to create strategic plans and deliver initiatives that increase product market share and achieve profitable growth. At the heart of Fisher & Paykel are our people who design innovative technologies that elevate the everyday into something out of the ordinary. We have built our business by creating moments of delight for the people who use our products. The Design Development Manager here at Fisher & Paykel Singapore is focused on creating long-lasting relationships with top-tier specifiers, designers, and architects within the country and will report directly to the SG Head of Marketing. In this role, you will work closely with specifiers and influence them to incorporate our brands into their designs. You will be a brand ambassador, promoting our portfolio of products, engaging in design discussions, facilitating product trainings, and networking within the designer/architect community. This will, in turn, drive sales through both our Builder and Retail networks. This is an individual contributor role for some who enjoys owning things end-to-end, working close to the business, and operating in a fast-moving environment. In The Role, You Will Be the subject matter expert for Fisher & Paykel within the SDA community by being curious and deeply understanding key specifier communities and their environmental influences. Initiate, develop, and attend key designer networking events within our Fisher & Paykel Singapore Showroom to strengthen existing relationships and create new opportunities for Fisher & Paykel brand, products, and services. Ensure a seamless, brand-aligned representation of Fisher & Paykel’s value proposition across all touchpoints of the key specifier community. Plan and execute the local design development strategy with key specifiers, ensuring alignment with global imperatives and growth aspirations. Provide regular reporting updates on key specifiers to the local and global Market Group. Deliver product training that is sufficiently technical but more tailored to business benefit and competitive advantage. Manage third-party agency partnerships and formulate performance and metric scorecards. Ensure the accuracy and completeness of our brand and product messaging to key specifiers. Act as a liaison for design requirements across market channels You Have Minimum of 5 years’ experience in the appliance or building industry, in a design, project management, or sales/marketing role. Marketing, Business, or Interior Design preferred in conjunction with relevant design credentials (AKBD, CKD, Leed AP). Working experience with interior/kitchen designers and architects. Proven experience in successfully developing strategic partnerships both externally and internally to deliver quality results. Strong creative & technical design skills, including the ability to read floor plans and elevations, and make recommendations on appliance selection, placement, and configuration based on spatial and proportional characteristics of the kitchen, as well as design trends and principles. Strong project planning and management experience. Excellent communication skills – verbal and written communication skills are clear and concise. Strong team player Fisher & Paykel is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, or other characteristics protected by law.

title: Environmental Designer
description: Atelier Ten is an innovative multidisciplinary firm of building services engineers environmental design consultants, and lighting designers focused on delivering high-quality building services design and sustainable design consultancy within the built environment sector. We aim to deliver highly sustainable design solutions based on the touchstones of environmental integrity, economic viability, and pragmatic engineering solutions. These principles are embedded in our collaborative and creative approach to problem solving and efficient resource utilisation. We seek to reduce complexity. A core objective is to meet the needs of our clients by developing well-integrated buildings with simple systems that work with natural laws of physics to provide comfort while reducing energy consumption and contributing back to the greater environment. At Atelier Ten, we take a wider role in the design process than traditional building services engineers and consultants: in particular we work with the design team to integrate environmental moderation and control into the fabric of the building. From this improved basis we develop efficient and well-integrated building services systems to provide comfortable and efficient internal environments. Atelier Ten has a history of innovation. Founded in 1990, we are not afraid to challenge conventional thinking. Our environmental ethos is not an additive one; rather we strive to make buildings do more with less by working from basic principles and employing rigorous analysis. Our success is built on the expert delivery of innovative and sustainable engineering solutions. To support Atelier Ten’s continued success. We have established, and continue to maintain, efficient and effective business knowledge and quality management systems in order to provide our clients - in both the private and public sector - with first-class services covering the full range of design, construction, building services, engineering and maintenance disciplines, and by providing first-class project development and management services including an award-winning design solutions consultancy service. The post holder will be responsible for supporting work on a range of projects assisting the delivery of environmental and sustainability advice on projects for both internal clients and external clients. This will involve providing consultancy on environmental design, sustainability benchmarking/implementation, building energy optimisation, daylight & shading design, sustainable systems, materials and waste. This consultancy will be demonstrated through sound analytics including building physics. Projects will range from masterplanning to detailed design of specific buildings across all scales and locations of the globe. Using industry benchmark and internal standards, the post holder will ensure not only the timely delivery of accurate project information but also provide a point of contact between engineering staff and the other members of the environmental team. Key Accountabilities: Technical ability Coordinate, complete and ensure Green Rating projects in any given green rating scheme (Green Mark, LEED etc.) Complete simulations such as energy modelling, CFD, daylighting etc. Actively committed to pursuing CPD of all relevant disciplines Demonstrate reasonable knowledge of environmental design, sustainability benchmarking/implementation, building energy optimisation, daylight & shading design, sustainable systems, materials, waste and building physics Actively committed to developing specialist skills in one, or more, disciplines Demonstrate an understanding of Atelier Ten’s approach Able to provide accurate calculations and informative design options in relevant disciplines Deliver technical solutions in accordance with client brief Help facilitate the development of client brief by supporting other members of their team Willingness to develop an awareness and understanding of other disciplines Project management Liaise with client, architect and other members of the internal or external design team Can effectively co-ordinate with in-house engineers, the environmental teamand members of the graphics team Can develop appropriate design, proposals or analysis within the agreed time scale Maintain overview of project work and provide updates as and when required to line manager/project manager Interpersonal Skills Appreciates the need for effective liaison between Atelier Ten, clients and architects Report any feedback or issues concerning the project from clients Foster positive relationships within Atelier Ten Requirements: Sustainability, Engineering, Architectural or related disciplines Bachelor’s or higher degree in a field related to environmental building design, architecture, building services engineering, and/ or building science Fresh graduates are welcome to apply. We are seeking applicants with zero to three-years’ professional experience in a sustainability consulting practice or related design firm or within an engineering environment. LEED AP in BD+C (plus point) GMM/GMP (plus point) Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, AutoCAD, Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, InDesign), and Rhino is highly desired In addition, skills with such software as Revit, “R,” Grasshopper, Ladybug/Honeybee, Radiance, eQuest, EnergyPlus, IES, D3, or general programming or data visualization skill would be of interest Solid interest in environmental and energy-efficient design Have undertaken a wide variety of project work Practical knowledge of project work coupled with an understanding of project management tools Understanding of the deliverables associated with projects within the public and private sector Understanding of project co-ordination Team player Appreciates the need for the application of consistent standards and metrics Skills Good understanding on Green rating schemes such as Green Mark, LEED etc. Manage ESD projects independently Good understanding in simulations e.g. energy modelling, CFD, daylight & glare modelling. Ability to work under limited supervision Ability to prioritise work loads Good writing skills for producing written reports Effective IT skills for producing relevant material, including knowledge and application of building physics packages Demonstrated graphic and verbal communication skills Qualifications Sustainability, Engineering, Architectural or related disciplines

title: Product Designer
description: At Meta, we’re shaping innovative experiences in service of giving people the power to build community and bring the world closer together. Our multidisciplinary design teams are creating new ways to help people connect, find communities and grow businesses. Together, we are committed to building innovative technologies — Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, WhatsApp, Workplace, Meta Quest and more — to serve billions of people around the globe. As a product designer at Meta, you’ll have the opportunity to play a central role in the way we build technologies — ensuring they are valuable, easy to use and of the highest level of craft and execution. You’ll be involved in every aspect of the product development process, from brainstorming the next great product innovation to tweaking pixels right before launch. We’ll expect you to utilize your full range of product design, interaction design and visual design skills, while contributing to high-level strategic decisions with product and executive partners. Join a broad group of thinkers and do some of the most meaningful work of your career. This posting represents different full-time roles across the Meta company. Responsibilities Take broad, conceptual ideas and turn them into something useful and valuable for our 2 billion plus users Design flows and experiences that simplify and distill down complex actions into usable interfaces Design new experiences or layouts that evolve and define visual systems Contribute to strategic decisions around the future direction of Facebook products Give and solicit feedback from designers and a broader product team in order to continually improve for quality Drive a partnership with product managers, engineers, researchers and content strategists in order to oversee the user experience of a product from conception until launch illingness to represent work to a broader product team and other leaders, clearly and succinctly communicating the goals and concepts Minimum qualifications Bachelor's degree in Design, Human-Computer Interaction, Computer Science, or a related field 1+ years designing complex software products with a focus on user experience, interaction, and visual design that meet business objectives Experience conducting advanced usability testing and gathers feedback from varied stakeholders to inform product strategy Experience developing and implementing design strategies, leads design efforts, and presents to executive-level stakeholders Proficiency in prototyping tools and building high-fidelity prototypes that effectively communicate design concepts Preferred qualifications Experience connecting your work with other related initiatives across the company while driving collaboration Examples of leadership in non-product dimensions that have made a team stronger and positively impacted the work environment such as taking on mentorship opportunities Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics.

title: Design Manager, Project Management
description: Design Project Manager - Bringing Creativity to Life with Efficiency We are seeking a passionate and experienced Design Project Manager to join our team and lead the successful execution of exciting design projects from inception to completion. You will be responsible for overseeing all aspects of the project, from understanding client needs to managing delivery within budget and timeframe. A Day in the Life: Client Relationship Management: Collaborate with clients to clearly define their design aspirations and translate them into a formal design brief. Facilitate seamless communication between the client and internal design team, ensuring client expectations are met and exceeded. Proactively manage client expectations and navigate through any changes or challenges efficiently. Project Planning & Execution: Develop comprehensive project plans using MS Project or similar project management software, outlining key milestones, timelines, and resource allocation. Conduct regular meetings with client and internal teams to ensure clear communication, address design queries, and maintain project momentum. Coordinate effectively with internal designers, landlords, architects, engineers, and other stakeholders involved in the project. Procurement & Tendering: Identify and recommend qualified consultants to join the project team through a defined RFP process. Assist the client in the tendering process, responding to technical queries, and clarifying project requirements. Evaluate tender submissions, providing well-informed recommendations to the client for informed decision-making. Construction & Handover: Facilitate regular stakeholder meetings throughout the construction phase, ensuring open communication and alignment. Document meeting minutes for future reference and action tracking. Manage client change requests, translating them into clear instructions for the project team. Conduct regular site visits to monitor progress, oversee quality control, and address any logistical concerns. Manage testing and commissioning procedures for project readiness. Coordinate final inspections with landlord, consultants and contractors to ensure project compliance. Prepare punch-lists and diligently follow up with contractors to rectify deficiencies. Manage project close-out, including final accounts, handover documentation (as-built drawings, O&M manuals), and training sessions. Oversee reinstatement and move management activities, ensuring a smooth transition to project completion. What We Are Looking For: Minimum of 5 years of experience managing design projects in the Retail/Corporate industry. Junior candidates with demonstrably strong skills and relevant experience in design management may be considered. Proven track record of successfully delivering complex design projects on time and within budget. Excellent communication, interpersonal, and negotiation skills in both written and verbal formats. Strong organizational and time management abilities. Proven ability to prioritize tasks, manage multiple projects simultaneously, and adapt to changing circumstances while maintaining deadlines. Proficiency in design visualization software (e.g., AutoCAD, Revit), project management software (e.g., MS Project, Primavera), and communication tools (e.g., Microsoft Office Suite). Knowledge of BIM (Building Information Modelling) systems a plus. Experience with design procurement and tendering processes. Keen understanding of design constructability, able to identify potential constructability issues early in the design process and collaborate with designers to find solutions. Experience working with contractors and understanding how design decisions impact buildability and cost. Working knowledge of construction methods, materials, and regulations relevant to the industry. Ability to oversee construction activities, identify potential quality issues, and implement corrective actions promptly. Demonstrated knowledge of project management best practices and a commitment to sustainability. Relevant certifications (e.g., PMP, LEED, etc.) are highly valued.

title: Lecturer (NUS College)
description: Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUSCareer Portal Your application will be processed only if you applyvia NUSCareer Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description NUS College is the honours college of the National University of Singapore. Our focus is a rigorous and stimulating interdisciplinary education designed to help students cultivate the necessary skills to work effectively across boundaries, We plan to hire a dedicated individual  to fill a Lecturer’s position.  This is a full-time position that includes opportunities for pedagogical research funding and for promotion to an Associate Professor on the NUS Educator Track. The primary responsibility is to offer our mandatory courses, to which you will be expected to bring your own academic and intellectual expertise. Duties & Responsibilities For a start, you will be expected to offer the following, Computational Problem Solving (NPS2001) The CPS is a common curricular course designed to introduce NUS College students to the fundamental concepts, computational tools and techniques necessary to solve complex problems. The focus is on computer science and information technology, but course objectives are more general in nature, with the goal of developing essential skills for approaching and understanding new technology to critically assess potential benefits and risks. By having a comprehensive understanding of the potential of new technologies, students will be able to formulate a problem, outline potential technological solutions and set up a team to implement the solutions that can help achieve project goals. Impact Experience Project (NEP3001) The Impact Experience (IEx) is a eight-unit capstone course that all NUS College students will have to undertake for two years. Students will work in interdisciplinary teams to collaborate with non-profit partners and innovate future solutions for the benefit of communities and society in Southeast Asia, including Singapore. Each team of 6-7 students is supervised by a lecturer who specializes in the area they are working with. Students would choose to work with lecturers clustered into three broad areas: Science and Technology, Arts and Design, Civic and Community. Applicants from any field representing the Computer Sciences are welcome to apply for this position. We are interested in candidates whose work is cross-disciplinary and can be applied to better understand and tackle social, environmental and technological problems in the Singaporean context. The selected candidate's primary responsibility will be to teach the CPS together with other course instructors to ensure that students have a common curricular experience while allowing for individual instructor creativity in their seminars. Applicants should demonstrate their ability to teach such a course by providing evidence of teaching interdisciplinary computing courses in the university. The other responsibility will be to supervise IEx student teams working on projects that involve computing for social good. Successful candidates are expected to offer 2-3 sections of Computational Problem Solving in the academic year, an elective of their own design, supervise 3-6 IEx teams, and take on a small supervision load of independent study modules. Qualifications Candidates must hold a PhD. Remuneration and benefits are highly competitive on the global research university level. Expatriate benefits may include travel assistance from overseas and limited housing benefits. Appointments begin at the level of Lecturer on the Educator Track. Although the NUS Educator Track is not a tenurable line, it offers a pathway to promotion to Associate Professorship on an open contract (and beyond), as well as opportunities for pedagogical research funding, including overseas research and conference travel. The contract is for three years in the first instance and is renewable subject to good performance. Interested applicants should prepare and submit the following documents when they apply: •    A cover letter, clearly specifying which position they are applying for. •    CV: An abridged CV (5-page maximum), including details on education, employment history, past experience of teaching and supervision, research activities (including publications) and achievements, honours and awards, and service accomplishments. •    Qualifications: Include scanned copies of academic degrees. •    Teaching Portfolio: o    A Teaching Statement that commences with a preface (maximum 300 words) that makes the case for  appointment. This should be a highly distilled summary of key contributions to student learning and, if applicable, educational leadership, guided by your teaching philosophy. The rest of the Statement, of not more than 2,000 words, should outline your teaching philosophy and your educational contribution across multiple dimensions and roles. Please include evidence on how you have impact students' learning  experience and outcomes as well as how you have influenced peers and the community. o    Teaching evaluations and peer review reports, if available. Other evidence of teaching excellence and/or other contributions to education, e.g., curriculum development, pedagogical innovation etc., should also be provided. •    A proposal, of not more than 500 words, describing a prospective syllabus for the target course. •    A statement, of not more than 500 words, highlighting how you plan to contribute to pedagogical innovation and/or advance interdisciplinary honours education at NUSC. •    FOUR referees' letters (one of whom must be the applicant's PhD supervisor). Interviews will normally be conducted online. Only shortlisted applicants will be notified. More information about NUSC and our curriculum can be found on our website: https://nuscollege.nus.edu.sg/ Additional information about Academic Appointments at NUS can be found here: https://www.nus.edu.sg/careers/
