title: Creative & Marketing Intern
description: About Us Bandwagon is a leading music media and marketing company in Asia. We work with forward-looking brands and communities to create culturally relevant campaigns that live across digital platforms and real-world experiences. We are looking for a driven and creative, Creative & Marketing Intern to join our team. This role sits at the intersection of creative design, content, and marketing, offering hands-on exposure to how ideas are developed, shaped, and executed across multiple touch points. If you are passionate about visual storytelling, digital culture, and creative marketing, and want to build real-world skills beyond the classroom, this internship offers an opportunity to learn, create, and contribute meaningfully to impactful campaigns. What You Will Do As a Creative & Marketing Intern, you will support the creative marketing team in ideating, designing, and executing campaigns across digital and physical platforms. You will be involved in the full creative and marketing process, from concept development and content ideation to design production and on-ground execution. This role is ideal for someone who wants to actively create, experiment, and understand how creative and marketing strategies translate into real-world outcomes. Key Responsibilities 1. Creative & Marketing Ideation Contribute to brainstorming sessions for creative campaigns, content concepts, and marketing initiatives. Develop visual and conceptual ideas for digital and social media content. Create mood boards, visual references, and creative directions for campaigns. Translate marketing objectives into creative concepts and visual narratives. 2. Design & Content Production Design visual assets for digital platforms, including social media posts, stories, posters, EDMs, and presentation decks. Adapt and resize key visuals across formats and platforms. Apply design principles such as layout, typography, and colour to produce visually compelling content. Edit and refine creative assets to maintain visual consistency and quality. Support the development of creative outputs for campaigns and activations. 3. Marketing & Campaign Execution Support the planning and execution of integrated marketing campaigns across digital and physical touchpoints. Assist in coordinating campaign deliverables, timelines, and creative assets. Support on-ground activations and campaign rollouts where required. Collaborate with creative, content, and marketing teams to ensure timely and effective execution. Organise and manage campaign materials and creative assets. 4. Video & Motion Content Edit short-form video content for digital platforms. Create basic motion graphics and visual transitions where required. Prepare video assets for social media and campaign use. Explore creative approaches to visual storytelling in video formats. 5. Creative Operations & Team Support Assist in preparing creative and marketing decks for internal and external use. Organise design files, references, and creative assets. Support ad-hoc creative and marketing tasks as required. Learn and adapt to agency workflows and creative processes. Who We’re Looking For Currently pursuing or recently completed a diploma or degree in Design, Visual Communication, Marketing, Communications, Multimedia, or a related field. Strong interest in creative design, visual storytelling, and digital content. Working knowledge of design tools such as Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator, with familiarity in video editing tools. Understanding of design fundamentals including layout, typography, and colour. Comfortable translating ideas into visual and content outputs. Curious, proactive, and willing to experiment with creative ideas. Able to take feedback and iterate on work. Organised, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment. What You’ll Gain Hands-on experience across creative design, content, and marketing execution. Exposure to real-world campaigns and on-ground activations. Mentorship from experienced creatives and marketers. Opportunities to contribute ideas and see them brought to life. A strong portfolio of creative and campaign work. Insight into agency workflows and creative strategy. How to Apply Send your CV, portfolio, and a short introduction to join@bandwagon.sg. This internship is based in Singapore.

title: Product Designer (UI/UX)
description: Duration: 12 months Salary: Up to S$6,000/month Work Hours: Mon–Thu 9:00am–6:30pm, Fri 9:00am–6:00pm Location: Central Region Seeking an experienced Product Designer to join a digital team focused on delivering high-quality web and mobile experiences. This is a 12-month contract role , offering the opportunity to work on impactful digital initiatives. Key Responsibilities: - Design visually appealing and functional user interfaces for web and mobile applications. - Create high-fidelity mockups, prototypes, and design systems that maintain consistency across products. - Ensure designs are responsive, accessible, and optimized for different devices and platforms. - Conduct user research, including interviews, surveys, and usability testing, to understand user needs and behaviours. - Develop user personas, journey maps, wireframes, and user flows to inform design decisions and enhance the overall user experience. - Maintain and contribute to design systems, style guides, and component libraries. - Stay up to date with design trends, tools, and best practices in UX/UI design. - Ensure all designs comply with accessibility standards and guidelines. - Work closely with product managers, developers, and stakeholders to translate business requirements into effective design solutions. - Present design concepts and rationale to cross-functional teams and senior management. - Participate in design reviews, incorporate feedback, and iterate designs to meet project objectives. - Collaborate with development teams to ensure design feasibility and proper implementation of UI elements. - Support the team in documentation, reporting, and other operational tasks as required to ensure smooth project delivery. Requirements: - 3+ years of UX/UI design experience with a strong portfolio demonstrating design process and outcomes. - Proficient in Figma, Sketch, and Adobe Creative Suite. - Experience with user research methodologies, usability testing, and analytics tools. - Familiarity with Agile development processes and design thinking methodologies. - Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively in cross-functional teams. This role provides the opportunity to design and deliver meaningful user experiences across web and mobile platforms in a structured, professional environment. Interested candidates, kindly email your resume to: charmaine.kok@tg-hr.com We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Visiting Professor in Animation and/or Game Design
description: School of Art, Design and Media College of Computing and Data Science Nanyang Technological University, Singapore Visiting Professor in Animation and/or Game Design The School of Art, Design and Media (ADM) at Nanyang Technological University Singapore (NTU) invites applications for a one-year Visiting Professor position in Games and/or Animation. ADM is part of the College of Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences (COHASS). This position is intended for eminent overseas academics such as international prize recipients or distinguished scholars whose presence will enhance the academic standing of the faculty. The primary responsibility of this role is teaching. Therefore, candidates should be able to demonstrate a strong teaching track record in a current or previous Associate Professor academic level position. We seek candidates with expertise in one or more areas of Games and/or Animation, including fields such as game design, interactive media, concept development, animation, and creative technologies. The appointed faculty will play a key role in supporting ADM’s position as a leader in contemporary digital media and design education in the region. Located in multi-cultural Singapore, the School of Art Design Media (ADM) in Nanyang Technological University (NTU) offers a studio-based education in a comprehensive university setting, providing students with the knowledge and skills needed to develop and produce groundbreaking creative works and scholarly activity. ADM also promotes Singapore’s creative culture and identity and is positioned to play a central role in the transformation of Singapore into a regional center of media and creativity. Designed and taught by international faculty drawn from nearly 20 countries, the school offer 4-year BFA programs in Design Art and Media Art, with an adaptive and evolving curriculum building upon excellence in our core disciplines of Animation, Film, Game, Photography, Interaction, Product Design, and Visual Communication, The programs are designed to meet the highest professional and academic standards worldwide and are responsive to student interests and evolving global needs. Post-graduate programs combine theory and practice, offering MA and PhD by research and a MA in Museum and Curatorial Practice. In 2026 ADM will start an innovative MSc Game Design Art & Technology, taught jointly with the College of Computer and Data Sciences (CCDS). ADM ranks amongst the top 5 art and design schools in Asia and top 20 worldwide (QS World University Rankings by subject 2025). NTU is a research-intensive university, home to 33,000 undergraduate and postgraduate students in Engineering, Science, Humanities, Social Science, Business, Education and Medicine. Ranked amongst the world’s top 30 universities by QS, NTU has also been named amongst the world’s top young universities for the past 7 years. Further information about the University and the School can be viewed at the following websites: NTU : www.ntu.edu.sg ADM : www.ntu.edu.sg/adm Applicants must be confident navigating the rapidly evolving landscape of digital media and technology and demonstrate a strong commitment to teaching innovation and student mentorship. The role is teaching-focused; applicants should bring an established record of tertiary-level teaching experience and curricular engagement. As a creative practitioner and educator with expertise in animation, games, or interactive media, candidate will demonstrate: A strong record in tertiary education (A terminal MFA (or its equivalent) or PhD degree in a relevant field is required), with the ability to guide student-led projects from concept to completion across games, animation, visual development, or interactive media. · A recognised practice in animation, games, or related fields, with an impactful portfolio showcasing innovation, storytelling, and design across emerging technologies such as AR/VR, motion graphics, generative AI, or installations. · Evidence of scholarly output in contemporary forums related to games, animation, or creative computing, and a commitment to advancing the discipline through research or creative practice. · A willingness to engage with cultural, ethical, and philosophical dimensions of media practice, and the ability to connect with fields like digital humanities, creative coding, or social platforms. · A passion for education and the ability to foster a supportive, inclusive, and diverse learning environment, with strong interpersonal and organizational skills. · National or international recognition by academic or industry, with a track record of leadership and contribution to the evolution of games, animation, or interactive media. Applicants are invited to submit their applications through the NTU career portal by clicking on the “Apply” button above. Please provide the following in your application: 1. Cover letter; 2. Curriculum vitae (including records of competitive grants and graduate supervision, significant creative works and scholarly publications; if applicable); 3. Teaching statement; 4. Research statement; The closing date for applications is 26 September 2025. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified via email.

title: Lead/Principal Software Engineer
description:
We partner with other firms to accelerate their product capabilities through embedded modernisation and innovation initiatives whilst establishing permanent in-house teams that drive broader transformation. The firm brings together exceptional engineering, product, and design talent to work directly within agencies, delivering world-class solutions and building sustainable capabilities that serve Singapore. As a member of this firm, you'll leverage your expertise to deliver high-impact modernisation projects while mentoring the firms teams and establishing permanent product capabilities that create lasting change for other firms As a Lead / Principal Software Engineer, you will: • Advise and guide client agencies from problem definition through to solution design, in both greenfield and brownfield implementations. •  Advise and guide agency partners from problem definition through to solution design, in both greenfield and brownfield implementations •  Address complex, high-impact challenges at a national scale, driving innovation that improves efficiency and effectiveness across other firms •  Champion engineering excellence by influencing the adoption and continuous improvement of engineering standards and best practices across the firm. •   Serve as the lead architect working with the firm and industry
partner teams to ensure smooth handover from recommendations to implementation—owning the outcomes •  Establish and mentor permanent engineering teams within
agencies, transferring technical expertise and engineering best
practices •   Develop standardised approaches for assessments, discovery, and solution design, grounded in the firm and industry best practices You’ll thrive here if you •  Enjoy untangling complex technology and business problems •  Can translate complex tech into clear, non-technical language •  Focus on value and outcomes, not just technical solutions •  Influence through trust, collaboration, and clarity •   Love working upstream to define problems and shape the right solutions Requirements; •   10+ years of experience in multi-squad development teams with a career progression to Architect •   Adaptable with experience working in different industries, in-house development, consultancy. •   Curious and a problem-solver - understands the need to balance building things right with building the right things •   Actively mentors and cultivates future technical leaders, fostering a culture of continuous learning and excellence •   Exceptional communicator and collaborator - able to communicate effectively with technical and non-technical audiences, with a belief that working across teams and organisations is more effective than operating as a solo unit The firm is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering
an inclusive workplace that values diverse voices and perspectives, as we believe it is key to innovation.

title: Product Engineer (Marine)
description: The company specializes in providing the marine and offshore industry with sustainability solutions. We focus on providing our customers with innovative solutions so that they can meet their companies’ ESG (Environment, Sustainability and Governance) goals, providing solutions for the energy transition, and marine maintenance, repair and overhaul. Responsibilities: · Mechanical Design & Development Skill: Translate product and system requirements into manufacturable mechanical designs, considering performance, safety, and production constraints. · Product Development: Build and test prototypes, analyze test results, and iterate designs to improve reliability, performance, and manufacturability throughout product lifecycle. · Machinery Arrangement & Layout Planning: Develop machinery arrangement and layout plans to optimize space utilization, accessibility, safety, and maintenance. · Conduct research to understand user needs, market trends, and product requirements. · Create detailed design concepts and prototypes using CAD software and Solidworks modelling software. · Develop detailed mechanical designs, including drawings, models, BOMs, and specifications. · Collaborate with engineers to ensure designs meet technical requirements and performance standards. · Work closely with cross-functional teams, including industrial designers, electrical engineers, and manufacturing teams, to bring products from concept to production. · Participate in design reviews, provide feedback, and make necessary adjustments based on input from stakeholders. · Communicate effectively with suppliers and vendors to ensure the feasibility and cost-effectiveness of designs. · Create and maintain detailed documentation of designs, including specifications, calculations, and revisions. · Any other ad-hoc duties as assigned. Requirements: · Minimum Diploma in Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Design, or a related field. · At least 2 years of experience in mechanical product design. · Proficiency in CAD software (e.g., SolidWorks, AutoCAD). · Strong understanding of mechanical principles, materials, and manufacturing processes. · Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and communicate effectively. · Fresh graduates with relevant skills and a strong portfolio are welcome to apply.

Propose end-to-end technical approaches—software,      hardware, and networking architectures—to support immersive and      interactive installations. Produce detailed technical specifications and      manage external interactive suppliers as needed. 2. Experiment with emerging tools and integrate them      into project pipelines to enhance creative possibilities. Contribute to development and launch of new      digital products, documenting case studies that highlight innovation. Identify opportunities to leverage technology for      new services and business leads. 3. Team Collaboration & Knowledge Sharing Assist in recruiting, onboarding, and mentoring      technical staff. Document and standardize workflows, tools, and      best practices for in-house production tasks. Collaborate closely with designers, producers,      and partners to align technical solutions with creative vision. Support cross-team proposals by providing      technical rationale, estimated scopes, and high-level architecture. 4: Increase In-house Production Capabilities Grow the portion of production budget kept in-house. Establish a strategy for production components to achieve targeted production profit from in-house tasks. Implement a cost-tracking system to monitor in-house vs. outsourced production costs. 5: Build & Optimize Internal Production Capacity Recruit and onboard new technical staff. Develop, document, and train teams on standardized workflows for touchscreen and visualization interactives. Establish a centralized code repository and module library. Demonstrate measurable improvement in production efficiency through standardized processes. 6: Drive Innovation & Quality in Digital Experiences Launch innovative digital products with case studies. Generate new paid projects from inquiries on innovation. Achieve recognition for technical innovation in a digital experience. What We Are Looking For: Education: Bachelor’s degree in computer science or related field, or      equivalent self-taught coding expertise. Hardware Integration: Understanding of AV, lighting, electronics,      and mechanical components. Infrastructure: Able to configure and manage networks for interactive      installations e.g. experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) for      hosting interactive applications. Code Quality: Writes clean, efficient, and well-documented code. Eligibility: Must be legally able to work in Singapore.

title: Senior Designer, APAC
description:
Senior Designer, APAC As a Senior Designer at The Great Room (“TGR”), you will lead and deliver TGR’s design vision across multiple
projects, from concept through construction and launch. You will oversee space planning, finishes, artwork, and styling, ensuring design quality, brand consistency, and operational requirements are met. You will act as the senior custodian of TGR’s design standards, working closely with external design consultants and internal teams including Real Estate, Construction, Operations, and Facilities. The role requires strong project
leadership, cost and schedule awareness, and the ability to drive consistency while enabling innovation across projects. You will also mentor junior designers and contribute to the ongoing evolution of TGR’s workplace design approach. The role reports to the Design & Construction Director and will be based in Singapore, with regular travel across the APAC region. What you’ll do Design Leadership ●      Lead the design of multiple TGR projects from concept through design development, documentation, construction, and launch. ●      Define and steward the design vision for each project, ensuring alignment with TGR’s brand, hospitality standards, and operational requirements. ●      Oversee the selection and specification of finishes, furniture, artwork, accessories, and greenery to deliver cohesive, experience-led spaces. ●      Review base-building documentation and identify design implications, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements, programme, and budget. ●      Partner closely with Construction and Project Management teams to ensure design feasibility, buildability, and cost efficiency through value engineering. ●     Review and sign off on consultant deliverables to ensure design intent, quality, and operational performance are met. Team & Process Management ●     Lead, support, and mentor designers, providing clear direction, feedback, and opportunities for professional growth. ●     Coordinate closely with internal teams including Real Estate, Construction, Operations, and Facilities to deliver projects on time, on budget, and on brand. ●     Establish and maintain consistent design processes, templates, and review milestones to improve efficiency and consistency across projects. ●     Contribute to the ongoing refinement of TGR’s design standards, identifying opportunities for innovation and continuous improvement. ●     Stay attuned to design, material, and hospitality trends that enhance the TGR memberexperience. Strategic & Financial Oversight ●     Collaborate with Pre-Construction and Finance teams to develop and manage project budgets and design-to-budget strategies. ●     Maintain strong financial awareness throughout all project phases, proactively managing design decisions that impact cost, timeline, and delivery. ●     Support Operations and Facilities teams on capital improvement projects, applying design thinking to enhance existing spaces. ●     Partner with senior stakeholders to align design outcomes with broader business objectives and long-term growth strategy. Who you are Requirements & Qualities ●     Bachelor’s degree in Architecture or Interior Design, with substantial experience designing office, hospitality, or mixed-use environments. ●     Minimum 8–10 years of professional experience, including at least 3 years in a senior or lead design management role overseeing multiple projects. ●     Proven track record leading design from concept through construction on high-end commercial interiors across multiple regions. ●     Strong knowledge of local building regulations, accessibility standards, and permitting processes. ●     Familiarity with Revit is essential; with AutoCAD and Adobe Creative Suite preferred. ●     Ability to read and mark-up architectural, mechanical, electrical, and plumbing drawings to ensure design intent and technical coordination. ●     Demonstrated strength in managing design budgets, schedules, and consultant scopes to maintain control across all project phases. ●     Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to clearly articulate design concepts to both creative and non-design audiences. ●     Experience managing cross-functional design teams and external consultant networks. ●     Highly organised, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities simultaneously. ●     Comfortable working in a virtual, distributed team environment and travelling for site visits and project launches. ●     Commitment to modelling TGR’s values of integrity, teamwork, and ownership day to day. What’s in it for you ●      Competitive remuneration ●      Career progression ●      5-day hybrid work week ●      Annual Leave ●      Birthday Leave ●      Monthly mobile allowance ●      Flexible benefits for medical and other approved wellness, healthcare activities etc. ●      Beautiful workplace environment that fosters collaboration and interactions with others from a diverse background of cultures ●     Opportunity to work with various teams across regional locations Does this sound like you? Connect with us at singapore.careers@thegreatroom.co with your recent CV and Portfolio. *PDF portfolios or links to your website portfolio accepted. Please ensure your PDF portfolios are not larger than 20MB.
Applications without portfolio attachments or website references will not be accepted. * Note to our talent acquisition partners: Unfortunately, The Great Room cannot accept candidate submissions from partners who we do not have an existing agreement with. We will not be responsible for introduction or placement fees for hiring of candidates whose profiles were submitted to The Great Room team members or offices without The Great Room's prior knowledge or permission

description: REPORTS TO: Middle & High School Principal JOB GOAL: A High School Design Technology teacher at ICS must have a strong understanding of the ISTE standards and have experience closing the achievement gap. They must also develop engaging and innovative lessons that allow our students to grow academically, socially and emotionally. The teacher should be confident in incorporating technology (including emerging technologies such as GenAI) and hands-on learning experiences into the classroom with enthusiasm and also believe in collaboration with other colleagues, promoting understanding and communication in a diverse learning and cultural environment. Follow the prescribed scope and sequence of curriculum and assessment as scheduled. Eager to explore and learn about emerging technologies, such as Generative AI, with a commitment to integrating them effectively into the classroom to foster critical thinking and guide students in developing ethical and responsible use. Ensure Biblical Worldwide Integration (BWI) is mapped throughout the curriculum. Complete lesson/unit plans, Atlas, ESO documentation, and PLC expectations within specified time frames. Maintain student records as required by the school. Communicate with parents, students, and other professional staff regarding student progress (behavioral and academic). Follow school policies, procedures, rules, regulations, and guidelines and the provisions of the contract. Maintain proper discipline in the classroom and on the school premises utilizing teacher created discipline plans in conjunction with a school-wide discipline plan. Inform the administration if/when unable to fulfill any duty assigned. Prepare adequate information and materials for a substitute teacher. Plan and lead a student Chapel when called upon. Supervise extracurricular activities, organizations, and outings as assigned. Complete the Christian Philosophy of Education in-service program within the first three years of teaching at ICS. Attend and participate in devotions, teachers’ meetings, evening programs (if involving his or her students or as requested by the administration), and other meetings as assigned. Carry out supervisory duty as assigned (before school, break, lunch, after school). Maintain a classroom in a clean and educationally stimulating environment. Maintain regular and accurate attendance and grade reports that provide comprehensive information for each student’s learning progress. Keep students, parents, and the administration informed of students’ progress or deficiencies, and provide sufficient and timely notice to parents when students fail to meet learning standards. Submit all appropriate reports to the administration, guidance office, and business office as required by school regulations or as requested. Work with the administration in implementing all policies, procedures, and directives governing the operation of the school. Complete professional development programs as required by the administration and/or ICS Board. Maintain teaching credentials as required through sponsoring agencies (state/provincial education department, ACSI, etc.). Acquire and maintain teaching credentials as required by the laws of Singapore, NICS, and/or ICS. Demonstrate the character qualities of enthusiasm, courtesy, flexibility, integrity, kindness, self-control, perseverance, and punctuality. Exemplify strong interpersonal and communication skills. Meet everyday stress with emotional stability, objectivity, and optimism. Develop and maintain rapport with students, parents, and staff by treating others with friendliness, open-mindedness, dignity, and consideration. Respectfully submit to and demonstrate loyalty to constituted authority. Maintain a personal appearance that models cleanliness, modesty, good taste, and is in agreement with school policy. QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor’s Degree Teaching credential from a US state, Canadian province or from ACSI (exceptions will be approved by the Academic Board) Experience working with students Knowledge and experience teaching STEM is preferred.

description: Atelier Ten, www.atelierten.com, is looking for a full-time Senior Environmental Designer to join our Singapore office. Buildings that give back to the wider environment, do not waste resources and make people feel good. You will work on the creation of highly innovative and sustainable solutions involving a mix of technologies. We encourage the design of highly innovative and sustainable solutions that incorporate a mix of technologies. About the role We are looking for a Senior Environmental Designer with sustainable design experience and specialist knowledge in building energy optimisation to consult on projects. You will be responsible for delivering environmental and sustainability advice on projects for both internal and external clients. This will involve consulting on environmental design, sustainability benchmarking/implementation, daylight & shading design, sustainable systems, materials and waste, with emphasis on building energy optimisation. This consultancy will be demonstrated through sound analytics including building physics. Projects will range from masterplanning to detailed design of specific buildings across all scales and locations of the globe. Work will often be coordinated with an approach to the mechanical and electrical servicing concept, and where the project is for an internal client you will support design engineers to achieve this. You will be responsible for leading and maintaining a range of projects, leading and maintaining environmental design inputs on a range of projects for internal clients and/or providing specialist knowledge to other projects/teams. Using industry benchmarks and internal standards, you will ensure the timely delivery of considered and accurate project information. Where projects are not being delivered to internal clients you but also provide a point of contact between Atelier Ten and our clients/collaborators. You will also be responsible for leading and co-ordinating the internal efforts of junior staff across Atelier Ten, providing appropriate frameworks and tools, agreeing appropriate standards and co-ordinating delivery against these standards. We are looking for someone who has experience working in a fast-paced office environment with the ability to manage multiple deadlines and work as part of a team as well as having the initiative to work under limited supervision. You are proactive, self-motivated with strong interpersonal skills and comfortable working with colleagues on all levels. Key accountabilities Technical Ability Demonstrate specialist knowledge in building energy optimisation with at least 2 or more practices’ expertise Demonstrate a proven ability to provide consultancy for environmental design, sustainability benchmarking/implementation, building energy optimisation, daylight & shading design, sustainable systems, materials, waste and building physics Demonstrate an empathy with Atelier Ten’s approach Committed to the development of high-quality, high-performance and sustainable buildings via the appropriate technical standards, costs and programme Help to frame client briefs in terms of environmental and sustainable aspirations Deliver technical solutions in accordance with client brief Support and nurture junior/graduate members of the team Demonstrate an awareness and understanding of other disciplines Project management Liaise with client, architect and other members of the internal & external design team with clarity and confidence Organise junior staff to achieve solutions within agreed time scales Maintain project overview: explain the status of relevant section of a project at any time Be aware of both our agreement and the contractual relationships on the project Mentor junior staff Marketing Appreciate and assist in the need to generate business Report any feedback or issues concerning the project Foster positive relationships with clients and other members of the design team Skills Natural leader and can operate as part of a team Self motivated Ability to work with minimal supervision Ability to prioritise and to think strategically about the timing and delivery of project information Ability to structure the delivery of project information against evolving client brief in such a way as can be clearly explained to senior managers at the start of a project Strong writing skills for producing written reports Effective IT skills for producing relevant material, including knowledge and application of building physics packages Effective IT skills to develop new and bespoke assessment methods for project specific tasks and rendered in a manner that allows re-use Qualified candidates ideally possess Bachelor’s or higher degree in a field related to building engineering, environmental building design, architectural engineering, building services engineering or building science Candidates who are Chartered or working towards Chartership are highly desired 5+ years’ professional experience in an engineering or sustainability consulting firm Proven track record of leading and executing dynamic thermal modelling and energy modelling analysis Experience in daylight modelling, computational fluid dynamic (CFD), parametric modelling and programming Proficiency in IES VE, Radiance and Microsoft Excel Skills with softwares such as Grasshopper, eQuest, EnergyPlus, AutoCAD, Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, InDesign) and Rhino or general programming or data visualization skill Proven track record of successfully co-ordinating and delivering sustainable & environmental design input to projects within the public and private sector, with special focus on building energy performance Proven track record of engaging and negotiating with senior management and peers on projects Proven track record of engaging with and influencing peers outside of formal management relationships Practical knowledge of managing small project teams coupled with the understanding to select frameworks/tools that are appropriate to particular circumstances Strong writing skills for producing written reports Applicants should have demonstrated skills in computer and hand drafting, have proficiency in written and spoken English, and good verbal, inter-personal and writing skills are required Ability to work under limited supervision Ability to prioritise work loads Excellent analytical skills Strong ability to multitask

title: Senior Industrial Designer
description: *Please submit 2 separate documents for this application* 1) Your CV 2) Your portfolio (in PDF document) About us Dyson are looking for a Senior Industrial Designer, Beauty to join our Global Design and Product Experience team. This role is based at our St James Power Station Headquarters in Singapore. You will be highly knowledgeable of production, manufacturing processes, and materials development. You will champion design and user experience to other parts of the business. You will travel frequently to collaborate with our Research Design & Development teams as well as establish and build relationships with our global development and manufacturing network. About the role Own the look and feel of Dyson's products. Quickly visualise multiple design solutions at a very hi-resolution. Ensure design alignment and consistency through-out multiple projects. Support Design and Product Experience reviews with senior stakeholders. Be the voice of design while collaborating with Engineering and Product Management. Collaborate daily with our global network of designers and engineers. Negotiate and challenge development, manufacturing, and cost limitations. Ensure delivery of products according to the established design vision. Create innovative design solutions that balance visuals, user-interaction, performance, manufacturing, and cost. Sweat the intricate details of the design while keeping the overall design intent in mind. Develop refined 3D surfaces for rendering, model building, and production. Deliver production A-class Surface CAD to Engineering and Tooling teams. Utilise digital and physical tools (models, physical rigs, renderings, 2D prints) to validate design solutions. Direct model making resource to accurately demonstrate and communicate solutions. About you A degree in Industrial Design, Product Design, or equivalent 5+ years of hands-on design experience. Skilled in sketching, form development, 3D surface quality, and 3D modelling. Skilled in 3D CAD applications (NX, Rhino, Alias, Solidworks, etc). Knowledgeable in continuous CAD surfacing theory. Skilled in rendering software (Keyshot, VRed, etc). Skilled in 2D design software (Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, In Design, etc). Knowledgeable in manufacturing processes and down-stream product delivery. Excellent communicator with the ability to clearly present ideas both verbally and visually. Assertive and diplomatic – a great collaborator and receptive of constructive criticism. Benefits Dyson Singapore monitors the market to ensure competitive salaries and bonuses. Beyond that, you’ll enjoy a transport allowance and comprehensive medical care and insurance. But financial benefits are just the start of a Dyson career. Professional growth, leadership development and new opportunities abound, driven by regular reviews and dynamic workshops. And with a vibrant culture, the latest devices and a relaxed dress code reflecting our engineering spirit, it’s an exciting team environment geared to fuelling and realising ambition. Dyson is an equal opportunity employer. We know that great minds don’t think alike, and it takes all kinds of minds to make our technology so unique. We welcome applications from all backgrounds and employment decisions are made without regard to race, colour, religion, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, protected veteran status or other any other dimension of diversity.

title: Principal Architectural Associate
description: Provide technical leadership on architectural design matters. Provide guidance to team members to ensure that design solutions are optimised in order to meet clients' requirements. Be involved in business development meetings to target specific markets, clients and project opportunities Carry out architectural designs. Carry out feasibility studies and make design proposal presentations. Liaise with and discuss project-relevant issues with clients. Negotiate with contractors on design requirements and building progress. Oversee progress of on-site work; ensure project is built according to design plans and resolve any issues that may arise. Work with engineers and surveyors to make sure that the project meets the necessary standards. Project and/or design management of more than 3-5 projects at any given time. Job Requirements: Minimum Master's Degree in Architecture or equivalent At least 5 years of working experience in the related field Proficient in BIM (Revit), Rhino, Adobe Suite and Escape Proficient in Microsoft Office applications Possess good interpersonal skills and a team player A hands-on person who is positive, proactive and driven Meticulous and able to organise work effectively

title: Senior ICT Engineer
description: Mott MacDonald Singapore currently has a brilliant opportunity for a Senior ICT Engineer to join an international global, management and development engineering consultancy, with opportunities for excellence every day. ​ Part of an innovative, global community of practices, solving important challenges. ​ Contributing to advancing our economic, environmental and social sustainability goals. ​ Where achieving excellence is celebrated​ We currently have an exciting opportunity for a driven Senior ICT Engineer to join our Buildings market in Singapore. ​ As a Senior ICT Engineer, you will be experienced in integrated smart building systems and services. In this role you will find opportunities for strong career progression by contributing to boutique and large scale local and international projects. As the member of our team, you will have experience in a similar role and will be given a degree of autonomy and responsibility that suits a self-starting and self-reliant person who can deliver ICT/ELV design and supporting multi-discipline development, such as commercial, residential, hospital, hotel, leisure, mixed-use, critical facilities, data centers, airports, etc. You also will have opportunity to be involved in advisory work, providing advice and high-level consultancy services to clients from various industry. We work as a connected global team, delivering impactful work that is shaping the future across design and delivery, and it is our people who are powering this change. As a proudly employee-owned business, we’re here to benefit our clients, each other and our local and global communities. Our progress enables us to invest in creating the right space for everyone to feel empowered, supported and valued. What you will typically do: Client and stakeholders management and relationships. Communicating with clients, manage clients’ requirements and expectation in alignment with the project’s contractual scope. High-level and conceptual architecting of “state-of-the-art” integrated smart building/precinct systems and infrastructure, incl. client consulting, requirement management, conduct studies, options, gap analysis and solutioning. Design and development of ICT/ELV infrastructure and systems, including spaces and pathways, fiber and copper structured cabling system, communication networking systems, integrated BMS platform, integrated security systems, building systems and data integration. Collaborate on the development of the design package for tendering, incl. functional and performance specification, schedules, drawings, integration and interface management plan, interface coordination, integration and testing plan, etc. Support the tendering process and supervise the implementation on behalf of client. Fluent use of MS Visio, PowerPoint, AutoCAD, Revit, Revu and other equivalent mark-up/engineering tools for drawing, report, and presentation production. About You: You will be a qualified Electrical, Electronics or ICT/Telecommunications Engineer or equivalent who is experienced in the design and specification of ICT/ELV systems and services within buildings. This role ideally suits someone with 4+ years of relevant experience and looking to join a growing team that holds technical excellence to high regard and offer an excellent opportunity for career progression. To be the right person for the role, you will have: Degree in Engineering Current and proven experience as ICT/ELV Engineer with design experience within Buildings, Data Centres, Mission Critical Facilities, Airports, Commercial, Transport sectors. Knowledge and experience in: Building ICT infrastructure (incl. customer-owned Outside Plant) – required Network and communication systems – required Integrated Building Management System – required Building ELV Systems - required ICT/ELV and Building Systems and Data Integration - required Smart Building, IoT, Data Analytics – required Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning – highly desirable Cybersecurity – highly desirable Sustainability - highly desirable ICT/ELV industry certification (e.g. credential from BICSI, WiredScore/SmartScore, EPI, CNET, CompTIA, UTI, Cisco, etc.) – highly desirable Knowledge of Building Industry Standard and regulations Highly developed interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills are essential. Enthusiastic, self-motivated, a team player and customer focused

title: Product Design Lead (Home Fragrance / Consumer Products)
description: Company Description Our client is a premium fragrance and lifestyle brand expanding globally across home fragrance, hotel scenting systems, and consumer lifestyle products. The brand emphasises refined aesthetics, natural materials, and structured design language, and is strengthening its in-house product development capability. Job Description The Product Design Lead will be responsible for leading product design direction and development for consumer fragrance products. This role combines design leadership, product understanding, and collaboration with production and fragrance teams to ensure commercially viable and brand-aligned products. Key Responsibilities Lead the concept development of new product series Define and maintain a consistent premium design language Work on product packaging, material selection, and structural design Collaborate with fragrance development and production teams to ensure product feasibility Apply understanding of materials, performance, and product functionality in design decisions Guide and coordinate design execution across internal or remote design teams Support the development of structured product design systems and workflows Requirements Relevant experience in product design or product development within consumer or lifestyle products Strong aesthetic sense aligned with premium product positioning Understanding of materials, structural design, and production feasibility Ability to work cross-functionally with technical and production stakeholders Portfolio demonstrating product design work is required EA License No: 23C1703 EA Personnel No: R1762582 EA Personnel Name: Wong Choo Sian, Jabez

title: Graphic Designer
description: Responsibilities: Create high-quality visual assets for digital campaigns (social media graphics, EDMs, web banners) and print materials (brochures, flyers, event signage) that align with marketing goals. Support end-to-end design needs with the team to create engaging content for campaigns, event activations, and related initiatives. Brainstorm with the team to translate campaign strategies into creative visual concepts that resonate with our target audience. Use trend intelligence and knowledge of historical and current markets when designing and executing specific classifications. Adapt designs across multiple formats and scales while maintaining visual consistency. Prepare final files for both print production and digital platforms. Assist in the designing and production of marketing collaterals. Manage multiple projects, interpret briefs, and meet deadlines. Ensure all design work aligns with the company's corporate identity and remains consistent across all touchpoints. Maintain, update, and properly archive records of all design-related work. Ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple ongoing projects simultaneously. Handle other duties and ad-hoc projects as assigned. Requirements: Minimum 1–2 years of relevant working experience Diploma in Graphic Design or equivalent Strong passion for design with creative flair High level of accuracy and attention to detail when finalising designs Proficient in design and photo-editing software such as Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) Knowledge in video-editing software (i.e., CapCut) is an advantage Able to commence work immediately or within a short notice period

title: Architect/Designer
description: Responsibilities: Report to senior architectural designer Collaboration in space planning assignments, conceptual design, schematic design, design development, design documentation and construction site supervision for design implementation. Plan, schedule and run design activities to meet the project programme. Preparation of communicative presentations (in pptx or other media) Preparation of coherent sets of design-focused tender drawings Coordination with consultants, vendors, contractors, fabricators, regulatory agencies and other collaborators to meet overall project objectives. Ensure all designs meet with local regulatory compliance requirements. Review of shop drawings submittals and finish samples Conducting site observations to monitor construction progress and to ensure that planning and design intent are fully realised at site. Contribution to refinement of office design processes Requirements Singaporean or Singapore PR only 3-5 years of experience in the design industry. Bachelor or Master Degree in Architecture or Interior Design. Experience in Healthcare, Laboratory, Nursing homes, Special needs facilities and workplace preferred but not necessary. However, willingness to learn is a must. Excel in 3D modelling, visualization, and graphic software; Revit skillset is strongly preferred. (Others include acad, Sketch-up, Enscape, adobe and Microsoft suites). Effective verbal and written communication skills Motivated with the ability to work independently and as a team. Available for business travel if necessary. Have a keen desire to produce good and aesthetic designs. A good eye for good aesthetics is a plus. Familiar with local codes and building practices is preferred, but not necessary.

title: Low‑Code Developer (Mendix)
description: Mendix Low‑Code Engineer — Build Enterprise‑Scale Impact at Kyndryl If you're looking for a role where your low‑code expertise genuinely shapes the way large organizations operate, this is the place for you. At Kyndryl, you won’t just build small apps—you’ll design enterprise‑grade Mendix solutions that thousands of users rely on daily. You’ll join a global engineering culture where your ideas matter, your skills are trusted, and your work directly improves how businesses run. Most companies use low‑code for basic prototypes or internal utilities. At Kyndryl, you’ll use Mendix to modernize mission‑critical systems and transform complex processes at scale. You’ll own the full solution lifecycle, work with enterprise architects, and influence how entire organizations adopt digital transformation. What You’ll Do You’ll play a key role in designing intelligent, scalable Mendix applications that streamline operations and deliver real business outcomes. Your work will be hands‑on, strategic, and highly visible—giving you the autonomy to build solutions that truly make an impact. 1) Design, Build & Deploy Enterprise Mendix Applications You’ll create end‑to‑end applications using Mendix Studio Pro—including domain models, microflows, UI pages, reusable components, and secure user roles. You’ll build applications that reduce manual work, improve decision‑making, and solve real operational challenges. 2) Streamline & Automate Business Processes You’ll use Mendix workflows, logic flows, rules, and automation capabilities to eliminate inefficiencies. You’ll turn manual steps into intelligent workflows, integrate data across systems, and ensure everything runs smoothly with proper validation and governance. 3) Integrate Systems & Build Scalable Data Models You’ll design clean, scalable data models and connect Mendix apps with enterprise systems—via REST/SOAP APIs, Databases, Microsoft services, or ERP/CRM platforms. Your integrations will form the backbone of seamless cross‑system operations. Why You’ll Love Working at Kyndryl You’ll build for the enterprise, not just for a department. Your applications will support real business operations—governments, banks, global supply chains, healthcare institutions, and more. You get ownership. Real ownership. You’ll influence solution design, architecture decisions, and integration patterns—not just “configure forms” or “drag and drop screens.” You grow into the next version of yourself. Kyndryl invests heavily in talent development. As you expand your Mendix capability, you’ll have clear paths toward roles such as: Low‑Code Solution Architect Technical Lead (Mendix) Enterprise Integration Engineer Platform Owner / Low‑Code Governance Lead Full Stack or Hybrid Developer (if that’s a path you want) Here, you’re not limited to low‑code—you can grow your engineering, cloud, architecture, and leadership career in whichever direction you choose. Required Skills & Experience At least 4 years of hands‑on Mendix or low‑code development experience (Mendix Studio Pro strongly preferred). Experience working with Git and Agile teams (Scrum, CI, pair programming). Knowledge of HTML, CSS, JavaScript, SQL/NoSQL databases. Experience integrating apps with REST/SOAP APIs and enterprise systems. Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, plus the ability to collaborate with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders. High attention to detail and commitment to delivering quality applications. Preferred Skills & Experience Mendix certifications (Rapid, Intermediate, Advanced, Expert). Experience with CI/CD pipelines or DevOps practices. Exposure to mobile development in Mendix. Familiarity with other low‑code platforms (OutSystems, Power Apps, Appian, ServiceNow, Dynamics 365, SharePoint Online). Experience managing platform‑level features such as rules engines, workflows, access roles, validations, UI flows, and reusable modules. At Kyndryl, you’ll be part of a global engineering powerhouse where low‑code isn’t an afterthought—it’s a strategic capability. You’ll design impactful applications, solve meaningful problems, and grow into a technical leader who shapes how enterprises operate. If you're ready to build solutions that matter, this is where your next chapter begins.

title: Engineer - Mechanical
description: The Opportunity We have an exciting opportunity for an Engineer – Mechanical to join our CENEX team in Singapore and become part of a network of over 400 Building Services Engineers across the Asia Pacific. Work with diverse clients on various projects, enhancing your technical skills. Be part of a motivated team in a supportive culture that values your contributions. We offer numerous technical and leadership challenges to help you grow professionally. About You We’re looking for someone who loves to learn and share knowledge – someone who can bring a fresh perspective to ensure the development of the people around them and who constantly seeks better practice for the overall quality of our work and our client experience Your passion, design and problem-solving mindset will be matched with a desire to work on meaningful and significant projects with the team. If you enjoy working for an organization that values partnership, tenacity, enjoyment and cares for its people and clients, then look no further than Beca. If you enjoy working for an organization that values partnership, tenacity, enjoyment and cares for its people and clients, then look no further than Beca. You will be joining our Centre of Excellence (CenEx) team, led by one of our Technical Director who was recently conferred the SGBC-BCA Professional Leadership in Sustainability Award. Responsibilities In this role, you will be responsible to: Perform initial engineering task on the mechanical services detailed design (including calculations, computer simulations, etc.) for building projects under the supervision of senior engineers. Prepare submission to authorities for approval of design Attend to project meeting with Client, PM and Consultants from other disciplines Attend site project meeting and site inspection and respond to technical queries Liaise with authorities on plans submission and compliance Key Requirements Degree in Mechanical Engineering discipline or Degree in Sustainable Infrastructure Engineering (Building Services) Able to work independently and exercise initiatives Excellent in both written and verbal communication skills A team player; Able to interact and work with colleagues and member of other consultants at various level Schedule driven and self-motivated About Beca Beca is one of Asia Pacific’s largest independent advisory, design and engineering consultancies. After a century of operation, we have grown from a family-owned business to one of the most progressive, client-centric professional services consultants in our region. We have more than 4,000 employees in 25 offices around the world and have delivered projects in more than 70 countries. Our employee-ownership model remains key to our future. It underpins our commitment to building and sustaining long-term relationships with our clients, it helps drive our positive, purpose-driven and growth-oriented culture, it sets us apart from our competitors, and it gives meaning to our social contract. ‘Creative people together transforming our world’ is our vision. It reflects our culture, our aspirations and our purpose - to make every day better. We create value through understanding and delivering successful solutions, exceptional service and our enduring relationships and use our skills and systems to empower innovation; helping our clients shape communities, optimise their assets and streamline their operations. Find out more about us at www.beca.com . Apply Now Ready to make everyday better? Apply online today! If this sounds like the opportunity you have been waiting for, apply online now with your CV. Please include the following information in your resume: · Education background · Work experiences · Current and Expected salary · Date of availability / Notice Period The Beca Group of Companies is an Equal Opportunity Employer and all applications are kept strictly confidential.

title: Full stack engineer
description: About PebbleRoad PebbleRoad is a Singapore-based design and innovation consultancy. We envision a world where digital business transformation is not just a buzzword but a catalyst for meaningful, sustainable growth. We want to help our clients change their digital businesses for the better. We achieve this by providing them with the experience and knowledge they need to clarify their plans, develop new digital products, and enhance their skills. Our work We help organisations make meaningful change by helping them create strategic clarity, co-teaming with them to design and build digital products and services, and also helping them improve the health of their organisation. We excel in 3 areas: We specialise in solving enterprise problems. This includes B2C, B2B and B2E (business to employee). We go beyond design specs and build fully functional digital products and services to test out in the real world. We believe this is the only way to manage uncertainty and help our clients gain confidence in their design investments. We don't just design and build. We also help clients with user adoption, change management, and governance. We assign a team to each client (an engagement). Every team is cross-functional, co-located, and dedicated. The team roles include Product Owners, Product Designers, Content Strategists, UX Researchers, Full-stack Developers and Automation Engineers. These engagements may last between 3 to 18 months or more. As a Full-Stack Developer, you will be a key member of the team, contributing to the shaping of digital products and services. A typical engagement that you will lead might go something like this: Understand the context of the engagement. Understand the product requirements. Work closely with the team to define the product backlog and plan the development sprints. Work with the Tech Lead and other developers to define quality and testing parameters. Use best practices to build digital products. Be proactive and participate in daily standups, product reviews, and quality meetings. The role We’re looking for someone who: Has 5+ years of experience in Full-stack development (hands-on project experience is mandatory) Solid understanding of Node.js, React JS, JavaScript, CSS, HTML, etc Strong knowledge of JavaScript MVC patterns Has worked with Agile methodologies Has a good understanding of Rapid Application Development (RAD) best practices and implementation. Has a good understanding of best practices for web application security. Is proficient in data modelling and enforces proper referential integrity constraints Good to have experience with no-code and low-code (e.g., Microsoft Power Apps, OutSystems) environments and their interplay with pro-code. Is proficient in SQL and databases Is proficient in web services Knowledge and experience with Git Keen to learn new technologies and low-code platforms Your responsibilities are: Work with the team to build high-quality digital products using a modern stack Be proactive in absorbing new knowledge and raising development standards. Help integrate the solution into the client’s enterprise ecosystem. Work with backend systems integrators to build and implement the solution. Improve transformation practice at PebbleRoad

title: Product Design Team Lead
description: Role Overview We are looking for a highly experienced Product Design Team Lead to lead, review, and elevate the work of our UX/UI and ProductDesigners. This role is both hands-on and leadership-focused : you willmanage and mentor designers while also contributing directly to design work onkey initiatives. The core responsibility of this role is to ensure thatour products are extremely easy to use, deeply logical, globally scalableand world-class in execution . You will partner closely with ProductManagers, Developers, and Business Stakeholders to take products from earlyconcept through to final delivery, ensuring clarity, consistency, and qualityat every stage. Our products include mobile and web applications used globally by millions of users , primarily in B2B environments ,with some B2C products as well. Key Responsibilities Design Leadership & TeamManagement Lead, manage, and mentor a team of UX/UI and Product Designers. Review design work across the team to ensure high standards of     usability, logic, consistency, and visual quality. Provide clear, constructive feedback and guidance to help designers     grow their skills and thinking. Set and uphold design principles, patterns, and best practices     across all products. Hands-On Design Contribution Actively contribute to design work on complex or high-impact     projects. Create and refine user flows, wireframes, prototypes, and     high-fidelity mockups. Ensure designs are not only visually polished but also intuitive,     scalable, and practical to build. End-to-End Product Design Ownership Work closely with Product Managers to fully understand business     goals, user needs, and constraints. Proactively question, clarify, and challenge requirements that are     unclear, misleading, incomplete, or contradictory. Help the product team think through edge cases, workflows, and     real-world usage scenarios that may have been overlooked. Translate business and technical requirements into clear, logical,     and user-friendly design solutions that are scalable and link fully     throughout the platform. Cross-Functional Collaboration Partner closely with Developers to ensure designs are feasible,     well-documented, and implemented accurately. Collaborate with Business and Stakeholders to align on priorities,     timelines, and outcomes. Act as a bridge between design, product, and engineering to ensure     shared understanding and smooth execution. Quality & Consistency Ensure design outputs meet a “world-class” standard across all     platforms and products. Maintain consistency across mobile and web applications while     adapting appropriately for different contexts and user types. Advocate for simplicity, clarity, and usability in all design     decisions. Requirements Experience & Skills Ability to think logically and systematically about complex     workflows and user journeys. Ability to understand broader use cases for features and design     them in a scalable way across different user types, while ensuring they     connect logically to other parts of the platform to create a cohesive and     intuitive user experience Proven experience leading or managing a product design or UX/UI team,     mobile & web design is a plus Experience designing software used at scale, ideally with global     users. Solid experience working on B2B products, with exposure to B2C is a     plus. Collaboration & Communication Excellent ability to communicate design rationale clearly to     designers, developers, and non-design stakeholders. Comfortable challenging assumptions and asking the right questions     to uncover gaps or risks early. Strong collaboration skills and experience working in     cross-functional product teams. Mindset & Approach Highly detail-oriented, with a strong bias toward clarity and     simplicity. Pragmatic and solution-focused, balancing ideal user experience with     real-world constraints. High standards for quality, but able to move fast and iterate when     needed. Passionate about building products that feel effortless to use.

title: interior designer/ Drafter
description: Careers at Interior Kaki Build With Purpose. Grow With Intention. Full Time job- you can choose to be  Salary Paid or commission based or a hybrid. Interior Kaki is the sister company of Contractor Kaki Pte Ltd , established as a design-led interior practice where design intent and craftsmanship are conceived and executed under one roof . With our own in-house carpentry workshop and curated showroom, we take a hands-on approach to creating spaces that reflect the signature identity, lifestyle, and sensibility of each homeowner — thoughtfully resolved within the architectural realities of the home. Our work sits at the intersection of design, buildability, and human experience . We believe in doing things properly — with care, discipline, and respect for the craft. Why Join Interior Kaki As the studio continues to grow, we are looking for driven, disciplined, and design-minded individuals who want more than just a job. Interior Kaki is for those who: Take pride in precision and craftsmanship Are motivated to turn creative ambition into built reality Value learning, accountability, and professional standards Seek meaningful work and long-term career growth We don’t simply fill roles. We invest in people. Our Culture & Philosophy We believe creativity takes many forms — and that talent flourishes when given the right platform, guidance, and freedom , supported by strong standards. At Interior Kaki: Ideas are respected Skills are sharpened through real responsibility Growth is nurtured through close collaboration Individuals are encouraged to develop their own strengths and voice We place greater emphasis on the growth of the individual than on the growth of the company alone — because when our people succeed, the studio grows naturally with them. Who You’ll Work With You will work closely with a team of experienced designers and alongside the founder herself, Kiren Pak . Kiren is trained at Temasek Polytechnic in Product & Industrial Design , and further qualified through the BCA Academy in Interior & Landscape Design and Building / Facility Management . Her multidisciplinary background bridges creativity with buildability, regulatory understanding, and operational rigour . Interior Kaki is intentionally structured to grow with depth, not just scale. Senior professionals are brought in not only to deliver excellence, but to mentor, collaborate, and elevate the studio as a whole . Job Scope (General) Depending on your role and level of experience, your responsibilities may include: Design & Project Execution Assist in or lead interior design development, space planning, and detailing Prepare drawings, material selections, and specifications aligned with design intent Coordinate with in-house carpentry, suppliers, and contractors to ensure buildability Client & Stakeholder Management Participate in client meetings, presentations, and discussions Manage expectations through clear communication and professional conduct Support project coordination from concept through completion Technical & On-Site Exposure Gain hands-on exposure to site conditions, carpentry works, and construction processes Learn technical coordination, detailing accuracy, and on-site problem-solving Ensure design intent is carried through during execution Teamwork & Studio Contribution Work closely with designers, project teams, and management Contribute ideas, improvements, and solutions within the studio Uphold Interior Kaki’s standards in quality, discipline, and professionalism What You’ll Learn & Master At Interior Kaki, you will be trained to operate with increasing independence , building both design sensitivity and technical competence over time. You will gain hands-on experience in: Interior design development and detailing Technical coordination and buildability awareness Client communication and relationship management Team collaboration and project coordination Learning happens daily through real projects, accountability, and problem-solving. Additional technical and design training is available beyond standard working hours for those committed to deepening their expertise and accelerating their growth.

title: Principal/Associate Electrical Engineer
description: The Team Our Building Services team’s mission is to play a key role in making buildings more energy efficient, sustainable and cost effective to operate – and we pride ourselves on our exemplary track record of designing innovative and sustainable mechanical and electrical solutions, that minimise the use of natural resources. The Building Services team are a source of world leading expertise – and offer a pro-active and collaborative approach to skill development, and professional excellence. title: Research Fellow
description: About SUTD SUTD is the world’s first Design AI university. With Design AI, artificial intelligence is treated as a partner and a member of the team – not just a tool. As a result of this unique SUTD treatment, AI and humans brainstorm, spar and prototype together, resulting in solutions that are elevated several-fold. This human-AI team concept has been made possible because of SUTD’s unique cohort-based interdisciplinary pedagogy. As a trailblazer in the field of design and technology education and research, SUTD has been pioneering innovative programmes and initiatives since its formation 16 years ago. As part its new mid-term growth strategy called SUTD Leap, it will work closely with industry to co-create solutions in both education and research – for a better world. About The Job We are in search of a highly motivated and accomplished Research Fellow to join our team and significantly contribute to our ongoing research project “AI-Native Token Communication for Physical AI". The successful candidate will collaborate closely with our research team, working on the development of ground-breaking algorithms and methodologies for Physical AI. This role offers an exciting opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research and have a substantial influence on the field. What You'll Do Undertake research in the field of token communication and Physical AI over wireless networks. Develop distributed and scalable inference frameworks and execute experiments to validate the proposed approaches. Co-supervise graduate students and research assistants. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in token communication, Physical AI, and related fields, and integrate new findings into research projects. Prepare research papers for publication in prestigious conferences and journals. Deliver presentations on research findings at conferences and other pertinent events. Who We’re Looking For A Doctoral degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline, with a specialization on wireless communication and distributed machine learning A strong background in communication theory, signal processing, and optimization. Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and MATLAB. A proven track record of publishing research papers in esteemed conferences or journals in wireless communication and AI. Strong communication skills and the capability to work effectively in a collaborative research environment.
