title: UX Design Lead for RSAF
description: [12 months contract, renewable] We are seeking a UX Design Lead to shape the future of digital experiences for citizens, public officers, and servicemen . This role is central to ensuring our digital platforms are inclusive, accessible, and trusted by the public, while also enabling government agencies and services to work more effectively. You will combine design thinking, research, and service design to deliver solutions that strengthen public trust, streamline operations, and support mission readiness. Key Responsibilities ● Citizen-Centric Design: Lead the creation of intuitive, inclusive, and accessible digital services that improve how citizens and businesses interact with the government. ● Workforce-Focused Design: Design internal platforms and applications that enhance the daily work of public officers and servicemen — simplifying processes, enabling faster decision-making, and improving operational outcomes. ● Policy & Standards Alignment: Ensure all designs comply with national digital, accessibility, and cybersecurity standards (e.g., WCAG, IM8, SHIP-HATS). ● Cross-Agency Collaboration: Partner with policymakers, operational leaders, and technology teams across government and defense to co-design solutions that balance user needs with policy, security, and organizational priorities. ● Design Leadership: Oversee research, prototyping, and usability testing for both public-facing and internal systems to ensure designs are practical, scalable, and impactful. ● Capacity Building: Mentor design teams and foster UX capability within agencies, empowering public officers and servicemen to adopt user-centred approaches in their domains. ● Evidence-Based Practice: Use citizen and officer feedback, data insights, and usability testing to inform design decisions and drive continuous improvement. ● Design Governance: Develop and maintain design systems that ensure consistency and accountability across services used by both citizens and the public sector workforce. Qualifications ● Experience: 7+ years in UX design, with at least 2–3 years in a leadership role. Experience designing for both citizen-facing and internal enterprise/operational platforms is highly valued. ● Portfolio: Demonstrated impact in delivering digital experiences that improve public service delivery and/or internal workforce productivity (e.g., portals, operational dashboards, mission-critical systems). ● Skills: Strong expertise in service design, user research, and accessibility. Skilled in usability testing and translating complex workflows into streamlined digital experiences. ● Leadership and communication: Proven ability to lead multidisciplinary teams and influence stakeholders across policy, operations, and technical domains. Conversant in communicating and rationalizing design concepts with the tangible impact that comes along with them. ● Mindset: Mission and purpose-driven, with a strong sense of accountability to enable officers and servicemen to perform at their best. ● Delivery : Led the design and implementation of high impact UX designs in products with a large user base. ● The candidate has to be Singaporean by citizenship due to the security clearance needed for this role. Why Join Us? ● Directly improve how users engage with government services, while also empowering public officers and servicemen with tools that improve mission effectiveness and daily work . ● Lead the design of services that must balance inclusivity, security, and operational needs at scale. ● Collaborate across diverse stakeholders — from policymakers and technologists to frontline officers — in shaping trusted platforms that serve the nation. ● Contribute to the country’s digital transformation agenda, ensuring that public resources are used responsibly and effectively. ● Be part of a fun, dynamic and innovative team that is constantly challenging the norm

title: Research Fellow
description: About SUTD SUTD is the world’s first Design AI university. With Design AI, artificial intelligence is treated as a partner and a member of the team – not just a tool. As a result of this unique SUTD treatment, AI and humans brainstorm, spar and prototype together, resulting in solutions that are elevated several-fold. This human-AI team concept has been made possible because of SUTD’s unique cohort-based interdisciplinary pedagogy. As a trailblazer in the field of design and technology education and research, SUTD has been pioneering innovative programmes and initiatives since its formation 16 years ago. As part its new mid-term growth strategy called SUTD Leap, it will work closely with industry to co-create solutions in both education and research – for a better world. About the Role We are in search of a highly motivated and accomplished Research Fellow to join our team and significantly contribute to our ongoing research project "Empowering Real-World 3D Scene Understanding: Navigating Noise, Distribution Shifts, and Incremental Learning". The successful candidate will collaborate closely with our research team, working on the development of ground-breaking algorithms and methodologies for Real-world 3D scene understanding problems. This role offers an exciting opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research and have a substantial influence on the field. What You'll Do Undertake research in the field of real-world 3D scene understanding. Design and execute experiments to validate the proposed approaches. Analyze and interpret data sourced from inputs, including images or point clouds. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in real-world 3D scene understanding and related fields, and integrate new findings into research projects. Prepare research papers for publication in prestigious conferences and journals. Deliver presentations on research findings at conferences and other pertinent events. Who We're Looking For PhD in computer science (artificial intelligence, machine learning, computer vision, or related fields), with a solid theoretical foundation and extensive research experience. Ability to handle high-intensity research work and demonstrate independent working capabilities. Significant research achievements in computer vision and machine learning, with strong publication records in 3D computer vision and top international journals or conferences. Strong programming skills, proficient in Python, C++, and familiar with common deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch. Team player with strong academic communication skills and collaborates effectively with team members from diverse backgrounds. International academic exchange and project management experience. Proficiency in both written and spoken English, with the ability to write academic papers and reports and communicate fluently with project collaborators. Strong self-motivation and problem-solving abilities, with capability to drive project progress within a team. Passionate and curiosity for research, willing to actively undertake research projects, mentor students, and participate in academic presentations and seminars.

title: Senior/Principal IT & AV Design Consultant

We pride ourselves on being a market leader in our field, working globally with high profile clients in data centres, fintech, HQ offices, and smart buildings. Our reputation has led to us advising on global technology standards for multi-national companies and benchmarking and technological certification of buildings across the globe. We pride ourselves on our ability to solve complex technical problems, demonstrate adaptability, work collaboratively as a team, Practice and Company, and share knowledge, problems, and solutions as a team to harness the power of all minds and perspectives. Operating globally, our Team is based in the heart of Singapore near Orchard Central, Central London right next to St Paul’s Cathedral, and in Dubai overlooking to Dubai Marina. The Role This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced and client-focused consultant to join the team at our Singapore office, to play a key role in ensuring the successful delivery of innovative ICT design solutions for some of the most exciting and high-profile projects in Singapore and internationally. Data centres are a key growth area for Cundall, and you will have the opportunity to take ownership of our new and existing projects in the APAC region. In this role, you will have the opportunity to work closely with senior and junior members of the team and wider project teams. You will lead the design and implementation of ICT and Telecoms systems for not only data centres, but across a range of different projects and sectors, with oversight of AV and Smart Building systems where required. As a Senior or Principal Consultant, you will play a key role in requirements capture, technical assessments, and developing strategies and solutions that meet project objectives, industry standards and best practices. You will have the opportunity to contribute to the development of ICT masterplans, site assessments and feasibility studies, design specifications, all whilst collaborating with internal and external multidisciplinary teams. Through your foundational knowledge of ICT systems, based on a strong academic background with plenty of work-based experience, you will: Act as technical lead for allocated projects, coordinating the production of designs with relevant team members, ensuring that designs conform to client requirements, health and safety laws, industry standards and best practice. Design ICT physical infrastructure to support passive, and at times active systems, such as structured cabling systems, outside plant, ICT rooms, Wi-Fi, containment systems and risers. Coordinate the production of project drawings (e.g. schematics, layouts, installation details, rack elevations etc.)  using Autodesk Revit and AutoCAD, in accordance with company and project standards Lead the QA and QC process as a checker and verifier. Develop complete design packages including detailed drawings, calculations, technical specifications, employer’s requirements, and tender documentation. Be involved with construction site visits to undertake compliance monitoring roles of ICT works, working alongside other disciplines. Collaborate within the wider Cundall team and external partners, working closely with colleagues of various disciplines to support the delivery of technically excellent and successful multi-disciplinary projects. Support the senior management staff within the team, embracing the collaborative and supportive behaviours Cundall exhibit. This includes line management, team mentoring and chartership mentoring. Contribute to commercial planning and management of projects. The Skills Educated to degree level in Electrical Engineering, ICT or Computer Science (MEng, BEng or BSc), and working towards, or already be, a member of a professional institute. You will be aiming for a career in ICT and telecoms systems design and construction in the built environment. We look for 5-8 years of experience working in an engineering design consultancy or for a contractor. Desired traits for the successful candidate include: Ability to take ownership for tasks and deliverables. Capacity to problem-solve and help guide the team and client towards solutions Organised and confident communicator, with the ability to gather client requirements and lead internal and external meetings Knowledge and experience in designing ICT and telecoms systems: Passive ICT systems, such as structured cabling systems and containment. Active ICT systems, such as Wi-Fi and GSM. Hyperscale and colocation data centre telecoms infrastructure, including outside plant. Satellite communications, antennas, and aerials. MEP facilities for ICT, including power and cooling. Interface between ICT and other building systems, including BMS and Security. Experienced in leading site visits for surveys, witnessing and commissioning, to support design work. Understanding of project resourcing and commercial management would be advantageous. Working knowledge of drawing and model production using Autodesk AutoCAD and Revit. Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications. Excellent communication skills in written and spoken English. WiredScore and/or SmartScore Accredited Professional would be an advantage. Interest or ability to code in languages such as Dynamo and Python would be desirable.

KEY COMPETENCIES · Strong technical drawing and detailing skills using AutoCAD and/or BIM software. · Solid understanding of façade systems, architectural finishes, and construction methods. · High attention to detail, accuracy, and consistency in documentation. · Strong coordination and communication skills across departments and disciplines. · Ability to prioritize and manage multiple drawing packages under tight deadlines. · Commitment to quality, precision, and continuous improvement. REQUIREMENTS · Diploma or Certificate in Civil Engineering, Architecture, Building & Construction, or a related field. · Minimum 3–5 years of drafting experience in architectural or façade projects. · Proficiency in AutoCAD is essential; experience with other BIM/drafting software is an advantage. · Familiar with architectural and structural detailing, fabrication drawings, and construction documentation. · Knowledge of BCA and local authority standards preferred. · Able to work independently with minimal supervision and part of a collaborative project team. · Strong problem-solving skills and willingness to learn and adapt to new tools or systems. · Self-motivated, proactive, and able to work independently with minimal supervision. title: DESIGN & CONSTRUCTION DIRECTOR – APAC

Industrious’s workplace product and leading its delivery across the region. You will be accountable for product strategy, design leadership, and delivery performance across the region, ensuring each space reflects our brand, supports operational excellence, and delivers a best-in-class workplace experience while remaining commercially disciplined and scalable. This role combines design-led product ownership, strategic leadership, and portfolio-level delivery oversight. You will lead senior design and construction leaders, oversee external consultants and partners, and work closely with Real Estate, Finance, Operations, and regional leadership to shape and execute The Great Room & Industrious’ APAC growth. A strong architectural background and exceptional planning and spatial strategy capability, as well as a deep understanding and experience of construction project management and procurement are essential for
this role. This position reports to the International D&C Director and will be based in Singapore, with regular travel across the
APAC region. What you’ll do Product & Design Leadership ●     Own the definition and evolution of Industrious’ and TGR APAC workplace product, including space typologies, planning principles, test-fit strategies, and design standards. ●     Set the architectural and spatial planning vision for the region, ensuring all projects balance experience, operational performance, buildability, and capital efficiency. ●     Ensure consistent, high-quality planning outcomes across all markets, with controlled localisation for regulatory and cultural differences. ●     Govern the evolution of design standards, kits-of-parts, and product playbooks to support scalability and speed to market. ●     Review base-building documentation and site constraints to guide early feasibility, test-fits, and commercial decision-making. ●     Partner with Operations, Commercial, and Real Estate teams to ensure strong product-market fit and operational performance. ●     Protect product integrity throughout value engineering and delivery. Delivery & Portfolio Oversight ●     Provide senior leadership and oversight across the full project lifecycle from feasibility through design, construction, and launch. ●     Own portfolio-level performance across cost, schedule, quality, risk, and predictability. ●     Ensure consistent governance, stage gates, and approval processes across all APAC projects and markets. ●     Oversee regional pipeline planning, forecasting, and performance reporting. ●     Intervene where projects, markets, or partners are underperforming to protect outcomes. ●     Ensure handover to Operations meets product, technical, and operational readiness
standards. Team & Organisation Leadership ●     Lead the APAC Product, Design & Construction organisation through senior managers. ●     Grow, support, and mentor, providing clear direction, performance management, and professional development. ●     Define team structure, resourcing models, and capability requirements to support current and future growth. ●     Build a design-led, commercially disciplined, and delivery-focused culture across the region. ●     Establish clear roles, responsibilities, and ways of working between regional and local teams. Vendor & Partner Management ●     Own the regional ecosystem of designers, engineers, project managers, general contractors, and key suppliers. ●     Establish and maintain regional and local partner strategies, including framework agreements and preferred partner lists. ●     Set standards for consultant performance, technical quality, and commercial accountability. ●     Lead senior-level commercial negotiations and strategic partnerships. ●     Regularly review partner performance, capacity, and risk across all markets. Strategic & Financial Oversight ●     Own the relationship between product ambition and capital discipline across the region. ●     Define and manage: o  CapEx frameworks o  Cost benchmarks o  Budget strategies o  Value engineering principles ●     Maintain financial and commercial oversight across all projects, ensuring early visibility of risks and variances. ●     Ensure design and product decisions are commercially grounded and scalable. ●     Contribute to regional growth strategy, market entry planning, and long-term development strategy for APAC. Who you are Requirements & Qualities ●     Degree in Architecture or Interior Architecture is essential. ●     Minimum 12–15+ years’ experience across design, development, and delivery of commercial interiors, hospitality, or mixed-use projects. ●     Exceptional architectural planning and spatial strategy capability, with a strong track record in test-fits, feasibility, and layout optimisation. ●     Proven experience owning and evolving a product or design platform, not just delivering individual projects. ●     Significant experience leading multi-disciplinary teams and senior managers. ●     Experience operating across multiple countries and regulatory environments, ideally in APAC. ●     Strong commercial and financial acumen, with experience managing portfolio-level CapEx, budgets, and cost control. ●     Ability to read and challenge architectural, MEP, and technical documentation at a senior level. ●     Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence at executive level. ●     Highly organised, strategic, and comfortable operating in a fast-growth, multi-market environment. ●     Comfortable working in a virtual, distributed team environment and travelling frequently across APAC. ●     Commitment to modelling Industrious’ and TGR values of integrity, teamwork, and ownership day to day. ●     Open to candidates willing to relocate to Singapore What’s in it for you ●     Competitive remuneration ●     Career progression ●     5-day work week (some weekends or evenings if required) ●     Annual Leave ●     Birthday Leave ●     Monthly mobile allowance ●     Flexible benefits for medical and other approved wellness, healthcare activities etc. ●     Beautiful workplace environment that fosters collaboration and interactions with others from a diverse background of cultures ●      Opportunity to work with various teams across regional locations Does this sound like you? description: About GlobalFoundries GlobalFoundries is a leading full-service semiconductor foundry providing a unique combination of design, development, and fabrication services to some of the world’s most inspired technology companies. With a global manufacturing footprint spanning three continents, GlobalFoundries makes possible the technologies and systems that transform industries and give customers the power to shape their markets. Your primary mission will be to develop and deploy interactive data visualizations and digital applications that directly optimize and improve our manufacturing processes. You will be key to turning complex data into the actionable insights required to drive our digital transformation success. Your Job : Visualization Design & Development: Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau) to provide key business insights and support data-driven decision-making. Data Analysis & Insight Generation: Perform statistical analysis and data plotting to solve complex business problems, derive actionable insights, and create clear correlation models for functional teams. Project & Stakeholder Management: Manage the full life cycle of data visualization projects, from requirements gathering with functional teams to final delivery, serving as the primary liaison between business stakeholders and IT/Engineering teams. Data Sourcing & Modeling: Execute data discovery and cataloging to efficiently locate and understand internal data sources. Apply foundational knowledge of data models and schemas to perform source-to-target mapping, ensuring accurate, structured data for reporting. Data Pipeline Collaboration: Work closely with data engineering teams, utilizing familiarity with AWS services and exposure to PySpark to understand and collaborate on the transformation scripts and data pipelines that process large volumes of manufacturing data. Cross-Functional Teamwork: Work closely with the Analytics Center of Excellence Manager and other stakeholders to define project objectives, deliverables, and timelines. Required Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in a computer science, information technology, or related fields 2 years of experience and knowledge in data visualization with a strong understanding of both front-end and back-end technologies. Proficiency in data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker). Familiarity with AWS cloud environment and data transformation tools such as PySpark. Proficiency in front-end and back-end technologies. Experience with API development and integration. Experience with version control systems and familiarity with Agile development methodologies. Excellent communication and collaboration skills. Ability work independently and as part of the cross-functional team. Strong problem-solving and analytical skills with a passion for building high-quality digital applications. GlobalFoundries is an equal opportunity employer, cultivating a diverse and inclusive workforce. We believe having a multicultural workplace enhances productivity, efficiency and innovation whilst our employees feel truly respected, valued and heard. As an affirmative employer, all qualified applicants are considered for employment regardless of age, ethnicity, marital status, citizenship, race, religion, political affiliation, gender, sexual orientation and medical and/or physical abilities. title: Senior Project Manager – Procurement & FF&E (Interior Design)
description:
Role Overview The Senior Project Manager – Procurement & FF&E is responsible for the strategic planning and hands-on execution of procurement , alongside the end-to-end delivery of multiple interior design projects across hospitality, commercial, and high-end residential sectors . The role spans all interior design disciplines and manages projects for both B2B clients (developers, operators, corporate clients) and B2C clients (private residential clients), across Singapore, the UAE, and Australia . This position holds full accountability for procurement strategy —including sourcing approach, supplier and manufacturer selection, budgeting, lead-time planning, logistics planning, and risk mitigation—as well as the physical placement and management of orders , from issuance of purchase orders through to delivery, installation, and final reconciliation. Working closely with the Creative Director, General Manager, and Senior Design Team , and in daily coordination with the Finance team , the role ensures that procurement and project decisions align with design intent, programme, cash flow, and overall commercial performance . Key Responsibilities 1. Project Management & Turnkey Delivery Manage multiple interior design projects concurrently across hospitality, commercial, and residential sectors Work in close alignment with Lead Designers to support and facilitate delivery from early coordination and feasibility , through design development, procurement, construction, installation, and final handover Develop and maintain project programmes, milestones, budgets, and cash flow schedules Support and coordinate internal design teams , external consultants (architects, MEP, QS), contractors, and suppliers Lead and attend site meetings as required, monitor site progress, and ensure delivery aligns with approved drawings, specifications, and design intent Identify and manage project risks, scope changes, and variations , ensuring clear documentation, approvals, and early escalation of variations, out-of-scope works, or cost impacts 2. Procurement Strategy (Planning &
Control) Develop and lead, in collaboration with the Senior Design Team , the procurement strategy for each project, including: Sourcing approach (local vs international) Supplier and manufacturer selection Lead-time planning and procurement phasing Logistics, warehousing, and installation sequencing Risk assessment and mitigation strategies Prepare and manage procurement schedules, specifications, budgets, and cash flow forecasts , aligned with the project programme Advise the Creative Director and General Manager on procurement decisions that impact design intent, programme, and financial exposure Present procurement strategies, recommendations, and approvals clearly and professionally to B2B and B2C clients 3. Procurement Execution & Order Placement Take direct responsibility for the placement of all FF&E and related procurement orders , including: Issuing purchase orders Managing supplier confirmations and documentation Tracking production, delivery, and installation timelines Lead price negotiations, payment terms, and delivery conditions with suppliers and manufacturers Ensure all orders are placed strictly in line with approved budgets, scopes, cash flow plans, and internal approvals Manage substitutions, re-selections, and supply chain changes as required, while protecting design intent and quality 4. Financial & Commercial Management Work closely with the Finance team to: Track procurement commitments and expenditure Manage deposits, staged payments, and final balances Support accurate forecasting and invoicing Monitor project margins, procurement exposure, and cash flow risks Ensure all financial controls, approval protocols, and internal governance requirements are followed Ensure SOPs are strictly adhered to for order placement, including verification that deposits and client payments have been received prior to placing orders, to ensure ALBD is not financially exposed or out of pocket 5. Supply Chain, Logistics & Installation Oversee production timelines, quality control, shipping, customs clearance, warehousing, and delivery Coordinate on-site delivery, installation, styling, snagging, and defects rectification Ensure all FF&E and finishes are installed in accordance with approved specifications, drawings, and design intent close-out documentation, warranties, and final financial and procurement reconciliations 6. Client & Stakeholder Management Act as a senior client-facing lead , providing confident, structured, and solutions-oriented communication Manage expectations across B2B and B2C clients , balancing the design ambition of the Design Lead with programme, budget, and delivery realities Support the Creative Director and General Manager in client presentations, procurement approvals, and progress reporting , ensuring all information is client-ready, accurate, and aligned to enableeffective expectation management Build and maintain trusted relationships with clients, consultants, suppliers, and contractors 7. Quality, Process & Team Collaboration Uphold high standards of design integrity, workmanship, and finish quality Contribute to the refinement and improvement of internal project management, procurement systems, and SOPs Work collaboratively across design, operations, and finance to ensure aligned, efficient delivery Required Experience & Qualifications Minimum 8 years’ experience in interior design–led project management and FF&E procurement Proven experience delivering turnkey interior projects from ground-up to handover Demonstrated ownership of both procurement strategy and order placement Experience across hospitality, commercial, and residential interiors Strong understanding of construction sequencing, FF&E, and site coordination International sourcing and logistics experience across UAE, Australia, and China is highly advantageous Singaporean / Permanent Resident / Work Permit Skills & Competencies Strong project, procurement, and financial management capability Commercially astute with excellent negotiation skills Highly organised, detail-driven, meticulous , and solutions-focused Confident communicator across senior stakeholders and clients Design-literate with a deep respect for creative intent and execution quality Personal Attributes Calm under pressure and comfortable managing complexity Proactive, accountable, and able to operate autonomously Collaborative leader with strong professional presence Adaptable across cultures, geographies, and client types Mandarin-speaking preferred Comfortable working with industry-standard software (e.g. project management, procurement tracking, and financial systems)

title: Mechanical Design Engineer
description: Job Purpose We are seeking a highly skilled and self-motivated Mechanical Design Engineer capable of independently handling mechanical design tasks. The candidate will be responsible for designing, analyzing, and optimizing mechanical systems and components for various projects, ensuring compliance with industry standards, safety regulations, and client requirements. The engineer will also participate project tender and site to understand customer enquiry and expectation, develop concept design and support procurement team, communicate with customer, vendor and supplier to improve proposal process and quality. Key Responsibilities 1. HVAC System Design : Establish cleanroom design according to client desire and production need, prepare cleanroom architectural layout dwg by using AutoCAD, Revit, or other design software. Develop mechanical HVAC system design following cleanroom design, prepare HVAC P&ID. Calculate mechanical load requirements and size components such as HVAC heatload calculation, fan ESP sizing, pump head calculation, duct/pipe sizing, HVAC equipment model selection. Establish HVAC control philosophy write-up, cause and effect analysis, develop alarm list and interlock mechanism. 2. Mechanical System Design : Establish process utility design following customer’s tools matrix summary, such as CDA, Vacuum, PCW, Steam, Exhaust, Drain, Specialty Gas, RODI, WWTP and etc Develop process utility P&ID and piping/ducting layout. Develop plant room space management study and services routing from plant room to production floor. Calculate mechanical load requirements and size components such utility pump/fan sizing, pipe/duct sizing, utility flow calculation. Establish process utility system control philosophy write-up, cause and effect analysis, develop alarm list and interlock mechanism. 3. Project Tendering : Review tender requirements, participate site visit, communicate with client to ensure the proposals meet client and project specifications. Develop concept dwg and equipment schedule to enhance vendor and supplier knowledge on tender specification and specific challenges. Participate tender costing exercise, support procurement team on proposal preparation. Support procurement team to evaluate vendor/supplier proposal qualification, materials/equipment selection, ensure proposed solution sound practicable and reasonable, meeting quality and regulatory compliance, adhere tender scope of work, delivery schedule, deviations and partial compliance element. Collaborate with vendors to improve competitive pricing for project tenders. 4. Project Management : Coordinate with clients, contractors, and project stakeholders to clarify requirements and resolve design-related issues. Conduct system design presentation to explain proposed design, equipment selection, comparison of different design approach, convince client’s doubt and persuade business decision. 5. Documentation : Prepare technical documents, includes MEP dwg, equipment schedule, RFQ/RFP, sub-contract work package specifications. Maintain accurate and up-to-date project files and records. 6. Collaboration : Work closely with the electrical, control, civil, and architectural teams to ensure seamless integration of HVAC and mechanical systems into overall project designs and execution. Review and evaluate supplier/vendor proposal, assist QS and project team on equipment selection and seek supplier/vendor clarification on design specification compliance and performance assurance. Qualifications and Skills 1. Educational Background : Bachelor’s degree in mechanical, manufacturing, process engineering, or Industrial related engineering field. 2. Experience : Minimum of [4] years of experience in mechanical design engineering, with a proven track record of handling projects independently. 3. Technical Skills : Proficient in mechanical design software such as AutoCAD, Revit or similar tools and Microsoft Office. Decent knowledge of hvac and mechanical codes and standards (e.g. ASHRAE, ISO-14644, SMAGNA) Computer skills - ACAD, MS Project, BIM Experience with project bidding, develop BOQ, material sourcing, compile project quotations. 4. Soft Skills : Strong problem-solving and analytical skills. Self-motivated and capable of working independently with minimal supervision. Effective communication: capable of drafting clear transmittals, datasheets and technical queries; comfortable liaising with vendors and site teams to clarify requirements. 5. Additional Skills (Option) : Electrical LV wiring design experience, such as cable sizing and develop SLD for LCP of mechanical system. Understand general control and instrumentation logic diagram for mechanical system. Understand BIM and able to perform simple BIM modelling. Mobility as when needed.

title: Global Facilities and Construction Staff Architect
description: Job Description As the Global Facilities & Construction (South Asia)– Senior Architect , you provide high‑quality architectural leadership and technical authority for the planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance of facilities systems supporting Micron’s global semiconductor manufacturing network. You will act as a subject matter expert throughout all project phases – from concept design and construction to installation, on-going operations, and maintenance – ensuring environmental, safety, regulatory, and code compliance. Key Responsibilities Architectural Design & Leadership Lead and define architectural design strategies for semiconductor facilities, including FABs, CUPs, Probe, Admin Building, and Miscellaneous Buildings. Develop conceptual designs, detailed plans, and specifications in compliance with Micron’s global standards and local/international codes. Provide technical support during construction, including submittal reviews, evaluating GC-initiated changes, developing project modifications, and conducting QA/QC reviews of design documents (e.g., architectural plans, specifications, and calculations). Perform technical reviews of structural design work by A&E firms, GC design teams, and sub-consultants to ensure quality and compliance. Prepare architectural design, RFPs, design documentation reports, and develop architectural layout plans, specifications, and cost estimates for projects. Oversee layout, interior design, and space planning for FABs, CUPs, Probe, Admin, and Miscellaneous Buildings. Conduct field inspections during construction to verify compliance with scope, schedule, and budget. Apply International Building Code (IBC) for exiting, fire compartmentation, LEED, Green Mark, sustainability, occupancy, and building classifications. Design specialized cleanroom components, including raised floors, demountable walls, and ceiling grid systems. Ensure constructability in complex environments, minimizing disruptions near operating buildings. Lead alignment of facility design standards, construction project management practices, and O&M best-known methods (BKM). Perform regular field inspections and monitor projects to ensure adherence to Micron specifications, codes, regulations, and agreed scope, schedule, and budget. Validate cost estimates and construction schedules for architectural aspects of projects. Review and approve design drawings and specifications for completeness and accuracy. Evaluate architectural drawing submissions and supporting documentation to ensure design integrity. Review Autodesk and BIM 3D models for functionality, egress, maintenance accessibility, constructability, and clash analyses coordination with other engineering disciplines. Inspect construction and installation progress for conformance to established drawings, specifications, and schedules. Support all sites, including on-site presence for construction projects and resolution of critical architectural issues Collaborate with consultants, contractors, and multidisciplinary teams to ensure timely, cost-effective project execution. Site Master Planning (SMP) & Space Optimization Develop, propose, and collaborate with global and site teams to generate preliminary site master plan (SMP) concept designs for greenfield and brownfield projects aligned with company strategic goals. Collaborate with multi-functional facilities teams to identify opportunities for space planning optimization and establish standards for critical facilities, rooms and areas. Standardize and continuously improve facilities space management for critical systems, leveraging industry best practices and lessons learned. Assess and mitigate risks related to space constraints in brownfield projects by conducting clash checks during preliminary stages and providing support for project continuity. Project Leadership & Strategic Alignment Lead design and construction of regional greenfield and brownfield projects in alignment with global standards. Collaborate with central sustainability, cost, construction, and line planning teams to ensure strategic alignment. Provide technical support for site projects, Alpha to Beta technology transfer, and sustainability initiatives. Review and approve site capacity expansion and sustainability CAPEX proposals. Evaluate priorities to enable business-driven decisions on Facilities Reliability Improvement project CAPEX. Reliability & Strategic Program Support Provide architectural support for site improvement projects, including System Reliability Improvements, Quality Enhancements, and Equipment End-of-Life (EOL) replacements. Oversee regional efforts and identify opportunities to align strategic programs (e.g., BCP, Critical Specifications, Quality Defense Line). Support major Site Infrastructure Planning reviews and ensure architectural compliance. Sustainability & Certifications Champion LEED and Green Mark certification processes for new and existing projects. Integrate sustainable design principles and energy-efficient solutions into all architectural plans. Support the network effort to implement, monitor and drive continuous improvement for success of Micron’s Sustainability programs in alignment with the long-term Sustainability strategy. In depth, review of site sustainability projects and sustainability data monitoring to support sites and company’s sustainability targets. Support sustainability projects achievement data acquisition and validation in central tracking depository. Interior Design & Space Optimization Oversee interior design for cleanrooms, office spaces, and collaborative environments. Ensure ergonomic, functional, and aesthetic excellence in space planning. Project Oversight & Coordination Manage architectural deliverables throughout design and construction phases. Collaborate with consultants, contractors, and multidisciplinary teams to ensure timely, cost-effective project execution. Compliance & Standards Ensure adherence to local and international building codes, safety regulations, and Micron’s internal standards. Manage permitting and regulatory approval processes. Qualifications Education: Master’s or bachelor’s degree in architecture. Experience: Minimum 10 years of architectural experience in industrial construction projects & minimum 5 year in Semiconductor and/or facilities related industry. Technical expertise, knowledge, and experience on building design and construction of Semiconductor Wafer Fab building and CUB building. Strong background in consulting and construction for fast-track large-scale semiconductor/industrial projects. Certifications: LEED and Green Mark (or equivalent) experience required. Skills: Proficiency in CAD software and BIM tools Expertise in semiconductor cleanroom design and high-tech manufacturing environments. Good knowledge and understanding of semiconductor manufacturing plants and local construction codes (preferably with fundamental knowledge of semiconductor manufacturing processes). Excellent interpersonal skills, strong collaboration and analytical abilities, ability to work independently, and proven team leadership experience. Ability to prioritize and schedule workload and work on multiple tasks concurrently. Ability to travel 15-25% of the time on average, but up to 30% of the time during Critical Project phases or Site Activities. Preferred: Professional Architect licensure.

title: Adjunct Lecturer
description:
A premier university in Asia, the Singapore Management University (SMU) is internationally recognised for its world-class research
 and distinguished teaching. Established in 2000, SMU’s mission is to generate
 leading-edge research with global impact and produce broad-based, creative and
 entrepreneurial leaders for the knowledge-based economy. SMU education is
 known for its highly interactive, collaborative and project-based approach to
 learning, and for its technologically enabled pedagogy of seminar-style
 teaching in small class sizes. The School of Computing and
 Information Systems (SCIS) offers an
 exceptionally supportive and inspirational environment for academic
 scholarship and research, applied R&D projects, and innovative education
 initiatives. We currently have over 40 tenure-track faculty members working
 across research areas in computer science & engineering, and information
 systems management. They are complemented by over 30 practice & education
 faculty members who focus on teaching, translation and pedagogical research. Our Research. We are passionate about our research . We aim for excellence both in fundamental contribution to academic
 knowledge, as well as in creating technology with practical impact. Real-world
 industry sectors provide SCIS with a testbed and laboratory for
 experimentation, as well as a fertile breeding ground for new ideas. Our
 faculty and students apply their research results to solve real problems in a
 variety of industry settings and to create IT applications and systems. At the
 same time, our faculty actively publish in top-quality Computer Science and
 Management Science conferences and journals. On our faculty are several
 world-class scholars and rising stars in their respective disciplines. We are committed
 to supporting the development of our faculty. Besides start-up support and
 career mentoring for junior faculty, their Ph.D. students are funded by the
 school rather than paid from their research grants. SCIS faculty members are
 encouraged to collaborate on innovative research projects and also on
 integrative and interdisciplinary projects. SMU as well as Singapore
 government agencies, such as the National Research Foundation, provide
 generous support for high-quality research proposals. Our Education. SCIS offers the Ph.D. in Information Systems and Ph.D. in Computer Science programmes. Ph.D. students can
 concentrate in any of our computer science oriented research areas, or in our
 management science oriented Information Systems & Management research
 area. SCIS launched a
 Professional Doctoral Degree, Doctor
 of Engineering (EngD) in 2020. The
 programme aims to train students to become IT leaders with deep technical
 expertise for innovating, designing and managing complex IT systems. SCIS also
 offers Master of IT in
 Business (MITB), a professionally oriented
 coursework programme. Drawing from thought leadership in the ambits of data
 analytics, technological platforms and business strategies, the MITB programme
 delves into five specialisation tracks: Financial Technology & Analytics, Data
 Science & Analytics, Artificial Intelligence, Digital Transformation and
 Cybersecurity. Each of these tracks uniquely resources students to lead
 competently and decisively . Additionally, the
 undergraduate programmes offerings consist of the B.Sc.
 (Computer Science) , B.Sc.
 (Information Systems), B.Sc.
 (Computing & Law) and B.Sc. Software Engineering programmes. The programmes have been remarkably successful in
 demonstrating educational innovations and creating a culture of learning,
 establishing external linkages and partnerships with industry, government and
 the social sector, and with job placement. We have an entrepreneurial student
 body, and many SCIS undergraduates have gone on to create or participate in start-ups . Job Requirement Candidate
 requirements are as follows: Capable of
      delivering postgraduate courses in the area of Payment
      Technology and Operations. Masters degree or PhD
      degree in a closely related discipline from a well-recognized university . o   For example, a
 Masters or PhD in Computer Science, Information Technology, Information
 Systems, Statistics, Management Science, Decision Sciences, Marketing
 Analytics, Computational Physics, Computational Engineering or Science, and
 the like. Outstanding
      communication skills A
      passion for learning and teaching Prior
      experience with consulting, training, or teaching is a strong advantage.

description: Welcome! Are you ready to be at the forefront of ground breaking Engineering within the semiconductor industry?! Micron Technology, Inc., a global leader in memory and storage solutions, is seeking a dedicated and experienced Senior Engineer to join our Chemical team within the Global Facilities Engineering department. In this crucial role, you will play a vital part in providing high-quality Engineering services and support for the planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance of our subject area specific Facilities systems across our Semiconductor Manufacturing sites worldwide for Chemical systems and Slurry systems. Responsibilities: Technical Support, Leadership, and Expertise Act as the Technical Field Domain Expert, offering consultation and support to all Site Facilities and Manufacturing teams. Collaborate with internal partners such as Technology Development, Procurement, Finance, and Legal to champion multi-functional collaboration. Develop, review, and maintain companies global system, equipment, and component Standards, Specifications, and Best Known Methods (BKMs) to ensure excellence in engineering practices. Implement Standardization and Alignment across Micron's Global Network and Sites for Engineering and Design concepts, Construction Project execution, Reliability Programs deployment, and Operations and Maintenance practices. Find opportunities for improvement in system designs to improve performance, efficiency gains, reduce maintenance, and achieve cost savings. Benchmark both within and outside Micron to capture Engineering Best Practices and Well Known Methods for Facilities buildings and systems. Stay up-to-date with industry trends, technological advances, emerging technology roadmaps, design innovations, and sustainability practices. Design and Construction Project Support: Provide valuable support to the Design teams in the Engineering development of new large-scale Facilities Infrastructure Expansion projects. Ensure that designs align with all applicable standards, codes, and regulations. Build and refine system design concepts for deployment. Ensure effective communication with local Facilities, Design, Construction, Procurement, and Manufacturing partners to deliver project objectives seamlessly. Conduct timely and thorough review of design packages, offering detailed comments and feedback to ensure the success of the projects. Assist in providing cost and schedule estimates and justifications for project approvals. Supervise progress and provide engineering expertise during construction as required per project if vital. Participate and lead capacity databases and system operating trends throughout the network, validating utilization and diversity factor assumptions. Review and guide on scope, schedule, and cost estimates for Facilities infrastructure additions or modifications. Global Initiatives Program Support: Take charge of Global Facilities Programs & Initiatives, including establishing and defining program objectives, roadmaps, and high-level implementation plans. Lead Sustainability Programs in alignment with Micron's Corporate Sustainability goals and initiatives. Job Requirements: Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical, Chemical, Process, or Industrial Systems Engineering required. Minimum 7 to 10years experience in Semiconductor and/or Facilities related industry. Technical expertise, knowledge, and experience on Facilities and Semiconductor Chemical Systems including Bulk & Normal Chemical Systems, Chemical Blending Systems, Slurry Systems, Valve Manifold Boxes, Hazardous and High Purity Chemicals and Piping, and Chemical Distribution Systems. Ability to prioritize and schedule workload and work on multiple tasks concurrently. Self-motivated worker, good organizational skills and service demeanor. Excellent communication skills and good team working skills, English Required. Familiarity with Semiconductor Manufacturing and Facilities systems. Ability to travel internationally 25-40% on average, but up to 50-66% of the time during Critical Project phases or Site Activities. Provides engineering for semiconductor manufacturing site facilities changes for plant, office, and production equipment layouts, including semiconductor tool installations and R&D applications, working toward economy of operation, maximum use of facilities and equipment, and compliance with laws and regulations. Prepares bid sheets and contracts for construction and facilities acquisition. Completes engineering for requested changes to develop estimates for design costs including equipment, installation, labor, materials, preparation and other related costs for semiconductor manufacturing sites, including R&D applications. Inspects construction and installation progress for conformance to established drawings, specifications, and schedules. Develops criteria and performance specifications required to meet unique operating requirements and building and safety codes.

title: Senior UX Designer - X Delivery
description: Who We Are Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact. To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive. We Are BCG X We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions. What You'll Do As a part of BCG X, you will work closely with consulting teams on a diverse range of advanced topics. You will have the opportunity to leverage software development methodologies to deliver value to BCG's Consulting & BCG X (case) teams, BCG X Product teams and Practice Areas (domains) through providing design and user experience subject matter expertise, and accelerated execution support. You will collaborate with teams to gather requirements, specify, design, develop, deliver and support software solutions serving client needs. You will provide technical support through deeper understanding of relevant software solutions and processes to build high quality and efficient technology solutions. Assignments will range from short term Proof of concepts/Minimum viable products to long term cases with enterprise grade software development as a critical enabler through the project level description of the role responsibilities and impact within the organization. As a UX/UI Designer you will work towards establishing a customer-centric design culture and developing a fit-for purpose self-service data product as the company moves to become a product centric organisation. Responsibilities Demonstrate solid understanding and experience of common visual and interaction design principles and practice, and create appropriate sketches, wireframes, flows, and prototypes. Value and apply existing customer insights from research to guide design decisions as well as initiating and supporting research efforts with research partners. Frame problems and opportunities clearly through analysis of qualitative and quantitative inputs, and customers and stakeholders’ perspectives. Propose creative communicative visualizations to deliver research insights to teams and stakeholders (eg. User journeys, personas, systemic maps). Triangulate stakeholder vision, user insights, and cross-functional team discussion inputs, and integrate insights into design proposition. Master the phases of design from initial sketching to wireframing and high-fidelity prototyping. Apply and maintain design systems to deliver scalable, consistent UI components and interactions; adapt designs across platforms and devices to ensure cohesive user experiences. Demonstrate experience in testing and iterating based on feedback, both with stakeholders and with users. Create routine, informative communication materials explaining design decision-making rationale to team members and stakeholders. Partner with product managers, engineers, and consultants to solve for customer problems leveraging an agile way of working. Understand and incorporate desirability, feasibility, viability and strategic stakeholder constraints into the design. Use ideation, design, and testing tools effectively (e.g. Figma, Miro, Dovetail); experience with GenAI and vibe coding tools (e.g. Bolt, Cursor, V0, Lovable) is a plus. What You'll Bring We are looking for candidates with 1–3 years of experience (Analyst Level) or 4–5 years of experience (Senior Level) in UX Strategy, UX Research, UX Design, and UI Design. You've worked with a variety of tools; you'll be able to recommend and use what you think is best e.g. Figma, Adobe Suite and others. You have a wealth of experience creating design wireframes, prototypes, and mock-ups for developers to convert to code. You have design thinking experience to discover problems and come up with creative solutions by thoroughly understanding users' goals, frustrations, and end-task. You're collaborative and pragmatic with excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, happy to mentor and develop others. You have experience working in multi-disciplinary settings (with design, engineering and product teams). Bachelor's degree or equivalent in; Visual Design, Human-Computer Interaction, User Experience, or related Design field. You have a curious and entrepreneurial mindset, and you are ready to dive into different industries, types of user and stakeholder problems.

Oversee multiple projects to resolve issues and enhance product performance, reliability, and efficiency. Lead / mentor group of Supplier Development team on best practices and develop skillsets to support group growth and expansion What we are looking for MS, or PhD in Engineering, Science, or related technical field (Engineering, Physics, Material Sciences) relevant to semiconductor equipment, or proven expertise. At least 10 years of experience or significant expertise in engineering, designing, and manufacturing of complex mechanical systems. Experience in the semiconductor capital equipment industry with technical background in semiconductor manufacturing technologies required. Strong English communication skills for complex technical topics. Effective collaboration across regions, cultures, and disciplines. Proven Lead Engineer in matrix environments and ability to work independently while managing a team. We make the tech that enables the chips in devices which improve lives around the world. We do this with an eye to the future, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible through cutting-edge innovation, and driving the next wave of technological breakthroughs that shape how we live, work, and connect. To learn more about ASM, find us at asm.com and on LinkedIn , Facebook , Instagram, X and YouTube . title: SALES MANAGER/EXECUTIVE
description: If you are a highly motivated, results-driven professional with a passion for the architecture and interior design industry, we encourage you to apply. We are seeking an experienced and well connected individual with strong relationships across the design, furniture, interior design, architecture, and tradeshow sectors, supported by a proven track record in event sales. The ideal candidate is a proactive hunter with the ambition to grow and expand an existing client portfolio, and who is motivated by attractive rewards for meeting and exceeding targets. There is also clear potential for growth into senior roles for candidates whose next career step aligns with this path. About Fiera Milano Asia Pacific & Events Fiera Milano Asia Pacific sets out to manage a portfolio of international exhibitions, conferences, and trade events across sectors including design, lifestyle, furniture, architecture, hospitality, industrial technology, and food. Our events, held across Asia, bring together exhibitors, buyers, and industry professionals to foster business growth, networking, and knowledge exchange. Our initiatives support a variety of products, including flagship trade shows like FIND – Design Fair Asia, as well as industry-specific exhibitions, sponsored events, and bespoke initiatives for clients across the region. Event Description FIND - Design Fair Asia is a premier platform showcasing contemporary design trends and innovations. Dedicated to connecting suppliers of furniture, lighting, kitchen, bathroom, and homeware with the flourishing Asian market, the event opens pathways for business expansion in the region. Hosted at the prestigious Marina Bay Sands, Sands Expo & Convention Centre in Singapore, FIND - Design Fair Asia gathers the regional architecture and design community in a lively environment. It serves as a hub for inspiration, networking, and business opportunities. For more details, visit www.designfairasia.com. Role Description This is a full-time, on-site Sales Manager role based in Singapore. Reporting to senior sales manager / director, you will help design and drive the overall sales strategy to secure exhibitors, sponsors, and strategic partners. The position focuses on proactively identifying and converting new business opportunities, deepening relationships with existing clients, and leading contract negotiations through to closure. You will work closely with the marketing and event operations teams to align campaigns, ensure seamless delivery of sold commitments, and consistently hit or exceed revenue targets. Additionally, you will keep a close watch on market trends, pricing movements, and competitor activity to refine positioning and maintain the fair’s competitive edge. This role has no direct management responsibility but may involve mentoring of early career individuals which may become direct reports and supervision of an extensive sales agent network. Qualifications Experience in sales, client relationship management, and negotiation skills. Familiarity with market research, business development, and creating effective sales strategies. Strong communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills to engage with stakeholders successfully. Detail-oriented with organizational and time management abilities to meet deadlines. Proficiency in CRM tools and a solid understanding of the design or events industry is advantageous. Extensive experience (min. 3 years) in B28 conference and exhibition or sponsorship sales Strong knowledge of and familiarity with the dynamics throughout the traditional furniture and interior design sectors, architecture, manufacturing brands or distributors is desired A solid understanding of the tradeshow and exhibition industry including managing sales agents Previous responsibility for ambitious sales targets, demonstrating ability to consistently meet and exceed these targets Collaborate with the marketing team to develop effective marketing strategies to attract exhibitors. Coordinate with the event team to ensure seamless execution of exhibitions. Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, ensuring high levels of customer satisfaction and resolve client issues or concerns promptly and professionally. Ability and proven experience of working with a wide range of cultures. Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously. Prior experience in the design, architecture, or events sector is an asset. About Fiera Milano: Fiera Milano has been organising, hosting and managing international events in Italy and worldwide since 1920. Fiera Milano is one of Europe’s leading exhibition and conference companies, recognized for its world-class venues, deep industry expertise, and a diversified portfolio of trade shows and events. The Group combines venue management, exhibition organisation, and event services into a fully integrated model supporting more than 50 international trade fairs and major congresses every year. Its exhibition district in Rho and the MiCo Convention Centre in Milan rank among the most advanced in the world, hosting global leaders across design, lifestyle, furniture, hospitality, food, fashion, industrial technology, and more. Each year, the Group brings together tens of thousands of exhibitors across more than 50 exhibitions and draws millions of visitors, while generating substantial economic and social impact through its exhibition platform. Fiera Milano is also the official venue for Milano Cortina 2026, hosting key Olympic competitions including ice hockey and speed skating. Across Europe and globally, Fiera Milano is committed to delivering high-quality events, sustainability, digital innovation, and long-term industry development. Application: Do note that Fiera Milano Asia Pacific is currently not accepting any talent that is located outside of Singapore for this position. Interested candidates please apply with an updated CV to: info@FieraMilanoAsiaPacific.com

title: Interior Design & Architectural Coordinator
description: Job Summary: We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Interior Design & Architectural Coordinator to join our construction team. The role involves managing and coordinating interior design (ID) and architectural aspects of projects, ensuring that design intent is accurately implemented on site, in line with project requirements, quality standards, and timelines. Key Responsibilities: Coordinate ID and architectural drawings, shop drawings, and materials with consultants, subcontractors, and site teams. Review design documents to ensure compliance with architectural intent, contract specifications, and site conditions. Identify and resolve design conflicts or discrepancies between trades. Monitor implementation of ID and architectural works on site to ensure quality, workmanship, and adherence to approved plans. Prepare, track, and manage RFIs, design submissions, and project documentation. Attend coordination and technical meetings to support project execution. Assist in sourcing materials, finishes, and fittings in line with project requirements and budgets. Requirements: Minimum 3 years of relevant experience in architectural and interior design coordination within a construction or main contractor environment. Bachelor’s degree in Architecture, Interior Design, or related discipline. Strong knowledge of architectural detailing, interior finishes, and construction processes. Proficient in AutoCAD, Revit, SketchUp, or other design software. Excellent communication, coordination, and problem-solving skills. Detail-oriented, organized, and able to manage multiple tasks under tight deadlines.

title: Lifecycle Product Manager
description: Coffee Meets Bagel is a women-founded, women-led organization created to give everyone a chance at love. The sense of belonging and connectedness we get from relationships not only helps us survive, but thrive, and we’re working to make it a little easier for people to find that. We’re inspired by the stories we hear from employees, friends, and family who have used CMB to transform their lives- and you too can make a difference by joining us! Job Summary As a Lifecycle Product Manager at our company, you’ll be able to work on and solve many interesting challenges. This is a great opportunity for you to define, build and deliver compelling experiences that will increase customer value as measured by user activation, retention, and conversion to paying users. The ideal candidate will have bias for action, have a passion for experimentation, and can roll-up their sleeves to . They are also extremely detail-oriented, careful, and conscientious. The Lifecycle Product Manager will perform product discovery by performing CRM experiments; doing data analysis; creating regular reporting to understand the progress of the campaigns; and consolidating them into PRDs. Finally, as we are a lean team, he or she should be strong at operations, as they will directly be in charge of setting up CRM campaigns. This role will be based in Singapore and report directly to the Head of Product. The Role Responsibilities Lifecycle Strategy and Execution Develop and own the overall user lifecycle roadmap- from testing and experimentation to scaling automated lifecycle programmes Lead the programmatic transformation of lifecycle management in CMB: Brainstorm and suggest new ideas to motivate behaviour based on user psychology (eg. how to get guys to take better photos?) Conduct ad hoc tests and experiments to validate hypotheses Establish a base set of effective, automated lifecycle campaigns Continuously design new segments and test personalised copy, triggers, and message types to improve performance Collaborate with Product and Engineering teams to prioritise and schedule life cycle-related product and data work Iterate based on data insights and learnings to enhance effectiveness Implement CRM campaigns in Braze, both adhoc and programmatic Lead the ideation and execution of sales (eg. how many % off for who?) strategies and products Lead the direct improvement of onboarding conversion rates through prospective user management Segmentation and Personalisation Strategise, build, and manage audience segments across channels    Programmatically personalise both engagement and monetisation user journeys based on: Demographics: gender, age, country Behaviour: likes received, chat activity, login activity Account Quality: profile completeness, photos, and prompt quality. Triggers: actions requiring follow-up (e.g., abandoned onboarding, inactive users, incomplete purchases) ...via sales campaigns, in-app pushes, push notifications, and email experiences Data-Driven Optimisation Write and execute SQL queries identify key engagement and conversion drivers Set up experimentation frameworks (A/B tests, holdout groups) to measure impact Analyse results and translate insights into actionable improvements across CRM touchpoints Report and recommend on the improvement of lifecycle health according to engagement, retention, and revenue metrics Cross-Functional Collaboration Work closely with Marketing, Design, and Engineering to ensure seamless CRM execution and integration with product experiences Influence messaging logic, in-app surfaces, and technical infrastructure needed for lifecycle management scalability Our Ideal Candidate 0-3 years of managing a consumer-facing product as a Product Manager in a Technology company (open to fresh grads) Has worked in a day-to-day capacity with a scrum team building actual products Strong user empathy and analytical skills Can confidently write and execute their own SQL with no data team support (or, with other programming language knowledge, can prove to us that  they can learn) Is detail-oriented, willing and conscientious to execute on and QA operational lifecycle campaigns by him/ herself Has experience working with other functions (marketing, operations) to launch a product from end to end Is comfortable in a fast-paced environment interacting with global teams Ideally possess practical communication skills across English and Mandarin, both written and verbal to work with mandarin stakeholders. Join us and be part of a team dedicated to crafting meaningful connections through innovative design solutions. If you thrive in a collaborative environment and are passionate about building great products, we’d love to hear from you!

title: UX Researcher
description: As a UX Researcher, you will play a critical role in helping company develop deep, actionable insights into the needs, behaviors, and desires of our customers. This is essential for the creation and continuous improvement of our products and services. This role involves planning and executing research studies, interfacing directly with customers, synthesising qualitative and quantitative data and insights across the organisation, contributing to the research strategy, and working closely with senior stakeholders and the wider UX research team. What you'll be doing You will contribute to the UX Research function by carrying out both qualitative and quantitative research across company’s digital product portfolio. Your responsibilities include: Research Execution: Oversee and conduct a broad range of generative, iterative, and evaluative design research activities, such as user interviews, surveys, usability tests (web and mobile), A/B testing, and card sorting. Insight Generation: Synthesise complex qualitative and quantitative data to identify trends, patterns, and actionable insights, ensuring meticulous documentation of findings. Strategic Mixed-Methods Synthesis: Move beyond single-study reports to blend qualitative insights with quantitative datasets (like CSAT trends and product analytics), creating a single, high-confidence narrative for product strategy. Organisational Translation: Act as a research consultant across multiple product lines, identifying patterns that impact the whole company while translating specific nuances for individual teams. Stakeholder Communication: Create compelling research reports and presentations for senior management that present analysis and recommendations succinctly and clearly, translating research findings into actionable product, design, and business strategies. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work collaboratively with designers, product managers, engineering teams, and business partners. Participate in design brainstorming sessions and workshops to guide design decisions and collaborate on potential solutions to issues in a timely manner. Requirements At least 4 years of experience in UX research. Proven ability to design and synthesise quantitative research, leveraging data from CSAT, surveys, and analytics to triangulate findings and recommend next steps. Direct experience with diverse research methods, including both traditional and modern approaches to recruitment and reporting. Proficient in user research principles, methodologies, and deliverables, such as service design blueprints, journey maps, and process flows. Ability to select the right research and synthesise timely insights into coherent, actionable deliverables for design, product, business, and technology teams. Expertise in defining research objectives with product, design, brand, and engineering teams to support the entire product lifecycle. Strong understanding of product design processes and emerging technologies. Comfortable collaborating with Product, Tech, Data, and Business partners to deliver quality services and experiences. Familiarity with design and prototyping tools (e.g., Figma, Adobe Creative Suite). Demonstrates systems thinking, planning, and has successfully implemented projects in dynamic, agile environments. Good-to-have B.A. or M.A. in Psychology, HCI, Service Design, or other behavioral sciences. Advanced degree, training, or certification in service design, UX design, or related design fields. Experience with advanced quantitative research methods and tools. Business proficiency in a vernacular language (Chinese, Indian or Malay).
