title: Project Engineer
description:
Do you want to work for a leading marine contractor in technically challenging offshore projects from our Singapore Office? At Heerema Marine Contractors, we do something new every day. We operate the world’s largest crane vessels and with them we transport, install and remove structures for the offshore energy markets. Our projects range from installing wind turbines, removing retired offshore oil and gas platforms to installing platforms amongst the largest in the world. You can find our headquarters in Leiden, and there are offices in Houston, London, and Singapore while our vessels work globally. By joining us, you will play an important part in our unique maritime adventure. As a Project Engineer you take the lead in the technical work preparation for product development, studies, tenders and commercial projects. You will assist the project manager and lead a team of engineers to execute the project. In this role you will be the single point of contact for the technical interface with the Client and subcontractors. After completing the project preparations in the office and fabrication yards, your team will support the vessel crew during the execution on board our crane vessels. Daily activities Your daily tasks and responsibilities include the following: Leading at team of enthusiastic engineering professionals. Perform engineering, technical project preparation and execution support of offshore projects, i.e. heavy lift installation projects. Assist project manager with project-based logistics, procurement and subcontracting. Develop and maintain good relationships with Clients and Subcontractors. Liaise with internal stakeholders to identify and mobilize project equipment. Develop execution methods for offshore projects, selection of required equipment, concept and detail design
of lifting arrangements etc. Planning, coordination and control of assigned work and definition of required resources and competences for own people and subcontractors. Review and organize reviews of internal and external deliverables (drawings, reports and procedures). Perform technical management of subcontractors from specification to completion of function test of equipment and hand-over to user. Providing inputs to planning team to prepare and maintain Project schedule. Who will you work with? The Project Engineer is member of the Technology Department, working closely with the Project Manager. You
will lead a team engineers in various disciplines and actively liaise with colleagues in other departments and offices, like procurement, fabrication, operations, logistics etc. Is this the job for you? You are a team player who organizes and plans the work and the involvement of others. You are quality and
result oriented and have the ability to adapt easily when needed. You are creative and come up with original solutions for job-related problems. You recognize the effects and results of your own decisions or activities on other parts of the organization and act accordingly. You involve colleagues in the analysis of (problem) situations and stimulate them to come up with solutions. Furthermore, you are/have: A Bachelor and/or Master's degree in Offshore Engineering, Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering or Similar. Fluent in English (spoken and written) Minimum 8 year experience in engineering, project preparation and execution of offshore projects. Heavy lift experience is a must. Experience working offshore is mandatory. A good understanding of offshore assets and equipment capabilities. Familiar with the offshore oil & gas and renewables business. Pragmatic and solution oriented. Supervisory and coaching skills to provide guidance to less experienced engineers. The ambition to work (temporarily) offshore on our vessels, yards, overseas or in our other offices. Working at Heerema Marine Contractors Making the impossible possible offshore, this is what drives us at Heerema! To execute our offshore projects
we must innovate, engineer and develop to find solutions for our clients’ challenges. Working with Heerema means working in an international, informal, and friendly environment where you will meet likeminded and passionate colleagues. What we offer Excellent secondary labor conditions, such as health insurance, a bonus payment, overtime payment, business trip-, yard-, and offshore allowances. Starting at 18 working days and increasing to 25 days of annual leave. Ample opportunities for personal and professional development. Opportunities to develop your initiatives within a team. An informal working environment with international colleagues. A centrally located office with a good accessibility and parking.

title: Assistant Project Manager

Headquartered in Singapore, ST Engineering ranks among the largest companies listed on theSingapore Exchange. Our history spans more than 50 years, and our strategy is underpinned by our core values – Integrity, Value Creation, Courage, Commitment and Compassion. These 5 core values guide every aspect of our business and are embedded in our ST Engineering culture – from thepeople we hire, to working with each other, to our partners and customers. This diversity of capabilities presents a range of roles through which you can contribute to the development of innovative, secure, and patented products. Your work will have a global impact as we empower customers through advanced communications, intelligent sensors, and the deployment of AI-enabled Edge applications for mission-critical roles. of project planning, accounting and reporting tools Able to work in shipyard and shipboard environment Ability to innovate and adapt to fast and dynamic environment Excellent interpersonal, A fun, passionate and collaborative workplace. Competitive remuneration and comprehensive benefits.

title: Engineering Lead - X Delivery
description: Who We Are Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact. To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive. What You'll Do As a part of BCG's X DELIVERY BUILD team, you will work closely with consulting teams, contributing your oversight while actively developing on projects. You will be responsible for architecting technical solutions and driving business decisions. Your project management skills will come in handy for client projects as well as internal products. Take accountability for the quality of deliverables. Continuously help in developing best practices for the team to implement. Demonstrate the ability to review and approve code confidently, and actively seeking project opportunities through internal connections and networking. Impart technical trainings to team members. Be flexible with working hours to work on critical issues and/or connect with client/project teams/clients when required. Take ownership of internal non-project initiatives related to software development, ensuring their successful planning and execution. Contribute actively to the recruitment initiative by taking interviews, providing inputs on how to improve the overall recruitment process and experience for candidates. Communicate confidently and deliver messages in a concise manner with strong and effective written and verbal communication. Take thoughtful and logical decisions that are in the best interest of the team. Ability to take ownership of the escalations, assist in finding the root cause and implementation of the mitigation steps What You'll Bring Bachelor's / Master's degree in Computer Science engineering / Technology or equivalent 7 - 9 years of experience in Software Engineering. Good experience in designing scalable systems. End to end experience in coding, deployment, managing and monitoring for enterprise solutions. CI/CD, DevOps tools Microservices, Docker, Kubernetes, and Terraform. Track record of designing, developing, and supporting highly scalable, responsive web applications and services. Understanding of UI integration with REST APIs. React Native, Mobile app development and Flutter is considered as a plus. Strong problem-solving skills and willingness to deep dive to get the desired output. Understanding and desire to work in an Agile fast paced environment. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Able to work effectively with cross functional teams and with senior stakeholders. Consulting experience will be considered a plus. Prior experience in developing HLDs and LLDs. Experience of DevOps implementation across the full stack cycle.

title: Product Manager – SharePoint Online
description: Shape the Future of the Modern Intranet at Kyndryl If you’re passionate about creating exceptional digital workplace experiences and want to lead the vision for a modern, enterprise‑grade SharePoint Online intranet, this role gives you a platform to make an impact. At Kyndryl, you won’t just manage features — you’ll shape how thousands of employees collaborate, communicate, and access knowledge every day. Other companies might limit you to maintaining static sites. At Kyndryl, you drive a full transformation journey , from product strategy and roadmap ownership to adoption, governance, and continuous improvement. You’ll work with leaders across business, IT, engineering, HR, and Communications — giving you visibility, influence, and real ownership. If you want to lead a high‑impact product that touches the entire organization, this is the role for you. What You Will Do In this role, you’ll own the direction, delivery, and evolution of the SharePoint Online intranet. You’ll bring together strategy, design, engineering, governance, and change management to build a digital workplace experience employees love using. Here are your main responsibilities: 1) Own the Product Vision & Strategy You’ll define the intranet’s purpose, direction, and roadmap — translating business goals into modern capabilities like content publishing, collaboration, personalization, search, and knowledge management. You’ll gather insights through user research, stakeholder interviews, and competitive benchmarking to prioritize what matters most and ensure the intranet evolves in the right direction. 2) Lead Requirements & Solution Design You’ll run workshops and work closely with business teams to understand needs, turn them into clear requirements, and shape user stories, epics, and acceptance criteria. Partnering with UX/UI designers, you’ll bring ideas to life through wireframes, prototypes, and governance models that support a clean and scalable information architecture. 3) Drive Delivery & Execution Across Teams You’ll work directly with engineering teams, SharePoint developers, and Microsoft 365 platform specialists to bring the roadmap to life. You’ll manage sprints, prioritize the backlog, and ensure features are delivered on time and aligned with best practices for Microsoft 365, SharePoint Online, and enterprise governance. Where needed, you’ll help shape migration plans, content structures, metadata strategies, and site architecture. 4) Engage Stakeholders & Communicate Clearly You’ll be the central point of contact for executives, site owners, and stakeholders across the organization — aligning priorities, balancing competing needs, and ensuring transparency on timelines, risks, and decisions. 5) Drive Change Management & User Adoption You’ll work closely with Communications and HR teams to deliver launches, training, playbooks, FAQs, demos, and adoption campaigns. Your goal is simple: make the intranet a daily habit, not just another tool. 6) Lead Operations & Continuous Improvement You’ll oversee governance, content lifecycle management, permissions, compliance, and analytics. You’ll use SharePoint insights, Viva tools, and feedback loops to continuously refine the experience — while staying up to date with Microsoft 365 roadmap enhancements and proactively recommending improvements. Why You Should Join Kyndryl At Kyndryl, you have the rare opportunity to lead a product that influences how an entire global organization communicates and collaborates. You’re trusted as the owner of the intranet vision, you drive meaningful change, and you work with experts who value thoughtful design and modern digital experiences. Here, your ideas matter. Your leadership matters. And the work you deliver has clear, visible impact. Required Skills & Experience Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Business, or a related field At least 5 years of experience in product management, digital workplace solutions, or enterprise platform delivery Strong hands‑on experience with SharePoint Online , Microsoft 365, and intranet or digital workplace platforms Solid understanding of information architecture, content management , and digital employee experience principles Experience working with cross‑functional teams (engineering, UX, stakeholders) in complex enterprise environments Proven ability to lead end‑to‑end platform rollouts , from discovery to launch and continuous improvement Strong communication and stakeholder‑management skills, with the ability to influence senior leaders Experience working within Agile frameworks , such as Scrum, for product delivery Preferred Skills & Experience Experience leading enterprise intranet or digital workplace transformation initiatives Familiarity with Viva Connections, Viva Topics, Viva Engage , or other modern M365 experiences Knowledge of governance models, content lifecycle frameworks, metadata design , and compliance requirements Background in consulting , IT strategy, digital transformation, or enterprise collaboration programs Experience using analytics from SharePoint Online, Viva Insights , or similar tools to inform product decisions

title: Baseload Maintenance Engineer
description: About ST Engineering ST Engineering is a global technology, defence, and engineering group with offices across Asia, Europe, the Middle East, and the U.S., serving customers in more than 100countries. The Group uses technology and innovation to solve real-world problems and improve lives through its diverse portfolio of businesses across the aerospace, smart city, defence, and public security segments. Headquartered in Singapore, ST Engineering ranks among the largest companies listed on theSingapore Exchange. Our history spans more than 50 years, and our strategy is underpinned by our core values – Integrity, Value Creation, Courage, Commitment and Compassion. These 5 core values guide every aspect of our business and are embedded in our ST Engineering culture – from the people we hire, to working with each other, to our partners and customers. About our Line of Business – Advanced Networks & Sensors Our Advanced Networks & Sensors business specializes in platform digitalization, advanced connectivity, and cutting-edge manufacturing. This diversity of capabilities presents a range of roles through which you can contribute to the development of innovative, secure, and patented products. Join our team and play a crucial role in developing tomorrow’s technology and connectivity solutions and services to sectors spanning defence, public security, government, and thecommercial realm. title: R&D Senior Business Analyst
description: As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer. The Sr. Business Analyst role will be responsible primarily for preparing product requirements and specifications, participating software hazard analysis activities, contributing to user interface design, and improving the user experience of our software products. The Sr. Business Analyst will also serve as a customer facing resource in a technical capacity to determine workflow configurations for our instrumentation. The role will conduct requirements workshops with team members and meet with individuals of various key partners: Product Managers, SW Leads, R&D, FAS, Marketing, UX, etc.) and product development teams to capture and detail measurable, testable, and traceable requirements and specifications. Responsibilities will also include interviewing customers for feedback, conducting software usability testing, and contributing to UI/UX design activities. The candidate’s experience with QMS will provide design compliance input to the software engineering teams, including, but not limited to authoring Software Requirements and Specifications; and, reviewing, and approving software-related documents, such as Risk and Hazard Analysis, Design Verification, and Validation Plans. The candidate will provide support to other business units such as service/support team to assist in preparing training materials of the new workflow and technical writer team to review customer-facing documents (e.g. user guide, white paper, help).  The candidate will also contribute to process improvement. Attention to technical detail, proven written and verbal communication skills, and the ability to analyze and navigate technical problem domains are required. Responsibilities: Prepare well-written technical requirements, functional specifications, and diagrams (e.g. UML, data flow, etc) for software and life sciences instrument systems, including detailing workflows and error-handling scenarios. Apply standard process methodologies in developing and maintaining requirements, including user stories, use cases, functional requirements, non-functional and performance requirements, and business rules. Facilitate requirements workshops with cross-functional science, engineering, and product management teams to elicit and review technical requirements and risk controls for new and sustaining software products. Manage requirements, prepare analysis models and diagrams, and generate and generate reports using tools (e.g. JAMA, JIRA, HPALM, Visio and Miro or similar). Collaborate with the test team to ensure requirements and specifications are concise, accurate, measurable, and testable. Establish traceability between customer requirements, product requirements, and specifications. Prepare trace matrix reports. Participate in customer research and interview activities to elicit user needs for product development. Contribute to user interface design and usability testing activities to provide a seamless user experience in navigating complex scientific laboratory workflows. Collaborate with UX engineers and UI designers to prepare mockups and prototype user interfaces for complex life-science domain software that meets the needs of our customers. Prepare and contribute to design compliance documentation and best-practice disciplines across all aspects of software development within the SDLC. Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science or equivalent. Minimum 5 years of experience with software development best practices, such as Agile or SCRUM. 8+ years industry experience in a systems/business analyst role. Proficient in the use of business analysis tools and techniques. (e.g., Jira, Miro Board, Visio) Understanding of computer systems, networking of Windows and Linux-based systems. Possess strong written and verbal communication, presentation, and facilitation skills. Comfortable working as part of a distributed, global, and cross-functional team. Prepared to work to aggressive timelines in an iterative software development environment. Highly Preferred: Experience in Life Sciences and genomics industries and/or DNA technology Certification in Business Analysis (e.g., Certified Product Owner, Certified Scrum Master)

We proudly empower our employees to get involved with corporate social responsibility. The Opportunity You’re the person who turns Adobe’s platform into business outcomes customers can see, measure, and act on. This role sits at the intersection of strategy, data, and executive influence. Your mandate: help our most strategic Digital Experience customers in the JAPAC region quantify value, accelerate time-to-impact, and build solid reasons that drive expansion, retention, and advocacy. Partnering with Customers and Adobe teams (e.g., Account teams, Ultimate Success, Professional Services), you will anchor Adobe’s work in hard numbers and strategic narratives — not fluffy marketing claims. Conduct Value Measurement Workshops to align key customer collaborators on priority use cases and the measurement framework/important metrics needed for Value Realisation. Build compelling, data-driven value hypotheses anchored in industry benchmarks, customer data, and Adobe telemetry. Impact Development Collaborate with customers to showcase return on investment, cost savings, efficiency improvements, and revenue impact directly tied back to Adobe technology investments. Translate technical capabilities into financial outcomes using clear economic models, Adobe tools, and benchmarks. Work together with Adobe Teams such as Sales and Customer Success to assist with renewals, expansion, and executive alignment discussions. Value Realisation & Measurement Propel progress within Adobe's Value Realisation Acceleration (VRA) framework by addressing challenges and tracking value results across consumption, use cases, and N$R. Attend frequent value meetings with Adobe teams to grasp the context more clearly and assist them in advancing value more rapidly. Attend customer meetings e.g., SBR or equivalent experience with executives to help the Adobe teams demonstrate realised return on investment and identify new value pathways. Help promote adoption of Adobe’s value methodology throughout the ecosystem and JAPAC customers. What you need to succeed: 5–10+ years in value engineering, management consulting, transformation, strategy, finance, or similar roles. Champion of value methodology. Proven success building arguments for enterprise technology, digital marketing or transformation programs. Strong financial modelling capability. Ability to translate complexity into crisp, executive-ready insights. Comfortable engaging with senior executives at large enterprises across JAPAC. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Demonstrated ability to influence with authority across cross-functional teams. Familiarity with Adobe Experience Cloud or related marketing/commerce/customer experience technologies to drive personalization and optimization programs (ideal, but not required). Past experience in program management, scaled agile delivery or similar as a squad member; Scrum master experience beneficial.

description: Step into a career with ASM, where cutting edge technology meets collaborative culture. ​ For over 55 years ASM has been ahead of what’s next, at the forefront of innovation and what’s technologically possible. Minimum 3 years of experience in semiconductor or manufacturing industries. Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, mechanical diagrams, and electrical schematics. Ability to lift up to 23 kg and perform physical tasks such as climbing ladders and kneeling. Skilled in using hand and power tools and precision measurement devices safely. Ability to work in environments with strong magnets during assembly and testing. What sets you apart Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail. Good interpersonal skills and ability to work in a team-oriented environment. Flexibility to adapt to changing product volumes and tasks. Apply today to be part of what’s next. We make the tech that enables the chips in devices which improve lives around the world. We do this with an eye to the future, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible through cutting-edge innovation, and driving the next wave of technological breakthroughs that shape how we live, work, and connect. To learn more about ASM, find us at asm.com and on LinkedIn , Facebook , Instagram, X and YouTube . description: As a Junior Product Designer at Ask & Embla, you will support the design and development of innovative, trend-relevant jewelry that aligns with the brand’s vision and customer identity. Contribute to the development of mood boards, design decks, and seasonal concept presentations. Support the Creative Director and Product Design team by sketching and proposing jewelry concepts aligned with the brand’s vision and assortment plan. We are building the foundation to expand several business verticals beyond e-commerce, while supporting existing business functions for fulfilling thousands of orders each month. Ready to Join Us? description: Our vision is to transform how the world uses information to enrich life for all . Micron Technology is a world leader in innovating memory and storage solutions that accelerate the transformation of information into intelligence, inspiring the world to learn, communicate and advance faster than ever. As a Product Development Team Leader (PDTL) for High Bandwidth Memory you will lead the introduction, ramp and high-volume production success of our groundbreaking products targeted to deliver lowest power per bit solutions in the field of ML(machine learning) and AI (artificial intelligence). PDTLs are the technical program owners and leaders, providing direction and leadership to cross-functional teams, including partners such as business units, quality, test, supply chain, packaging development and manufacturing. The success of a sophisticated product such as HBM relies vastly on PDTLs driving vertical integration of this wide network of partners across the company. title: Safety Engineer
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? We are looking for you, Safety Engineer to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on supporting and reporting to the Singapore Refinery Operational Safety Manager in the area of operational safety, workplace safety, Emergency and Crisis Management to deliver full compliance with Corporate Safety, local and regulatory safety requirements. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Safety Engineer Drive and execute operational safety, workplace safety, emergency response and crisis management Ensure refinery legal right to operate safely is delivered Support refinery safety manager in strategic and tactical safety planning, implementation and execution of safety programs Operational Safety Support and participate in refinery operational risk assessments, safety critical job and deviations reviews Support corporate and refinery implementation of creating safety together to strengthen refinery safety cultural shift Ensure the executions of critical safety risk controls, monitoring, trending, verifying and communicated for learnings & improvement Workplace Safety Together with refinery key stakeholders, implement contractors and suppliers safety management Support the Turnaround Safety Lead and team in turnaround planning, preparations executions and lesson learnt Support Workplace Safety & Health Committee initiatives and programs Support and execute site fire safety management Incident Management Participate as safety subject matter expert in incident investigation team, facilitate Safety department owned investigations or as directed Prepare, maintain emergency response plan and execute training of company emergency response team to mitigate incidents, emergencies and compliance to National CERT Audits and Standard Support preparation of refinery crisis leader and crisis management team training and dive into the role and responsibility of crisis management team support during crisis Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Minimum 6 years of safety working experience in the oil, gas or petrochemical industry Experienced to support and operationalise workplace safety, fire safety, emergency response and crisis management Good understanding of local WSH regulations and have experience dealing with local regulators such as MHD, MOM, SPF, SCDF, etc. Good communication, writing and analytical skill Recognized Bachelor Degree in Safety or similar, master degree in Safety highly desired Registered  WSHO and FSM, Noise Monitoring Officer added advantage Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application. We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com

title: Satellite Engineer
description: Job Overview We are seeking an experienced satellite Engineer with at least three years of professional hands-on experience in satellite design, development, and deployment. The successful candidate will leverage their technical expertise to conduct training programs in satellite engineering for undergraduates, young professionals and youths. This role involves both technical development and educational outreach, ensuring participants gain practical skills in satellite design, assembly, and operation. Key Responsibilities Training Program Development and Delivery •	Design and deliver comprehensive training programs on satellite building, covering topics such as: o	Systems engineering o	Subsystems (power, communication, attitude control, etc.) o	Payload integration o	Satellite testing and deployment protocols •	Develop instructional materials, including manuals, presentations, and hands-on projects, to suit various audience levels (youth, professionals, and educators). •	Conduct hands-on workshops and labs, guiding participants through real-world satellite assembly and testing activities. •	Collaborate with academic institutions, industry partners, and space agencies to expand training opportunities and resources. •	Other Topics outside of Satellite Engineering that would be good to cover: o	Space mission design and planning o	Principles of Space Engineering and Applications o	Project Management in Space Evaluation and Continuous Improvement •	Evaluate training program outcomes through feedback, assessments, and participant performance. •	Continuously update training content to reflect advances in satellite technology and industry trends. •	Provide mentorship to participants during and after training programs to support their career or project goals. Qualifications and Skills Required •	Bachelor’s degree in Aerospace Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related field. •	Minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience in satellite development, including design, integration, and testing. •	Strong understanding of satellite subsystems, including power systems, communications, payload integration, and attitude determination and control. •	Proficiency with satellite design tools (e.g., STK, MATLAB, SolidWorks, or similar). •	Experience with satellite testing equipment and procedures, including vibration testing, thermal testing, and deployment mechanisms. •	Excellent communication and teaching skills, with the ability to simplify complex concepts for diverse audiences. •	Experienced in conducting training programmes for youths, undergraduates and professionals •	Experiences: Satellite Engineering •	Apply expertise in designing, building, and testing satellites, including CubeSats and other small satellite platforms. •	Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure training programs align with the latest developments in satellite technology and industry standards. •	Maintain and operate testing facilities, including cleanrooms, thermal vacuum chambers, and other satellite testing equipment. Preferred •	Master’s degree or certification in satellite technology or systems engineering. •	Experience in delivering STEM or technical training programs. •	Familiarity with satellite mission operations and ground station integration. •	Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, C++, or MATLAB for satellite systems. •	Experience working with space agencies or commercial satellite companies. Key Attributes •	Passionate about inspiring and educating others in satellite technology. •	Innovative and adaptable in designing hands-on learning experiences. •	Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail in both technical and instructional tasks. •	Team-oriented, with the ability to collaborate across departments and with external stakeholders. ________________________________________ Benefits •	Opportunity to shape the next generation of satellite engineers and innovators. •	Competitive salary and benefits package. •	Professional development opportunities in both technical and educational fields.

description: The successful candidate will be a part of the Business Development Team, reporting to the Business Development Manager. The Field Operations Engineer supports the expansion of VISCOY’s market space in Asset Inspection, Monitoring and Maintenance (AIMM) through field operations (proof-of-concepts (POC), product demonstrations and field deployments) and applications development for products in our portfolio. The Field Operations Engineer is the technical expert of our portfolio of Robotics and Digital Technologies. He/she is empowered to experiment and push the technical boundaries of the technologies that we represent in the field. Asset Inspection, Monitoring and Maintenance presents challenges that require the cross deployment of multiple technologies and customisation to the local context to overcome. The Field Operations Engineer leads the field deployment of our portfolio of technologies with our customers with the objective of overcoming these challenges through the development of more effective, cost efficient and safer solutions for the customer’s AIMM regime. The Field Operations Engineer works closely with our Technology Partners and Customers in the scoping, testing and deployment of our products and solutions. He/she will be the bridge between technology and real-world applications in the markets that we serve. To be successful in the role, the Field Operations Engineer is also required to have a good understanding of the limitations of our products. He/she works closely with the Repair and Maintenance Engineer in trouble-shooting, repair and developing the optimal operating parameters for the products in our portfolio. With deep understanding of technology gaps in AIMM, the Applications Development Engineer will be actively involved in scanning the market for relevant new technologies and developments. Technology is not bounded by geography or culture. The successful Field Operations Engineer enjoys the opportunity to travel and work in international teams with Technology Partners and Customers. The role will require the successful candidate to be proactive, possessing initiative, and a strong sense of ownership to take on and see-through projects assigned. He/she will need to collaborate closely both with internal and external stakeholders (technology partners and customers). Responsibilities Support the Business Development Team in: Development of use cases & applications for our Robotics and Digital Technology solutions in Asset Inspection, Monitoring & Maintenance Field Testing and Deployment of our portfolio of Robotics and Digital Technologies Solving AIMM challenges presented by our customers Be an expert of the Robotics and Digital Technologies in our portfolio To establish strong network, build close rapport and work effectively with both internal and external stakeholders. title: OutSystems Developer
description: PebbleRoad is a Singapore-based strategy, design and innovation practice. We help organisations in their digital transformation journey by aligning their strategic vision, creating digital experiences and building internal capabilities. We started our practice in 2004 with one objective: to do impactful work. This single-minded pursuit has guided who we hire, who we work for, the work we do and the decisions we make. We work with clients such as DBS (Singapore), Digi (Malaysia), Sinar Mas (Indonesia), ADB (Philippines), CLP (Hong Kong) and many government agencies in Singapore. PebbleRoad is a remote-first company . Yes, we have an office in Singapore, but it is used for reading books, team meetings, client meetings and celebrations. In addition to work-from-anywhere culture, we adopt a horizontal structure emphasising self-management, flexibility and growth. You can also read more about our culture on our LinkedIn page . Our work We design and build human-centred, modern digital services. We excel in 3 areas: Business : We are focused on creating business value. We engage business leaders on productivity, customer satisfaction, operational excellence and workplace culture. Design : We are design-led. We approach problems from an outside-in perspective, engage various stakeholders, test ideas and build prototypes. Tech : We build digital services using a modern tech stack that includes low-code tools, AI/ML and APIs. And yes, we use Scrum as our development methodology. We assign a team to each project (an engagement). Every team is cross-functional and dedicated. The team roles include Engagement Directors, Product Owners, Product Designers, Content Strategists, UX Researchers and Engineers. These engagements may last between 3 to 18 months or more. A typical engagement that you will lead might go like this: Understand the context of the engagement. Understand the product requirements. Work closely with the team to define the product backlog and plan the development sprints. Work with the Tech Lead and other developers to define quality and testing parameters. Use best practices to build using OutSystems. Be proactive and participate in daily standups, product reviews and quality meetings. Responsibilities Your responsibilities are: Work with the team to build high-quality digital products using OutSystems. Be proactive in absorbing new knowledge and raising the standards of development. Help integrate the solution into the client’s enterprise ecosystem. Work with backend systems integrators to build and implement the solution. Improve transformation practice at PebbleRoad Requirements We’re looking for someone who: Has 3+ years of experience developing using OutSystems (Service Studio & Integration Studio). Has worked in enterprise environments. Has worked with Agile methodologies Has a good understanding of Rapid Application Development(RAD) best practices and implementation. Has a good understanding of best practices for web application security. title: Marketing Strategist
description:
Marketing Strategist Full-Time | Singapore | In-House Advertising Team We are looking for a Marketing Strategist to join our in-house advertising team. This role sits at the intersection of
strategy, creative, copywriting, and performance marketing. You will be responsible for developing advertising hooks, writing copy, conducting market research, and shaping how ideas are translated into effective ad creatives. You will work closely with leadership, graphic designers, motion graphics designers, and media buyers to pitch ideas, guide creative execution, and improve campaign performance through insight-driven testing and iteration. This is a hands-on role where research, copy, and creative decisions directly impact advertising outcomes. Key Responsibilities Market Research and Competitive Insights Conduct ongoing market and competitor research to identify trends, messaging gaps, and creative opportunities. Analyse competitor ads, offers, and positioning to inform new hooks and creative directions. Research audience pain points, objections, motivations, and buying triggers relevant to advertising performance. Translate research findings into actionable recommendations for ad creative and copy. Advertising Strategy and Hook Development Work with leadership to develop and refine advertising hooks, angles, and campaign narratives. Translate business objectives, offers, and market dynamics into clear messaging strategies. Provide strategic context to the creative team to guide ideation and execution. Copywriting and Message Framing Write clear, persuasive copy for paid advertising, including primary text, headlines, descriptions, and short-form variations. Frame messages to optimise clarity, attention, and performance in feed-based advertising environments. Adapt copy across different hooks, formats, and audience contexts while maintaining consistency. Collaborate with designers and motion graphics team to ensure copy and visuals work together effectively. Creative Ideation and Collaboration Participate actively in creative ideation sessions and pitch messaging and creative ideas. Work with designers and motion graphics team to explore different ways to present hooks through copy and visuals. Contribute to first-frame messaging, sequencing, and emphasis decisions. Creative Testing and Experimentation Plan and structure creative testing frameworks, including hypotheses, variations, and success criteria. Define what variables to test such as hooks, copy framing, offers, pacing, or formats. Prioritise tests based on potential impact and learning value. Performance Analysis and Insights Analyse campaign performance data to identify trends, insights, and opportunities for improvement. Translate results into clear recommendations for creative and copy iteration. Maintain feedback loops with the creative and media teams based on what is working and why. Campaign and Messaging Consistency Ensure consistency of messaging and positioning across campaigns, platforms, and touchpoints. Support alignment between paid ads, landing pages, and the broader customer journey. Maintain clarity and discipline in how messaging evolves over time. Requirements Minimum 3 years of experience in marketing strategy, performance marketing, or advertising. Proven experience in advertising copywriting and messaging development. Experience conducting market or competitor research for marketing or advertising purposes. Strong understanding of paid media environments, especially Meta platforms. Ability to think in hypotheses, testing frameworks, and measurable outcomes. Strong written and analytical communication skills. Comfortable working in a fast-paced, iterative advertising environment. Preferred Qualifications Experience in performance or direct response advertising. Familiarity with creative testing and A/B testing methodologies. Experience in automotive, consumer retail, or high-consideration purchase categories. Exposure to working closely with creative, motion, and media buying teams.

title: Frontend Engineer
description:
About Us Ropedia is a Singapore-based, early-stage AI startup focused on building the data infrastructure for spatial and physical intelligence. We develop proprietary wearable and mobile capture devices and models to collect, recover, and reconstruct multimodal human Xperience in real-world scenarios. Role Overview Location: Singapore Type: Full-time Job Description We are looking for a Frontend Engineer to build and scale Ropedia’s user-facing products, including our data platform web application , company website , and mobile app experiences . You will work closely with product, design, and backend/ML teams to deliver high-quality, performant, and reliable interfaces , turning complex workflows into intuitive interactions for customers and internal operators. In this role, you will own key frontend architecture and implementation, drive UI engineering best practices, and help shape the overall product experience across web and mobile. Responsibilities 1.      Design and implement frontend experiences for Ropedia’s data platform , including core workflows, dashboards, and interactive tools. 2.     Build and maintain Ropedia’s marketing website and product pages with strong attention to visual quality, responsiveness, and performance. 3.    Develop and iterate on mobile app user experiences (e.g., capture/operations tooling, user-facing product flows), ensuring consistency
across platforms. 4.    Collaborate closely with design and product to translate Figma specs into production-quality UI, and continuously improve interaction quality. 5.    Work with backend/infra teams to define and implement API contracts ,
data fetching patterns, caching, and error handling. 6.    Establish and maintain frontend engineering standards: component libraries/design systems, testing, CI/CD, accessibility, and monitoring. 7.     Optimize for performance, reliability, and maintainability , including bundling, rendering performance, and cross-browser/device compatibility. Requirements ·       Bachelor’s degree or above in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience). ·       Strong proficiency in modern frontend development with TypeScript/JavaScript and React (or equivalent frameworks). ·       Experience building production web apps with state management , routing, authentication/authorization, and API integration. ·       Solid understanding of frontend fundamentals: HTML/CSS , responsive design, browser rendering/performance, and debugging. ·       Familiarity with modern tooling and best practices: bundlers (Vite/Webpack), linting/formatting, testing (unit/e2e), and CI/CD. ·       Strong product sense and collaboration skills—able to work effectively with designers and cross-functional stakeholders. ·       Ownership mindset, high execution bar, and attention to detail in UI quality. Nice to Have (Optional) ·       Experience with Next.js (SSR/SSG), SEO, and performance optimization for marketing sites. ·       Mobile experience with React Native / Flutter (or native iOS/Android exposure). ·       Familiarity with UI component systems (e.g., Tailwind, MUI, Ant Design) and building/designing design systems . ·       Experience with data-heavy UIs (tables, filtering, search, visualization) and workflow tools (admin panels, ops dashboards). ·       Exposure to observability and product analytics (e.g., Sentry, OpenTelemetry, GA/Mixpanel).

This means continuously inventing and developing technologies for the next generation of experiences. To continue our efforts in the path to AGI, and as we move closer to a future with intelligent robots and advanced AI models, we're hiring talent across a broad range of disciplines from robotics hardware to system software and artificial intelligence. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics.

title: Senior Data Scientist
description: Synthesis x Senior Data Scientist At Synthesis, we push the boundaries of what’s possible with Open Data. It’s a fast-paced, rapidly-changing environment at the intersection of data science, cultural analysis & brand consultancy. We call this Open Source Intelligence for brands. As a Senior Data Scientist at Synthesis, you will play a key role in shaping our data science practice and driving innovation across both client projects and internal products. You will lead end-to-end data initiatives, delivering project work from shaping datasets and building models to designing scalable data workflows that power the insights and decision frameworks we deliver. You will work with diverse open data sources, ranging from search, social, and e-commerce, to academic papers and patents, to understand audience behaviour and anticipate how needs will evolve over time. In this role, you will not only contribute hands-on but also guide junior data scientists, act as a thought partner to cultural strategists, and help define the roadmap for our data products and infrastructure. We are a small, collaborative team with diverse backgrounds, spanning computer science and engineering, anthropology, and law. We value curiosity, creativity, and a spirit of experimentation. If you are excited about combining data and culture to solve real-world problems, we would love to hear from you! What you’ll need. 5+ years of experience in data science, with a strong record of delivering impactful projects from data exploration through to production deployment. Deep proficiency in Python and SQL, with strong software engineering fundamentals (clean, modular, well-documented code, testing, and version control with Git). Proven experience designing and building scalable data workflows or data-driven products, including architecting pipelines, modular components, and reusable frameworks. Experience applying large language models or advanced NLP techniques (e.g. embeddings, semantic parsing, topic modelling, text classification); familiarity with processing Asian languages is a plus. Strong grasp of statistical methods and core machine learning algorithms, with practical experience using libraries such as scikit-learn, statsmodels, and the ability to select appropriate models based on data characteristics and problem context. Comfortable working with large, messy, and unstructured datasets, particularly text and image-based data. Ability to translate complex technical insights into clear, actionable recommendations for non-technical stakeholders, and collaborate closely with strategists to drive business outcomes. A collaborative mindset, with openness to feedback and diverse perspectives. Bonus points if you have the following. Experience leading or mentoring junior data scientists, including setting technical direction and supporting professional growth. Experience building and maintaining production-grade data pipelines (e.g. Airflow, Dagster) or integrating data from APIs, web scraping, or third-party providers. Familiarity with cloud environments such as Google Cloud or AWS, and deploying data science workflows at scale Why Synthesis? Synthesis is known for delivering exceptional data models and products that uncover the stories of the people they represent and inspire our partners to act with confidence. Partnering with the world’s most successful brands in fashion, beauty, content, and travel, we’re building specialised solutions to solve problems of planning for the future. Rooted in culture, tested in data science, we spot and anticipate changes and connections in culture to inspire action and help brands grow with a new wave of audiences. After three years of iterative development and refinement, we are preparing to launch a series of products over the next 5 years. We will be at the forefront of a shift towards leveraging open data to develop rich, honest, human insight. In an industry that has, for too long, relied on a ‘question-response’ approach to understanding changes in behaviour, we prioritise layering behavioural, performative, search, and sales data to highlight discrepancies between what people say they do and what they actually do. We see a $40 billion industry that has failed to innovate for too long, and we are just getting started. About Synthesis We do Human Centred Data Science. It’s a way of reimaging open data sources from a human perspective. It prioritises behaviour and context to unravel why people do what they do, at scale.  By blending data science with human and market intelligence, we help our clients spot early signals of change and predict implications for business. Diverse by design. Synthesis is a team of digital researchers, gaming designers, data forecasters, network scientists, ethnographers, and engineers.  Cultural and category experts train our models – ensuring they detect measures that matter most – whilst data scientists uncover patterns the eye cannot see. Building bespoke, comprehensive datasets where none exist. We work with open data - search, ratings, social, and sales.  By layering many signals, we build robust and relevant data-driven stories around brands, businesses, and consumer behaviour. Asia first, global applied. Our products must work in Asia first - the hardest test markets.  We continue to make significant investments in language and capabilities across Asian-specific platforms.  Now applied in Europe, LatAm, and the USA.

description: About ST Engineering ST Engineering is a global technology, defence, and engineering group with offices across Asia, Europe, the Middle East, and the U.S., serving customers in more than 100 countries. title: Software Developer (Learning Sciences & Assessment Department) [NIE]
description: ABOUT THE NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF EDUCATION (NIE) The National Institute of Education (NIE), Singapore , is Singapore’s national teacher education institute and we are proud to be an integral part of the nation’s education service. We play a key role in the preparation of teachers and in the provision of teacher professional and school leadership development programmes. We are committed to our vision of being An Institute of Distinction: Leading the Future of Education and our mission to Inspire Learning, Transform Teaching and Advance Research . Read more about NIE here . NIE invites applications for the position of part-time software developer for the project “PhET-style simulations to promote AI literacy”. The project’s goal is to develop a curated library of interactive, high-quality online resources for teachers that puts NIE at the forefront of AI literacy. To work towards this goal, the project will start out by developing customizable web-based simulations and corresponding avatar-based narrative instructional videos catering to three fundamental topics in AI literacy - Personalization using Machine Learning and Neural Networks , Ethical Issues in AIED , Human-AI Collaboration. These simulations would allow student teachers to experiment with relevant critical task features in a hands-on manner, thus improving their understanding of abstract technical concepts in AI and the implications of their usage in AI systems for education. Project evaluation would focus on usability evaluation and learning evaluation of the programmed simulations. Facilitation resources and validated design guidelines for developing effective AI literacy simulations will be made available to educational stakeholders upon project completion. This is a contract position till 30 November 2026. Responsibilities Gathering requirements for simulation design from domain experts Agile prototyping of mock UI/UX interfaces for the simulations Programming and iteratively refining the simulations based on expert feedback and usability testing Building data logging capabilities in the designed simulations Developing facilitation guides to assist stakeholders’ work with designed simulations Working with NIE’s IT teams to host simulations securely and ensure seamless interoperability with existing NIE platforms Managing the project and ensuring timely completion of milestones Requirements Essential A Polytechnic Diploma Full stack software development and debugging experience Knowledge of computer graphics and interface design Fluent in coding with generative AI Solid understanding of fundamental machine learning and AI techniques Creativity, good communication skills and ability to work as team player Able to work independently with minimal supervision Preferred Work, teaching, or corporate training experience in AI for education Familiarity with AI literacy efforts worldwide, in APAC and Singapore Up-to-date with latest advancements in generative AI (for education) Passion for improving teaching and learning with technology Application We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Other Information NIE staff can take chartered buses at their own expense from or near their home to the NIE campus. This is subject to the availability of seats.

title: Safety & Integrated Management System Engineer
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Safety & Integrated Management System Engineer to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on adopting Neste Operations Excellence Management System (OEMS) and translate it to Singapore refinery Integrated Management System (IMS) with ISO 9001, 14001, 45001, SS651 and best practices. You will provide your expertise to lead our Singapore Refinery IMS, ensuring safety compliance are analysed, updated and sustained. In this job you will collaborate and work closely with the Singapore Safety department. Your key stakeholders will range from engineers, supervisors and front-line personnel in Singapore to the Corporate Safety Team in Finland. As the local administrator of Neste Continuous Improvement Reporting System, you are expected to manage, analyse, trend, gain insights and learnings from Refinery unsafe conditions, near misses, incidents and audit management. In our Safety team, you will report to the Safety Manager and support the safety team in safety-related tasks and achieve our common goals. Together with you and your expertise, we can make an impact today and keep our promises for tomorrow. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Audits Lead & coordinate audits, such as ISO certification, recertifications, internal/external audits, regulatory audit (eg. SS651), Corporate Insurance Survey and Neste OEMS (Operations Excellence Management System) Principle / compliance audit and self assessment. Ensure audits action closure, preparation and follow up are sustainable. IMS (Integrated Management System -  Neste OEMS, SS651, ISO 9001, 14001, 45001) Lead, develop and implement Singapore refinery IMS Prepare and facilitate annual Management Review, support the Management Representative, follow up annual Management Review recommendations and track area of improvement closure Be the NMS (Neste Management System) Space-owner and liaise with the cross functional team on QMS  activities (i.e.NMS Space/procedure/Audits) Gatekeeping/Maintaining NMS documentation framework to ensure compliances are met throughout the certification period. Compliance/Legal Register Review quarterly Legal Register related to Health, Safety, Environmental legal requirements & ISO Standards Work with relevant stakeholders to interpret legal requirements Review compliance activities on a monthly basis Owner of Neste Continuous Improvement Reporting System Own Singapore refinery NCR System, processing of NCR reportings, programs owner and be its user administrator Lead & drive Near Miss reporting program such as data trending, analysis, proposals and  recommendations for corrective and preventive actions Incident Learning Own and manage the Incident Learning processes (i.e. reporting, classification, investigation & actions) to ensure compliance with corporate, regulatory & site requirements Advise stakeholders on incident classification and reportable incidents Participate in incident investigations as Safety SME or as team leader Lead cross functional team to trend and propose improvement plans to reduce incident rate and improve safety culture Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Minimum 8 years safety working experience in petrochemical or chemical industry Bachelor’s Degree in chemical / process / process safety engineering; having a Masters Degree in a related field is highly desired Strong understanding and knowledge to lead, support, implement and operationalise IMS to the workforce Able to effectively lead organization in audit preparations, execution, follow up closure and analysis of long term effectiveness, durability and on-going compliance of implemented actions Ability to interpret corporate and regulatory requirements and effectively translate them into practical, organization-specific solutions while ensuring stakeholder alignment Experienced incident investigation leader or facilitator Strong communication, writing and analytical skill Registered WSHO is an added advantage Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application (with a salary request). We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com

title: Lead System Solution Architect
description:
Lead System Solution Architect Role focus: delivery-heavy solution architecture for
integrated hardware and software systems, including POS and kiosk workflows
used by students for card-tap verification and ordering. Company Background We are a hardware and software system integrator delivering end-to-end automated meal dispenser systems for schools in Singapore. Our solutions integrate field devices (dispensers, controllers, sensors), POS and kiosk terminals (e.g., card readers and peripherals), backend services, web
portals, and Android applications to support daily school operations with high reliability and secure transactions. Role Overview As the Lead System Solution Architect, you will own the end-to-end solution architecture and drive delivery outcomes across multiple deployments. You will lead engineers, guide vendors/partners, and ensure systems are secure, scalable, maintainable, and operationally robust in real-world, multi-site
environments. Key Responsibilities • Architecture ownership: Define target architecture, integration patterns, and system standards spanning devices, POS/kiosks, backend, web, and mobile. • Delivery leadership: Partner with product/project
leadership to plan and execute delivery; identify technical risks, dependencies, and mitigation plans; ensure production readiness. • POS & kiosk workflows: Architect student verification and ordering flows (card-tap, profile/entitlement validation, order confirmation, item collection/dispensing), including offline/degraded modes where required. • System integration: Define API contracts, data models, and interfaces for third-party systems (e.g., identity/student information, payments where applicable, monitoring/alerting). • Quality & operability: Define NFRs (availability, latency, observability, resilience) and guide implementation of monitoring, audit logging, and recovery procedures. • Security & compliance: Ensure secure device communications, access controls, data protection, and auditability suitable for school environments. • Governance & mentorship: Set architecture principles and review major designs; mentor technical leads; raise engineering maturity across teams. • Vendor/partner management: Lead technical engagement with external vendors, ensuring deliverables meet agreed architecture and operational requirements. Requirements • Experience: Typically, 8-12+ years in solution architecture/system design with proven delivery ownership; prior experience leading teams (people management) is required. • Relevant domain exposure (mandatory): Field-deployed, hardware-enabled systems such as system integration, automated/self-service systems, POS/kiosk platforms, IoT or similar operational environments (beyond internal enterprise-only applications). • Software stack knowledge: Strong understanding across backend ( Java and/or .NET ), modern web ( React ), and Android application development. • Integration & data: API design (REST), distributed systems patterns, databases, messaging/integration middleware; strong grasp of data flows and event-driven interactions. • Operational design: Experience designing for reliability (fault tolerance, intermittent connectivity), observability, deployment/upgrade strategy, and incident response support. • Stakeholder skills: Able to translate business and operational requirements into system designs; confident leading design reviews with customers and partners. Interested candidates are invited to submit their resumes and expected salary. We regret that only short-listed candidates will be notified.

title: Manager / Senior Manager (Semiconductor Process & QC)
description: Job Summary: Leading a team in responsible for the strategic design, implementation, and continuous improvement of all quality and process control systems within Fab Operations. This role is critical for ensuring world-class product yield, reducing defectivity, and maintaining strict compliance with customer specifications, internal quality standards, and relevant industry certifications. Responsibilities: Develop and drive a strategic roadmap for yield improvement and defect reduction across critical process modules. Lead the design and maintenance of advanced, real-time Statistical Process Control (SPC) , Fault Detection and Classification (FDC) , and Advanced Process Control (APC) systems to stabilize and optimize all fabrication steps. Cross-contamination management to achieve balance between research and development flexibilities in integration runsheet flow path vs process tools health and process repeatability. Small volume prototype protocol and product and process control plan alignment and set-up with requestor, process margin runs till demonstration of product SPC repeatability and line defect and electrical yield. Serve as the technical expert and primary quality interface for key customers, leading customer audits, addressing quality-related concerns, and driving customer-specific quality improvement plans. Problem Solving: Direct and oversee high-level, cross-functional root-cause analysis (RCA) and corrective and preventive action (CAPA) teams for major process excursions, non-conformances, and field returns, utilizing methodologies like 8D or Six Sigma Metrology & Inspection: Oversee the selection, qualification, and maintenance of all in-line and off-line metrology and defect inspection tools, ensuring measurement system accuracy (Gage R&R) and high data quality. Process Window Definition: Collaborate with Process Integration and R&D teams to define, qualify, and stabilize robust manufacturing process windows for all new product introductions (NPI), ensuring Design for Manufacturability (DFM) principles are met. Data Analytics: Leverage large-scale manufacturing data (Big Data) to identify subtle process drifts, predict failures, and proactively implement controls. Lead, mentor, and manage a team of Quality Engineers, Process Control Engineers, and Technicians, setting performance goals, developing career paths, and fostering a culture of ownership and data-driven decision-making. Collaborate closely with all stakeholders to balance the trade-off between quality stability, process efficiency, and tool uptime. Requirements: Degree in Engineering, Materials Science, Chemical Engineering, or a related technical field. Preferable 10+ years of experience in the semiconductor industry, with a minimum of 5 years in a leadership/management role focusing on Process Control, Quality Assurance, or Yield Engineering within a Fab environment. Deep technical knowledge of semiconductor fabrication processes in different modules. Expert-level knowledge of Statistical Process Control (SPC) , Design of Experiments (DOE) , Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) , and Lean/Six Sigma methodologies (Black Belt certification highly preferred). Strong leadership, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the proven ability to influence and align technical and executive stakeholders. title: Staff Engineer, Gas, Global Facilities
description: Welcome! Are you ready to be at the forefront of ground breaking Engineering within the semiconductor industry?! Micron Technology, Inc., a global leader in memory and storage solutions, is seeking a dedicated and experienced Senior Engineer to join our Gas team within the Global Facilities Engineering department. In this crucial role, you will play a vital part in providing high-quality Engineering services and support for the planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance of our subject area specific Facilities systems across our Semiconductor Manufacturing sites worldwide for Bulk Gas and Specialty Gas Systems. As a Senior Engineer, Gas for the Global Facilities Engineering department at Micron Technology, Inc., you will be responsible to provide high quality Engineering services and support for the planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance of your discipline specific Facilities systems in support of the Semiconductor Manufacturing sites across the Micron Global Manufacturing Network – for Bulk Gas systems and Specialty Gas systems. In this role, you will support the Facilities Teams and Manufacturing sites across the network as the Technical Subject Matter Expert through all phases of design, construction, installation, start-up, operations and maintenance with focus and emphasis on Global Standardization and Alignment, Innovation, Reliability and Performance, and Sustainability. Responsibilities: Technical Support, Leadership, and Expertise Act as the Technical Field Domain Expert, offering consultation and support to all Site Facilities and Manufacturing teams. Collaborate with internal partners such as Technology Development, Procurement, Finance, and Legal to champion multi-functional collaboration. Develop, review, and maintain companies global system, equipment, and component Standards, Specifications, and Best Known Methods (BKMs) to ensure excellence in engineering practices. Implement Standardization and Alignment across Micron's Global Network and Sites for Engineering and Design concepts, Construction Project execution, Reliability Programs deployment, and Operations and Maintenance practices. Find opportunities for improvement in system designs to improve performance, efficiency gains, reduce maintenance, and achieve cost savings. Benchmark both within and outside Micron to capture Engineering Best Practices and Well Known Methods for Facilities buildings and systems. Stay up-to-date with industry trends, technological advances, emerging technology roadmaps, design innovations, and sustainability practices. Design and Construction Project Support: Provide valuable support to the Design teams in the Engineering development of new large-scale Facilities Infrastructure Expansion projects. Ensure that designs align with all applicable standards, codes, and regulations. Build and refine system design concepts for deployment. Ensure effective communication with local Facilities, Design, Construction, Procurement, and Manufacturing partners to deliver project objectives seamlessly. Conduct timely and thorough review of design packages, offering detailed comments and feedback to ensure the success of the projects. Assist in providing cost and schedule estimates and justifications for project approvals. Supervise progress and provide engineering expertise during construction as required per project if vital. Participate and lead capacity databases and system operating trends throughout the network, validating utilization and diversity factor assumptions. Review and guide on scope, schedule, and cost estimates for Facilities infrastructure additions or modifications. Global Initiatives Program Support: Take charge of Global Facilities Programs & Initiatives, including establishing and defining program objectives, roadmaps, and high-level implementation plans. Lead Sustainability Programs in alignment with Micron's Corporate Sustainability goals and initiatives. Job Requirements: Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical, Chemical, Process, or Industrial Systems Engineering required Minimum 7 to 10years experience in Semiconductor and/or Facilities related industry Technical expertise, knowledge, and experience on Facilities and Semiconductor Gas Systems including Bulk Gas Systems, Specialty Gas Systems, Gas Purification, Gas Cabinets and Valve Manifold Boxes, Hazardous and High Purity Gases and Piping, Compressed Air, and Gas Distribution Systems. Ability to prioritize and schedule workload and work on multiple tasks concurrently Self-motivated worker, good organizational skills and service demeanor Excellent communication skills and good team working skills, English Required Familiarity with Semiconductor Manufacturing and Facilities systems Ability to travel internationally 25-40% on average, but up to 50-66% of the time during Critical Project phases or Site Activities
