title: Executive/Senior Executive, Venue Operations
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION Who we are Constellar is Asia's preferred partner for convening businesses, curating ideas and creating opportunities for sustainable business growth and global impact. Based in Singapore, we curate and develop influential trade events for key industries and sectors, connecting people, global marketplaces and networks for sustainable growth. We also manage the Singapore EXPO, Singapore's largest purpose-built venue for Meetings, Incentives, Conventions and Exhibitions (MICE). Our vision is to be a global leader made in Asia, activating impactful networks to enable cross-industry collaboration and innovation through our holistic portfolio of intellectual property (IP) in the MICE industry. Learn more about us at constellar.co. What it's like to work here Meetings, Incentives, Conventions and Exhibitions, or MICE, is a vehicle for new businesses, partnerships and regional co-operation. We're privileged to have a front seat at the intersection of creativity, communities and collaboration, with a unique opportunity to connect a global eco-system of partners, businesses and consumers in the MICE industry daily. Our ambition is to become a global leader made in Asia, and Asia's top MICE company: A vision shared by our shareholders Temasek and Cuscaden Peak Investments. We recognise the immense opportunity we have to lead with purpose, demonstrate leadership and commitment to sustainable and responsible business practices, and activate impactful networks for sustainable growth. You don't need to have MICE experience to work with us. We're looking for ambitious, entrepreneurial self-starters with a strong sense of ownership, passion and curiosity, driven by our values of care, ownership, trust, passion and innovation. RESPONSIBILITIES The Operations team at Singapore EXPO is seeking a dedicated individual to ensure the seamless daily execution of venue activities. This role involves direct participation in managing guest experiences and supporting international events, offering practical experience, fostering cross-functional teamwork, and providing significant career development opportunities in the MICE sector. Venue and Event Operations Oversee daily operations across Singapore EXPO to ensure all public areas, exhibition halls, and event spaces are clean, hazard-free, and ready for guest arrival. Coordinate site visit logistics and ensure all event signage is prepared accurately and aligned with branding and wayfinding requirements. Conduct pre-event operational checks and lead joint inspections with organisers to ensure smooth handover and takeover of venue spaces. Manage and fulfil all logistical and technical requirements requested by Organisers, including equipment setup, service coordination, and event-day support. Liaise with cleaning, security, and logistics vendors to ensure services are delivered in line with agreed service levels and timelines. Drive Safety and Compliance Conduct regular Fire and Safety inspections to identify hazards and ensure timely resolution in coordination with Organisers and internal stakeholders. Manage crowd control and traffic flow during high-footfall events to uphold safety standards and guest experience. Perform duties as a trained First Aider and Company Emergency Response Team (CERT) member, supporting on-site emergency response and preparedness (training provided). Administration and Operational Support Raise and track operational work orders promptly and accurately to support efficient event-related service delivery. Support procurement activities, including sourcing quotations, raising purchase orders, coordinating vendor services, and verifying invoices. Oversee the scheduling, accuracy, and real-time functionality of digital signage systems to ensure timely and clear communication to visitors. Undertake additional duties and cross-functional support tasks as assigned by the Reporting Officer. QUALIFICATIONS Higher NITEC or Diploma in Events Management or related field. Minimum 2 - 3 years of relevant experience in event operations. Strong organizational skills and ability to solve problems under pressure. High level of integrity and professionalism. Meticulous, detailed, resourceful, independent and a team player. Rotating shift work required, including weekends and public holidays as events demand. Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - Constellar Venues Pte Ltd

title: Manager/ Senior Manager, Process and Quality Control (Fab Operations) 3 Years Renewable Contract
description:
Job Description: Leading a team in responsible for the strategic design, implementation, and continuous improvement of all quality and process control systems within Fab Operations. This role is critical for ensuring world-class product yield, reducing defectivity, and maintaining strict compliance with customer specifications, internal quality standards, and relevant industry certifications. Key Responsibilities: Develop and drive a strategic roadmap for yield improvement and defect reduction across critical
     process modules. Lead the design and maintenance of advanced, real-time Statistical Process Control (SPC) , Fault
     Detection and Classification (FDC) , and Advanced Process Control (APC) systems to stabilize and optimize all fabrication steps. Cross-contamination management to achieve balance between research and development flexibilities in
     integration run sheet flow path vs process tools health and process repeatability. Small volume prototype protocol and product and process control plan alignment and set-up with requestor, process margin runs till demonstration of product SPC repeatability and line defect and electrical yield. Serve as the technical expert and primary quality interface for key customers, leading customer audits, addressing quality-related concerns, and driving customer-specific quality improvement plans. Problem Solving: Direct and oversee high-level, cross-functional root-cause analysis (RCA) and corrective and preventive action (CAPA) teams for major process excursions, non-conformances, and field returns, utilizing methodologies like 8D or Six Sigma Metrology & Inspection: Oversee the selection, qualification, and maintenance of all in-line and off-line metrology and defect inspection tools, ensuring measurement system accuracy (Gage R&R) and high data quality. Process Window Definition: Collaborate with Process Integration and R&D teams to define, qualify, and stabilize robust manufacturing process windows for all new product introductions (NPI), ensuring Design for Manufacturability (DFM) principles are met. Data Analytics: Leverage large-scale manufacturing data (Big Data) to identify subtle process drifts, predict
     failures, and proactively implement controls. Lead, mentor, and manage a team of Quality Engineers, Process Control Engineers, and Technicians, setting
     performance goals, developing career paths, and fostering a culture of ownership and data-driven decision-making. Collaborate closely with all stakeholders to balance the trade-off between quality stability, process efficiency, and tool uptime. Required Qualifications: Degree in Engineering, Materials Science, Chemical Engineering, or a related technical field. Preferable 10+ years of experience in the semiconductor industry, with a minimum of 5 years in a leadership/management role focusing on Process Control, Quality Assurance, or Yield Engineering within a Fab environment. Deep technical knowledge of semiconductor fabrication processes in different modules. Expert-level knowledge of Statistical Process Control (SPC) , Design of Experiments (DOE) , Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) , and Lean/Six Sigma methodologies (Black Belt certification highly preferred). Strong leadership, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the proven ability to influence and align technical and executive stakeholders. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. To support this growth, KK39 is seeking a Voice of Brand (Senior Associate) who will spearhead our branding, communications, and engagement efforts to ensure KK39’s presence in the investment community is visible, credible, and differentiated. Role Overview The Voice of Brand (Senior Associate) will act as KK39’s branding and communications lead, responsible for building a consistent external voice for the firm. This role blends strategic communications, event curation, and stakeholder engagement to elevate KK39’s profile in the investment ecosystem. You will work closely with the investment team, translating KK39’s activities, perspectives, and initiatives into compelling narratives and touchpoints that resonate with LPs, GPs, and partners. Key Responsibilities 1. Brand Strategy & Positioning · Develop and refine KK39’s brand narrative to reflect our unique positioning as a listed-group-backed investment office. · Ensure consistency of tone, voice, and design across all communication channels. · Build branding frameworks (templates, messaging guides, outreach playbooks) that the investment team can easily adopt. 2. Events & Community Engagement · Lead the end-to-end planning, coordination, and execution of KK39-hosted events (eg. LP/GP networking sessions, roundtables, thought-leadership dinners). · Manage event logistics, invitations, attendee engagement, and follow-up communications. · Build and maintain a calendar of industry events and conferences; coordinate KK39’s participation, visibility, and speaking opportunities. 3. Content & Communications · Craft thought-leadership content (LinkedIn posts, press releases, newsletters, and op-eds) to highlight KK39’s activities and perspectives. · Produce post-event summaries and follow-up campaigns to ensure engagement is maximised beyond the event itself. · Coordinate with external partners (media, PR, design vendors) as needed. 4. Stakeholder Engagement · Work alongside the investment team to strengthen relationships with LPs, GPs, and ecosystem partners by creating professional and timely touchpoints. · Track attendee feedback, engagement data, and industry response to measure brand-building effectiveness. · Support development of investor-facing materials (event decks, thought pieces, conference presence). 5. Internal Enablement · Free up the investment team by owning the operational and execution-heavy aspects of branding and events. · Maintain an internal repository of content, event templates, and standard communications for reuse. Job Requirements · Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or related field. · 3–5 years of experience in branding, corporate communications, or marketing within financial services, investment firms, or professional services. · Strong understanding of brand strategy, positioning, and messaging. · Excellent written and verbal communication skills; ability to craft compelling narratives. · Familiarity with content creation for LinkedIn, press releases, newsletters, and thought-leadership pieces. · Ability to manage external vendors (PR agencies, media, design). · Strong organizational skills and attention to detail. · Proficiency in digital marketing tools and social media platforms. · Comfortable working in a fast-paced, multi-asset investment environment.

Technical Expertise: Strong SAP skills, particularly in Project Portfolio Management (PPM), Project System (PS), Master Data Governance (MDG), and/or Information Management System (IMS). Knowledge and experience in Document Management Processes, IT, and Quality Systems. Knowledge of industry standards and regulations in this domain is an advantage. Good computer and business solutions software skills; experience with SAP and Google tools is considered an advantage. Soft Skills & Competencies: Quick learner, independent, with good analytical and problem-solving skills. Good Communication skills to different organizational levels. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application by 20 February 2026. We will do our best to contact you within 2 weeks after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The first interviews are preliminarily scheduled to take place soon. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. With about 200 employees we're a growing company with large aspirations to scale in the next five years. It can get intense and it will be messy at times. But few places can give you a seat at the table where you can contribute directly to the development of the business, the cultural DNA of an emerging MNC, and the building of a company proudly made and headquartered in Singapore with global business impact. And with two lines of businesses across events and venues, we provide opportunities for you to cross over from one to the other to build range in your career. You don't need to have MICE experience to work with us. We're looking for ambitious, entrepreneurial self-starters with a strong sense of ownership, passion and curiosity, driven by our values of care, ownership, trust, passion and innovation. If you thrive in a fast-paced, target-driven environment and excel at building strategic partnerships, this role is for you. Identify and develop new sales opportunities and build relationships with potential partners. Develop targeted new key account relationships and drive revenue growth. Build and maintain a strong and healthy sales pipeline to ensure that revenue targets are met and exceeded where possible. You will also be required to oversee the event from the initial sales process to completion of the event. Drive results by soliciting and securing business through proactive, consultative sales approach. Effectively prepare and present quotes and proposals to clients. Identify market trends and keep abreast of latest events and brand activations to stay relevant. Other duties as assigned by your Reporting Officer from time to time. QUALIFICATIONS Minimum 4 - 5 years of proven sales track record of hitting and exceeding targets, preferably in the exhibition industry. Exceptional communication and negotiation abilities. Strategic thinker with a data-driven mindset and a knack for spotting opportunities. Highly motivated, self-starter who thrives in a dynamic, target-driven environment. Proficient in CRM systems and sales tracking tools. Positive, proactive, and eager to make a meaningful impact. Excellent interpersonal, written and spoken communication skills. description:
AP Media is a creative media tech company that represents a new multi-disciplinary intersection point in the industry. At a time when audiences are craving for more immersive digital content, we are here to provide the technical expertise, experimental thinking and marketing innovation brands need to thrive. We aim to make futurealistic experiences across a spectrum of domains, from real-time 3D set mapping, virtual and hybrid activations to interactive live streaming, immersive multimedia and experiential marketing. We are looking for an open-minded, proactive, resourceful and dynamic individual, to join as a Virtual Production Camera Technician Job Description: • Set up, configure, operate, and perform
preventive maintenance on all studio and virtual production equipment,
including cameras, LED/VP processing units, tracking systems, video routers,
and AV infrastructure, ensuring every system is production-ready and
functioning reliably. • Troubleshoot camera and imaging
equipment and provide clear technical reports to the Studio Manager and
manufacturers to support timely issue resolution and minimise downtime. • Maintain system performance by updating
firmware and software across all devices and ensuring compatibility with
real-time production workflows. • Ensure that all technical equipment
meets operational and safety standards, with minimal disruption to studio
activities and production schedules. • Read, interpret, and design camera and
signal-flow diagrams, supporting clear and efficient routing for both physical
and virtual production environments. • Manage and route video signals,
including split camera feeds, genlock, timecode, and data lines, ensuring
synchronised and stable connections during shoots. • Apply strong knowledge of professional
video standards, including HD-SDI, DisplayPort, HDMI, RAW workflows, colour
metadata, and data-wrangling practices used in virtual production and broadcast
pipelines. • Maintain and support Virtual Production
systems, including tracking objects, markers, cabling, power management, and
full AV signal flow across the LED volume. • Assist the camera crew with calibration
workflows, including lens calibration, spatial calibration, and mounting of
virtual production tracking hardware on set. • Troubleshoot video feeds, tracking
stability, and calibration issues in collaboration with the Virtual Production
team to maintain accurate real-time rendering and scene playback. • Communicate changeover updates, camera
configuration changes, and system status to the Virtual Production team to
ensure smooth transitions and operational clarity. • Continuously develop skills and stay
updated on new technologies, maintaining a forward-thinking and adaptable
approach to evolving industry tools and workflows. • Proficient in video editing, supporting
quick reviews, technical tests, and content preparation when required.

description: About MIJ Hub Ltd MIJ Hub is a leading non-profit organization dedicated to transforming the lives of individuals with diverse learning needs. Through ventures like Ashraf's Cafe and the upcoming Komunal, we offer high-quality food and beverages while empowering individuals with special needs through employment and skill-building opportunities. Komunal is a specialty café startup focused on redefining the café experience. With a menu that blends high-quality coffee and experimental offerings, Komunal is poised to set new industry standards while providing an inclusive hiring platform. This is a unique opportunity to spearhead a fresh brand, shaping its culinary direction, innovation, and culture from the ground up as it grows. Komunal will serve both specialty coffee and crafted meals, offering a dynamic and creative environment where you can leave your mark. Ashraf's Cafe operates in the B2C and B2B space, focusing on online sales of baked goods, including cookies and other specialty bakes. The café operates at high volume, delivering top-notch products while providing meaningful employment for individuals with special needs. Job Title: Head Baker & Culinary Operations The Head Baker & Culinary Operations will oversee all baking operations for Ashraf's Cafe and Komunal, ensuring high standards in product quality, consistency, and innovation. You will manage all aspects of bakery production, from day-to-day operations to R&D for new items. A strong foundation in baking techniques is essential, and culinary expertise is a plus, especially to support Komunal's specialty food offerings. You will be responsible for ensuring strict adherence to quality control and processes, while working with a team of job coaches, special needs work clients, barista, fulfillment officer and part-time staff. This role involves maintaining efficient production processes, overseeing menu development, and managing inventory to deliver exceptional products consistently. Head Baker – Primary Responsibilities 1. Baking & Culinary Operations Execute and Coordinate the full spectrum of daily baking operations for Ashraf's Cafe (high-volume baked goods) and Komunal (specialty cafe), ensuring consistent quality and adherence to established SOPs. Oversee the timely production of all baked goods based on weekly projected quantities provided by the Fulfilment Officer, ensuring both Ashraf's Cafe and Komunal are adequately stocked at all times. Drive culinary excellence by continuously testing, refining, and improving recipes to meet customer expectations and uphold brand standards. 2. Operational Efficiency & Process Improvement Identify, implement, and refine innovative production techniques to enhance kitchen efficiency and reduce wastage without compromising quality. Lead the evaluation, recommendation, and acquisition of new kitchen equipment to improve workflow, production output, and overall operational performance. Ensure seamless coordination between in baking operations for both Ashraf's and Komunal Cafe 3. Menu Development & R&D Working with Social Enterprise Manager and Senior Barista to understand customer preferences for both Ashraf's Cafe and Komunal to align with the menu development and r&d Execute and coordinate new product development, including seasonal offerings, specialty bakes, and experimental menu items for both cafes. Ensure all menu creations meet high standards of taste, quality, presentation, and consistency. Align menu innovations with customer preferences, market trends, and cafe branding. 4. Inventory & Supply Chain Management Oversee inventory levels to ensure the timely ordering of ingredients, maintaining quality and cost-effectiveness at all times. Work closely with suppliers to ensure consistent ingredient availability, quality control, and competitive pricing. Implement inventory tracking systems to optimize stock rotation, minimize waste, and support accurate forecasting. 5. Health, Safety & Compliance Uphold strict compliance with food safety regulations, hygiene practices, and industry standards within the kitchen. Conduct routine health and safety checks to maintain a clean, organized, and hazard-free kitchen environment. Ensure all team members follow safe food handling procedures and proper sanitation protocols. Secondary Responsibilities 1. Order Fulfilment Step in to coordinate order fulfilment during the Fulfilment Officer’s absence, ensuring timely packing of orders and delivery of all customer orders and smooth operational continuity. 2. Support for Special Needs Clients Supervise and guide special needs working clients in the production cycle when the Job Coach is not present. Ensure tasks are clearly communicated and completed safely, effectively, and in line with production requirements. Requirements 5-10 years of experience in baking, pastry arts, or culinary arts, with expertise in high-volume production and specialty baking. Proven experience managing a baking team and overseeing culinary operations in a fast-paced environment. Culinary expertise is a plus, especially with experience in both baking and meal preparation for specialty cafés. Expertise in baking techniques, culinary arts, quality control, and menu development. Strong leadership skills, with the ability to train, mentor, and empower a diverse team. Excellent organizational skills and a proven ability to implement process improvements and optimize kitchen operations. Culinary qualifications or equivalent professional experience in baking or culinary arts. Certifications in food safety or kitchen management are a plus.

title: Supervisor, Production (Night shift)
description: Step into a career with ASM, where cutting edge technology meets collaborative culture. ​ For over 55 years ASM has been ahead of what’s next, at the forefront of innovation and what’s technologically possible. With more than 4,500 ASMers representing 70 nationalities, our people and our advanced semiconductor devices are playing a crucial role in trends such as 5G, cloud computing, AI, and autonomous driving.  But we’re more than just a tech company. We value diversity, inclusion and sustainability as we strive to make a positive impact on the world.  Our development programs help support your growth, shaping your future and pushing the boundaries of innovation to unleash potential. Job's mission We are seeking a proactive and experienced Production Supervisor to lead a manufacturing team in achieving production goals while maintaining high standards of quality, safety, and efficiency. The role involves overseeing daily shift operations, resource planning, team management, and continuous improvement initiatives. Reporting to the Senior Manager, the successful candidate will ensure smooth production handovers, enforce discipline, and drive lean manufacturing practices. This position requires strong leadership skills, hands-on technical expertise, and the ability to manage a team in a fast-paced capital equipment manufacturing environment. What you will be working on Lead the manufacturing team to ensure timely completion of production plans, while maintaining and improving workmanship standards. Schedule and allocate adequate resources, monitor progress, and resolve production issues proactively. Report shift production status and key updates to the Senior Manager. Act as a key personnel in independently leading team discipline, shift planning, and day-to-day operations. Oversee team recruitment, performance appraisals, and ensure skills training is aligned with current and future shift requirements. Maintain a clean, organized, and safe work environment in accordance with workplace safety and housekeeping standards. Lead or participate in lean manufacturing and continuous improvement projects (e.g., Kaizen, 5S, waste reduction). Coordinate effectively with other Shift Supervisors to ensure smooth and well-communicated production handovers. Analyze operational practices and recommend or implement system/process improvements. What we are looking for Minimum Bachelor's in Engineering related studies. Minimum 5 years of experience in a technical environment within the capital equipment, or aviation or manufacturing industries. Minimum 3 years of supervisory experience managing a highly skilled technician workforce Practical, hands-on experience in equipment manufacturing, including mechanical/electrical systems and troubleshooting. Willing and able to work on an 8-hour fixed shift schedule Strong communication, organizational, analytical, and interpersonal skills. Proven experience in implementing Lean/Kaizen or other continuous improvement projects. Demonstrated capability in resource planning and team management. What sets you apart Experience in semiconductor or high-tech capital equipment manufacturing. Familiarity with advanced manufacturing systems and automation. Apply today to be part of what’s next. We make the tech that enables the chips in devices which improve lives around the world. We do this with an eye to the future, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible through cutting-edge innovation, and driving the next wave of technological breakthroughs that shape how we live, work, and connect. To learn more about ASM, find us at asm.com and on LinkedIn , Facebook , Instagram, X and YouTube . ASM is an equal opportunity employer and considers qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, nationality, social or ethnic origin, sexual orientation, gender, gender identify or expression, marital status, pregnancy, political affiliation, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: CAD/CAM Engineer
description: Job Description & Requirements Cyrus Technology started in 2005 with the vision to approach polymeric tooling design in unconventional methods and providing manufacturing solutions to exacting specifications for ultra-precision and complex moulding. We pride ourselves as a valued partner to our clients, offering expertise at every stage of the product design to manufacturing, to transform market opportunity ideas to a marketable product.  With the progress we achieved so far, we aim to continue to push boundaries and overcome constraints to be a leader in the industry. Your key responsibilities are as follows: To develop, document and support the build of high precision plastic injection tooling and molded parts for manufacturing. Support Program team during upfront new project quotation stage by providing accurate upfront tooling/product DFM that matches the right quality requirements. To meet customer for DFM and mold concept review to ensure customer’s technical requirements and concerns are addressed. Support Tooling Department by conducting internal tooling design and DFM reviews before tool launch. Support Production in areas like molding process optimization, implement manufacturing fixtures and automation for reliability and efficiency improvements. Support Quality Team in the design and fabrication of test/measurement fixtures. To coordinate mold trials, tooling and molded part troubleshooting and carry out necessary engineering changes to ensure parts can be produced smoothly with the required quality. Your areas of knowledge and expertise that matter most for this role: Excellent technical analytical ability with strong interest for problem solving. Self-motivated team player with good interpersonal and communication skills. Past working experience or knowledge of Precision Tooling Design/Fabrication, Micro Plastic Injection Molding Process or Manufacturing Process Engineering related field preferred. 3D CAD software skill for Siemens UG NX or PTC Creo 4.0 and above

title: Technical Support Engineer (Regional)
description: We are seeking a Technical Support Engineer for our client in the chemical manufacturing industry to be responsible for providing technical expertise and application support to drive the growth of the engineering plastics business regionally. This role supports customers and internal stakeholders through material evaluation, application development, failure analysis, and technical solutions, working closely with Commercial and R&D teams & require someone with a strong plastic injection molding background. Responsibilities: Provide technical support to customers and internal commercial teams, including technical data, material selection guidance, and competitor grade benchmarking for pre-sales and after-sales activities. Deliver comprehensive technical solutions beyond material supply, including tooling recommendations, part design analysis, CAE simulations, and injection molding support. Plan, conduct, and evaluate analysis methods based on actual application requirements to generate relevant technical data for customer development projects. Conduct molding trials with customers to validate material and application performance. Investigate mechanical and physical properties of engineering plastics using scientific instruments to support new product and application development. Perform failure benchmarking, root cause analysis, and destructive or non-destructive testing to identify material or application defects. Translate customer application requirements into material property specifications and collaborate with R&D to develop or formulate new materials. Support technical discussions and negotiations with customers on performance limitations of newly developed materials. Work closely with the commercial team to develop mid-term and long-term business strategies for target applications and markets. Provide technical support to the commercial team to achieve short-term sales and budget targets. Conduct market research on emerging technologies and industry trends relevant to current and future applications. Support technical marketing initiatives to develop new markets and customers. Provide technical training to distributors, traders, and business partners; extend training to customers when required. Maintain accurate technical documentation, test reports, experimental records, and procedures. Support and participate in trade shows, exhibitions, and technical seminars to enhance product branding and market presence. Requirements: Degree in Polymer Science, Materials Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or a related discipline. Experience in engineering plastics, polymers, or related materials industries is a must Strong knowledge of material testing, failure analysis, and injection molding processes. Ability to translate application needs into technical and material solutions. Strong communication skills with the ability to work cross-functionally with commercial, R&D, and customer teams. Willingness to travel regionally For more jobs: please visit https://www.onesevenc.com/jobs Interested candidates may email your resume to Alycia Lim (alycia@onesevenc.com) We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Alycia Lim Hui Choo alycia@onesevenc.com EA License No.: 23S2032 | R24124179

title: Assistant Editor
description: To enhance the productivity and output quality of Click2View’s video and animation teams by managing routine post-production tasks efficiently, maintaining project organisation, and ensuring that senior creatives can focus on high-value storytelling and leadership work. Outcome Success 1. Smooth Post-Production Workflow • Assets, footage, and project files are properly named, tagged, and stored according to C2V’s file management system. • Senior editors report at least a 20–30% time saving per project due to delegated tasks. 2. Efficient Prep and Assembly • Rough cuts and assembly edits are delivered ahead of schedule with clean timelines. • Editor/producer feedback loops are fast and friction-free (≤2 rounds for base edits). 3. Cross-Team Support • Provides daily editing, motion or graphics support across at least 2–3 concurrent projects. • Positive feedback from editors, designers, and animators for reliability and responsiveness. 4. Continuous Learning & Growth • Learns and applies at least one new editing/motion technique per quarter. • Progresses toward handling small projects independently by end of year one. 5. Quality & Consistency • Maintains brand guidelines, LUTs, templates, and animation presets consistently. • Zero rework due to avoidable technical or organisational errors. Competency 1. Technical Proficiency Skilled in Adobe Premiere Pro and After Effects. Comfortable managing media, timelines, proxies, exports, captions, and simple motion templates. 2. Attention to Detail Spots continuity errors, sync issues, and inconsistencies before senior review. Maintains tidy project structures. 3. Proactivity Anticipates editors’ needs (e.g., pre-cut selects, gather b-roll, prep LUTs) before being asked. 4. Collaboration & Communication Keeps editors and producers updated; clarifies creative direction; responds fast and clearly. 5. Time Management Juggles multiple project tasks, prioritising based on deadlines and team dependencies. 6. Creative Curiosity Keen to learn colour correction, sound design, animation, and storytelling fundamentals. 7. Adaptability Handles feedback positively and adjusts quickly to evolving project needs or styles. Values 1. Diligence Double-checks exports, QC’s every deliverable, keeps assets backed up. 2. Empathy Understands what senior creatives need to do their best work. Offers help before being asked. 3. Collaboration Shares learnings and shortcuts with peers; contributes to process improvement. 4. Creativity Finds elegant, fast ways to solve editing or motion bottlenecks. 5. Customer Focus Ensures everything passed to the producer or client-facing editor is clean and polished. Candidate Profile Experience: • 0–2 years in video post-production (internship, freelance, or agency). • Familiarity with Adobe Creative Cloud (Premiere, After Effects, Media Encoder). • Basic understanding of storytelling, pacing, and visual design. Mindset: • Hungry to learn, humble enough to take direction, and highly organised. • Energised by supporting a creative team and making them look great. • Excited about a long-term career path toward video editor, motion designer, or producer. 12-Month Milestones: Q1 Master Click2View’s file and delivery systems; reliably assist on small edits. Q2 Independently assemble social edits, reels, and lower-tier internal projects. Q3 Begin light motion graphics work; support senior animators with assets. Q4 Handle simple branded content projects end-to-end with senior oversight.

title: Assistant Course/Program Director
description: Stag Match Institute (SMI) is seeking a Independent , resourceful, dynamic and collaborative Assistant Course/Program Director to provide strategic leadership and management for a portfolio of courses and programs, with a significant focus on fostering strong linkages with the Continuing Education and Training (CET) sector and the wider Institute of Higher Learning (IHL) landscape in Singapore. This pivotal role encompasses business development , sales and academic leadership across the assigned portfolio, curriculum development aligned with industry needs and CET frameworks, ensuring student success across diverse offerings, and crucially, driving enrollment growth through effective outreach and strategic partnerships within the CET and IHL ecosystem. The ideal candidate will be a visionary leader, Hand-on  ,resourceful and sale oriental with a passion for lifelong learning, a deep understanding of the CET landscape and IHL collaborations, and proven experience in managing multiple programs and engaging with diverse stakeholders. Responsibilities: Academic Leadership & Portfolio Management (CET & IHL Focus): Provide strategic direction and academic leadership for a portfolio of courses and programs, ensuring alignment with the institute's overall academic vision, quality standards, and the evolving needs of the CET sector and relevant IHL initiatives. Support all aspects of program delivery within the portfolio, including curriculum design, development, review, and continuous improvement, with a focus on modularity, stackability, and relevance to working professionals and industry demands within the CET framework. Support and responsible for sale and business development with existing and new partnership Manage and mentor course coordinators and faculty members teaching within the portfolio, fostering a collaborative environment that encourages engagement with CET methodologies and IHL best practices. Ensure all programs within the portfolio adhere to relevant accreditation standards, regulatory requirements, and the specific quality benchmarks of CET programs in Singapore (e.g., SkillsFuture). Support student progress across the portfolio, provide guidance on academic and career pathways relevant to CET, and implement strategies to enhance student retention and success, including recognition of prior learning (RPL) where applicable. Develop and manage the overall budget for the assigned portfolio, ensuring resources are allocated efficiently to support program goals and collaborative CET/IHL activities. Cultivate and maintain strong relationships with industry partners, SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG), other CET providers, and relevant departments within other IHLs to ensure the portfolio's relevance, create opportunities for student upskilling and reskilling, and explore potential collaborations across different programs. Supporting learners in their career placement pathway . Creating Recruitment fair for industry partners and learners to network and create placement event. Sales & Enrollment Growth (Portfolio & CET/IHL Engagement): Develop and implement strategic enrollment plans for the portfolio of courses and programs, with a specific focus on attracting working professionals, career switchers, and individuals seeking CET qualifications for various offerings. Represent the institute's CET-related programs at recruitment events, industry seminars, and collaborative initiatives with other IHLs and CET providers. Collaborate closely with the marketing and admissions teams to develop targeted marketing materials, website content, and digital campaigns that resonate with the CET audience for the diverse program offerings and highlight their alignment with national skills frameworks. Engage with prospective CET learners and their employers across the portfolio, providing detailed information about the various program curricula, career advancement opportunities, and the benefits of utilizing SkillsFuture credits and other CET funding schemes. Conduct presentations and workshops for prospective CET students and corporate clients across the portfolio, emphasizing the relevance of different programs to industry needs and their articulation with other IHL qualifications where applicable. Track and analyze enrollment data across the portfolio, including the profile of CET learners in different programs, to evaluate the effectiveness of recruitment strategies and partnerships within the CET and IHL ecosystem. Build and maintain relationships with alumni from various programs who have leveraged their education for career progression within the CET framework. Explore and implement innovative recruitment strategies tailored to the CET audience for the diverse portfolio, including partnerships with employers, online learning platforms, and collaborations with other training providers and IHLs. Qualifications: A degree in a relevant field. Minimum of 2 years of experience in academic administration or program management within a higher education institution, with demonstrable experience in or strong understanding of the CET sector in Singapore. Demonstrated experience in developing curriculum relevant to industry needs and aligned with CET frameworks (e.g., modularization, stackability) across multiple programs. Proven track record of successful student recruitment, particularly within the adult learning or CET market, for a range of educational offerings. Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage and influence diverse audiences, including industry professionals and CET stakeholders, across different program areas. Strong leadership, organizational, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects and initiatives concurrently and a collaborative mindset. Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment and across different institutions and organizations. Familiarity with the higher education and CET landscape in Singapore, including SkillsFuture initiatives and relevant funding schemes. Experience in developing and managing partnerships with industry and/or CET providers across a portfolio of programs is highly desirable. Experience with CRM systems and enrollment management software, as well as platforms relevant to CET learners, is an advantage. Personal Attributes: Passionate about lifelong learning and empowering working professionals through a diverse range of educational opportunities. Highly motivated and results-oriented with a proactive approach to building partnerships and driving enrollment within the CET sector for a portfolio of programs. Strong understanding of the needs and motivations of adult learners across different fields. Excellent networking and stakeholder management skills. Strong ethical standards and a commitment to academic integrity and the principles of continuing education. Adaptable and resilient in a dynamic environment, with a willingness to engage with the evolving CET landscape across various program areas. A strong advocate for the institute and its diverse program offerings within both the IHL and CET communities.

title: Platform Engineer (DevSecOps) - A26023
description: Activate Interactive Pte Ltd (“Activate”) is a leading technology consultancy headquartered in Singapore with a presence in Malaysia and Indonesia. Our clients are empowered with quality, cost-effective, and impactful end-to-end application development, like mobile and web applications, and cloud technology that remove technology roadblocks and increase their business efficiency. We believe in positively impacting the lives of people around us and the environment we live in through the use of technology. Hence, we are committed to providing a conducive environment for all employees to realise their full potential, who in turn have the opportunity to continuously drive innovation. We are searching for our next team members to join our growing team. If you love the idea of being part of a growing company with exciting prospects in mobile and web technologies that create positive impact on people’s lives, then we would love to hear from you. Co-Development Business Unit is looking for Platform Engineer This is a fixed term contract role. The engagement is 12 months Internal Code: A26023 If you are: Engineers that are excited by building internal platforms similar to Vercel or Railway. Builders who enjoy turning complex infrastructure into simple developer workflows. Engineers who want to work across backend platform services, GitOps workflows, and frontend developer experience. this is the role for you. What will you do? Platform & Abstraction Design (Primary Focus) Design and build developer-facing abstractions over AWS and Kubernetes that hide infrastructure complexity. Build platform services, APIs, CLIs, controllers, or operators that enable simple deployment workflows similar to modern PaaS platforms. Define opinionated defaults for networking, security, scaling, and reliability, while allowing controlled extensibility. Treat platform components as long-lived products with clear interfaces, documentation, and adoption metrics. Software Engineering Write production-grade code primarily in: Go or Python for platform services, automation, and Kubernetes integrations. TypeScript for contributing to the internal developer portal and related APIs. Apply strong software engineering fundamentals: API and interface design Versioning and backward compatibility Error handling and failure modes Testing and maintainability Design code with the expectation that other engineers and tenant teams build on top of it. Internal Developer Portal (Custom-Built) Contribute features and improvements to a custom internal developer portal built with TypeScript and NextJS. Design workflows, forms, and APIs that enable self-service provisioning, deployment, and observability. Ensure the portal reflects platform standards, guardrails, and golden paths. Collaborate closely with backend platform components to deliver a cohesive developer experience. CI/CD, GitOps & Deployment Workflows Design and maintain GitOps-based deployment workflows using ArgoCD. Build reusable CI/CD abstractions using GitLab CI/CD for build, test, and promotion workflows. Integrate CI pipelines with ArgoCD for safe, auditable, and repeatable deployments. Support deployment strategies such as canary and blue-green deployments to minimise downtime. Reduce deployment friction for tenants by standardising workflows and removing the need for deep CI/CD or Kubernetes expertise. Infrastructure as Code (IaC) Design and maintain reusable Terraform modules that encode platform standards and guardrails. Abstract AWS and Kubernetes primitives behind stable, versioned interfaces. Ensure infrastructure changes are auditable, repeatable, and safe for multi-tenant usage. Databases and Data Protection Design platform-level patterns for database provisioning and operations. Support and validate backup and restore strategies, including automated backups, snapshot management, and recovery testing. Work with tenants to ensure data durability, recoverability, and minimal downtime during incidents. Observability and Monitoring Build platform-level observability capabilities using Elastic Cloud for logs, traces, dashboards, and alerting. Integrate Prometheus for metrics scraping and collection, with metrics surfaced via Elastic. Apply the Four Golden Signals (latency, traffic, errors, saturation) as a core observability principle. Provide standard dashboards, alerts, and SLOs that tenants inherit by default. Production Support and Incident Management Participate in incident response for platform components. Design platform features that improve debuggability, enable safe rollbacks, and reduce MTTR. Drive post-incident improvements by evolving platform design rather than relying on manual processes. Performance, Optimisation, and Reliability Design platform components for scalability, performance, and cost efficiency. Identify and address bottlenecks at the platform layer. Continuously improve reliability through automation, better defaults, and guardrails. Tenant Engagement, Developer Experience & Knowledge Sharing Engage tenant teams as a technical advisor on platform usage and best practices. Produce clear documentation, diagrams, runbooks, and examples that improve onboarding and adoption. Gather feedback from tenants and iterate on platform capabilities based on real usage. Continuous Learning Stay current with developments in AWS, Kubernetes, GitOps, and the cloud-native ecosystem. Evaluate new tools and patterns relevant to internal developer platforms and PaaS-style workflows. Share learnings within the team and contribute to continuous improvement What are we looking for? Strong hands-on experience with: AWS Kubernetes Terraform GitLab CI/CD ArgoCD Elastic Cloud (logs, traces, dashboards, alerting) Prometheus (metrics scraping and collection) Strong programming skills in Go or Python. Working knowledge of TypeScript, with a willingness to contribute to a NextJS codebase. Proven experience building tooling, services, or abstractions that other engineers depend on. Experience supporting production systems, including incident response. Experience with databases, including backup, restore, and recovery considerations. Ability to communicate clearly with tenant teams and internal stakeholders. Strong experience with TypeScript, React, or NextJS. Experience building custom Internal Developer Portals or self-service platforms. Experience building Kubernetes controllers, operators, or admission webhooks. Familiarity with canary, blue-green, or other progressive delivery techniques. Experience with SLOs and reliability engineering practices. What do we offer in return? Fun working environment Employee Wellness Program To work in Singapore Government Agencies projects We provide structured development framework and growth opportunities. (We are a “SHRI 2025 Gold winner” in “Learning & Development; Coaching & Mentoring”) Why you'll love working with us? If you are looking for opportunities to collaborate with leading industry experts and be surrounded by highly motivated and talented peers, we welcome you to join us. We provide all employees with equal opportunities to grow and develop with us. We believe your success is our success. Does it sound like something you are interested in exploring further? Please be in touch with our team for an initial chat at chi@activate.sg Activate Interactive Singapore is an equal opportunity employer. Employment decisions will be based on merit, qualifications and abilities. title: Project Manager (Arch)
description: OVERVIEW The Project Manager (Architectural & Façade) is responsible for the overall planning, coordination, and execution of architectural and façade construction projects from initiation to completion. This role focuses on ensuring timely delivery, quality workmanship, cost control, and compliance with safety and regulatory standards. The position requires a blend of technical expertise, leadership, and strong communication skills to manage multiple stakeholders — including consultants, subcontractors, and internal departments—while driving project success and client satisfaction. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES 1. Project Planning & Execution - Develop comprehensive project plans that define scope, schedule, budget, manpower, and resource allocation. - Lead and coordinate all architectural and façade installation works, ensuring compliance with design intent, specifications, and safety standards. - Oversee day-to-day site operations, ensuring progress aligns with project milestones and timelines. - Identify and resolve technical and coordination issues promptly in collaboration with consultants, drafters, and site teams. 2. Design & Technical Coordination - Coordinate with architects, engineers, and drafting teams to ensure shop drawings and fabrication details are accurate and approved before production. - Review and validate technical submissions, samples, and mock-ups for client and consultant approval. - Work closely with the fabrication and installation teams to ensure buildability and adherence to design intent. - Lead technical meetings with consultants and clients to resolve design discrepancies or interface issues. 3. Cost & Commercial Control - Prepare and manage project budgets and forecasts, ensuring projects are delivered within approved financial targets. - Monitor expenses, variations, and claims throughout the project lifecycle to maintain cost control and safeguard profitability. - Review and endorse subcontractor progress claims, material requisitions, and project payments. - Provide regular financial and progress updates to senior management. 4. Quality, Safety & Compliance - Ensure all works comply with architectural specifications, building codes, and statutory requirements. - Implement quality control measures and perform regular site inspections to uphold workmanship and material standards. - Enforce Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) requirements and Environmental Management System (EMS) compliance across all site activities. - Promote a culture of safety awareness, quality, and continuous improvement among project teams and subcontractors. Promote a culture of continuous improvement by capturing lessons learned and integrating best practices into future projects. 5. Team Leadership & Coordination: - Lead, mentor, and motivate project teams, including site managers, engineers, subcontractors, and drafters, to achieve performance excellence. - Establish clear roles, responsibilities, and communication protocols to ensure alignment and accountability. - Foster collaboration across internal and external stakeholders, promoting teamwork and knowledge sharing to enhance project outcomes. 6. Stakeholder & Client Management - Liaise with clients, consultants, and authorities on all project matters. - Provide professional communication, timely updates, and proactive issue resolution to maintain high client satisfaction. - Manage change orders and variation submissions, ensuring proper documentation and approvals. 7. Project Handover & Close-Out - Ensure completion of all project-related works within the stipulated timeline and quality requirements. - Coordinate final inspections, testing, and commissioning activities. - Oversee the submission of as-built drawings, warranties, O&M manuals, and handover documentation to clients. - Conduct post-project reviews to capture lessons learned and recommend process improvements. KEY COMPETENCIES - Strong project management and organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. - Solid technical knowledge of architectural finishes, façade systems, and construction methodologies. - Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills. - Proficiency in interpreting drawings, specifications, and construction documentation. - Strong financial awareness and ability to manage budgets, cost controls, and claims. - Commitment to quality, safety, and continuous improvement. REQUIREMENTS · Degree or Diploma in Civil Engineering, Architecture, Building & Construction, or equivalent discipline. · Minimum 5–8 years of project management experience, preferably in architectural and/or façade related works within the Singapore construction industry. · Proven track record in managing subcontractors, design coordination, and on-site execution for medium to large-scale projects. · Familiar with BCA codes, workplace safety regulations, and local authority submission requirements. · Possess Safety for Project Managers course or equivalent certification. · Strong analytical, communication, and interpersonal skills. · Ability to lead and motivate teams under tight schedules and demanding project environments. WORKING CONDITIONS The standard working week is five and a half (5.5) days; however, you may be required to work on Saturdays and Sundays

description: Purpose of the role To design, develop and improve software, utilising various engineering methodologies, that provides business, platform, and technology capabilities for our customers and colleagues. OR for an individual contributor, they will lead collaborative assignments and guide team members through structured assignments, identify the need for the inclusion of other areas of specialisation to complete assignments. They will identify new directions for assignments and/ or projects, identifying a combination of cross functional methodologies or practices to meet required outcomes. Collaborate with other areas of work, for business aligned support areas to keep up to speed with business activity and the business strategy. Engage in complex analysis of data from multiple sources of information, internal and external sources such as procedures and practises (in other areas, teams, companies, etc).to solve problems creatively and effectively. Communicate complex information. 'Complex' information could include sensitive information or information that is difficult to communicate because of its content or its audience. Influence or convince stakeholders to achieve outcomes. All colleagues will be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Values of Respect, Integrity, Service, Excellence and Stewardship – our moral compass, helping us do what we believe is right. They will also be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Mindset – to Empower, Challenge and Drive – the operating manual for how we behave. We are seeking a strong C++ developer for designing and developing ultra-low-latency end-to-end Direct Market Access (DMA) trading platform. As a Barclays Developer within Equities QPS (Quantitative Prime Services) Technology, you will join a team focused on building out the functionality and technology of the SubM , SUBM-R, SubM Native systems for our flagship product offering for giving ultra-low latency to a very niche domain of very high volume and low latency sophisticated Equites trading clients, mostly Hedge funds as well as develop Cash Connectivity Platform for our Equities Cash Business. Key Accountabilities Design and development of industry-leading, ultra-low-latency Equities trading systems. Individual should be taking up the full responsibility and drive multiple deliveries at a time. Work closely with existing leads to review current platform architecture and propose and implement changes geared towards performance improvements, simplification, and technical debt reduction. Be a go to person for Business/ Desk and provide estimates for tasks including design, development, and testing. Holistic simulation and testing of the trading system for high reliability. Support analysis, testing, and operations teams. Participate in code reviews and architectural decisions. Mentor junior developers. Problem Solving Working with business analysts and project managers to understand the requirements. Analysing those requirements to find the most appropriate technical solution and creating a technical specification where needed. Developing a pragmatic software solution as per the requirement specifications. Unit / system / regression / performance testing of the solutions. Fixing bugs coming out of System / UAT testing. Reviewing code produced by others. Supporting the implementation of each new code. Resolving any post-implementation issues as needed. Participating in post-project reviews to improve the project lifecycle where necessary. Essential Skills/Basic Qualifications: Excellent 5+ years of experience with expertise in C++ and conversant with Modern C++ topics and features. Expert in data structures, algorithms, multi-threading, and C++ standard library. C++ performance and low latency understanding to make latency reduction on the critical path of flow. Good working experience of Linux/Unix operating system and Linux commands. Working experience, collaboration in the regional and global team structure. Desirable skills/Preferred Qualifications: Front office trading development on the critical path to the exchange and may have developed low latency system specially for APAC markets. Proven experience in working in the Investment banking, working with directly with Traders and Desk Heads. Algorithmic Trading experience with a High Frequency Trading Firm. User Space Networking: Solar flare open onload. Lower level TCPIP Experience. title: Institute of Higher Learning Course / Program Director
description: Stag Match Institute (SMI) is seeking a Independent , resourceful, dynamic and collaborative Course/Program Director to provide strategic leadership and management for a portfolio of courses and programs, with a significant focus on fostering strong linkages with the Continuing Education and Training (CET) sector and the wider Institute of Higher Learning (IHL) landscape in Singapore. This pivotal role encompasses business development , sales and academic leadership across the assigned portfolio, curriculum development aligned with industry needs and CET frameworks, ensuring student success across diverse offerings, and crucially, driving enrollment growth through effective outreach and strategic partnerships within the CET and IHL ecosystem. The ideal candidate will be a visionary leader ,resourceful and sale oriental with a passion for lifelong learning, a deep understanding of the CET landscape and IHL collaborations, and proven experience in managing multiple programs and engaging with diverse stakeholders. Responsibilities : Academic Leadership & Portfolio Management (CET & IHL Focus): * Provide strategic direction and academic leadership for a portfolio of courses and programs, ensuring alignment with the institute's overall academic vision, quality standards, and the evolving needs of the CET sector and relevant IHL initiatives. * Oversee all aspects of program delivery within the portfolio, including curriculum design, development, review, and continuous improvement, with a focus on modularity, stackability, and relevance to working professionals and industry demands within the CET framework. Oversea and responsible for sale and business development with existing and new partnership Manage and mentor course coordinators and faculty members teaching within the portfolio, fostering a collaborative environment that encourages engagement with CET methodologies and IHL best practices. Ensure all programs within the portfolio adhere to relevant accreditation standards, regulatory requirements, and the specific quality benchmarks of CET programs in Singapore (e.g., SkillsFuture). Monitor student progress across the portfolio, provide guidance on academic and career pathways relevant to CET, and implement strategies to enhance student retention and success, including recognition of prior learning (RPL) where applicable. Develop and manage the overall budget for the assigned portfolio, ensuring resources are allocated efficiently to support program goals and collaborative CET/IHL activities. Cultivate and maintain strong relationships with industry partners, SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG), other CET providers, and relevant departments within other IHLs to ensure the portfolio's relevance, create opportunities for student upskilling and reskilling, and explore potential collaborations across different programs. Sales & Enrollment Growth (Portfolio & CET/IHL Engagement): Develop and implement strategic enrollment plans for the portfolio of courses and programs, with a specific focus on attracting working professionals, career switchers, and individuals seeking CET qualifications for various offerings. * Represent the institute's CET-related programs at recruitment events, industry seminars, and collaborative initiatives with other IHLs and CET providers. * Collaborate closely with the marketing and admissions teams to develop targeted marketing materials, website content, and digital campaigns that resonate with the CET audience for the diverse program offerings and highlight their alignment with national skills frameworks. * Engage with prospective CET learners and their employers across the portfolio, providing detailed information about the various program curricula, career advancement opportunities, and the benefits of utilizing SkillsFuture credits and other CET funding schemes. * Conduct presentations and workshops for prospective CET students and corporate clients across the portfolio, emphasizing the relevance of different programs to industry needs and their articulation with other IHL qualifications where applicable. * Track and analyze enrollment data across the portfolio, including the profile of CET learners in different programs, to evaluate the effectiveness of recruitment strategies and partnerships within the CET and IHL ecosystem. * Build and maintain relationships with alumni from various programs who have leveraged their education for career progression within the CET framework. * Explore and implement innovative recruitment strategies tailored to the CET audience for the diverse portfolio, including partnerships with employers, online learning platforms, and collaborations with other training providers and IHLs. Qualifications : A degree in a relevant field. Minimum of 2 years of experience in academic administration or program management within a higher education institution, with demonstrable experience in or strong understanding of the CET sector in Singapore. Demonstrated experience in developing curriculum relevant to industry needs and aligned with CET frameworks (e.g., modularization, stackability) across multiple programs. Proven track record of successful student recruitment, particularly within the adult learning or CET market, for a range of educational offerings. Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage and influence diverse audiences, including industry professionals and CET stakeholders, across different program areas. Strong leadership, organizational, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects and initiatives concurrently and a collaborative mindset. Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment and across different institutions and organizations. Familiarity with the higher education and CET landscape in Singapore, including SkillsFuture initiatives and relevant funding schemes. Experience in developing and managing partnerships with industry and/or CET providers across a portfolio of programs is highly desirable. Experience with CRM systems and enrollment management software, as well as platforms relevant to CET learners, is an advantage. Personal Attributes: Passionate about lifelong learning and empowering working professionals through a diverse range of educational opportunities. Highly motivated and results-oriented with a proactive approach to building partnerships and driving enrollment within the CET sector for a portfolio of programs. Strong understanding of the needs and motivations of adult learners across different fields. Excellent networking and stakeholder management skills. Strong ethical standards and a commitment to academic integrity and the principles of continuing education. Adaptable and resilient in a dynamic environment, with a willingness to engage with the evolving CET landscape across various program areas. A strong advocate for the institute and its diverse program offerings within both the IHL and CET communities. Basic + commission (Sale)

title: Line Maintenance Training and Development Manager
description:
Purpose Engineering’s role is focused on reassurance, keeping our people and our customers    safe, ensuring that in every aspect of our operation we have industry leading performance and that our various fleet
programmes are delivering to plan, budget and specification. Engineering’s ambition is to be one of the world’s highest performing, customer centric and progressive engineering teams. Cathay Pacific Line Maintenance provides world class maintenance support and services to the Cathay group and to third party customers across the globe. The Maintenance Training Centre (MTC) has been launched to develop a continuous pipeline of future talent by delivering tailored theoretical and hands-on training content across the Line Maintenance network. The Line Maintenance Training and Development Manager is responsible for managing the MTC and formulate staff training and development strategies to map with LM staff career structure and regional manpower profile. The role requires a high degree of autonomy, agility and critical thinking ability to ensure staff are trained up to the latest company standards and meeting HKAR-145 requirements, and collaborate effectively across a wide range of stakeholders to enable MTC to deliver training that matches with the company’s fleet development and business needs. The role will set training direction for Cathay Line Maintenance and ensure MTC the cradle of top class mechanics and licensed engineers for the Company. Key Responsibilities Training Planning & Development Set training direction according to company’s business operating plan and regulatory requirements by aviation authorities to ensure the syllabus of MTC are up to standard. Ensure MTC promotes safety, operational, and technical awareness. Design a syllabus for MTC with the target to map with Line Maintenance career structure and network’s manpower profile. Provide subject matter expertise in the creation or modification of
lessons, training materials and/or training device. Recruitment of external training service provider to deliver training per the syllabus and training schedule of MTC. Contract review and negotiation with external training service providers Identify potential trainers in regions to assist with course delivery,
accountable for the performance standards of regional trainers and their development.
Collaborate with stakeholders to identify and prioritise learning and development needs for Line Maintenance to meet HKAR-145 requirements Production of training forecast and analytics to ensure MTC training
strategy is in the right direction and all plans are on tracks to achieve
the expected targets and within the projected budget. Provide HLM references on staff recruitment, promotions and staff career matters Maintenance Training Centre (MTC) Management Manage the MTC on a day to day basis and ensure it is run cost
   effectively. Ensure sufficient staff with appropriate qualifications are selected, trained and developed, to plan, perform, teach, supervise, examine and assess students as required. Supervise the performance of external and internal trainers, align technical training standards to ensure they are achieved and subsequently maintained. Perform regular evaluation and review of MTC training syllabus and training plan to ensure the knowledge conveyed is up to date and compliant to HKAR-145 requirements. Ensure training materials are updated per latest development in the operation and training needs of frontline engineers and mechanics. Develop an evaluation process for each MTC course to ensure staff engagement and training has been effectively conducted and outcome produced meets the quality requirement of the course syllabus Manage training demands with line managers and work with LM Ops Teams on the training schedules and setting the enrolment requirements. Provide a working environment appropriate to the instructional and examination tasks being undertaken. Maintain MTC records and documentation in accordance with Company and regulatory requirements. Provide follow up and rectification of all external and internal quality audit findings required to re-establish the required standards of  examination, training standards and training course-ware. Deputise Head of Line Maintenance (HLM) and carry out operational duties when required. Travel to line stations for training conduction when
  required. Monthly visit to CX Headquarter in HKG for KPI/progress reporting is required should the staff be outport-based. Qualifications/Experience/Skills/Knowledge Engineering degree or Aircraft Maintenance Licence (HKCAD license or equivalent) Minimum 10 years of experience in Line Maintenance or Maintenance Control (in which minimum 2 years are in a management role) Minimum 5 years of experience in training conduction for 145 organisation Experience in creation of training program and contents Experience in managing a team and delivering high performance Experience of leading changes and delivering productivity and operational improvement Experience of using IT or technology to assist in the delivery of training programmes. Strong in communication and able to deliver knowledge in an effective manner Strong team player, willing to travel and work with internal and external stakeholders

title: Senior Construction Operations Manager - Founding Member, New Strategic Business Unit
description: An Exceptional Opportunity to Shape the Future of a Growing Medium-Sized Enterprise's Greenfield Construction Venture in Singapore The Company: A successful and growing medium-sized enterprise with a strong track record in related industries is strategically establishing a new construction business unit in Singapore to capitalize on significant market opportunities. This is a unique and high-impact opportunity to be a foundational member of this venture, leveraging the established reputation and agility of a thriving company while building a dynamic and focused operation from the ground up. We are seeking an exceptional and entrepreneurial Senior Construction Operations Manager to spearhead site operations, drive business development, and establish robust regulatory and HR frameworks. The Role: As the Senior Construction Operations Manager, you will be a pivotal figure in the success of this new strategic business unit. This multifaceted role demands a seasoned construction professional with a proven track record in managing complex projects, a strong business acumen for client acquisition, and a deep understanding of Singapore's regulatory landscape, particularly within the construction sector. You will be instrumental in setting the operational blueprint, securing initial projects, and building a high-performing team. Key Responsibilities: Strategic Leadership & Operational Blueprint: Collaborate with executive leadership to define the operational strategy and establish core procedures, workflows, and performance standards for the new construction business unit. Lead the identification, evaluation, and acquisition of essential operational resources, including initial equipment, technology platforms, and strategic supplier partnerships, aligned with the company's growth       strategy. Define and champion the company's approach to project execution excellence, stringent quality assurance protocols, and industry-leading safety management systems. Strategic Business Development & Key Client Acquisition: Spearhead business development initiatives by leveraging your extensive network and proactively identifying and engaging with key potential clients, including developers, architects, and other businesses. Contribute strategically to the development of compelling business development materials, presentations, and proposals that align with the company's brand and value proposition. Cultivate and maintain strong, long-term relationships with strategic clients, industry influencers, and potential partners to secure initial and future projects. Provide critical operational and costing expertise in the preparation of complex bids and tenders, ensuring competitive and profitable proposals. Establish robust tracking and reporting mechanisms for business development activities and  outcomes. Project Leadership & Elite Execution (Flagship Projects): Directly lead and oversee the execution of the company's initial, high-profile construction projects, ensuring delivery on time, within stringent budgetary parameters, and to high-quality standards that reflect the company's commitment to excellence. Build, mentor, and empower a team of capable project managers, supervisors, and skilled workers, fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Serve as the primary point of operational leadership, reporting directly to the designated General Manager or Business Unit Head on all strategic and tactical matters related to site and project performance. Chair comprehensive project briefings and strategic kick-off meetings, setting clear expectations and ensuring alignment across all stakeholders. Develop and present concise, data-driven periodic progress reports for internal executive review and external client communication. Strategic Construction Planning & Resource Optimization: Provide strategic direction in establishing advanced construction planning methodologies and resource allocation strategies for the new business unit. Establish sophisticated and efficient coordination protocols between internal project teams, pre-qualified subcontractors, key clients, and external consultants, ensuring seamless project delivery aligned with master schedules. Contribute to the development of standardized, company-level cost estimation and control processes, overseeing progress claim reviews, interim certifications, and payment authorizations. Strategically forecast and secure all necessary resources (highly skilled manpower, appropriate equipment fleets, suitable machinery, and quality materials) from project inception to completion, with a strong emphasis on optimized resource utilization and adherence to the company's financial guidelines. Enterprise-Level Risk Management & Regulatory Compliance: Proactively identify and mitigate potential risks across initial projects, translating lessons learned into robust company-wide risk management frameworks and best practices. Ensure unwavering compliance with all Singaporean regulatory requirements, including stringent quality, safety, and environmental standards, establishing a culture of compliance that aligns with the company's ethical and operational standards. Oversee the submission and clearance processes with relevant Singaporean authorities, establishing efficient and compliant protocols for all future projects. Implement and enforce adherence to approved specifications and relevant industry standards,       embedding a commitment to quality assurance throughout the organization.
