Lead the application and acquisition of all essential business licenses and permits, ensuring a smooth and timely setup process. Strategically plan and manage initial project staffing to optimize workforce deployment while adhering strictly to MOM regulations and quota limitations. Strategic Ad-hoc & Foundational Initiatives: Undertake strategic ad-hoc assignments directed by executive leadership, the Construction Manager, and the Business Unit Head, particularly those critical to establishing the new company's operational, business development, and regulatory frameworks in alignment with the company's overall strategy. Job Requirements: Bachelor's Degree in Construction Management, Engineering (Civil, Structural, or equivalent), or a related field from a reputable institution. Advanced degree preferred. Equivalent to 15+ years of progressive and impactful experience within the construction sector in Singapore, with significant and demonstrable success as a Senior Construction Site Manager or equivalent management role on complex, large-scale projects. Exceptional and comprehensive knowledge of best-in-class project management methodologies, tools, and technologies across all project lifecycle stages, with a strategic understanding of the unique challenges and opportunities of a new business unit within a growing enterprise. Proven and demonstrable track record of successfully soliciting new business, building strong client relationships, and contributing to significant business development wins within the Singaporean construction market. Deep and current understanding of Singapore's Ministry of Manpower (MOM) regulations concerning foreign worker quotas, work permit processes, and strategic HR management within the construction industry. Comprehensive knowledge of the necessary business certifications, licenses, and regulatory requirements for operating a construction firm in Singapore, including interactions with relevant government agencies. Mastery of MS Project and the MS Office suite, with proficiency in other relevant project management and collaboration tools. A strategic and passionate leader with exceptional people management skills, demonstrated ability to build and motivate high-performing teams in a start-up or new business unit environment, and a commitment to fostering a culture of excellence and innovation. A results-oriented and decisive individual with exceptional analytical, problem-solving, and negotiation skills, crucial for navigating the complexities of a new business and the Singaporean regulatory landscape. World-class written, communication, and interpersonal skills, essential for engaging with executive leadership, diverse stakeholders, building strategic client relationships, securing new business, and effectively interacting with regulatory bodies. Unwavering willingness to work flexibly, including extended hours as required during critical project phases, business development initiatives, and the strategic establishment of the new business unit. A proven ability to thrive under pressure, manage complex priorities, and deliver exceptional results within demanding timelines. A strategic, proactive, and highly entrepreneurial mindset with a strong personal drive to build and shape a significant new construction business unit within a growing enterprise, with a direct and measurable impact on its growth and success across operations, business development, and regulatory compliance. Working Conditions: A dynamic and challenging leadership role based in Singapore, requiring a commitment of 5 working days per week, with the understanding that significant flexibility and extended hours will be necessary, particularly during the      initial strategic phases of the business unit's establishment, critical flagship project milestones, and strategic business development activities. This is a rare and compelling opportunity to leave a lasting legacy by building a significant new construction business unit within a thriving medium-sized enterprise. If you possess the strategic vision, deep industry expertise, entrepreneurial spirit, and proven leadership capabilities to excel in this foundational role, we encourage you to submit your confidential application.

title: Internal Communications and Engagement Associate Manager
description: About us At Dyson, we believe in the relentless pursuit of improvement, raising the bar and achieving new heights.  We’ve spent decades pioneering inventions and continually improving. It’s about inventing the future. Our philosophy remains the same as it was when James Dyson invented the first cyclonic vacuum cleaner. We remain family owned. We plot our path, unshackled from conventional thinking. At Dyson, we constantly strive to find pioneeringways to solve everyday problems. About the role Based in Singapore, you will report to the Engagement and Events Senior Manager and be a key member of the team that shapes how we communicate and engage with our employees across the world. You will be joining us at a pivotal moment, as we elevate our internal communications strategy and deepen integration acrossthe organisation. You will help to implement Dyson’s internal communications and engagement agenda, inspiring employees through events and engagement opportunities, key product moments, experiences and corporate milestones and inspiring content. You’ll play an active part in bringing our strategy and employee experience to life. You are highly organised and skilled in project management, you handle shifting priorities and complex challenges with agility and a proactive mindset. You are confident in collaborating across functions, including with senior leaders. You are creative and develop creative solutions by thinking outside the box. You help set benchmarks for communication best practices—ensuring Dyson’s internal messaging is clear, engaging, consistent, and supports greater employee connection and involvement. Key responsibilities Support the execution of Dyson’s global employee engagement and events strategy, contributing ideas and best practices to enhance impact and reach. Plan, coordinate, and deliver memorable internal events and engagement opportunities—such as town halls, leadership forums, senior leadership conferences, product launches, CEO initiatives, and celebrations—across Dyson offices globally. Assist in the design and delivery of innovative engagement programmes that connect employees to Dyson’s mission, vision, and values. Work closely with Product Communications teams to showcase product launches internally - bringing technology stories to life, highlighting behind-the-scenes perspectives, and celebrating Dyson’s people and culture. Champion standards of excellence and best practices for planning, delivering, and evaluating employee engagement activities and events. Manage communications and engagement initiatives for the Malaysia campus, ensuring a cohesive and engaging local employee experience. Collaborate with stakeholders across functions, business units, and geographies to ensure that engagement activities align with business priorities and offer a consistent employee experience. Build and maintain strong relationships with third-party suppliers, agencies, and venue partners as necessary for event delivery. Develop and implement enterprise-wide campaigns and communications for key milestones and significant Dyson moments including product launches. About you Minimum 3 years of relevant experience in communications, with significant exposure to internal or employee communications within a global business environment. Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and build productive partnerships across various levels, including senior leaders. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a talent for creative storytelling and engaging diverse audiences. Demonstrated ability to work effectively under tight deadlines and in fast-paced, dynamic settings, consistently delivering outstanding results for the organisation. Creative and innovative mindset, tech savvy, always seeking opportunities for continuous improvement and commitment to delivering high-quality work. Proactive approach, with strong drive and resilience; able to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution when required. Energetic and engaging, with the ability to motivate and inspire colleagues within a matrixed environment. Confident, decisive, and able to see projects through to completion, while remaining adaptable, positive, and comfortable working with ambiguity and change. Willingness and ability to travel internationally as business needs require.

title: Quantity Surveyor – Interior Fit Out
description:
As
a Quantity Surveyor, you will play a key role in the commercial delivery of our
interior fit-out projects. Reporting to senior commercial management and

cost, procurement, and commercial aspects of projects from tender through to

cost control. Key responsibilities : 1.       Cost Estimation and
Budgeting ·

Experience as a Quantity Surveyor in interior fit-out or
construction projects ·      
Practical experience managing procurement, variations, and
subcontractor accounts ·      
Comfortable working in short-duration, fast-track project
environments ·      
Strong attention to detail, commercial awareness, and
communication skills ·      
Proficient in Excel. ·      
Able to speak and write in English and Mandarin

title: Principal SRE Engineer
description: Through proprietary software and AI, along with a focus on customer delight, Sleek makes the back-office easy for micro SMEs. We give Entrepreneurs time back to focus on what they love doing - growing their business and being with customers. With a surging number of Entrepreneurs globally, we are innovating in a highly lucrative space. We operate 3 business segments: Corporate Secretary : Automating the company incorporation, secretarial, filing, Nominee Director, mailroom and immigration processes via custom online robots and SleekSign. We are the market leaders in Singapore with ~5% market share of all new business incorporations Accounting & Bookkeeping : Redefining what it means to do Accounting, Bookkeeping, Tax and Payroll thanks to our proprietary SleekBooks ledger, AI tools and exceptional customer service FinTech payments : Overcoming a key challenge for Entrepreneurs by offering digital banking services to new businesses Sleek launched in 2017 and now has around 15,000 customers across our offices in Singapore, Hong Kong, Australia and the UK. We have around 500 staff with an intact startup mindset. We have recently raised Series B financing off the back of >70% compound annual growth in Revenue over the last 5 years. Sleek has been recognised by The Financial Times, The Straits Times, Forbes and LinkedIn as one of the fastest growing companies in Asia. Backed by world-class investors, we are on track to be one of the few cash flow positive, tech-enabled unicorns based out of Singapore. At Sleek, we are on a mission to streamline operations and elevate customer experience through intelligent automation and modern engineering. About the Role: Mission: At Sleek, we are on a mission to streamline operations and elevate customer experience through intelligent automation and modern engineering. We are seeking a Principal SRE Engineer who will be a key individual contributor responsible for architecting, building, and scaling Sleek’s next-generation infrastructure and AI-powered capabilities. As one of Sleek’s most senior technical contributors, you will partner closely with Product, Engineering, and AI teams to define our infrastructure strategy, design resilient cloud architectures, and ensure our platforms remain secure, scalable, and high-performing. You will play a central role in integrating AI systems into production environments, enabling efficient delivery, observability, and reliability across Sleek’s products and internal operations. You will ensure: High-quality, secure, and scalable infrastructure capable of supporting modern applications and advanced AI workloads Robust automation across CI/CD, infrastructure provisioning, and operations to increase reliability and reduce manual overhead Thoughtful and pragmatic integration of AI into operational workflows to improve efficiency, detect anomalies, and accelerate delivery Reliable systems engineering practices , including monitoring, incident response, performance tuning, and capacity planning Strong DevOps standards, including reproducibility, testing, documentation, and operational excellence Clear technical communication and cross-team alignment to enable predictable delivery and collaborative problem-solving Mentorship and technical leadership that elevates platform engineering, DevOps maturity, and overall engineering quality across the organisation Outcomes: Strengthen Reliability, Scalability, and Infrastructure Health Conduct a full review of Sleek’s cloud infrastructure and propose a roadmap for reliability and scalability improvements Lead upgrades or redesigns of core platform components such as networking, containers, orchestration, or databases Improve incident response processes, SLIs, SLOs, and on-call readiness. Introduce Platform Enhancements to Support AI Workloads Ensure platform and infrastructure are capable of supporting AI-powered features Build or refine pipelines for model hosting, embeddings, vector search, or related AI services if required Implement monitoring and guardrails for AI service performance, cost, and stability Increase Engineering Velocity Through Automation Enhance CI/CD pipelines for speed, safety, and reliability Introduce infrastructure automation, testing automation, and deployment tooling to reduce manual steps Champion modern DevOps and AI-assisted tooling to improve engineering productivity. Improve Observability and Operational Excellence Strengthen logging, monitoring, tracing, and alerting across services Reduce noisy alerts and improve the signal-to-noise ratio for incidents Implement readiness checks, runbooks, and automated recovery paths for critical services Security and Compliance Improvements Ensure secure configuration, secrets management, access control, and identity management Implement automated security scanning, dependency monitoring, and hardened pipeline practices Prepare platform-level requirements needed for reliable and secure AI usage Mentor and Support Engineering Teams Coach engineers on cloud best practices, reliability, and operational readiness Lead architecture reviews, workshops, and platform knowledge-sharing session Build tooling, templates, and patterns that elevate team productivity To do this you will have: 8+ years of progressive experience in Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) 5+ years of strong, hands-on experience across multi-cloud environments such as AWS, GCP, Azure including expertise in networking, compute, storage, security, and cost optimization. 5+ years of deep expertise in containerization and orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes, EKS, ECS) 8+ years of extensive experience with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) (e.g., Terraform, Pulumi, CloudFormation). System Reliability: Proven ability to design, build, and operate highly reliable, scalable production systems utilizing advanced Zero-Downtime Deployment Patterns (e.g., Blue/Green, Canary, progressive delivery). Modern Delivery & Tooling: Expertise in modernizing deployments via GitOps practices (e.g., ArgoCD, Flux) and building Self-Service Developer Platforms that enable engineering efficiency (e.g., environment automation, internal tooling). Networking & Edge Routing: Experience implementing and managing Multi-Cloud API Gateways and Edge Routing solutions (e.g., Kong, Traefik, Cloudflare, multi-cluster ingress). Security & Hardening: Strong background in platform security, including secrets management, Identity and Access Control (IAM), and Runtime/Security Hardening with tools like Falco/eBPF and WAFs. Observability: Solid understanding and practical experience with modern observability stacks (e.g., Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, OpenSearch, ELK, CloudWatch). AI/ML Infrastructure: Experience supporting or deploying AI/ML workloads (e.g., model inference, vector databases, GPU workloads), or strong familiarity with the infrastructure requirements for these systems. Mentorship & Communication: Excellent communication and collaboration skills with a proven ability to describe complex infrastructure decisions clearly and a background in mentoring engineers and driving improvements in engineering practices. Development Expertise: Familiarity with modern programming languages like Node.js, NestJS, and Python is highly desirable for extending DevOps capabilities or integrating tooling. Behavioural fit is also important at Sleek, and we will be looking for candidates that have a proven track record of embodying the below attributes in their recent roles: Ownership: This shows reliability and helps build trust within the team. We move fast and need to know that everyone will see things through to completion and proactively help to get things back on track when challenges arise. Accountability is really important to us. Humility: There is so much we don’t know. Humility allows for open-mindedness to feedback and a willingness to learn from others. It paves the way for collaboration and creates a positive work environment. It is a key ingredient of self awareness and emotional intelligence. Structured Thinking : Our business is complex with many layers (many services, many countries, many cultures). Regardless of whether you’re more analytical or creative in nature, being able to show sound judgement is important to us. It ensures solutions are pragmatic and balance the needs of the organisation, team and customers. Attention to detail : You’ll be managing multiple, complex workstreams from numerous stakeholders. It will be important for you to keep track of everything and notice when information is missing or inconsistent. Excellent listener and clear communicator : We have a variety of nationalities and for many people, English isn’t their first language. For you to excel, you’ll need to be present in your calls and make sure you properly receive and thoughtfully send messages to others in the business. About the interview process The successful candidate will participate in the below interview stages (note that the order might be different to what you read below). We anticipate the process to last no more than 3 weeks from start to finish. Whether the interviews are held over video call or in person will depend on your location and the role. Screening call A ~30 minute chat with our Talent Acquisition team to learn more about you, your background, and what you’re looking for. Take Home Assessment A 2-3 hour take-home assessment that would assess your SRE Skills. Technical Interview A ~90 minute technical interview with Delivery Lead and/or Head of Engineering Final interview A closing conversation with our CTO, giving you the opportunity to ask any final questions and hear more about our vision and priorities. Offer + references We’ll make a non-binding offer verbally or by email, followed by a couple of short phone or video calls with references that you provide. Requirement for background screening Please be aware that Sleek is a regulated entity and as such is required to perform different levels of background checks on staff depending on their role. This may include using external vendors to verify the below: - Your education - Any criminal history - Any political exposure - Any bankruptcy or adverse credit history We will ask for your consent before conducting these checks. Depending on your role at Sleek, an adverse result on one of these checks may prohibit you from passing probation. By submitting a job application, you confirm that you have read and agree to our Data Privacy Statement for Candidates, found at sleek.com.

title: Product Growth Analyst
description: We’re looking for growth leaders for products used by billions of people and businesses all over the world across our apps including Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp and Oculus. This is a leadership opportunity to drive impact end-to-end through the product development life cycle. The product growth analyst role is embedded within product teams and requires using a mix of skills including analysis, product ideation, and cross-functional collaboration. Success in the role is tied directly to product goals and the team rewards results-based performance. The Growth practice was started right here at Meta, putting you at the forefront of the industry with a great opportunity to learn about growth from experts. It is a lean and highly regarded team with an emphasis on regular sharing of successful strategies, tactics and insights across products. Our team values are to own the outcome, focus on driving sustainable impact, and put team success first. There is great opportunity to be a leader and influence the direction of high-visibility and high-impact initiatives, and to have your work come to life in some of the most widely used apps in the world. Responsibilities Lead growth strategy across a large product area and drive cross-team alignment Drive long term growth of Meta Inc products through a combination of data analysis, product ideation, and experimentation to optimize product experiences Understand trends in user behavior and product usage to influence growth strategy Identify opportunities to drive growth and prioritize them to maximize long-term growth Execute on projects by working closely with engineering and other members of product teams to land impact on product goals. Lead experimentation from designing tests to analyzing results that lead to launch decisions Minimum qualifications Bachelor's degree in a directly related field, or equivalent practical experience 9+ years experience working in an analytical or product role 9+ years of work experience in analytics, data querying languages such as SQL 9+ years of experience solving analytical problems using quantitative approaches, understanding ecosystems, user behaviors & long-term product trends, and leading data-driven projects from definition to execution, including defining metrics, experiment design, and communicating actionable insights Proven to effectively collaborate across variety of technical and non-technical functions to drive towards common product goals Experience working with and influencing multi disciplinary product teams consisting but not limited to software engineers, designers, product managers and data scientists Proficiency in quantitative analysis geared towards drawing actionable insights from complex datasets Experience performing exploratory analysis with minimal direction to answer ambiguous open ended questions Experimentation experience to design multivariate tests, synthesize test results and build frameworks to make data-informed launch decisions Product ideation skills to apply growth tactics broadly to different product areas and think through detailed elements of a product experience Experience working on a product or service used by a wide-range of communities Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics.

and other specialty coffee professionals - with a relentless dedication to building a sustainable coffee program. title: Engineering Change Engineer
description: JOB SUMMARY We are seeking a detail-oriented and collaborative professional to support our manufacturing operations by creating and maintaining assembly instructions and critical documentation. In this role, you will play a key part in New Product Introduction (NPI) activities, Engineering Change Orders (ECOs), and Bill of Materials (BOM) management. You’ll work closely with engineering, production, materials, and purchasing teams to ensure accurate documentation, smooth process flow, and on-time execution. ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES •	Create, maintain, and control manufacturing documentation and master files in compliance with ISO standards. •	Prepare, implement, and coordinate Engineering Change Orders (ECOs) to support manufacturing assembly instructions. •	Review and interpret customer documentation, drawings, and blueprints to define and improve manufacturing process flows. •	Partner with engineering and process teams to develop efficient and effective production workflows. •	Maintain accurate Bills of Materials (BOMs), routings, and production status within the SAP system. •	Support the New Product Introduction (NPI) process, including material setup and documentation verification. •	Order stencils as needed in accordance with established procedures. •	Verify ECO documentation packages for completeness, accuracy, and proper approvals. •	Communicate ECO impacts across engineering, materials, and production teams; interact with customers or suppliers when required. •	Ensure requote and material pricing data in SAP is accurate and up to date. •	Participate in special projects, including SAP and system improvement initiatives. •	Assist with excess and obsolete inventory transactions. •	Maintain documentation supporting process metrics and performance tracking. •	Update and manage documentation control programs as required. •	Train and mentor junior team members on ECO processing and execution. •	Support weekly and monthly departmental performance reporting as needed. •	Perform additional duties as assigned; overtime may occasionally be required. QUALIFICATIONS – KNOWLEDGE & SKILLS •	Strong communication skills with the ability to present information clearly to cross-functional teams, customers, and leadership. •	Analytical mindset with the ability to identify problems, evaluate data, and implement solutions. •	Advanced proficiency with Windows-based systems and business software, including ERP tools such as SAP. •	Experience using word processing, spreadsheets, data management, and presentation tools. •	Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, documentation, and visual aids. •	Solid mathematical skills, including percentages, ratios, and basic data analysis. •	Ability to follow standardized processes while applying sound judgment and attention to detail. PREFERRED KNOWLEDGE •	Familiarity with GMP and ISO standards. •	Understanding of product and process design, materials, standardization, and applied statistics. •	Exposure to project management and cost analysis. •	Experience with ERP systems, quality systems, and documentation control platforms. EDUCATION & EXPERIENCE •	Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering required. •	3–5 years of experience in a manufacturing environment; medical device industry experience is a plus. •	Advanced technical training in relevant areas preferred. •	Typically requires five (5) years of related professional experience.

title: Architecture Designer
description: Job Description: Oversees the architectural elements of a project, including its design and feasibility. Meeting, work closely with clients to understand the requirements of projects. Develop project plans and budgets for projects including architectural drawings, specifications, estimates, cost reports, schedule, material documents, and specification lists for client approval. Visiting the site regularly to ensure construction adheres to state regulations. Ensuring the project remains on time and within specifications. Creating progress reports, time schedules, and revised budgets. Coordinate with Project Manager on daily updates and weekly reports on the work progress and issues on site, including necessary checklist and documentations. Coordinate, preparation of tender, evaluation, recommendation, and award processes. Maintain proper records of all site files on project correspondences, documents and drawings for easy retrieval and reference. Prepare and update the list of outstanding / non-compliance works for submission to the Architect / Management for discussion during site meetings on regular basis. Ensure all work is carried out to the requirements of the client, with the design team working to the correct specifications and delivering a high quality and timely product to the client. Adaptable and problem solving, managing internal and external stakeholders, manage risks and issues, and monitor progress to ensure that the project stays on track. Suggest innovative solutions to improve sustainability, efficiency, or aesthetics. Conducting a final inspection after the project is complete. Jobs Requirements: Min master’s degree in architecture, design, or similar. 3-5 Years Experience Preferred. Local Project with some overseas experience preferred. State architecture certification. Portfolio of completed projects (hospitality & commercial work experience preferred) Advanced knowledge of drafting software including AutoCAD and SketchUp. Excellent communication and reporting skills. Ability to translate concepts into functional construction designs. Advanced project-management skills. Strong problem-solving and analytical skills. Must be able to speak English, Mandarin, Cantonese. Ability to coordinate with multiple stakeholders daily. Must be able to travel overseas for work occasionally.

These toolkits are essential for validating the functionality and performance of the ATM support systems, which support essential Air Traffic Control (ATC) operations such as flight plan processing and NOTAM dissemination. Build intuitive and responsive user interfaces for test configuration, execution, and reporting using modern web technologies. Develop backend services and APIs to support automated testing and data simulation. Integrate with third-party services, APIs, and gateways as required Implement test automation frameworks and CI/CD pipelines to streamline testing and deployment processes. Monitor application performance, troubleshoot issues, and implement optimisation strategies Collaborate with system engineers and testers to define test requirements and ensure comprehensive coverage of operational scenarios. Ensure the toolkits are scalable, maintainable, and aligned with software engineering best practices. Maintain technical documentation including system design and review, user guides, test plans, and system architecture. Ensure compliance with aviation standards (e.g., ICAO, Eurocae) and cybersecurity requirements in the development of testing tools. Provide technical support during system integration, testing, and commissioning phases. Stay current with emerging technologies and recommend improvements to development processes. What are we looking for? Minimum 2 years of experiences in full stack software development, preferably in mission-critical or safety-critical systems. Proficient in frontend technologies such as HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript/TypeScript, and frameworks like React, Angular, or Vue.js. Strong backend development skills using Python, Java, Node.js, or similar languages. Experienced with RESTful APIs, microservices architecture, and database systems (SQL/NoSQL). Experienced with system simulation, data modelling, and test data generation. Familiar with modern software practices including version control, automated testing, CI/CD pipelines, code reviews, and agile methodologies Experience with Cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure) and containerization (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) is a plus. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail. Good communication and documentation skills. Organised and self-motivated, able to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. May be required to travel for system integration or stakeholder engagements. What do we offer? Fun working environment Employee Wellness Program To work in Singapore Government Agencies projects We provide structured development framework and growth opportunities. (We are a “SHRI 2025 Gold winner” in “Learning & Development; Coaching & Mentoring”) Why you'll love working with us? If you are looking for opportunities to collaborate with leading industry experts and be surrounded by highly motivated and talented peers, we welcome you to join us. We provide all employees with equal opportunities to grow and develop with us. We believe your success is our success. Does it sound like something you are interested in exploring further? Please be in touch with our team for an initial chat at chi@activate.sg Activate Interactive Singapore is an equal opportunity employer. Employment decisions will be based on merit, qualifications and abilities. title: Engineering Manager
description: About Us: At Booking.com, data drives our decisions. Technology is at our core. And innovation is everywhere. But our company is more than datasets, lines of code or A/B tests. We’re the thrill of the first night in a new place. The excitement of the next morning. The friends you encounter. The journeys you take. The sights you see. And the memories you make. Through our products, partners and people, we make it easier for everyone to experience the world. Role Description: Booking.com for Business is a travel product designed for the needs of small and medium size enterprises. It lets companies book and manage business trips — stays, flights, and car rental — in one place. We provide additional business oriented functionality on top of core Booking.com benefit offering – loyalty programmes, 24/7 customer support, etc. The Booking.com for Business foundations team is responsible for extending Booking.com capabilities for the business context (i.e. identity management, payments, loyalty programmes) and developing bespoke solutions that are relevant for that space (i.e. incentivisation, invoice collection). Located within an “incubator” at Booking, the successful candidate will be responsible for building the culture and team, shaping the way that we work together and with our partners. Key specifics of our team are: Large amount of R&D due to the wide scope that our team is covering. Large amount of integrations with a diverse array of services and components within and outside of Booking.com ; Focus on rapid experimentation and general agility in order to find and benefit from available opportunities; Please note: some travel to New Zealand, Europe and the US is required throughout the year. Key Job Responsibilities and Duties: Manage of 1 or more teams of software engineers - between 5 and 10 people in total Create a culture of ownership and technical excellence, leading by examples. Empower engineers to develop their careers by providing ad-hoc applicable coaching Help build and enrich an inclusive work environment comprised of people from diverse backgrounds Contribute to projects by guiding architectural discussions, providing technical coaching to software engineers and occasionally getting hands-on with coding Champion engineering and operational excellence, establishing metrics and processes to both supervise health and allow continuous improvement Provide technical guidance to both your team members and your project peers. Drive team efforts to establish bottom-up projects and roadmaps supporting our vision Qualifications & Skills: 3 years of experience managing a team of engineers. 5 years of experience in software development with proficiency in one of the following languages: Java, Objective-C, Swift, JavaScript, or Perl paired with a familiarity with SQL and NoSQL Databases. Experience developing large-scale distributed systems. Experience with DevOps best practices such as reliability fundamentals, CI/CD, Test Engineering, and Observability. Familiarity with Agile best practices for the full software development life cycle such as coding standards, code reviews, and version control in Git. Experience working with cloud technologies like AWS, GoogleCloud, Docker, or Kubernetes. Benefits & Perks - Global Impact, Personal Relevance: Booking.com’s Total Rewards Philosophy is not only about compensation but also about benefits. We offer a competitive compensation and benefits package , as well unique-to-Booking.com benefits which include: Annual paid time off and generous paid leave scheme including: parent, grandparent, bereavement, and care leave Industry leading product discounts - up to 1400 per year - for yourself, including automatic Genius Level 3 status and Booking.com wallet credit Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI) at Booking.com: Diversity, Equity & Inclusion have been a core part of our company culture since day one. This ongoing journey starts with our very own employees, who represent over 140 nationalities and a wide range of ethnic and social backgrounds, genders and sexual orientations. Take it from our Chief People Officer, Paulo Pisano: “At Booking.com, the diversity of our people doesn’t just build an outstanding workplace, it also creates a better and more inclusive travel experience for everyone. Inclusion is at the heart of everything we do. It’s a place where you can make your mark and have a real impact in travel and tech.” We ensure that colleagues with disabilities are provided the adjustments and tools they need to participate in the job application and interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Application Process: Let’s go places together: How we Hire This role does not come with relocation assistance. Booking.com is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We strive to move well beyond traditional equal opportunity and work to create an environment that allows everyone to thrive.

title: Chief Technology Officer (CIM)
description:
About the Role: As our Chief Technology Officer, you will define and lead the technological
vision and strategy for our next-generation Computer-Integrated Manufacturing
(CIM) platform. You will architect AI-driven solutions that power digital
transformation and Industry 4.0 for global clients, with a strategic focus on
the semiconductor industry. This role is critical in transitioning our product
suite into intelligent, scalable, and market-leading manufacturing systems. Key Responsibilities: Establish and execute the overall technology roadmap, ensuring alignment with business goals and market evolution in industrial AI and smart manufacturing. Lead and scale global engineering teams across the full software development lifecycle (SDLC) — from concept and design to deployment, iteration, and support. Drive architectural decisions and hands-on technical direction using modern stacks including C#, Java, Vue.js, SQL, and emerging AI/ML frameworks. Foster innovation by integrating AI/ML capabilities into core CIM products to enhance predictive analytics, automation, and operational intelligence. Act as the key technical voice for clients and partners, building trust through deep domain expertise and solution-oriented leadership. For more details, please view https://www.ntu.edu.sg/erian You will be part of a dynamic research team working on topics relevant to the Sustainable Built Environment (SBE). The SBE team focuses on building energy modelling and simulation, decarbonization strategies for the built environment and R&D of green building technologies such as HVAC systems, thermal energy storage, data-driven building control systems and other novel solutions for the urban built environment sector. This position is to recruit a member of the research team to work on a project on sustainable indoor farming. Key Responsibilities : You will need to actively support SBE projects at ERI@N in terms of project R&D, technology evaluation, project coordination, and stakeholder interfacing. The role will include the following: Perform as subject matter expert for ongoing projects e.g. in energy modelling and/or embodied carbon assessment; coordination with project partners, industry and other external stakeholders. Provide inputs for conceptualization of research proposals related to sustainable urban solutions, green buildings, low-carbon district level solutions and agritech applications. Lead in preparation and submission of new research proposals. Conduct research, analysis and assessment on embodied carbon for buildings, urban farming and other applications. Lead technical analysis and research on green building technologies, decarbonization strategies for new and existing buildings and smart building systems incorporating AI/ML/energy analytics. Engage stakeholders at management and working levels internally as well as with external funding agencies, collaborators and industry partners, including delivering presentations and guiding discussions or workshops as necessary. Lead in authoring of technical publications for completed or ongoing research projects. You will be an integral part of the SBE team and will need to participate in project discussions especially related to project implementation details. Job Requirements PhD degree in engineering (e.g. Mechanical, Clean/Renewable Energy or Building related) or sustainable buildings design. Experience in sustainable design/analysis for the built environment, Green Mark or LEED projects, M&E construction and development (min 2yrs preferred). Proficient in MS Office applications, BIM software and energy modelling software. Should be a very hands-on person, willing to take on non-routine tasks. Possess good communication (written and verbal) skills. Able to manage multiple stakeholders and work under tight timelines. Positive attitude, ability to work independently as well as in a team. Familiarity with green building technology, HVAC system study and optimization, sustainable building design, building energy modelling, CFD modelling, BIM and embodied carbon/lifecycle assessment for built environment applications is preferred. Knowledge in EN 15978 or similar standards for carbon assessment or lifecycle analysis. Prior experience in sustainability standards and green building accreditations would be advantageous. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Design Engineer (Structural)
description: (A)   Essential Job Functions: The Design Engineer (Structural) is responsible, but not limited to the following: Structural Design & Analysis: Develop structural designs for buildings, bridges, tunnels, and other infrastructure projects. Perform load calculations, stress analysis, and material selection to ensure structural integrity. Use software such as STAAD.Pro, ETABS, SAP2000, Tekla, AutoCAD, Revit, or ANSYS for design and analysis. Ensure all designs comply with local and international building codes (e.g., Eurocodes, ACI, AISC, BS, IS codes). Drafting & Documentation: Prepare technical drawings, blueprints, and specifications for construction teams. Work closely with CAD technicians and BIM modelers to create detailed 2D and 3D structural models. Maintain detailed documentation, including design reports, calculations, and technical notes. Project Coordination: Collaborate with architects, civil engineers, geotechnical engineers, and contractors to ensure design feasibility. Participate in design reviews, risk assessments, and feasibility studies. Assist in resolving design-related issues during construction and provide technical support on-site. Quality & Safety Compliance: Ensure that structural designs meet safety, durability, and sustainability standards. Conduct structural inspections and provide recommendations for modifications if necessary. Stay updated with industry trends, materials, and construction techniques. (B)  Minimum Qualifications/ Job Experience: Minimum Degree holder in Civil/Structural Engineering or Geotechnical Engineering or its equivalent. At least 5 years of experience in the Construction industry.

title: T&C Engineer - Electrification
description: T&C Engineer - Electrification Projects Navsar Global is a premier specialist services firm, connecting top-tier talent with leading companies in critical infrastructure sectors such as power generation, data centres, and oil & gas. We are currently seeking a highly skilled and dedicated T&C Engineer to join our client's dynamic team, focusing on impactful Electrification Projects. The Role: As a T&C Engineer, you will be a pivotal member of the project team, coordinating daily testing and commissioning workflows on-site. You will take full ownership of all testing activities for complex Electrification Projects, ensuring meticulous adherence to defined standards, customer requirements, economic objectives, quality benchmarks, schedule timelines, and stringent Environmental, Health, and Safety (EHS) protocols. Key Responsibilities: Collaborate effectively with internal teams, various departments, clients, and system-wide contractors and subcontractors to arrange and execute site activities seamlessly. Actively participate in critical meetings such as Works Train Notice Meetings, Operator / LTA Track work Access meetings, and LTA manpower planning meetings (e.g., for C853e and HGBD works). Prepare and submit necessary documentation, including SDAR (Site Daily Activity Report) and TAR (Track Access Request), to secure track access and ensure testing proceeds on schedule. Lead and manage commissioning activities across the entire lifecycle, from Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT), Pre-Site Acceptance Testing (PRE-SAT), Site Acceptance Testing (SAT), System Integration Testing (SIT), through to final handover to the client and successful service commencement. Support the Project Manager in the preparation of essential project documentation, including Method Statements, Risk Assessments, detailed step-by-step work plans, and other required paperwork. Develop and implement robust systems and platforms for monitoring project progress on a weekly basis, providing timely feedback to the project planner as assigned. Supervise and provide hands-on support to installation teams for SKD RT A&A (Installation activities) at the site, fostering a self-motivated and efficient working environment. Assist the Manager in preparing comprehensive work schedule programs. Undertake ad-hoc duties assigned by the Manager that are critical to the project's success. Demonstrate a strong commitment to teamwork, ensuring smooth project completion through collaborative effort. Be willing to work extended hours, including night shifts (engineering hours) and public holidays, as project demands necessitate. Exhibit strong decision-making capabilities in fast-paced project environments. Provide support to other project teams as and when required to achieve overall project objectives. Qualifications: Education & Training: High School Diploma or equivalent. Diploma in Electrical & Electronic or Mechanical Engineering. Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) in Electrical/Electronic Engineering is highly preferred. Experience: Minimum of 3 years of hands-on working experience in Electrification projects. Proven track record of taking responsibility and ownership of assigned tasks and outcomes. Demonstrated experience in using requirements traceability. Knowledge of Siemens electrification solutions is a significant advantage. Experience in reviewing and updating drawings, specifications, Factory Acceptance Test (FAT) plans, FAT reports, and Site Acceptance Test (SAT) reports. Hands-on experience with high voltage and secondary voltage test equipment in T&C works. Strong understanding of safety significance in the operation and maintenance of High, Medium, and Low Voltage systems, as well as Traction Power Systems in a railway environment, with safety as the highest priority. Proven ability to take complete responsibility for Site Acceptance Testing (SAT/ITC/TTR). Hands-on experience or knowledge of PLC systems (e.g., Siemens, Mitsubishi). Knowledge in ETTS/TSSS systems. Technical Competencies: Basic knowledge of competitor systems in the market. Basic understanding of Functional Safety (IEC 61508) fundamentals and safety functions within core components. Proficiency in Global Business English, both speaking and writing, to facilitate clear international communication. Strong communication skills, including active listening, clarifying, interpreting, and adapting to various situations and personalities. Basic knowledge and application of virtual communication and collaboration tools. Advanced understanding of internal and external laws and regulations to ensure ethically proper business acumen; ability to advise colleagues on compliance-related issues. Basic knowledge of contract formats, practices, and policies, understanding when and whom to involve for assistance. Basic understanding and application of information security practices to protect data and systems. Basic knowledge of regional labor laws and regulations, applying them to daily work and knowing escalation procedures. Basic knowledge of the relevant product, system, and service portfolio within the area of responsibility, including ability to suggest process improvements and solve simple technical problems. Basic technical product and system knowledge relevant to the organization, understanding technical differences and utilizing documentation effectively.

title: Principal Manager, Communications and Digital Marketing
description: As Communications team lead, you will shape and drive Lifelong Learning Singapore (LLSG) strategic narrative to position the organisation as a national catalyst for lifelong learning. You will lead the development of integrated marketing and communications strategies and deepen engagement across individuals, enterprises, and partners. This is a leadership role that demands both strategic foresight and operational excellence. You will steward LLSG’s brand, voice and outreach efforts across digital, media, and stakeholder channels, ensuring that LLSG’s programmes, advisory services, and initiatives are not only visible but valued. Your primary responsibilities include the following: · Craft and evolve LLSG’s strategic messaging to reinforce its role as a trusted enabler of lifelong learning and skills development in Singapore. · Lead the design and delivery of integrated, multi-channel marketing and outreach strategies that align with organisational goals and audience needs. · Drive brand visibility and develop messaging frameworks, ensuring consistent narratives across digital, social, EDMs, media, and events. · Drive audience-targeted campaigns (individuals, enterprises, partners) using Search Engine Optimisation (SEO) / Search Engine Marketing (SEM), social media, and paid/organic outreach. · Establish KPIs and measurement frameworks to track and analyse campaign performance, using insights to improve engagement, visibility, and conversions. · Partner with internal teams and external agencies to amplify LLSG’s programmes and ensure effective execution. · Lead, coach, and develop a team, ensuring accountability and high-quality delivery across branding, digital, and content. · Monitor emerging trends in communications, digital engagement, and public sentiment to proactively shape LLSG’s outreach strategies and future-proof its brand positioning. · Experiment with new formats (e.g., podcasts, webinars, interactive content, community activations) to deepen engagement and expand reach across diverse audience segments. · Strengthen internal communications to build organisational culture, align teams around strategic priorities, and foster employee advocacy. · Any other responsibilities deemed important to support the overall efforts of LLSG. Requirements · Has strong skills/competencies in communications, marketing and business. This should be demonstrated through candidates’ portfolio. · At least 8–10 years of relevant work experience in strategic communications, marketing, or brand management, ideally in public sector or complex stakeholder environments. · Strong command of spoken and written English, with proven ability to craft persuasive messages for diverse audiences. · Experience in digital marketing, content strategy, branding, and campaign analytics. · Data-driven, with strong analytical and problem-solving skills to evaluate campaign effectiveness. · Strategic thinker able to balance big-picture goals with hands-on execution. · Collaborative, adaptable, and resilient to change, with strong stakeholder management skills. · Proven leadership skills, able to inspire, guide, and grow a communications team. Candidates who do not have the required qualification or relevant experience are welcome to apply. Successful candidates will be offered a 2-year contract in the first instance and may be considered for an extension or be placed on a permanent tenure. Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Industrial Hygienist
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Industrial Hygienist to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on preventing occupational illness and disease by anticipating, recognizing, evaluating, and controlling environmental factors arising in the workplace which may cause sickness, impaired health and well-being, or significant discomfort to the workforce. In this job you will collaborate and work closely with the safety team, stakeholders from other functions and departments including the Corporate Safety Team in Finland. You will provide your expertise to our Refinery on matters of industrial hygiene (IH) and occupational health & safety (OH&S). In our Safety team, you will report to the Safety Manager. As the technical authority and expert in IH and OH&S, you manage and lead the refinery in IH and OH&S function to reduce operational risk exposure to the workforce through governance of OEMS principles, regulatory compliance and requirements. Together with you and your expertise, we can make an impact today and keep our promises for tomorrow. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Risk Identification, Evaluation and Control Proactively anticipate and recognize chemical hazards, physical hazards, biological hazards, and ergonomic hazards across refining areas Design, execute, and manage comprehensive Industrial Hygiene (IH) monitoring programs . Conduct qualitative and quantitative exposure assessments for airborne contaminants, chemical exposure, noise & physical agents and ergonomic risk Interpret monitoring results against local (MOM/WSH) and internationally recognized (ACGIH/OSHA) standards Conduct routine Occupational Hygiene site inspections and audits, focusing on exposure risks, ventilation systems (LEVs), and chemical storage/handling procedures Control Measures and Management Systems Recommend, implement effective industrial hygiene engineering as key control strategies and evaluate its effectiveness Oversight chemical inventory, Safety Data Sheet (SDS) management, and risk assessment process for hazardous substances Develop and enforce stringent chemical handling procedures Administer and manage IH Management system and programs such as Ventilation Systems, Hazard Communications, PPE standards, Ergonomic standards, heat stress program, medical examination & surveillance program, Hearing Conservation Program (HCP), including noise mapping, audiometric testing, noise control implementation, process and office ergonomic program Conduct industrial hygiene risk assessments for process work area, offices, laboratory, recommending necessary adjustments and controls measures Strategizing Regulatory Compliance (PEL, STEL, TWA, C) and Translating to Operable Compliance Translate PEL, STEL, OEL requirement into practical, executable operational compliance Deep working knowledge of Singapore and international acts, regulations, requirements related to industrial hygiene topics Ensure strict adherence to permissible/occupational exposure limit and relevant regulations and Codes of Practice relating to occupational health and safety Prepare detailed industrial hygiene reports, regulatory submissions and internal performance metrics. Maintain accurate records of all monitoring data, calibration records, and exposure assessments Provide technical support and expertise during health-related emergency response situations (e.g chemical spills) Training and Collaboration Develop and deliver targeted training programs on industrial hygiene topics, including chemical management and hazard communication, PPE use, respiratory protection, noise conservations, heat stress, ergonomics Collaborate closely with cross functional teams to integrate industrial hygiene into daily operations, projects and Process Safety Management (PSM). Participate as safety subject matter expert in incident investigation team, facilitate Safety department owned investigations or as directed Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Technical expert in industrial hygiene, occupational health role and operationalized industrial hygiene into day to day operations Minimum 10 years experience as a practicing industrial hygienist in a refinery, petrochemical or chemical manufacturing industry; track record in industrial hygiene monitoring (chemical, physical and biological to personal and area exposure)  IH engineering control, IH Management System Recognized Bachelor Degree in Safety or similar, master degree in industrial hygiene highly desired Professionally certified industrial hygienist (CIH) or registered industrial hygienist (RIH) or equivalent. Candidates without IH certification but demonstrate mastery and proven track record in IH will be considered Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application (with a salary request) We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com

title: Senior Data Scientist
description: Who We Are Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact. To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive. We Are BCG X We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions. What You'll Do Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions. As a Data Scientist, you'll be part of our rapidly growing team. You'll have the chance to apply data science methods and analytics to real-world business situations across a variety of industries to drive significant business impact. You'll have the chance to partner with clients in a variety of BCG regions and industries, and on key topics like climate change, enabling them to design, build, and deploy new and innovative solutions. Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X. Successful candidates are intellectually curious builders who are biased toward action, scrappy, and communicative. We are looking for talented individuals with a passion for data science, statistics, operations research and transforming organizations into AI led innovative companies. Successful candidates possess the following: Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner Love building things and are comfortable working with modern development tools and writing code collaboratively (bonus points if you have a software development or DevOps experience) Significant experience applying advanced analytics to a variety of business situations and a proven ability to synthesize complex data Deep understanding of modern machine learning techniques and their mathematical underpinnings, and can translate this into business implications for our clients Have strong project management skills What You'll Bring Master’s Degree or PhD with significant relevant experience providing advanced analytics solutions. The degree should be in computer science, applied mathematics, statistics, machine learning, or a related data centric field. Demonstrated deep technical and data science expertise, acute strategic and analytical skills, ability to lead and persuade, drive and energy, and desire to work in a project-based environment on strategic issues. Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner Strong record of professional accomplishment and leadership. Fluency in Python Additional info Ability to travel based on client and business needs.

title: Site Engineer
description: Job Description & Requirements We are seeking a dedicated Site Engineer to oversee the design, planning, and execution of structural and civil engineering projects. The role requires strong project management skills, technical expertise in structural analysis, and the ability to manage resources effectively to ensure safe and timely project delivery. Key Responsibilities Develop and manage project plans, schedules, and budgets using MS Project. Conduct structural analysis and design using STAAD Pro, Tekla Structures, and AutoCAD. title: Student Care Programme Educator (Eunos)
description: About MIJ Hub: My Inspiring Journey Hub (MIJ Hub) is a leading and rapidly growing private school and non-profit organisation (NPO) dedicated to transforming the lives of individuals aged 4 to 40 with diverse learning needs. Through our comprehensive educational programs, vocational training, and community integration initiatives, we have been creating positive change locally for over 13 years. Our mission is to be the ultimate one-stop centre for the special needs community in the region. At MIJ Hub, we are pioneers in innovation. By managing multiple for-profit ventures, including a food and beverage (F&B) arm, we generate sustainable income streams that allow us to provide subsidies, ensuring parents and students have access to affordable, high-quality education. Join MIJ Hub and become part of a vibrant, dynamic team committed to driving financial sustainability, fostering inclusivity, and expanding our reach to make a real difference in the community. Role Summary: The Special Needs Educator plays a critical role in delivering customized educational programs that cater to the unique learning needs of students with special needs. This educator will design, implement, and evaluate individualized education plans (IEPs) for students, ensuring holistic development across academic, social, and emotional domains. By fostering a nurturing and supportive learning environment, the educator will collaborate closely with multidisciplinary teams and engage with parents to drive positive student outcomes. Key Responsibilities: 1. Individualized Education Plan (IEP) Development • Collaborate with specialists (therapists, counselors, etc.) and families to design and implement personalized IEPs for each student. • Ensure the IEP aligns with the student’s goals and addresses academic, social, and behavioral needs. • Regularly assess, review, and adjust IEPs based on the student’s progress and changing needs. 2. Instructional Delivery • Develop and implement differentiated lesson plans that cater to various learning styles and abilities. • Use creative and adaptive teaching methods, including assistive technology, to meet diverse student needs. • Facilitate individual and group learning sessions, ensuring each student is engaged and supported. 3. Progress Monitoring and Assessment • Conduct ongoing formative and summative assessments to monitor student progress and document learning outcomes. • Maintain detailed records of each student’s academic performance, key observations, behavioral progress, and developmental milestones. nclusive classroom environment that promotes student engagement and active learning. • Implement positive behavior management strategies and de-escalation techniques, ensuring a safe learning space for all. • Address challenging behaviors with evidence-based interventions and document behavioral changes as needed. 4. Daily Care and Safety • Supervise and monitor students during classroom activities, outdoor play, and transitions to ensure their safety. • Follow established health and safety protocols to manage risks, prevent accidents, and maintain a safe learning environment for all students. • Be aware of and ready to implement emergency procedures and first aid protocols in case of accidents or health concerns. 5. Collaboration and Teamwork • Work closely with therapists, learning support staff, and other educators to provide a coordinated approach to student’s care. • Participate in team meetings to discuss student progress, share resources, and collaborate on strategies for improved learning outcomes. • Maintain regular communication with parents or caregivers, ensuring they are informed of their child’s progress and development. 6. [Only for SC] Shift Duties • Opening Shift : 7:30 AM to 4:30 PM. • Closing Shift : 9:30 PM to 6:30 PM. • Responsibilities during opening/closing include preparing the classrooms, ensuring safety procedures are followed, and maintaining the environment before and after school hours

title: Copywriter
description:
COPYWRITER A call for the concept-first, the wordsmiths, the storytellers. If you’re reading this, you’re probably looking for a place where your ideas matter, your words carry weight, and your creativity has room to grow. At BLKJ Havas, we’re looking for a Copywriter who’s ready to crack briefs open,
shape ideas into stories, and make work that people actually care about. You’ll be a voice behind the ideas — turning strategy into compelling narratives and briefs into bold creative. Working closely with art directors, strategists, and project teams, you’ll help craft campaigns that are smart, culturally relevant, and creatively sharp. You’ll write, rewrite, and push the work to be better — and in the process, sharpen your own craft. Job Responsibilities: Bring Big Ideas to Life : Contribute to the development and execution of creative concepts grounded in insight, strategy, and culture. Write Across Platforms : Craft copy for campaign platforms, scripts, social content, digital
experiences, OOH, and more — with clarity, craft, and personality. Build Brand Voice : Help shape and maintain a strong, consistent tone of voice across all touchpoints for the brands you work on. Collaborate Closely: Work hand-in-hand with art directors, strategists, and the wider creative team to ensure ideas meet both creative and client objectives. Present with Confidence : Share your work clearly and confidently with internal teams and, when required, clients. Refine the Work : Take feedback constructively and push ideas further in pursuit of stronger creative output. Stay Culture-Curious : Keep a close pulse on trends, conversations, and what’s happening in culture — bringing fresh references and thinking into the work. Grow Your Craft: Learn from senior creatives while continually developing your writing skills and creative point of view. Contribute to Creative Culture : Be an active, positive presence in a collaborative, curious, and ambitious creative environment. Requirements: 3–5 years of copywriting experience within a creative or advertising agency A strong portfolio demonstrating concept-driven thinking and solid craft in writing.
Experience working across integrated campaigns (digital, social, video, OOH, etc.). A sharp eye for language — from headlines and long-form copy to scripts and social captions. Strong communication and collaboration skills, with an openness to feedback and iteration. Ability to manage multiple briefs and deadlines without losing attention to detail. A proactive team player who enjoys working collaboratively with different disciplines. Good understanding of Singapore and regional audiences, with sensitivity to what resonates locally and across Asia. Bonus points for awards, recognitions, or work that shows your ideas stand out and travel — even better
if they’ve made people talk. About BLKJ Havas BLKJ Havas is a creative company that engineers marketing moments for brands in culture, entertainment and media. Powered by the global network of Vivendi including Lagardère, CANAL+, Prisma Media, Gameloft, and Dailymotion, we pride ourselves as the unadvertising agency. Because people hate advertising and we love people. Here at Havas, we pride ourselves on being committed to offering equal opportunities to all potential employees and have zero tolerance for discrimination. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applicants irrespective of age, sex, race, ethnicity, disability and other factors that have no bearing on an individual's ability to perform their job.

With over 20 years of experience in data collection and AI and machine learning, Riverbed’s open and AI-powered observability platform and solutions optimize digital experiences and greatly improves IT efficiency. We are seeking a Solutions Engineer for immediate hire. In this role you will function in a highly technical pre-sales environment, creating technical solutions to solve business problems for global enterprises. What you will do Serve as the technical subject matter expert throughout the sales process.  Owning and managing various pre-sales activities – including technical presentations, demonstrations, and proof of concept projects. Collaborate with the Sales team, jointly owning the sales cycle and account plan. Help to drive deeper & wider into the account – identifying requirements for additional resources from other departments within the customer, customer alternatives (i.e., other means of handling their current requirements), and potential internal sponsors. Understand and document customer business & technical requirements and use them to create value-based proposals, addressing business drivers & pain points – guiding the technical sales cycle towards a technical close & a business win. Build strong relationships with key contacts in the partner ecosystem. Work with Channel Sales team to build and strengthen relationships with partners; Oversee product and solutions training and enablement of the partner ecosystem so that they can be leveraged to help drive opportunities to technical close. What makes you an ideal candidate Experience in a pre-sales (or similar) role, with a deep understanding of enterprise sales cycles and various selling strategies. Relevant competitive knowledge – i.e., industry, technologies and product offerings, evolving technologies, and threats. Proven consultative skills from prior performance engineering engagements in presales or professional services. Able to travel 20% - 40% - traveling primarily throughout Singapore but also in other parts of APAC as required. Outgoing personality and passionate about technical sales and working with customers.

title: Chief Technology Officer
description:
932 Design Group is looking a hands-on Chief Technology Officer (CTO) to lead our technology vision and build the foundation for scalable growth. This role is ideal for a technology leader who thrives in a fast-paced, highly regulated fintech environment, balancing strategic leadership with deep technical execution. The CTO will lead the development of secure, high-performance, and scalable platforms, ensuring technology decisions align closely with business objectives, regulatory requirements, and customer needs. Responsibilities Technology Strategy, Leadership & Execution Define and execute the company’s long-term technology and IT strategy aligned with business goals. Advise executive leadership on technology trends, risks, and opportunities. Lead digital transformation initiatives to drive innovation and competitive advantage. Establish technology governance, standards, and best practices across the organization. Lead and assemble a diverse, high-performing team of technologists/ engineers/ innovators to develop AI-driven initiatives across the organization. Product Development, Architecture & Platforms Oversee the design and delivery of secure, scalable, and high-performance platforms. Ensure system reliability, scalability, and performance while meeting business and customer requirements. Optimize application architecture, system integrations, and development processes to support rapid growth. Security, Risk Management & Compliance Own the cybersecurity and technology risk management strategy. Safeguard digital transactions, sensitive data, and mission-critical systems. Ensure compliance with financial regulations, data protection laws, and industry standards. Lead audits, regulatory reviews, disaster recovery planning, and business continuity initiatives. IT Infrastructure, Operations & DevOps Oversee cloud infrastructure, networks, and enterprise systems to ensure high availability and resilience. Drive DevOps adoption, automation, and continuous improvement to enhance efficiency and reliability. Manage IT budgets, resources, and operational performance. Team Leadership, Collaboration & Vendor Management Build, mentor, and lead high-performing engineering and IT teams. Foster a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement. Collaborate closely with business leaders, product teams, and external stakeholders. Manage third-party vendors and partners to ensure quality, performance, and value. Innovation & Technology Enablement Evaluate and adopt emerging technologies such as cloud, AI, automation, and data platforms. Enable data-driven decision-making and support customer-facing product innovation. Ensure technology solutions remain modern, flexible, and future-ready. Champion creativity and experimentation in the design and development of AI-powered solutions,
automation, and intelligent systems that enhance operational efficiency, unlock insights, and support business growth. Translate business challenges into practical, scalable AI solutions while ensuring ethical use, security, and regulatory compliance. Qualifications Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in computer science, information technology, engineering, or a related field. Proven experience as a CTO or senior technology leadership role. Strong background in building and scaling secure, cloud-based platforms. Extensive understanding of software development, IT infrastructure and cybersecurity best practices. Strong strategic thinking, problem-solving skills, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Hands-on knowledge of AI-driven creative workflows, including generative design, BIM, advanced visualization technologies, and other AI applications, applied to interior design, architecture industry. Please apply by sending in your CV and cover letter to samantha.koh@932designgroup.com.
