title: Principal Software Engineer (C++, Dart)
description: Job Description Job Description Bring more to life. Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology? At Leica Microsystems, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact. You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life. Shape the Future with Us! At Leica Microsystems, we have been shaping the future for over 175 years with groundbreaking optical and digital solutions.  With a culture rooted in customer focus, innovation, and teamwork, we lead the market in microscopy, imaging, and analysis, unveiling the invisible and empowering our customers to build a better, healthier world. Joining Leica Microsystems means contributing to scientific discoveries and supporting surgeons in making critical decisions. Our advanced microscopes and AI-based image analysis solutions enable users to gain profound insights into development and engineering challenges. Here, you will work on meaningful projects alongside passionate colleagues, driving progress and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible. The Principal Software Engineer is responsible to design and develop full-stack solutions for real-time medical device embedded systems. Lead implementation of user interfaces and backend services, architect scalable solutions, optimize performance across the stack, and ensure code quality while maintaining regulatory compliance and embedded system constraints. This position reports to the Manager, Software and is part of the Research & Development Medical located in Singapore and will be an on-site role. In this role, you will have the opportunity to: Design and implement full-stack solutions across user interfaces and backend services, ensuring compliance with regulatory and embedded system constraints. Own the software development lifecycle from requirements refinement through design, coding, testing, documentation, and release, maintaining high standards of quality and performance. Create and review technical documentation, providing guidance to team members and ensuring clarity and accuracy in all deliverables. Provide technical support to internal teams (Customer Service, RA&QA, Production) by reproducing and resolving issues and toolchain challenges. Collaborate with global, cross-functional teams to align architecture, processes, and deliverables, while driving continuous improvement using DBS and agile methodologies. The essential requirements of the job include Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Human-Computer Interaction, or a related field, coupled with strong communication and collaboration skills for distributed team environments. 8–10 years of professional software development experience, with strong programming skills in C++, Dart, or other modern object-oriented languages (e.g., C#). Proficiency in Flutter or similar cross-platform frameworks for building responsive, high-performance user interfaces, and experience with AI coding tools. Solid understanding of software development methodologies, including Scrum, Agile, and waterfall practices, as well as software testing principles. Deep UI/UX knowledge for embedded systems and familiarity with regulatory compliance requirements in medical device development. Leica Microsystems, a Danaher operating company, offers a broad array of comprehensive, competitive benefit programs that add value to our lives. Whether it’s a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job. Check out our benefits at Danaher Benefits Info .

title: Supervisor (FOH)
description:
Elephant Grounds is an award-winning Hong Kong-born lifestyle brand renowned for its specialty coffee, Japanese influenced cuisine, and café culture. Founded in 2013 by Gerald Li and Kevin Poon, the brand began as a coffee kiosk within a retail shop and has since grown into a fully integrated coffee roastery, artisanal bakery, and restaurant concept with operations in Hong Kong, Manila, Jakarta Q1 2026, and now Singapore. Our coffee program is led by an award-winning team - including a champion roaster
and other specialty coffee professionals - with a relentless dedication to building a sustainable coffee program. Elephant Grounds focuses on the alchemy of space - design, music, and light - and the impact they have on guests, creating an unmistakable energy and vibe. We are a growing hospitality group preparing to launch the first local outlet of a renowned international café concept known for its specialty coffee, seasonal menus, and modern ambiance.  This launch marks a strategic expansion of our diverse F&B portfolio, we aim to deliver quality, consistency, and innovation at every customer touchpoint. We are looking for passionate individuals to join us as Supervisor.  If you’re driven by dedication and a love for exceptional culinary experiences, come be part of our team and help shape this extraordinary journey at Elephant Grounds. Job Responsibilities 1) Service Excellence & Brand Culture ·    Greet and assist customers with warmth and professionalism, ensuring a welcoming
atmosphere and providing timely service. ·    Present menus, make recommendations, take orders, and serve food and beverages, ensuring a high-quality dining experience. ·    Address customer concerns and complaints with a positive attitude, ensuring guest
satisfaction. ·    Lead the FOH team to promote organic, heartfelt hospitality in line with Elephant
Grounds’ core values. ·    Maintain uniform standards and FOH grooming – proper attire and appearance must be upheld at all times to present a professional and consistent brand image. ·    Assist with food preparation and multitask effectively during busy periods. 2) Roster Management (FOH & BOH) ·    Oversee weekly and monthly planning of staff rosters across Front of House and Back of House. · Submit weekly roster and timesheets for all casual and permanent part-timers to HR. · Submit month-end documentation to HR, including: - Final amended roster. - Shift allowance report for eligible staff. - Timesheets for all part-time employees. - Scanned and properly archived records. - Inventory Tracking & Controls. - Conduct monthly kitchen inventory, documented via Google Sheets for traceability    and ease of access. - Perform weekly bar inventory reconciliations against POS sales data and update stock sheets accordingly. - Oversee bar month-end inventory, also tracked via Google Sheets. 3) Operational Maintenance & Supplies ·    Establish and maintain a comprehensive checklist for daily, weekly, fortnightly, and
monthly maintenance audits across the restaurant. ·    Ensure all supply storage cabinets are regularly reviewed, cleaned, and restocked to
par levels.  Supplies include: - Cutlery, sleeves, takeaway wares, napkins. - Stationeries, gloves, masks, cleaning tools. 4) POS & Delivery Merchant Oversight ·    Regularly review and clean up the POS system to ensure all items are accurate and
current. ·    Ensure product images are uploaded for every POS item (applicable to kiosk and QR
online ordering). ·    Standardize item listings and pricing across all delivery platforms, ensuring consistency and accuracy. 5)    Any other duties as and when assigned by the management. Job Requirement ·    Strong team player with a customer service-oriented mindset. ·    Ability to lead a small team and maintain composure during peak hours. ·    Food Safety Hygiene Certification required. ·    Ability to work flexible hours/shift, including weekends and public holidays. Must Have Factor ·    Team player with a proactive, hands-on approach. ·    Calm under pressure and reliable in fast-paced environment. ·    Eager to learn and grow with a dynamic culinary team. Thank you for your interest in this position. Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

This is a high-impact leadership role for someone who wants to build, scale, and lead a business , not just manage a product. Key Responsibilities Business Leadership & Strategy Own the business performance of the IMA portfolio, including growth strategy, market penetration, and competitive positioning. Develop and execute a multi-year business roadmap aligned with corporate goals and market opportunities. Partner with Sales leadership to drive revenue plans, segment strategies, customer acquisition, and partner engagement. Identify and evaluate new business opportunities, product lines, and market expansions. Product Strategy & Roadmap Ownership Lead the end-to-end product lifecycle—from concept, validation, and development to launch, scaling, and phase-out. Define a compelling product vision and roadmap grounded in customer needs, commercial viability, and long-term business goals. Go-to-Market & Commercial Execution Co-lead GTM planning with Sales and Marketing, including pricing strategy, customer value propositions, launch activities, and competitive messaging. Work directly with key customers, partners, and ecosystem players to drive adoption, secure design-wins, and influence product decisions. Support major sales engagements and customer discussions as the product and business expert. Market Intelligence & Portfolio Management Conduct in-depth market, technology, and competitive analysis to steer product direction and business strategy. Monitor portfolio performance and P&L-related metrics, ensuring products achieve commercial targets. Continuously optimize product mix, cost structure, and roadmap priorities to maximize market impact and profitability. Cross-Functional Leadership Drive alignment across R&D, Sales, Operations, Supply Chain, and Marketing to execute business objectives cohesively. Champion the product internally and externally—guiding teams, influencing decisions, and representing the business unit at key engagements. Requirements Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Business, or related field. 5+ years of experience in Product Management, Business Management, or solution leadership—preferably in NAND flash, storage, memory solutions, or hardware-based technologies. Strong commercial acumen with proven experience partnering closely with Sales or driving business outcomes. Deep understanding of SSD, USB, SD, and MicroSD technologies and market dynamics. Ability to think strategically while executing with urgency and precision. Strong analytical and data-driven decision-making capabilities. Excellent communication, presentation, and stakeholder management skills. Entrepreneurial, ownership-driven mindset—comfortable making decisions and accountable for results. Who We’re Looking For Business Builders – You think like an owner and build with long-term value in mind. Strategic Leaders – You connect the dots across product, market, customer needs, and business goals. Growth Drivers – You work closely with Sales to open markets, win customers, and grow revenue. Execution Champions – You deliver results with speed, precision, and resilience. Collaborators – You align teams and move the business forward collectively. Flexxon is not just a workplace—it’s where bold ideas are turned into global impact. If you’re ready to lead a business, not just a product, we invite you to apply and grow with us.

title: Director of Marketing & Communications
description: The Opportunity Step into a pivotal leadership role where luxury meets innovation. As Director of Marketing & Communications , you will be the architect of Conrad Singapore Marina Bay’s brand story — shaping how the hotel is seen, experienced, and remembered. This is your chance to drive commercial success while positioning the property as Singapore’s premier destination for milestone moments, from glamorous weddings to world-class conferences. What You’ll Do Lead with Vision : Design and execute a fully integrated marketing and communications strategy that fuels revenue growth, elevates brand equity, and amplifies Hilton Honors contributions. Be the Storyteller : Craft compelling narratives and sales enablement materials — presentations, proposals, digital assets — that resonate with corporate clients, MICE planners, and couples seeking unforgettable celebrations. Own the Digital Stage : Drive engagement across all owned channels — website, CRM, email, and social media — ensuring every touchpoint reflects the hotel’s luxury positioning. Champion Social Media : Define and deliver a bold social strategy that sparks conversation, builds advocacy, and keeps Conrad Marina Bay at the center of Singapore’s lifestyle scene. Guard the Brand : Protect and elevate Conrad’s voice and visual identity, ensuring consistency across every guest-facing moment, from pre-arrival inspiration to post-stay loyalty. Shape Perception : Oversee PR, media relations, influencer collaborations, and agency partnerships to strengthen share of voice and reputation. Deliver Results : Track, analyze, and report on marketing effectiveness using KPIs such as ROAS, CTR, CPC, and conversion — ensuring every campaign drives measurable impact. Lead with Purpose : Inspire, coach, and develop a high-performing Marketing & Communications team, fostering creativity, collaboration, and accountability. Key Outcomes Achievement of commercial metrics: Revenue Generation Index (RGI), total revenue, Hilton Honors contribution . Growth in Weddings, MICE, and social events through targeted storytelling and campaigns. Elevated online reputation and guest sentiment across platforms. Seamless execution of PR, digital, and creative deliverables with agency partners. A culture of innovation and excellence within the Marketing & Communications team. Why This Role Matters This is more than a marketing position — it’s about shaping the future of a landmark hotel in one of Asia’s most dynamic markets. You’ll have the platform to create unforgettable guest experiences, drive commercial success, and leave a lasting mark on Singapore’s luxury hospitality scene. Qualifications - External What are we looking for? A Director of Marketing & Communications serving Hilton Worldwide Brand hotels and vacations is always working on behalf of our Guests and working with other Team Members. To successfully fill this role, you should maintain the attitude, behaviors, skills, and values that follow: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business or related field. Minimum 3 years in a leadership role within the hospitality industry (hotels/resorts) will be an advantage. Proven track record of developing and executing successful, result-driven marketing strategies. Expert knowledge of digital marketing, content marketing, analytics and social media tools. Exceptional communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills. Strong team player who collaborates well with stakeholders. Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and multi-task. What will it be like to work for Hilton? Hilton is the leading global hospitality company, spanning the lodging sector from luxurious full-service hotels and resorts to extended-stay suites and mid-priced hotels. For nearly a century, Hilton has offered business and leisure travelers the finest in accommodations, service, amenities and value. Hilton is dedicated to continuing its tradition of providing exceptional guest experiences across its global brands .  Our vision “to fill the earth with the light and warmth of hospitality” unites us as a team to create remarkable hospitality experiences around the world every day.  And, our amazing Team Members are at the heart of it all!

title: Research Engineer/Fellow (UX) - JL
description: As a University of Applied Learning, the Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) works closely with industry in its research pursuits. This position is situated within the Centre for Immersification, dedicated to applied research in immersive technologies and bridging the gap between academia and industry. Research staff at the Centre have the opportunity to be equipped with applied research skill sets relevant to industry demands while working on innovative projects within the university. The research team is seeking a Research Fellow or Research Engineer with strong expertise in Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) and social science methodologies to lead the user research and policy development, as part of an interdisciplinary team investigating harmful user-generated content in virtual worlds as part of a national research initiative on Online Trust and Safety. The primary responsibility of this role is to investigate users’ experiences with harmful content in these virtual environments, review existing reporting guidelines on these platforms, and evaluate users’ responses to alternative mechanisms. The successful candidate will work with a diverse team of faculty members, engineers, and industry partners, including labs at the College of Computing and Data Science (CCDS) at NTU. Technical skills, natural curiosity, versatility, and enthusiasm are highly desired traits that will enable success in this fast-paced environment. Key Responsibilities Manage the research project together with Principal Investigator (PI), Co-PI and the research team members to ensure all project deliverables are met. Conduct a comprehensive review of literature, existing safety policies, community standards, and moderation tools currently used in online social gaming platforms Design and execute surveys and interviews to collect data (including participant recruitment) to address the relevant research questions. Perform statistical analysis on survey/experimental data, and Conduct thematic analysis on interview transcripts and open-ended survey responses. To lead and report research findings in a form of high-quality publications and present in international peer-reviewed conferences and journals. Develop policy recommendations based on the research findings. Carry out Risk Assessment, and ensure compliance with Work, Safety and Health Regulations. Coordinate procurement and liaison with vendors/suppliers. Work independently, as well as within a team, to ensure proper operation and maintenance of equipment. title: FULL TIME ASSISTANT ART TEACHER
description: FULL TIME ASSISTANT ART TEACHER @ LyttleSpace (Bedok South) FULL-TIME POSITION STARTING IN FEB (5 days work week, must work on 1 weekends) $2500-$3000 depending on experience Do you enjoy nurturing young minds and supporting their creative growth? Are you someone who loves being hands-on — contributing to lesson planning, creative prototyping, and working in a thoughtful, dynamic environment where ideas come to life? If you’re passionate about guiding children through their artistic journeys, LyttleSpace would love to meet you. At LyttleSpace, we celebrate the individuality of every child and believe in building a nurturing community that supports creative expression. As we enter an exciting phase of growth, we’re expanding and looking for enthusiastic and committed individuals to join our team. If the following qualities resonate with you, we’d be delighted to invite you on this meaningful and fulfilling journey with us. QUALITIES WE VALUE: Child-Led Approach— Our ideal candidate understands the importance of genuine care when working with young children. Respect, patience, and a positive attitude are not just desirable, but essential qualities for fostering a nurturing environment. Embracing our child-led teaching philosophy to inspire creativity and imagination. Growth Mindset/Adapable to Change— We appreciate individuals who are open to learning and growing. A willingness to embrace new teaching methodologies, along with a growth mindset and the ability to accept constructive feedback, is vital in our collaborative space. Strong Communication Skills — Clear communication is key. Proficiency in English, both written and spoken, enables effective interaction with both our young artists and their families. A Collaborative, Hands On Environment — Experience the joy of working in a warm and collaborative atmosphere where creativity thrives. There is alot of hands-on involvement with prototype making, fostering a dynamic and interactive learning environment Self Artistic Exploration — Explore your own artistic expressions while encouraging children to explore theirs. We believe that an essential aspect of being a good teacher is not only guiding children on their creative journey but also embarking on an art practice of your own. It will be a source of inspiration and understanding of your very own creative process. POSITION OVERVIEW: As an Assistant Teacher, you will play a pivotal role in the growth and management of LyttleSpace Pte. Ltd. Your responsibilities will encompass various aspects, including teaching, preparatory work, social media content management, and administrative tasks. Full time assistant teacher will be supported and trained to teach and lead a class. Your role goes beyond instruction. You will engage in the learning and development of the students, encouraging them to be creative and imaginative. We adopt a child led approach and practice respectful teaching. Daily tasks involves lesson planning, preparation and facilitation of classes. There will be content creation where strong graphic and illustration skills comes into play. Your hands-on involvement in designing lesson plans and engaging in prototype creation, will contribute to our dynamic learning environment. Research, ideation and innovation process is key at LyttleSpace. For social media management, you will help assist, create and manage captivating social media content to showcase our class processes. This includes preparing stories, simple video editing, photo editing and organising materials. Full time assistant teacher will assist in admin duties such as lesson documentation. We hope that all teachers here at LyttleSpace will make a meaningful impact in the creative heart work we do (: As an Assistant Teacher grows into a Lead Teacher role, the pay will be adjusted to reflect the expanded responsibilities and experience. REQUIREMENTS: - Genuine love working with young children is a must. - Adaptable to change - Respectful - Patient with children - Positive attitude - Willingness to learn - Have a growth mindset and open to feedback. - Good command of English - Good social skills - Organised PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS: While prior experience in early childhood education and art classes is advantageous, it is not a strict requirement. We encourage applications from a diverse pool of candidates, including recent graduates with a Diploma or Degree in Fine Arts, Design, Art Education, or related disciplines. If you are excited about embarking on this merry and meaning making work with us, join the LyttleSpace family and help us shape a world where every child's creativity is celebrated and nurtured. We invite you to submit your application to lyttlespace@gmail.com along with you CV and cover letter in the below format: Email subject: Application for the position of Assistant Teacher at LyttleSpace Please be informed that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. OUR LOCATION: Blk 158 Bedok South Ave 3 #01-589, Singapore 460158 Job Type: Full-time Pay: $2,500.00 - $3,000.00 per month Work Location: In person

title: Senior Software Engineer
description: JOB SUMMARY SABRE is Standard Chartered’ s Analytics Booking and Risk Engine and is the strategic risk and PnL platform for Financial Markets. The development team is a world class technology group in Standard Chartered bank to provide technical solutions to support the bank’s Financial Markets business as well as support requirements from other projects with a wider scope such as regulatory books of work. We bring a deep understanding of the market, a scientific approach, and innovative technologies to bear on the challenges of profitable trading in the world's highly competitive environment. This is a rare opportunity to get into an organization working with smart, great technologists globally in financial markets domain. The culture in SABRE is open, intellectual, and fun. Learning opportunities are plentiful: organized classes and seminars; freewheeling discussions; intensive one-on-one mentorships. Career advancement is always waiting for those high-energized talents to pursue. The successful candidate will play a leading role in the implementation of highly visible strategic programmes with the primary responsibilities around building components within the SABRE risk platform. Any developed solution need to be scalable, robust and fault tolerant catering to the existing and future business needs in a timely manner. In addition the successful candidate will be expected to develop innovative solutions with a view to these being released quickly to gain feedback before advising on the full scale roll out. The candidate will be a natural leader and mentor with a driven yet pragmatic, delivery focused mindset and a desire to deliver constant business value through the effective and innovative use of technology with a proven background in delivering high quality business critical software. The candidate will be expected to take ownership, responsibility and accountability for critical deliveries. In addition, the successful candidate will have come from a mature and demonstrable agile background and be comfortable dealing with the resistance that comes with change. An important aspect of the role is second line support in a dynamic and fast-paced front-office environment. The candidate need to be comfortable talking directly with the business to understand the issue and take ownership of the issue to provide a timely and accurate resolution. The candidate will work closely with the other team members and senior stakeholders in order to design and implement critical strategic solutions whilst collaborating and engaging with Business Stakeholders to deliver to exacting requirements. The candidate is expected to work in a dynamic global team with business analysts, project managers, business stakeholders and other technical experts across multiple locations Key Roles & Responsibilities •    Work closely with the Development Manager, Project Manager, and a team of developers to deliver quality features and enhancements to the internal business users •    Demonstrate a systematic and disciplined architectural, system design and programming approach •    Deeply passionate about best-practice software design principles, you will write robust code along with a high-level of test coverage •    Self-starter who can drive/facilitate requirements and development effort •    Design smart and scalable solutions following the bank standards •    Coordinate with other teams in Technology •    Develop, prepare and maintain system documentation, including program descriptions and operational procedures •    Act as the second line of support during production problems. Diversity & Inclusion Standard Chartered is committed to diversity and inclusion. We believe that a work environment which embraces diversity will enable us to get the best out of the broadest spectrum of people to sustain strong business performance and competitive advantage. By building an inclusive culture, each employee can develop a sense of belonging, and have the opportunity to maximize their personal potential. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Lead to achieve the outcomes set out in the Bank’s Conduct Principles •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Our Ideal Candidate •	8+ years C#/WPF/.NET development experience building performance critical rich desktop applications •	8+ years hands on development within investment banking IT •	Strong knowledge and experience of working in Agile teams with Agile practices •	Strong understanding of the software development life cycle •	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills •	Strong understanding of technology and the ability to learn new technologies quickly •	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build and maintain relationships with all stakeholders •	Self-starter that is enthusiastic and proactive •	Be able to work in a fast-paced front-office environment •	Be able to prioritize and work on multiple workstreams simultaneously •	Be able to take ownership of issues and projects, and see them through to delivery •	Possess good judgement and decision making skills with a pragmatic and common-sense approach to solving problems Role Specific Technical Competencies •	C#/WPF/.NET development of rich desktop applications •	Multithreaded, concurrent, and asynchronous programming using TPL and Rx •	MVVM frameworks, preferably Prism •	Dependency Injection containers, preferably Unity •	LINQ/Rx with a functional mindset and coding style •	RESTful APIs and message queues such as ActiveMQ •	DevExpress, Infragistics, or any other popular WPF component library •	Object-Oriented Design and Programming (OOD/OOP) •	NUnit, Moq, TDD, BDD, UI Automation •	CI/CD Pipelines such as Azure DevOps •	Agile/Scrum practices •	Visual Studio and ReSharper About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. title: Business Development Manager (Strategic Partnerships)
description: CORA Environment is building a future where sustainability and circular economy principles drive real impact. Develop and manage the Closed-Loop Partners Network, fostering collaboration among key stakeholders such as recyclers, manufacturers etc. Drive adoption of the CORA Recycling Mark as a recognized sustainability standard. Lead negotiations and ensure mutually beneficial outcomes. Monitor and report on partnership performance, impact, and ROI. Business Development & Growth Develop strategies to scale partnership programs nationally and regionally. Identify new revenue streams and sponsorship opportunities tied to sustainability initiatives. Represent CORA at industry events, forums, and stakeholder meetings. Operational Excellence Work closely with internal teams and business units to ensure strategic alignment across all partnership initiatives. Support strategic planning and forecasting activities. Prepare reports and presentations for senior leadership and stakeholders. Collaborate with Business Intelligence team for data-driven insights on recycling trends. Support operational excellence in call center and customer engagement as programs grow. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Business, Sustainability, Environmental Management, or related field. 2+ years in business development, partnerships, or sustainability programs. Strong relationship-building and negotiation skills. Proven ability to manage complex projects and multiple stakeholders. Passion for sustainability and circular economy principles.

title: High School Science Teacher
description: REPORTS TO: Middle & High School Principal JOB GOAL: A High School Science teacher at ICS must have a strong understanding of the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS) and have experience closing the achievement gap. They must develop engaging and innovative lessons that allow our students to grow academically, socially and emotionally. The teacher should be confident in incorporating technology (including emerging technologies such as GenAI) and hands-on learning experiences into the classroom with enthusiasm and also believe in collaboration with other colleagues, promoting understanding and communication in a diverse learning and cultural environment. PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: Plan, prepare, and execute appropriate lessons, utilizing appropriate resource materials and activities. Follow the prescribed scope and sequence of curriculum and assessment as scheduled. Employ a variety of instructional aides, methods, and materials that will promote best practices in order to reach the whole child as stated in the ICS mission statement. Assess student learning against the appropriate standards on a regular basis and provide progress reports as required. Identify the needs of students through diagnostic procedures and align instructional objectives to these needs. Effect student learning through mastery of the subject material by utilizing valid teaching techniques to achieve curriculum goals within the framework of the school’s philosophy. Eager to explore and learn about emerging technologies, such as Generative AI, with a commitment to integrating them effectively into the classroom to foster critical thinking and guide students in developing ethical and responsible use. Ensure Biblical Worldwide Integration (BWI) is mapped throughout the curriculum. Complete lesson/unit plans, Atlas, ESO documentation, and PLC expectations within specified time frames. Maintain student records as required by the school. Communicate with parents, students, and other professional staff regarding student progress (behavioral and academic). Follow school policies, procedures, rules, regulations, and guidelines and the provisions of the contract. Maintain proper discipline in the classroom and on the school premises utilizing teacher created discipline plans in conjunction with a school-wide discipline plan. Inform the administration if/when unable to fulfill any duty assigned. Prepare adequate information and materials for a substitute teacher. Plan and lead a student Chapel when called upon. Supervise extracurricular activities, organizations, and outings as assigned. Complete the Christian Philosophy of Education in-service program within the first three years of teaching at ICS. Attend and participate in devotions, teachers’ meetings, evening programs (if involving his or her students or as requested by the administration), and other meetings as assigned. Carry out supervisory duty as assigned (before school, break, lunch, after school). Maintain a classroom in a clean and educationally stimulating environment. Maintain regular and accurate attendance and grade reports that provide comprehensive information for each student’s learning progress. Keep students, parents, and the administration informed of students’ progress or deficiencies, and provide sufficient and timely notice to parents when students fail to meet learning standards. Submit all appropriate reports to the administration, guidance office, and business office as required by school regulations or as requested. Work with the administration in implementing all policies, procedures, and directives governing the operation of the school. Complete professional development programs as required by the administration and/or ICS Board. Maintain teaching credentials as required through sponsoring agencies (state/provincial education department, ACSI, etc.). Acquire and maintain teaching credentials as required by the laws of Singapore, NICS, and/or ICS. Demonstrate the character qualities of enthusiasm, courtesy, flexibility, integrity, kindness, self-control, perseverance, and punctuality. Exemplify strong interpersonal and communication skills. Meet everyday stress with emotional stability, objectivity, and optimism. Develop and maintain rapport with students, parents, and staff by treating others with friendliness, open-mindedness, dignity, and consideration. Respectfully submit to and demonstrate loyalty to constituted authority. Maintain a personal appearance that models cleanliness, modesty, good taste, and is in agreement with school policy. QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor’s Degree Teaching credential from a US state, Canadian province or from ACSI (exceptions will be approved by the Academic Board) Expertise in working with students and teaching Advanced Placement (AP) courses Knowledge of specific subject(s)

Excellent leadership, organisational and communication skills to effectively collaborate with internal and external stakeholders. Proficiency in project, outsourcing / vendor and contract management. Detail and quality oriented with a driven mindset and initiative to make things happen. Ability to provide a balanced view of a problem situation, a clear thinker and good problem solver to guide the team to analyse and simplify complex issues and provide effective solutions. Leveraging ByteDance's cutting-edge intelligent technologies, we are devoted to developing innovative products and solutions to shape the future. Serve as the primary point of contact for technical issues and issue escalations. Identify and recommend solutions for customers' evolving needs and challenges. - Technical Guidance and Support - Serve as the primary customer liaison during the post-sales phase. Gain in-depth understanding of customers' business scenarios and summarize product application best practices in real customer use cases. - Collaborate with customers to plan and execute Cloud migration, deployment, and expansion projects. Monitor progress (e.g., cloud migration projects) and provide regular updates and technical support to stakeholders. title: Construction Quality Technical Program Manager, Electrical/Mechanical - Singapore
description: Job description Our mission as Construction Quality and Commissioning (QCx) is to design, build, and operate a fact-based quality management system that provides predictable quality and a closed loop learning culture from design through operations for our global data centers. We strive to optimize construction quality and the commissioning process through data-driven decision making, in order to deliver data centers with the highest quality and reliability in the industry. As a team, we are committed to growing a culture of excellence and collaboration, and to continuously improving the processes and outcomes in order to exceed industry standards and the expectations of the stakeholders. As a Technical Program Manager, you will use your technical expertise to lead complex, multi-disciplinary projects from start to finish. You will work with stakeholders to plan requirements, identify risks, manage project schedules, and communicate clearly with cross-functional partners across the company. You will be responsible for developing the commissioning strategy and relevant documentation (i.e., specifications, test plans, checklists, guides) for mechanical or electrical equipment, supporting the APAC region. In this role, you will utilize an understanding of data center construction standards, quality control and assurance processes, and commissioning procedures to ensure the successful delivery of high-quality, reliable, and efficient data center facilities. You will also support a portfolio of construction projects to ensure the successful implementation of the general construction quality program and reduce cost of poor quality in the construction projects. Qualifications Job responsibilities Source commissioning agents and develop strategy and relevant documents (i.e., specifications, test plans, checklists, guides, trainings). Hold necessary cross-functional team reviews and onboard project stakeholders. Participate/facilitate vendor meetings to set expectations on commissioning requirements, verify feasibility of strategy and documenting agreements. Ensure document workflow approval in collaboration with the QCx librarian. Report the quality and commissioning status for the full lifecycle of the construction project. Monitor and oversee the implementation of all construction quality and commissioning processes by the project management consultants, general contractors, electrical/mechanical trades, and quality and commissioning agents. Identify risks to a project and develop mitigation strategies with the project team. Work and collaborate on regional/global initiatives to improve the construction quality program. Minimum qualifications Bachelor's degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or equivalent practical experience 5 years of experience with managing the end-to-end construction projects, or product lifecycle (e.g., process developments, implementation and improvement 5 years of infrastructure experience in the operation and construction of data center, semiconductor, pharmaceutical, power, nuclear, or other 24x7 mission critical facilities Preferred qualifications 10 years of experience with managing the end-to-end construction projects, or product lifecycle (e.g., process developments, and improvement 10 years of infrastructure experience in the operation and construction of data center, semiconductor, pharmaceutical, power, nuclear, or other 24x7 mission critical facilities. Experience in leading cross-functional process improvements and the development and implementation of construction quality and commissioning programs. Experience with the construction of hyperscale data centers. Understanding of SQL, Tableau, metrics dashboards, data visualization, and Google Workspace. Knowledge of data center structural, electrical, mechanical, and controls equipment.

Support analysis, testing, and operations teams. Participate in code reviews and architectural decisions. Mentor junior developers. The candidate must be able to effectively understand requirements from their stakeholders and be able to proactively participate in ongoing product enhancement discussions with their stakeholders and provide informed inputs to help shape and evolve those discussions. Analysing those requirements to find the most appropriate technical solution and creating a technical specification where needed. Advanced expertise in computer networks, computer architecture, and operating systems like Linux. title: Principal Engineer, NAND TD, RDA
description: Our vision is to transform how the world uses information to enrich life for all . Micron Technology is a world leader in innovating memory and storage solutions that accelerate the transformation of information into intelligence, inspiring the world to learn, communicate and advance faster than ever. As a Principal Engineer in NAND TD RDA , you will lead a team in the use of next-generation defect detection equipment and processes to identify and solve issues. The goal of this position is to accelerate cycles of learning and technology transfer in Technology Development. We need leaders who are passionate about developing their team, delivering solutions to our internal customers, and driving our supplier's road maps to meet our future inspection needs. This position will provide the opportunity for you to collaborate with process integration, process development, data science, and yield analysis within and outside Technology Development. As a Technology Development RDA Lead, your leadership and innovation will have the ability to impact the team globally. You and your team will be responsible to support experimentation, data analysis, baseline health metrics, and systematic and random defect improvement through partnering with others in Technical Development and Global Manufacturing. Responsibility includes , but not limited to: Encourage a safe work environment Mentor your team in analyzing experimental process flows looking for defect anomalies and quarantining process tools that are out of control Build an environment of continuous improvement process (CIP) demeanor, looking for ways to improve operations, and how to make a difference today Develop inspection technology roadmaps to drive our internal and external partners enabling a clear path to future advanced memory needs Manage projects internal to your team and across functional areas Direct your team to recognize, identify, and raise awareness of process anomalies Develop clear metrics for program health and communicate in an effective manor to set program priorities Build and improve upon detection techniques and identify new ways to use existing tools to identify relevant process issues Active participation in technical meetings and subsequently, synthesize new opportunities or novel approaches from the same Collaborate with the global network to develop best known methods and maintain alignment between sites Proficiency in data analysis, machine learning, and statistical modeling to derive actionable insights from complex datasets is an added advantage Requirements : PhD /Masters in Applied Mechanics, Mechanical Engineering, Microelectronics or Electrical and Electronics Engineering 6-8 years RDA experience in the semiconductor industry working in the process development for Equipment/Applications 6 - 8 years of experience in RDA Defect, semiconductor optical and electron beam inspection methods and e-beam technologies Exposure in various tools like KLA, AMAT Proactive and passionate about learning and mentoring your team, and a leader drawn to creative problem solving and innovative solutions, will investigate process anomalies, root cause analysis, data manipulation, and correlations Proven track record in innovating and drive effective change in an energizing environment Has shown combination of flexibility and tenacity in the pursuit of goals Excellent technical, communication, and analytical skills Develops new or modified process formulations, defines processing or handling equipment requirements and specifications, and reviews processing techniques and methods applied in the manufacture, fabrication, and evaluation of products. Involvement may begin at any step from pilot plant to full-scale manufacturing. Coordinates design requirement review with appropriate engineering/scientific personnel to ensure compatibility of processing methods. Compiles and evaluates test data to determine appropriate limits and variables for process or material specifications. May conceive and plan projects involving definition and selection of new concepts, equipment automation technology, and approaches in the processing or development of new or improved processes.

title: Research Executive
description: About Dentsu Group (dentsu) Dentsu Group Inc. (Tokyo: 4324; ISIN: JP3551520004) is a global holding company established on January 1, 2020. The Group operates through two main networks: dentsu japan network, overseeing agencies in Japan, and dentsu international, headquartered in London, managing operations outside Japan. Operating in around 145 markets with 65,000 professionals, the Dentsu Group delivers integrated, client-centric communications, media, and digital solutions. Its five global leadership brands—Carat, dentsu X, iProspect, Dentsu Creative, and Merkle—alongside the Dentsu Japan Network, including Dentsu Inc., offer end-to-end services across media, creativity, customer experience, and content. Role Purpose Our client is seeking a Research Executive – Intelligence to support the delivery of data-driven insights primarily within the B2B category. This role sits within the Research & Data Strategy team and supports marketing and business decision-making through robust analysis, insight development, and clear storytelling. The successful candidate will work closely with senior team members to translate complex data into actionable insights, supporting research-led programmes, testing and optimisation initiatives, and the delivery of high-quality insight outputs. This role offers strong development opportunities for someone looking to grow their expertise in research, analytics, and insight-led strategy. Responsibilites • Analyse quantitative data (e.g. surveys, intent data, performance datasets) and qualitative data (e.g. interviews, open-ended responses) to identify patterns, tensions, and opportunities. • Synthesize findings across multiple data sources into end-to-end insight reports with clear conclusions and implications. • Apply statistical reasoning and advanced analytical techniques, including segmentation, propensity modelling, and correlation analysis, when required. • Support questionnaire design, script testing, and validation, including routing, logic, and user experience. • Manage fieldwork processes, ensuring data quality, quota management, and progress tracking. • Conduct desk research and market scanning to uncover relevant secondary data, trends, and contextual inputs. • Prepare charts, outputs, and narrative frameworks to support insight-led storytelling. • Lead defined sections of insight and strategy presentations, clearly articulating implications and recommendations. • Use Q for data setup, cleaning, and analysis. • Use Excel where appropriate for exploratory analysis, modelling, and validation. • Pull and interpret data from relevant audience and market insight tools to support B2B initiatives, including ABM, segmentation, and targeting. • Support ongoing testing and optimisation initiatives and contribute to continuous improvement of insight delivery. • Lead day-to-day client and stakeholder communications, providing clarity and direction throughout project delivery. • Collaborate closely with strategy, activation, creative, and client leadership teams to translate insight into integrated solutions. Experience & Skills • Minimum 2+ years’ experience in research, preferably within B2B. • Strong experience with quantitative and qualitative research methodologies. • Strong analytical skills, with the ability to translate data into clear conclusions and compelling stories. • Experience using Q, Displayr, or SPSS. • Experience across a range of research project types, such as brand, customer satisfaction and loyalty, segmentation, market assessment, concept testing, thought leadership, and ABM/ABX. • Strong communication and stakeholder management skills. • High attention to detail and accuracy. • Ambition to develop into a B2B research consultant with increasing client ownership and commercial responsibility. We welcome applications from all individuals, regardless of race, ethnicity, nationality, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, marital status, or any other protected characteristic. Beyond recruitment, we strive to create an environment where everyone feels respected, supported, and empowered to bring their authentic selves to work.

Strong analytical, problem-solving, and project management skills. Preferred/Good to have: Experience in semiconductor or advanced manufacturing environments. description: Role Overview The Student Recruitment Manager (China Market) is responsible for leading and driving student recruitment strategies across Mainland China for SagePaths and its partner institutions. This role oversees market development, recruitment operations, and team performance to ensure sustainable growth in student enrolment for higher education pathways, undergraduate, and postgraduate programmes. Identify new market opportunities, cities, schools, and channels to expand recruitment coverage. Monitor recruitment performance, forecast enrolment trends, and optimise conversion pipelines. 2. Set clear targets, performance benchmarks, and professional development plans for team members. Foster a results-driven, ethical, and student-centred recruitment culture. 3. Brand Building & Marketing Collaboration Work closely with marketing and communications teams to localise campaigns, messaging, and promotional materials for the China market. Ensure consistency of brand positioning and messaging across online and offline platforms. Drive high-impact recruitment campaigns through digital marketing, exhibitions, school visits, webinars, and roadshows. 4. Partnerships & Stakeholder Engagement Build and maintain strong relationships with: Chinese high schools and international departments Education agents and sub-agents Overseas partner institutions Alumni, parents, and key opinion leaders (KOLs) Represent SagePaths at education fairs, partner meetings, and industry events. 5. Market Intelligence & Data Analysis Conduct regular competitor analysis and market research on student behaviour, pricing trends, and channel effectiveness. Analyse recruitment data to identify gaps, opportunities, and strategic adjustments. Provide management with market insights, forecasts, and performance reports. 6. Process Optimisation & Compliance Ensure recruitment processes comply with partner institution requirements and regulatory standards. Optimise CRM usage, admissions workflows, and data reporting systems. Uphold ethical recruitment practices and protect institutional reputation. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in any discipline; Master’s degree preferred. Minimum 6 years of relevant experience, with at least 3 years in international student recruitment, higher education management, or education consultancy. Proven track record in managing recruitment teams and achieving enrolment targets in the China market. Strong understanding of: China education landscape and student decision-making behaviour Study abroad pathways and university admissions processes Digital recruitment channels (XHS, WeChat, Douyin, Bilibili, etc.) Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Highly analytical, data-driven, and commercially aware. Strong presentation and negotiation skills with schools, partners, and parents. Able to work under pressure, manage multiple projects, and meet tight timelines. Fluent in Mandarin and English (spoken and written). Preferred Attributes Experience working with international universities or private education institutions. Strong network within China’s international education sector. Strategic mindset with hands-on execution ability. High level of professionalism, integrity, and accountability.

title: Solutions Engineer
description: Grafana Labs is a remote-first, open-source powerhouse. There are more than 20M users of Grafana, the open source visualization tool, around the globe, monitoring everything from beehives to climate change in the Alps. The instantly recognizable dashboards have been spotted everywhere from a NASA launch and Minecraft HQ to Wimbledon and the Tour de France. Grafana Labs also helps more than 3,000 companies -- including Bloomberg, JPMorgan Chase, and eBay -- manage their observability strategies with the Grafana LGTM Stack, which can be run fully managed with Grafana Cloud or self-managed with the Grafana Enterprise Stack, both featuring scalable metrics (Grafana Mimir), logs (Grafana Loki), and traces (Grafana Tempo). We’re scaling fast and staying true to what makes us different: an open-source legacy, a global collaborative culture, and a passion for meaningful work. Our team thrives in an innovation-driven environment where transparency, autonomy, and trust fuel everything we do. You may not meet every requirement, and that’s okay. If this role excites you, we’d love you to raise your hand for what could be a truly career-defining opportunity. The Opportunity Our Commercial Solutions Engineering team are our in-house, customer-facing product experts. The Commercial SE team enables Grafana Labs worldwide growth by educating potential and existing customers to ensure they are happy and successful. We share our technical and product expertise with customers through demos, hands-on enablement, presentations, technical evaluations and ongoing interaction. We also partner closely with our Sales team to help qualify and close opportunities. The Commercial SE team is also one of the primary routes of communication from our customers through into the Product Management and Engineering teams. What You'll Be Doing Joining a high growth team working together to solve technical and customer problems the right way Partner with our Sales team to articulate the overall Grafana value proposition, vision, and strategy to customers Own the technical engagement with customers and help to close high-velocity opportunities through advanced competitive knowledge, technical skill, and credibility Deliver product and technical presentations to potential and existing customers Proactively communicate with customers and internal teams to provide a feedback loop on our products and the competitive landscape Drive product conversations based on need and problems learned during customer interactions Work with the team to enhance documentation, write blog posts, record videos, contribute knowledge base articles, and create other public and internal enablement material What Makes You a Great Fit We are not necessarily looking for people with previous solutions/sales engineering experience! We are building a team with diverse experiences and backgrounds so if you are up for a challenge and looking to make a pivot in your career, we’d love to hear from you! Your job duties will be varied and complex and will require strong judgment, collaboration, and leadership We are a remote-first company so you would need to learn to work and collaborate with an international team You will need first class written and oral communication skills to collaborate with our remote-first internal teams and with our worldwide customers. You will need to be able to skillfully articulate our value proposition and the technical advantages of our products You will love solving technical challenges and thrive on bringing creative solutions to our customers You should have a technical mindset and a desire to grow technically. Great candidates may be a recent grad of a coding bootcamp or have previous post-sales or engineering experience–we are excited to see what unique experiences and skill sets you bring to the table! You are self-motivated, initiative, creative, and maybe you’ve already done a brave thing or two in your life! Familiarity and hands on experience with OSS project & SRE / Devops practices Must be located in Singapore Must have Permanent Residency or Citizenship. Bonus Points For Experience with Open Source technologies, or in the Metrics/Monitoring space is an advantage Experience with sales methodologies like MEDD(P)ICC is a plus! Compensation ranges are country specific. If you are applying for this role from a different location than listed above, your recruiter will discuss your specific market’s defined pay range & benefits at the beginning of the process. Compensation ranges are country specific. If you are applying for this role from a different location than listed above, your recruiter will discuss your specific market’s defined pay range & benefits at the beginning of the process. Why You’ll Thrive At Grafana Labs 100% Remote, Global Culture - As a remote-only company, we bring together talent from around the world, united by a culture of collaboration and shared purpose. Scaling Organization – Tackle meaningful work in a high-growth, ever-evolving environment. Transparent Communication – Expect open decision-making and regular company-wide updates. Innovation-Driven – Autonomy and support to ship great work and try new things. Open Source Roots – Built on community-driven values that shape how we work. Empowered Teams – High trust, low ego culture that values outcomes over optics. Career Growth Pathways – Defined opportunities to grow and develop your career. Approachable Leadership – Transparent execs who are involved, visible, and human. Passionate People – Join a team of smart, supportive folks who care deeply about what they do. In-Person onboarding - We want you to thrive from day 1 with your fellow new ‘Grafanistas’ to learn all about what we do and how we do it. Balance is Key - We operate a global annual leave policy of 30 days per annum. 3 days of your annual leave entitlement are reserved for Grafana Shutdown Days to allow the team to really disconnect. *We will comply with local legislation where applicable. Equal Opportunity Employer: We will recruit, train, compensate and promote regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, disability, age, veteran status, and all the other fascinating characteristics that make us different and unique. title: Geospatial Data Engineering
description: About the Role At Kyndryl, you play a key role in shaping how organisations use geospatial intelligence at scale. If you’re excited about designing enterprise‑grade GIS platforms, building modern geospatial applications, and working with advanced spatial analytics, this role gives you the depth, complexity, and career runway to grow into a true GIS expert or architect. Here, you won’t just maintain maps or process data—you’ll design, automate, and modernise full geospatial ecosystems for large enterprises across Singapore and the region. What You Will Do 1) Geospatial Data Engineering You’ll shape how spatial data is structured and managed across the organisation. You’ll: Design and maintain enterprise geodatabases (file/enterprise geodatabases, feature classes, domains, subtypes, topology rules). Build ETL/ELT pipelines for vector, raster, and imagery using ArcGIS Data Interoperability, ArcGIS API for Python, ModelBuilder, or FME. Implement data quality checks, metadata standards, schema versioning, and coordinate system workflows. 2) Services, Applications & Web GIS You’ll create the tools and experiences that users interact with every day. You’ll: Publish and optimise map, feature, and image services on ArcGIS Server. Manage Portal for ArcGIS content, groups, permissions, and sharing models. Build web GIS applications using Experience Builder, Web AppBuilder, Dashboards, Field Maps, Survey123, and StoryMaps. Develop custom web applications using ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript, ArcGIS REST API, and modern web frameworks. 3) Spatial Analytics & Modeling You turn raw data into actionable insights. You’ll: Execute advanced analysis (network, proximity, spatial statistics, suitability, 3D/scene layers) in ArcGIS Pro. Use GeoAnalytics, Raster/Spatial Analyst, Image Analyst, and Notebooks for large-scale analytics. Build repeatable geoprocessing models, notebooks, and scheduled jobs for production analytics. 4) Automation & Integration You ensure the GIS ecosystem is efficient, scalable, and well‑connected. You’ll: Automate admin tasks with ArcGIS API for Python (users, services, content, backups, monitoring). Integrate ArcGIS with enterprise platforms like SQL Server, PostgreSQL/PostGIS, ERP/CRM, data lakes, or APIs. Support real-time/event-driven GIS using GeoEvent Server, streaming services, and IoT feeds. 5) Platform Operations & Security You help keep the GIS platform reliable, secure, and high‑performing. You’ll: Support ArcGIS Enterprise deployments (Windows/Linux, on‑prem, Azure, AWS). Monitor caching, indexing, performance tuning, and ArcGIS Online credit usage. Enforce governance for roles, permissions, SSO/Entra ID, and security policies. Why Join Kyndryl? You’ll be part of a global technology services organisation driving digital transformation across Singapore’s key industries. You’ll get: Opportunities to work on meaningful national-scale or enterprise projects Exposure to cloud, automation, security, and data engineering A culture that values engineering depth, innovation, and continuous learning A supportive environment where your ideas shape the GIS landscape Required Skills & Experience Minimum 5+ years of relevant hands‑on experience in geospatial engineering, GIS development, or enterprise GIS platform management Strong hands‑on experience with ArcGIS Enterprise , ArcGIS Pro , and core Esri geospatial technologies Solid understanding of enterprise geodatabases (e.g., SQL Server, PostgreSQL/PostGIS), spatial data models, and coordinate systems Practical experience with Python for geospatial automation, scripting, or administrative tasks Experience working with web GIS tools or applications (e.g., Portal/ArcGIS Online, Web GIS app builders) Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret, process, and validate complex geospatial datasets Familiarity with GIS system administration concepts such as roles/permissions, security policies, or platform performance tuning Ability to work collaboratively in a technical environment and communicate effectively with non‑technical stakeholders Preferred Skills & Experience Experience with ETL tools such as FME, ArcGIS Data Interoperability, or other data integration platforms Knowledge of ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript or modern frontend frameworks for building custom GIS applications Exposure to GeoEvent Server , real‑time data streams, or IoT‑driven GIS workflows Experience deploying GIS solutions in cloud environments (Azure, AWS, Kubernetes) Familiarity with spatial analytics extensions such as GeoAnalytics, Spatial Analyst, Image Analyst, or Raster analytics Understanding of enterprise integrations with ERP/CRM systems , data lakes, or REST‑based APIs Professional certifications in geospatial technologies, cloud platforms, or data engineering

title: Principal FPGA Verification Engineer
description: Bring more to life. Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology? At Leica Microsystems, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact. You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life. Shape the Future with Us! At Leica Microsystems, we have been shaping the future for over 175 years with groundbreaking optical and digital solutions.  With a culture rooted in customer focus, innovation, and teamwork, we lead the market in microscopy, imaging, and analysis, unveiling the invisible and empowering our customers to build a better, healthier world. Joining Leica Microsystems means contributing to scientific discoveries and supporting surgeons in making critical decisions. Our advanced microscopes and AI-based image analysis solutions enable users to gain profound insights into development and engineering challenges. Here, you will work on meaningful projects alongside passionate colleagues, driving progress and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible. The Principal FPGA Verification Engineer is responsible for leading the development and implementation of comprehensive testing procedures and protocols for FPGA-based systems. This role focuses on verifying and validating the performance, functionality, and integrity of FPGA designs for advanced microscopy products. The successful candidate will leverage their expertise to develop effective verification and test solutions, collaborating closely with the Research and Development team to identify and address issues throughout the product development cycle. Additionally, the engineer will work with cross-functional teams—including Operations, Supply Chain, Quality, and Logistics—to ensure that product quality, reliability, and robustness meet established standards and exceed customer expectations. This position reports to the Verification Manager and is part of the Research & Development Medical located in Singapore and will be an on-site role. In this role, you will have the opportunity to: Verification Plan and Report Development: Create detailed verification plans, strategies, test cases, and reports to evaluate FPGA functionality, performance, and compliance with project requirements. Provide clear, concise reports with insights and recommendations for improvement. Verification and Validation: Perform comprehensive verification of FPGA designs using various testing tools and equipment to ensure compliance with specified criteria such as speed, reliability, and power consumption. Issue Identification and Debugging: Identify, record, and diagnose issues in FPGA designs, ensuring thorough documentation and efficient resolution in collaboration with the development team. Automated Testing: Develop and implement automated test procedures to enhance testing efficiency and accuracy. Continuous Improvement and Quality Assurance: Oversee continuous improvement in FPGA design verification and validation processes, contribute to quality assurance by evaluating designs against industry standards, and collaborate with cross-functional teams for seamless integration. The essential requirements of the job include Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related field, with strong knowledge of FPGA technologies, FPGA development tools (Xilinx, Altera, etc.), and FPGA testing methodologies. Proficiency in FPGA testing equipment and software, ensuring accurate and efficient verification processes. Experience with scripting and programming languages for test automation, such as Python, VHDL, and Verilog. Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail, with the ability to diagnose and resolve complex technical issues. Preferred background in the Medical Device industry, bringing familiarity with regulatory and quality standards. Leica Microsystems, a Danaher operating company, offers a broad array of comprehensive, competitive benefit programs that add value to our lives. Whether it’s a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job. Check out our benefits at Danaher Benefits Info .

title: Geotechnical Design Engineer
description: We are looking for a Geotechnical Design Engineer with experience in geotechnical design, analysis and writing technical proposals. The Geotechnical Design Engineer should be knowledgeable in basic soil mechanics, as well as experienced in geotechnical analysis and design software. Reporting to the Design Manager, you will have the opportunity to collaborate with the Business Development Team to provide design support for tenders. You will be expected to review tender specifications, requirements and draft the technical proposals. Likewise, you will be working closely with the Operations team to perform design-related coordination work, analysing geotechnical issues at project sites, and preparing detailed drawings for the site team. To be successful as a Geotechnical Design Engineer, you should be independent, demonstrate strong analytical thinking and be able to have prompt, open and direct communications with internal and external stakeholders to achieve project expectations. Outstanding candidates should be meticulous, detail oriented, demonstrate willingness to learn and display a positive attitude with a drive for results. Think you’ve got what it takes? A day in the life of Geotechnical Design Engineer Design support for tenders : Review and summarize soil condition reports, tender specifications and requirements. Identify and summarise geotechnical problems and technical risk. Analyse geotechnical problems and draft technical proposal. Design-related coordination work for ongoing projects : Perform analysis and design in accordance with current legislation, standards and codes of practice Prepare detailed construction drawings with the assistance of draughtsperson. Conduct regular site visits to integrate design concept and actual ground conditions. Quality Control (QC) review and verification between design assumptions and actual site conditions. Carry out geotechnical analysis and design using tools such as Slope/W and Plaxis as well as Keller’s in-house software. Conduct back analysis and research for future improvements based on actual performance and as-built data. Prepare technical proposal for remedial work during construction when required. Liaise with clients/consultants/ sub-contractors on technical related issues. Note: This role requires candidates to perform regular site walks at outdoor construction sites You will need: A professional degree in Civil or Geotechnical Engineering 1-3 years of relevant working experience in Geotechnical Engineering Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Powerpoint), AutoCAD, Plaxis 2D and Plaxis 3D A valid Supervise Construction Work for WSH certificate (preferable) What we offer: Annual Leave, Medical Insurance, Medical Leave, Variable Bonus, Learning & Development Opportunities, Referral Rewards & Employee Assistance Programme Global & Regional networking opportunities Friendly team and enjoyable working environment We encourage applications from the right candidates regardless of age, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, belief or race. Learn more about Keller's Inclusion Commitment at https://www.keller.com/how-we-work/diversity-equity-and-inclusion Find out more here: https://www.keller.com/

title: Research Fellow / Engineer (Food Engineering) - AZN5
description: As a University of Applied Learning, SIT works closely with industry in our research pursuits. Our research staff will have the opportunity to be equipped with applied research skill sets that are relevant to industry demands while working on research projects in SIT. The primary purpose of this role is to deliver on a collaborative research project, Proteins for Singapore (P4SG), where you will be part of the research team to establish and understand the structuration of algae/soybean/mung bean proteins for high quality alternative protein foods including through high moisture extrusion. Key responsibilities will include: Explore innovative methods for food process optimization including the use of AI and machine-learning Develop and execute methods for characterizing and linking the texture and structure of alternative protein foods Design, execute, and interpret experiments independently. Participate in and manage the research project with Principal Investigators (PI), and the research team members to ensure all project deliverables including periodic progress reports are met. Author papers in high-quality journals and contribute to grant writing in areas that are related to the research. Carry out Risk Assessment, and ensure compliance with Work, Safety and Health Regulations. Coordinate procurement and liaison with vendors/suppliers. Work independently, as well as manage a team of junior researchers and students to ensure proper operation and maintenance of equipment. To communicate and liaise with any internal and external stakeholders to ensure project deliverables are met. Any other ad-hoc duties assigned by supervisor. Ideal qualities of the candidate include: Able to build and maintain strong working relationships with people within and external to the university. Self-directed learner who believes in continuous learning and development Highly proficient in technical writing and presentation Strong analytical and critical thinking skills Strong initiative and takes ownership of work The candidate should have relevant competence in the areas of processing technology, physical and structural analysis, material formulation, and mathmatical modeling. The candidate should also have a PhD or other advanced degree in a food or engineering related field. Knowledge of food formulation, sensory analysis, extrusion technology, machine learning, and rheology will be advantageous.
