title: Chief Commercial Officer (International Education)
description: About the Role This is a newly created Executive Leadership position with a strong regional growth mandate, with particular emphasis on the China and ASEAN markets. As SagePaths Global Education continues its expansion across Asia, the Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) will play a pivotal role in shaping the Group’s commercial strategy, driving sustainable revenue growth, and strengthening its international education ecosystem. The CCO will be responsible for building a scalable, data-driven, and high-performance commercial framework that integrates student recruitment, admissions, marketing, partnerships, and digital systems across all business units. This role is instrumental in translating strategic vision into commercial success and long-term market leadership. About SagePaths Global Education SagePaths Global Education is a leading regional education group headquartered in Singapore, operating a diversified portfolio of education and education-related businesses. Our brands include SagePaths Global Education, Singapore Education Development Centre, SagePartners Business Consultancy, SOVO Student Apartment, Pickering Institute, Pickering Global Campus, as well as a growing network of Overseas Teaching Centres across China. We specialise in building a fully integrated education ecosystem that supports international students across their entire academic journey, including: International student placement and recruitment Cross-business services centred around international students Student accommodation development and operations Academic pathways and university articulation programmes Foundation and Diploma programmes Online and blended education delivery Transnational education and cross-border programme delivery Our vision is to build a globally connected education ecosystem that empowers students through high-quality, accessible, and future-oriented learning pathways. We work closely with universities, private education institutions, and overseas partners to deliver well-recognised academic programmes and meaningful international collaboration opportunities. As we enter our next phase of strategic development, we are strengthening our Executive Leadership Team to support rapid regional expansion and organisational scaling. The Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) will be a core member of this leadership group, working directly with the Managing Director and the Board to lead the Group’s commercial transformation, revenue growth strategy, and international market expansion. This is a rare opportunity to shape the commercial architecture of a fast-growing international education group and to make a lasting impact on its regional and global footprint. Key Responsibilities Reporting directly to the Managing Director, the Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) will lead the Group’s end-to-end commercial strategy, with full responsibility across: Marketing, branding, and corporate communications for all SagePaths and Pickering brands Student recruitment strategy, sales performance management, admissions operations, and conversion optimisation Agent network development, channel management, and strategic partnerships (both domestic and international) Commercial systems architecture, including CRM, websites, digital marketing platforms, and data analytics infrastructure Pricing strategy, revenue diversification initiatives, and market expansion planning The role carries executive accountability to: Drive sustainable enrolment growth and strong revenue performance across both onshore and offshore markets Build a predictable, data-driven recruitment pipeline supported by disciplined sales governance and reporting structures Optimise admissions workflows, conversion rates, and overall student acquisition efficiency Lead CRM and digital platform integration to deliver a seamless, consistent, and high-quality student recruitment experience Strengthen commercial compliance with Singapore PEI regulations (SSG, EduTrust), overseas regulatory frameworks, and partner contractual obligations Champion an exceptional student experience from first engagement through to enrolment, consistently applied across all business units Develop and execute a customer-centric, brand-driven marketing strategy aligned with SagePaths’ premium and professional positioning Build, manage, and scale strategic partnerships with universities, private education institutions, overseas schools, and pathway providers Establish scalable commercial processes, structures, and performance frameworks that support rapid regional and international growth The Successful Candidate As a key member of the Executive Leadership Team, the CCO will work closely with the Managing Director, Executive Dean, and Operations to translate strategic direction into measurable and sustainable commercial performance. We are seeking a commercially astute, internationally minded leader with: A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Commerce, or a related discipline; postgraduate qualifications are strongly preferred 10 years of senior commercial leadership experience, MUST within international education, transnational education, or higher education services Bilingual proficiency in English and Mandarin Chinese is essential , with the ability to conduct professional-level communication, negotiations, and relationship management with business partners, universities, and partner institutions across Chin Proven experience leading large-scale student recruitment, admissions, and marketing operations across multiple markets Deep understanding of international education ecosystems, student mobility trends, agent networks, and education partnerships Strong familiarity with compliance frameworks, including Singapore PEI / EduTrust requirements and international regulatory standards A strong track record in building, scaling, and optimising CRM systems, digital marketing platforms, and performance dashboards Experience integrating CRM with LMS, finance, admissions, and student management systems A strategic mindset combined with strong execution capability and operational discipline Exceptional leadership skills with the ability to build high-performing, values-driven teams across marketing, recruitment, and admissions Comfort and effectiveness operating within a multi-country, multi-brand, and fast-growth environment Regular travel across Singapore, China, and Southeast Asia will be required to support regional operations and partnerships.

title: Research Fellow (Operations Research & Analytics)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description Role Summary: Lead and manage multi-stakeholder research projects in operations research, analytics, and optimization, including collaborations with NTUC FairPrice (Plug-in Flow Routing Optimization Engine; Store Friendly replenishment packing) and PSA (data analysis to improve efficiency in the container logistics ecosystem), as well as other industry and academic projects. Drive end-to-end project delivery—from problem framing and model formulation to implementation, evaluation, and dissemination—while coordinating milestones and deliverables with collaborators. Key Responsibilities: Act as project manager for assigned projects, developing work plans, timelines, risk registers, and progress reports; ensure on-time delivery of milestones and industry-facing deliverables. Work closely with external collaborators (e.g., NTUC FairPrice, Store Friendly, PSA) and internal stakeholders to define objectives, align expectations, and translate operational needs into rigorous research questions. Design and formulate models for routing, packing, replenishment, scheduling, and network optimization; develop algorithms and decision-support tools using appropriate techniques (e.g., mathematical programming, stochastic optimization, simulation, machine learning). Lead data acquisition, cleaning, integration, and analysis; establish data pipelines and quality assurance processes; conduct experiments, benchmarking, and sensitivity analyses to validate models. Prototype and, where applicable, support pilot deployment of solutions; document methodologies, assumptions, reproducible workflows, and user guides. Prepare high-quality publications, technical reports, and presentations; contribute to dissemination through conferences, seminars, and industry briefings. Guide and mentor PhD students, research assistants, and interns; provide technical supervision, review work outputs, and foster a collaborative research culture. Coordinate compliance with NUS and partner data governance, ethics, and security requirements; manage project documentation and IP considerations in line with agreements. Contribute to grant proposals, project scoping, and resource planning; assist with budgeting and procurement related to project needs. Support broader IORA initiatives, including knowledge sharing, seminar organization, and collaborations across the University and with external partners Requirements PhD in Industrial Engineering/Operations Research/Management Science (or closely related), with a strong research record in stochastic modeling and optimization. Hands-on experience solving real-world routing, packing, replenishment, scheduling, and network optimization problems. Proficiency with large-scale decision methods (e.g., approximate dynamic programming/rollout, primal–dual or penalty controls, Lagrangian decomposition/index policies, queueing/fluid models). Strong programming skills in Python and/or Java (MATLAB a plus), with experience in data pipelines, simulation/digital twins, and reproducible experimentation. Proven project management and industry collaboration experience, delivering milestones and translating operational needs into deployable solutions. Strong communication and mentorship: publications, technical reporting, presentations, and guidance of PhD students/RAs.

Mandatory Skills Description: • Experience in a Hedge Fund organization is must • 10+ years of experience as a Business Analyst working on Financial products with strong understanding of listed and OTC products (equities, futures, options, fixed income, FX), order lifecycle, and risk/PnL concepts. • Excellent analytical skills, understanding of project life cycles, and ability to act in an entrepreneurial manner to ensure the requirements from the business are gathered in the appropriate manner. • Proven analytical, planning, problem solving and decision-making skills • Comfortable with direct communication with all levels of technical and business resources • Excellent organizational and time management skills with the ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously • Experience with working in a highly collaborative and dynamic multi-team environment • Experience in writing user stories and working with JIRA /similar tools. title: Layout Engineer
description:
Job Description: You will be responsible for developing the physical layout of CMOS/BiCDMOS Integrated Circuit designs used in high-performance, high-speed analog ICs for SerDes applications, as well as for Automotive and Industrial Battery Management Systems. Key responsiblities: Design high-quality block and top block-level IC layouts for analog, mixed-signal, and high-speed interface
circuits Ensure that designs comply with foundry-specific rule like the DRC and LVS and support post-layout extraction and debugging Apply good layout techniques to meet matching, signal integrity, reliability requirements and good post layout performance Apply the best practices for 28nm, 22nm planar CMOS and FinFET technologies, including device placement, routing constraints and process-specific rules Collaborate closely with circuit designers and layout designers to optimize layout performance Contribute to layout methodology improvements, automation scripts and documentation Prepare and maintain accurate documentation of layout output, verification result judgement and explain them during design review Requirements : Bachelor’s Degree in Electronics/ Electrical Engineering or Diploma with relevant experience Good knowledge in IC layout design, verification and design rule checking Proficiency in IC design software, such as Cadence Virtuoso, Synopsys IC Compiler and Mentor Graphics Calibre Strong understanding of CMOS design principles, semiconductor manufacturing processes and design-for-manufacturability techniques Good problem-solving skills, attention to detail and capability to work effectively in a team environment with good communication skills Experience with 28nm, 22nm planar CMOS and FinFET technologies will be a plus 2-5 years of analog IC layout design experience will be a plus. Fresh graduates from semiconductor(IC) design
related course are also welcome to apply.

Coordinator is responsible for coordinating all Mechanical & Electrical

compliance with approved drawings, specifications, and statutory requirements

Coordination & Supervision Coordinate site works with site engineers, supervisors,

     interfaces. 4.
Programme & Progress Control Coordinate M&E work schedules in line with overall project

     risks. Assist in planning manpower and material delivery for M&E
     works. 5.
Quality & Compliance Ensure M&E works comply with approved drawings,

Testing, Commissioning & Handover Support Coordinate testing and commissioning (T&C) activities

     handover documentation . Requirements
& Qualifications Diploma or Degree in Mechanical, Electrical, or Building

title: Assistant Engineering Manager
description: Assistant Engineering Manager About this opportunity We are seeking an Assistant Engineering Manager to support our Engineering Manager in delivering exceptional facility operations and maintenance services. This role offers the opportunity to work with cutting-edge building systems while developing technical and leadership skills in a dynamic commercial real estate environment. What this job involves Supporting daily operations, You'll assist in overseeing building operations including HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and life safety systems. Your role involves monitoring building performance, coordinating maintenance activities, and ensuring continuous operation to maintain optimal tenant experience. Driving maintenance excellence, You'll support preventive and corrective maintenance programs, working closely with contractors and service providers to schedule repairs and upgrades. You'll help track maintenance costs and vendor performance to ensure efficient operations. Managing finance compliance, You’ll assist in supervising purchase recommendation submission. You’ll help to ensure finance tracker are kept up to date. You’ll assist in Ensuring all purchasing activities follow company policies and legal regulations while assessing risks like supplier instability. Ensuring compliance standards, You'll assist in maintaining building systems compliance with local codes, regulations, and safety standards. This includes supporting inspections, certifications, and regulatory reporting to protect occupants and assets. Managing improvement projects, You'll help plan and execute building enhancement projects, coordinating with contractors, vendors, and internal stakeholders. Your role ensures projects are delivered on time, within budget, and to JLL's quality standards. Developing our people, You'll support the training and development of maintenance staff, assist in performance management, and help maintain our high service delivery standards that clients expect from JLL. What we're looking for Technical foundation Diploma in Facilities Management, Building Engineering, or related technical field with minimum 5 years of experience in facilities management, building operations, or engineering services. Core competencies Strong technical knowledge of building systems and equipment, excellent communication and organizational abilities, proficiency in computerized maintenance management systems (CMMS), and proven problem-solving skills with attention to detail. Leadership potential Basic project management capabilities and ability to support team development initiatives while maintaining JLL's commitment to service excellence. What's in it for you Join JLL's dynamic engineering team and contribute to maintaining world-class commercial properties while developing your career in facilities management. This role offers growth opportunities, technical challenges, and the chance to work with industry-leading building systems and technologies.

title: Engineer – Marine and Reclamation Engineering
description: Job Summary: We are seeking a skilled and motivated Engineer with specialized experience in Marine & Reclamation project management and technology. This role is critical in providing technical expertise to support our Market Development and Project Management initiatives. The successful candidate will contribute to winning new business and ensuring the successful technical delivery of complex coastal and reclamation projects. Key Responsibilities (Job Description): 1. Technical Support for Market Development: Ø  Assist the business development teams by providing technical input for proposals, tenders, and client presentations related to marine and reclamation projects. Ø  Participate in client meetings and site visits to understand project needs and articulate technical solutions. Ø  Contribute to feasibility studies, preliminary designs, and cost estimations for new project opportunities. 2. Project Engineering & Delivery Support: Ø  Provide engineering design, analysis, and technical oversight for marine structures (e.g., seawalls, breakwaters, jetties) and land reclamation projects. Ø  Prepare and review technical drawings, specifications, calculations, and reports. Ø  Support project managers in planning, coordination, and monitoring of project phases (design, procurement, construction, supervision). Ø  Conduct site inspections and liaise with contractors to ensure works adhere to design specifications, quality standards, and safety regulations. 3. Design & Analysis: Ø  Perform engineering analyses related to coastal processes, hydrodynamics, sediment transport, geotechnical stability, and foundation design for reclamation areas. Ø  Utilize engineering software for modeling and design tasks. 4. Documentation & Compliance: Ø  Ensure all engineering deliverables comply with relevant national and international codes, standards, and environmental guidelines. Ø  Maintain accurate project documentation and assist in the preparation of progress and final reports. 5. Collaboration: Ø  Work closely with senior engineers, project managers, and multidisciplinary teams (geotechnical, environmental, planning). Requirements: 1.  Education & Certification: Ø  Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering, Coastal Engineering, Ocean Engineering, Geotechnical Engineering, or a directly related field. A Master’s degree is advantageous. Ø  Progress towards or holding a relevant professional engineering license/certification (e.g., EIT, PE, or international equivalent) is preferred. 2.  Experience: Ø  Minimum of 5 years of direct hands-on experience in the engineering design and/or project delivery of marine and reclamation projects. Ø  Demonstrated involvement in at least one major land reclamation project is highly desirable. Ø  Experience in a consulting or contractor environment is preferred. 3.  Technical Skills: Ø  Solid understanding of marine and reclamation engineering principles, including dredging, ground improvement, coastal protection, and marine structures. Ø  Proficiency with relevant engineering software (e.g., AutoCAD, Civil 3D, GIS tools, hydrodynamic or geotechnical modeling software). Ø  Familiarity with project management tools and methodologies. 4.  Soft Skills & Competencies: Ø  Strong technical writing and verbal communication skills in English. Ø  Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities. Ø  Proactive team player with the ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. Ø  Client-focused attitude and commercial awareness. 5.  Other: Ø  Willingness to travel to project sites and client offices as required. Ø  Must be eligible to work in the specified location.

title: Cloud Solution Architect - Copilot AI Business Solutions
description: Overview The Customer Success Architect (CSA) for AI Business Solutions is responsible for enabling Microsoft’s enterprise customers to achieve business transformation through AI-powered solutions. Operating within the Customer Success Unit (CSU), the CSA plays a strategic role in leading technical engagements across AI Workforce and AI Business Process portfolios. This includes designing and implementing secure, scalable, and resilient architectures using Microsoft technologies such as Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot Studio, and Power Platform. The CSA guides customers through the entire Copilot journey, from envisioning to deployment and adoption, applying value-based delivery frameworks and Success by Design methodologies. In this capacity, the CSA serves as a trusted advisor and advocate for customers and partners, translating feedback and insights into actionable solutions that align with industry best practices. Beyond technical delivery, the CSA acts as a business strategist and customer advocate, translating feedback and insights into actionable solution designs that align with industry trends and customer goals. The role demands strong business value literacy, the ability to articulate ROI and transformation impact, and experience in change management and customer success planning. The CSA identifies opportunities to drive consumption and usage growth, ensures operational excellence, and collaborates across engineering, field, and partner teams to scale delivery and support unified customer success. This position is recognized for technical excellence, innovation, business strategy, and customer obsession. Responsibilities Operates according to required operational excellence and proven practice standards throughout all sales stages/activities/tools of record (e.g., pipeline updates, time tracking). Collaborates across Microsoft and customer/partner teams through on-strategy delivery to achieve customer/partner objectives and increase customer/partner satisfaction. Proactively manages business and technical risks, adapts methodology and applies governance principles to identify, communicate, and minimize business and technical risks. Executes work in compliance with industry and Microsoft guidelines and procedures. Continuously prioritizes among competing demands in their work and identifies where impact occurs with customers, ensuring alignment with business priorities and goals. Delivers solutions in line with company methodologies (e.g., product offerings such as value-based deliverables [VBDs], advice, training, technical validation), ensuring proven practices and patterns are followed to prepare customers for operational readiness and achievement of their business goals and targets. Leveraging knowledge of change management proven practices and/or involving the change management team, proactively helps the customer deploy for long-term organizational adoption to increase customer satisfaction and drive consumption/usage. Guides customers/partners towards a well-architected (e.g., secure, resilient, artificial intelligence [AI]-enabled), and cost- and performance-optimized solution to increase retention and expansion opportunities. Provides feedback to Unified Delivery Team on VBDs to refine and further develop content. Proactively identifies new opportunities for Consumption, Usage & Unified expansion (especially Enhanced Solutions) at scale based on business value to customer/partner and clear understanding of the Microsoft value proposition for supported platforms to empower cloud success, foster and strengthen security and resiliency, and drive AI innovation. Actively listens to and respectfully challenges customers/partners, building trust to then advocate for alternative architectures/solutions/approaches that shape and/or enhance customer requirements. Identifies, and evaluates industry trends (e.g., customer industry verticals, information technology [IT] industry), gathers customer/partner insights (e.g., feedback around technical preferences, environments, business needs, competitive landscape), and maps existing architecture and digital transformation solutions to customer/partner business outcomes. Proactively captures opportunities in appropriate systems, processes, and tools, working collaboratively across the organization to execute on opportunities aligned with Microsoft's Customer Engagement Model. Proactively identifies, escalates, and mitigates blockers using appropriate tools and processes to accelerate solution deployment, value realization and usage/consumption. Applies business, technical, industry, and/or enterprise knowledge to architecture or support projects to meet business and information technology (IT) requirements and resolve identified constraints. Customer Centricity Proactively acts as the voice of the customer/partner, sharing ideas, feedback, insights, success stories and strategic/technical input with Engineering teams, Product Offerings teams and internal communities leveraging relevant insights from feedback tools and systems. Identifies patterns of feedback across customers/partners/territories/industries and leverages them to develop strategic and actionable insights. Presents business cases to program managers to influence product roadmaps, decision making, bug fix prioritization, and own and drive initiatives as appropriate. Identifies and/or translates customer/partner problems into industry solutions aligned with Microsoft product and platform strategy, and explains why and/or how they meet customer/partner outcomes and return on investment (ROI) goals (e.g., via proof of concept, minimally viable product [MVP], rapid prototype) relative to competitive offerings. Proactively helps the customer/partner adopt and use Microsoft product/platform strategy-aligned (cross-solution area) solutions. Builds relationships with technical decision makers (TDMs), and helps build the bridge between TDMs and business decision makers (BDMs). Independently interacts as needed with customers/partners' Chief Information Officer (CIO), Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) and other C-level (CXO) roles to bridge understanding of security, compliance, operational and risk requirements across BDMs, TDMs, CIO and CISO teams, appropriately engaging additional subject matter experts when deeper expertise is required. Drives self and guides other team members to focus on customer/partner experience through efficient delivery and ensuring a seamless and connected customer experience. Defines customer/partner conditions of success by leveraging an understanding of customer goals. Anticipates and addresses customer/partner dissatisfaction and unmet needs, and executes strategies to improve customer experience, value realization, and acceleration of transformation. Identifies and prioritizes several opportunities that align with revenue goals, and orchestrates growth of solution utilization pipeline with partners. Provides assistance with partner and sales teams to bring clarity to specific opportunities through proof of concept and technical pre-sales support. Technical Leadership Leverages market insights and demand signals to assist leadership in identifying relevant areas in which to drive up-skilling and/or accreditations based on demand. Role models technical readiness, both depth-aligned to solution area priorities and breadth-aligned to Customer Success Unit (CSU)/corporate initiatives (e.g., security, resilience, AI), and influences team to drive their own technical readiness. Acts as a mentor, leading readiness and upskilling activities in the team/organization by educating colleagues on technical and non-technical concepts and sharing proven practices. Proactively identifies gaps through delivery, communicates those gaps to relevant team members and internal/external stakeholders, and connects gaps and patterns across business and technology areas that drive changes and improvements to products, IP (both existing and new), technologies, and/or processes/practices that enable solutions to scale across customers. Contributes to the development and modification of Microsoft's structured frameworks and methodologies. Provides thought leadership and innovation to customers/partners and internal communities at the local level. Contributes to leading virtual teams (v-teams) around technologies and customer/partner challenges, and shares ideas, insights, and strategic, technical input with technical teams, internal communities across the field, and the larger virtual team across Microsoft using knowledge of Microsoft architectures and their context in the competitive landscape. Demonstrates strong industry knowledge and increases recognition for Microsoft solutions by contributing to and sometimes leading presentations and engagements with external and internal audiences (e.g., Tech Connect, Build, Ignite) Contributes to intellectual property (IP) and identifies patterns where no IP exists (e.g., CoPilot and AI scenarios) to help build scalable and repeatable models. Participates in external technical and non-technical community events (e.g., conferences, seminars, technical meetups, webcasts, blogs, hackathons) that elevate the Microsoft brand and shares learnings across internal teams Qualifications Driving Customer Outcomes Understanding and mapping customer requirements to the company's offerings effectively. Identifying opportunities, translating customer needs into tailored solutions, ensuring that products or services precisely meet customer expectations, resulting in high levels of satisfaction and success. Leading with Technical Intensity Being the trusted technical advisor to the customer, prioritizing technical skills growth aligned with our cloud strategy, and infusing technical insight into helping the customer make the right business decisions. Technical Skills M365 Administration Assigning Copilot licenses Configuring SharePoint Advanced Management Managing Microsoft Graph connectors Ensuring data readiness for semantic search M365 Copilot and Copilot Studio No-code agent creation using natural language Connecting to SharePoint, Outlook, and Teams Power Platform integration REST API and custom connectors Event-driven workflows Data engineering and MLOps Responsible AI practices Conversational UX design Prompt engineering (for advanced scenarios) Security & Compliance Implementing Microsoft Purview for data governance Managing sensitivity labels and access controls Oversharing prevention and audit logging Understanding the Business Understanding the business means having a firm grasp of the Microsoft strategy, consumption, and support and connecting that to customer outcomes, using data to find new opportunities and demonstrate customer success, and leverage our internal systems to accurately capture the state of your customer. The ability to identify business needs and determine solutions to business problems; solutions may include a software-systems development component, process improvement, organizational change or strategic planning and policy development. The ability to develop and maintain positive working relationships with supervisors, staff, managers, customers, and vendors. This includes expressing empathy and compassion when dealing with the needs and problems of others, being approachable, taking time to address the concerns of coworkers, and treating others with respect and dignity The ability to interact confidently with senior leaders of the organization in order to present/defend/clarify concerns or issues regarding an existing project, program, or solution. This includes the ability to confidently address difficult questions, handle push back from a high-level audience, and maintain an executive demeanor while engaging in difficult or sometimes high-pressure situations. Required/minimum qualifications Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, Business, Liberal Arts, or related field AND 4+ years experience in cloud/infrastructure technologies, information technology (IT) consulting/support, systems administration, network operations, software development/support, technology solutions, practice development, architecture, and/or consulting OR equivalent experience. Additional or preferred qualifications Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, Business, Liberal Arts, or related field AND 8+ years experience in cloud/infrastructure technologies, information technology (IT) consulting/support, systems administration, network operations, software development/support, technology solutions, practice development, architecture, and/or consulting OR Master's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, Business,  or related field AND 6+ years experience in cloud/infrastructure technologies, technology solutions, practice development, architecture, and/or consulting OR equivalent experience. 4+ years experience working in a customer-facing role (e.g., internal and/or external). 4+ years experience working on technical projects. Technical Certification in Cloud (e.g., Azure, AI,  Power Platform, M365 and security certifications). Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. title: Full Stack Developer ( Junior )
description: About Us Innovate. Create. Elevate. At Vertis, we believe innovation starts with people. We design and deliver exceptional digital solutions that elevate our clients' brands and the experiences they create for their customers. Through technology-driven creativity, we enable meaningful business transformation. Our team is made up of capable, confident, and motivated individuals who collaborate, challenge ideas, and grow together. About the job We are looking for a passionate and motivated Junior Full Stack Developer to join our growing technology team. You will assist in building modern web applications using backend technologies such as Node.js and/or .NET, and frontend frameworks like React, Angular, or Vue.js. This is an excellent opportunity to grow your skills in full-stack development and cloud technologies in a collaborative, agile environment. Key Responsibilities Support the development and maintenance of backend services using Node.js and/or .NET. Assist in building interactive and responsive front-end interfaces using modern JavaScript frameworks (React, Angular, or Vue.js). Help integrate backend services with the frontend using RESTful APIs. Participate in deploying and maintaining applications in cloud platforms like AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud Platform. Collaborate with senior developers, designers, and other stakeholders to define and implement new features. Contribute to code reviews and documentation. Learn and apply industry best practices, coding standards, and agile methodologies. Troubleshoot and resolve bugs or issues in applications. Requirement and Qualification 1-3 years of experience in full-stack or web development. Fresh graduates are encouraged to apply. Basic understanding of Node.js and/or .NET development. title: Vocational Coach
description: Employer: Grace Orchard School Job Summary As a vocational coach, you will develop and deliver the Vocational Education curriculum and shape the continuum of work experience for students, aged between 13 and 18 years old, with mild intellectual disability and/or autism spectrum disorder. You will also work closely with the community and industry partners to provide tailored and meaningful on-the-job learning opportunities for our students and ensure their smooth transition to the workplace. Duties and Responsibilities A) Curriculum Teaching and Learning Develop and implement training plans for students on Work Experience covering the teaching of relevant soft and hard skills in alignment with the curriculum. Design and customise training and assessment materials and tools to support and enhance student learning and engagement. Monitor and track student’s progress and provide ongoing feedback for their improvement Review and improve the curriculum and implement to enhance student learning, experience and outcomes. B) Design and Implement the Continuum of Work Experience Design and implement a continuum of Work Experience for the different student profile(s). Identify and engage community and industry partners to create relevant and meaningful work-based opportunities to meet students’ strengths, interests and aspirations Collaborate with teachers, Allied Professionals, and community partners to co-develop skills development activities for students as part of the pre-placement preparation and training. Skills could include grooming and interview skills. Collaborate with teachers and Allied Professionals in co-planning and co-delivering the Vocational Education curriculum (i.e. vocational guidance, soft skills and hard skills) through the Continuum of Work Experience. Plan, design and implement work experiences in school, community and workplaces catering to students’ interests, strengths and working goals Work closely with community and industry partners to increase their understanding and support of our students’ learning and skills development in the workplace. Design and conduct pre-placement training and assessment. Liaise with work site personnel to put in place appropriate job accommodations and modifications according to students’ profiles and needs as well as requirement of the employers. Conduct regular assessment, monitoring and evaluation of students’ progress and performance at Work Experience using appropriate tools. C) Provide Vocational Coaching and Career Guidance Develop the Individual Transition Plan for students with their teachers, Allied Professionals and parents to ensure smooth transition from school to post-school employment or further education. Identify suitable work experience for students according to students’ interests and strengths in alignment to their Individual Transition Plan. Accompany and provide on-site coaching and instruction to students on Work Experience. Plan and conduct career preparation/awareness activities such as site visits, field trips, school-based / community-based work experience for students . Ensure students attain the requirement for the work experience attachment, including the attainment of the WSQ Food Hygiene SOA, as applicable. D) Communicate with Internal and External Stakeholders Share knowledge and insights on students’ interests, strengths and aspirations, and work progress, to the relevant stakeholders so as to garner support and facilitate effective coaching. Provide timely feedback on students’ progress, key areas of strengths and growth to families at suitable platforms. Forge and sustain strategic partnerships with volunteers, community and industry partners through creating awareness and capacity building. Advocate for opportunities for students to practice their skills in authentic settings E) Administration / Other duties Manage administrative matters such as insurance and allowance for students on work attachments and internships Carry out procurement of training materials and tools for the work experience programme. Organise and maintain proper documentation in the School Management System on student attendance, data and records in relation to vocational training and work experience for reporting and decision making. Contribute to the assigned committee/team/taskforce in an engaged and constructive manner Perform other duties as assigned Requirements Recognised Diploma (minimum) and above academic qualification Relevant training in special education ** At least 3 to 5 years of working with individuals with intellectual disabilities and or autism ** Prior experience in vocational training, assessment or placement and job support ** Effective communication, and able to build or maintain positive working relationship with internal and external partners Good organizational skills to ensure optimal utilization of resources for the vocational training and work experience programme **preferred but not compulsory

title: Workplace Safety and Health Officer
description: • Establish and maintain a comprehensive Safety, Health, and Environmental (SHE) system, including the formation of SHE committees, site inspections, and site safety management. • Implement and manage the electronic Permit to Work (ePTW) system as required by the client. • Develop and implement a Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) program for new workers, including identification systems for qualified personnel and operators. • Attend SHE coordination meetings, plan work sequences, identify incompatible works and risks/hazards, and develop site maps for delivery and storage improvements. • Handle near-miss, accident, and incident reporting, and propose remedial measures to prevent recurrence. • Prepare, maintain, and manage the Project Safety, Health, and Environment System (SHEMs). Prepare safety briefing/training material; Conduct SHE training sessions, safety orientations or induction courses, and Safety Time-Out (STO) briefings. • Perform SHE audits as required by the contract. Requirements: • Degree or Diploma in an Engineering discipline. • WSQ Specialist Diploma in Workplace Safety and Health or higher. • Registration: Ministry of Manpower (MoM) Registered Workplace Safety & Health Officer (at least 10 years post-registration with MoM) and Registered Environmental Control Officer (ECO) with NEA. • Relevant experience in railway projects (LTA, NEL, NSEWL, BPLRT) is preferred.

Bring passion and dedication to your job and there's no telling what you could accomplish. You will work with significant and dynamic projects in support of Apple's Retail business and will deliver innovative solutions and software. The worldwide Retail & Online Stores team is responsible for delivering the electronic commerce (eCommerce) systems and solutions that enable and support the best in class customer experience for Apple Online Store (AOS) and Apple Retail Stores- one of world’s largest retail businesses, serving millions of customers around the globe. The team is part of Apple’s Information & Systems (IS&T) division, and is based primarily in Sunnyvale, California with teams in Austin, TX, London, Singapore and Hyderabad India - working as a single global team. Description This role is for an ambitious senior project management professional who has several years experience of working in a dynamic retail business environment. The primary responsibility will be for the delivery of complex multi-functional software engineering programs and products for Apple Retail platform, technologies and potentially leading a team of project managers, technical analysts, resources, budgets and the related reporting for program. You will provide leadership for project teams by acting as a domain lead for retail business and combining strong business insight with strong communication and technical skills. You will operate as an effective interface between engineers and product owners, bringing prospective and understanding of retail principles to help development time and improve their effectiveness in serving Apple's customers. You will understand and articulate the business and technical implications of decisions that are being made by their project teams, whilst handling senior partner relationships to ensure successful program delivery. A combination of gravitas and flexibility will be required to ensure that they can be effective and you have extensive experience working on consumer product launches, product merchandising in an electronic commerce environment. You will act as the SPOC for the project to both Biz and SSGs, support gate processes and provide PLOEs and DLOEs Minimum Qualifications Years of technical project management experience (including but not limiting payment partner integrations, geo expansions and etc.) in eCommerce or Retail Industries. Strong experience with resource management in complex environments for budgets in the millions of dollars. Strong understanding of controlling and running budgets, resource management and forecasting. Project and program management experience in a software development environment. Preferred Qualifications Specific deep experience and functional expertise in electronic commerce for consumer products. Experience leading multiple high priority projects at a time and driving them forward with a sense of urgency. A phenomenal teammate with a positive attitude who builds relationships quickly. Proven ability to partner and influence multi-functionally to identify, evaluate and execute strategic plans. Experience in building scalable and highly performant consumer facing web-based React applications Experience in Spring or other DI frameworks is a plus Experience in SQL databases QL , No-SQL DB like Cassandra, DynamoDB, Couchbase etc is desirable Experience scaling distributed systems to handle millions of concurrent requests is desirable Experience integrating with Content Management Systems is a plus Apple is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to inclusion and diversity, and thus we treat all applicants fairly and equally. Apple is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical and mental disabilities.

title: Integrated Marketing Manager (Hands-On Digital Specialist)
description: Job Description We are seeking a versatile and hands-on Integrated Marketing Manager who can balance strategic planning with strong execution capabilities. This role is responsible for driving brand growth through integrated marketing campaigns across digital, retail, and traditional channels. The ideal candidate is both a strategist and an executor — someone who can develop high-impact long-term and short-term marketing plans while personally running ads, crafting content, and delivering measurable results. This role also requires strong social listening skills to identify emerging trends, audience needs, and competitive insights, as well as the ability to translate those insights into innovative content ideation that fuels engaging, on-brand campaigns. Key Responsibilities 1. Strategy, Planning & Budget Management Lead the planning, development, and execution of integrated, multi-channel marketing campaigns to strengthen brand visibility and customer engagement. Manage and optimise the approved marketing budget in alignment with business goals and ROI targets. Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual performance reports with insights and recommendations for management. 2. Digital Marketing (Hands-On Execution) Independently plan, run, and optimise Google Ads, Meta Ads and other performance-driven campaigns. Manage SEO/SEM strategies including keyword research, content optimisation, and landing page performance. Execute email marketing campaigns (e.g., Mailchimp, Klaviyo), including A/B testing and audience segmentation. Monitor digital KPIs and provide insights using platforms such as Google Analytics and Meta Business Suite. 3. Brand, Content & Social Media Management Oversee official social media channels (Instagram, Facebook, Rednote, LinkedIn, where applicable), including content planning, copywriting, and community engagement. Work with influencers, celebrities, and KOLs to enhance brand reach and credibility. Content Ideation: Provide creative direction and clear briefs for designers, photographers, and videographers to ensure consistent branding across all touchpoints. 4. Public Relations & Media Communications Develop PR strategies to strengthen corporate and brand reputation. Craft press releases, media kits, and spokesperson briefs. Pitch stories to media outlets and cultivate strong relationships with journalists and media partners. Manage media enquiries, brand reputation issues, and crisis communication when needed. Track PR coverage and report impact. 5. Retail Marketing & CRM Plan and manage in-store promotions, premium campaigns, joint promotions, and membership initiatives. Build and maintain a robust CRM program to drive customer loyalty, retention, and targeted communications. Collaborate closely with Retail Operations on promotional calendars, retail activations, and offline marketing. 6. Website, CRO & Analytics Manage website updates and enhancements using CMS platforms such as WordPress or Shopify. Conduct A/B testing and implement optimisation initiatives to improve conversion rates and user experience. Build dashboards and analyse performance across the full funnel (awareness → consideration → conversion). 7. Market Intelligence, Vendors & Cross-Functional Collaboration Conduct market research, competitor benchmarking, and social listening to identify trends and opportunities. Work with internal teams and external vendors/agencies to deliver cohesive campaigns and marketing assets. Manage freelancers, agencies, and partners to ensure quality, timeliness, and alignment with brand objectives. Lead or support other marketing-related projects as assigned. What We’re Looking For Must-Have Skills & Attributes Strong hands-on experience executing SEM (Google Ads) and Meta (Facebook/Instagram) ads. Familiarity with email automation tools (e.g., Mailchimp, Klaviyo), CMS platforms (WordPress, Shopify), and SEO/SEM best practices. Skilled in content creation: content briefing, script writing, and video storytelling. Strong analytical, project management, and multi-tasking abilities with high attention to detail. A proactive team player who is able to work independently, think strategically, and perform well under pressure. Schedule: Mon-Fri 9am-6pm Workplace: Jln Bukit Merah Please note: The job title and salary range etc indicated are for reference purposes only. The final offer may differ based on experience, qualifications, and internal evaluation.

Cut Pro, CapCut) and graphic design tools (Adobe Photoshop, Canva). · Strong written and verbal communication skills, with a keen eye for detail and good copywriting abilities. · Ability to conduct sensitive interviews and extract compelling stories from individuals, particularly those from diverse backgrounds. · Good command of English and able to speak a second language (Mandarin, Malay, Tamil) and/or local dialects to communicate with. · Good understanding of social media trends, best practices, and analytics. · Ability to work independently and as part of a team · A passion for our charity's mission and a genuine desire to make a positive impact. · Comfortable to uphold, communicate and affirm Habitat for Humanity’s principles (vision and mission); Pls refer to https://www.habitat.org.sg/about-us for details. You will work closely with stakeholders from both your IT Division and aligned sectors to define, document, and translate business requirements into actionable software specifications. You will be a key advocate for business value, ensuring that our software solutions meet the needs of our users and contribute to the overall success of our operations. This specific role aims to work on the Calypso back office package for our FX IT division, in project mode as well as maintenance, and to support the “run the bank” function. It requires a deep understanding of the nature of business operations, timing of activities & data flowing through internal and external systems. The build and support team continuously works with business users, infrastructure engineers and system managers. Responsibilities: • Elicit, analyze, and document business requirements from various stakeholders, ensuring a clear understanding of their needs and objectives for FX and Money Market Business in Calypso. • Work with the Product Owner (PO) if any on this project to prioritize requirements and decompose them into detailed user stories, ensuring a clear roadmap for development. • Translate requirements into comprehensive functional specifications, including detailed descriptions of user interactions, data flows, and system behavior. • Collaborate with development teams to ensure that the software design aligns with the functional specifications and meets the defined requirements. • Provide functional expertise to the development team, ensuring they have a clear understanding of the business processes and user needs. • Research existing solutions, both internal and external, to determine if they can be leveraged to meet the business requirements. • Understand technical constraints and participate in software design discussions, ensuring that the solution is feasible and aligns with the overall system architecture. • Ensure the functional quality of the software, including participation in functional testing and providing functional support during production. • Analyze the impact of functional changes on current production Service Level Agreements (SLAs) and Operational Level Agreements (OLAs). • Contribute to Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) activities by providing user scenarios and supporting functional testing efforts. • Contribute to deployment activities by providing functional pre-requisites and performing post-deployment checks. • Support team members in their functional activities, train new joiners, and help resolve functional issues. • Ensure the seamless integration of the software into the existing IT infrastructure, working closely with the Functional Architect. • Specifications, workload estimates, planning, test scenarios, documentation. Mandatory Skills Description: • Master or Bachelor’s degree in Finance / Computer Science / Information Technology/ Programming & Systems Analysis/ Science (Computer Studies) faculties. • Candidates should have minimum 5 years experience in Business Analysis activities. • Knowledge of Static Data, Workflows, Confirmations, Settlements, Messages / Swift messages, Positions, Accounting, Corporate action, Filters, Reporting Framework, Reconciliation features. • Understanding of financial assets (FX, Money Market, cash products) and trade lifecycle. • Experience with regulatory reporting (e.g. HRF, NORIA, MMSR). • Experience on Back Office systems • Experience in interfacing systems • Familiar with requirements life cycle management, analysis planning and monitoring, requirements' analysis and design definition. • Hands-on experience as liaison function between the onshore IT business lines and offshore technical teams and as primary functional support for development team • Knowledgeable of techniques to elicit and manage requirements, as well as software development models including Agile methodologies. • Exposure to Capital Market FO/BO system (E.g. Calypso, Kondor+, Summit, Murex...) • Knowledge of Static Data, Workflows, Confirmations, Settlements, Messages / Swift messages, Positions, Accounting, Corporate action, Filters, Reporting Framework, Reconciliation features. • Understanding of financial assets (FX, money market, cash) and trade lifecycle. • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to effectively collaborate with diverse teams. • Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills. • Ability to work under pressure. • Appetite to follow technology trend and participate to communities. • Eagerness to learn and adapt to new technologies. • Strong perseverance and diligence towards attaining goals and effective time management • Passion for sharing expertise and grow team members’ skills. • Autonomous, self-motivated and excellent team player Nice-to-Have Skills Description: • Experience ideal on Calypso versions v14 to v18. • Knowledge in UNIX, SQL, Docker/Kubernetes systems.

title: Market Strategist & Presenter
description: So, who are we? Hello, we're IG Group. We are a publicly traded FTSE250 FinTech company who run mobile, web and desktop platforms that help our clients trade stocks & shares, leveraged products, Futures & Options and Crypto. We are ambitious. Over 340,000 people already use our platforms. We're global with offices in 18 countries and products in 16 regions. We're hungry to move faster, ship better product for our customers and grow our user base. We believe in high autonomy, and we want people who are looking to do things differently in order to create better experiences for our customers. We work in cross-functional teams and are laser focused on increasing the number of active clients we serve to drive sustainable growth. Your role in the team We are seeking a dynamic and insightful Market Strategist and Presenter to join our team. You’ll be the market face of IG Group in Singapore, delivering actionable insights, building thought leadership, and engaging with the media and the trading community. You’ll also help support our content strategy and represent IG in media and public forums – bringing expert-level credibility and personality to every screen, page, and platform you’re on. As a Market Strategist & Presenter at IG Group, you’ll be both the voice and face of the company—delivering sharp, actionable insights that resonate with traders. You’ll shape our market narrative and drive client engagement through timely analysis, compelling reports, media appearances, engaging seminars, and social content. This is a high-impact, high-visibility role for someone who thrives on real-time market moves and knows how to transform analysis into actionable insights. What You’ll Do Market Commentary : Deliver daily and weekly market commentary, providing actionable insights on macroeconomic trends and trading strategies. Content Creation : Write articles covering significant market events, including US earnings, BOJ meetings, and Singapore banks' earnings. Live Hosting : Host live shows and video content on social media channels to discuss market trends and events. Social Media Engagement : Provide regular content updates across Twitter, Telegram, and Instagram to grow the company’s social media presence. Media Representation : Act as the go-to spokesperson for market views in external media, including radio and TV interviews, to share insights and build brand credibility. Webinars and Live Streams : Host regular market webinars and live streams to drive trader engagement and education. Client/ Public Events : Support and participate in client events to enhance engagement and foster brand loyalty. Who We’re Looking For You’re a natural communicator who thrives in the market cycle and knows how to turn complex themes into compelling stories. You're equally comfortable writing macro commentary, appearing on Bloomberg or CNA, or jumping into a live market call on social. You understand the stakes, the speed, and the scale of what IG clients want – and you're ready to meet that moment, every time. What You’ll Need for This Role Education: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in finance, Economics, Business, or a related field. Professional certifications such as CFA, CMT, or similar are highly desirable. Experience : A minimum of 3 years of direct experience in a market analysis or related role within the financial industry, OR Certifications : Successful completion of the RES5 and CM-CMP examinations. Strong understanding of macroeconomic trends and financial markets. Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to distill complex information into clear, actionable insights. Proven ability to create engaging written and video content for diverse audiences. Comfortable with public speaking and media appearances, including radio, webinars, and TV interviews. Proficiency in leveraging social media platforms (Youtube, Telegram, Instagram) to build brand presence. Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple priorities effectively. How we work We try to take a thoughtful approach to our ways of working as a company. When it comes to how we deliver, there are 5 things we want everyone to do to drive high performance, better learning and career satisfaction: Lead and Inspire: Drives trust, alignment, and enthusiasm Think Big: Focus on the problems that most impact commercial outcomes Champion the client: Understand and prioritise client's needs Deliver at pace: Push for fast, sustainable growth; Raise the bar: Take ownership, be accountable and share feedback We believe that diversity is vital to success, it fuels creativity, drives innovation and sets us up for global success. We're committed to building teams with a variety of perspectives and skills to help us realise our vision and strategy, that's why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join us on this journey. Learn more about our D&I approach here . The Perks Your growth fuels our success! Thrive with tailored development programs, mentoring opportunities with leaders, and clear career progression. Expand your network through committees, sports and social clubs. Enjoy extra time off for volunteering and community work. Flexible working hours and work-from-home opportunities. Performance-related bonuses. Insurance and medical plans. Career-focused technical and leadership training in-class and online, incl. unlimited access to LinkedIn Learning platform. Contribution to gym memberships and more. A day off on your birthday. Two days’ volunteering leave per year. Join us for this exciting journey. Apply now!

title: Business Analytics and Data Specialist
description: At #TeamCPF, you’re not just joining a team; you are embracing a culture of excellence, collaboration, and meaningful impact. Collaboration is our way of life. We believe in the power of effective partnerships and seamless communications across teams. Together, we amplify each other’s strengths and achieve remarkable results. Our learning never stops. We encourage your inquisitiveness and courage to embrace new challenges head-on. Your agility, readiness to challenge conventions, embrace of data-driven strategies, dedication to learning and applying new skills fuels our innovation and progress. At the core of everything we do lies a genuine desire to make a difference. We serve our community and support each other with compassion, empathy, and unwavering dedications. Every action we take is guided by a deep sense of purpose and a commitment to those we serve. Join us at #TeamCPF! Together, let's redefine possibilities and leave a legacy that echoes for generations. What you will be working on As a Business Analytics and Data Specialist, you will be instrumental in transforming data into actionable insights that drive strategic decision-making and operational excellence across the Agency Services Group. You will analyse data trends to project future operational needs, detect shifts in citizen behaviour to anticipate policy implications, design automation workflows and explore emerging technologies to achieve cost, resource and manpower efficiency. In this role, you will: Analyse data and conduct research studies to support policy reviews and optimise business processes. Implement data initiatives and programmes across the Agency Services Group and build staff capabilities through training and knowledge transfer. Set up and maintain data marts with proper data governance framework and sharing guidelines. Explore and implement automation solutions to reduce manual effort and streamline work processes. Perform environmental scans to identify emerging technologies and automation opportunities to achieve greater operational efficiency. What we are looking for We value the diverse talents and experiences that each individual brings to the table. While mastery of every requirement may not be necessary, familiarity and expertise in some of the following areas will position you for success within this team. Relevant experience in business analytics, data analysis, or related fields with understanding of citizen disbursement schemes to support and drive business process optimisation and sustainability. Have experience in data analysis, critical thinking and problem-solving. Have experience in organisational and project management. Knowledge and prior experience with Databricks, Python, UiPath and/or other programming software is an added advantage. Keen interest in leveraging emerging technology to enhance data analytics capabilities. Communicates effectively, both in writing and speaking. Works independently and manages priorities effectively. Adaptable and resourceful in handling different tasks and challenges. The seniority of appointment and actual corporate job title will commensurate with individual work experiences. We welcome fresh graduates in any discipline and candidates without working experience to apply. To help us better understand your academic background, please provide an official or unofficial copy of your academic transcript, as well as any other relevant documents or certificates that you believe would support your application. Position is on 1-year full time contract directly under the payroll of CPF Board with potential for emplacement into permanent position. What you can expect Being part of #TeamCPF means embarking on a challenging and rewarding career in a progressive workplace that values productivity and growth. Here’s what awaits you: Opportunities to engage in a mix of formal and informal training, keeping your skills sharp in our ever-evolving technological landscape. Promotion opportunities based on your capability and on-the-job performance. A vibrant community of like-minded and friendly colleagues, where collaboration and creativity thrive. A hybrid work model that offers flexibility for remote work, subject to exigencies of service. Flexible dress code that empowers you to choose your appropriate outfit for the day. A comprehensive rewards package that includes annual leave, pro-family leave, medical and dental benefits, and access to recreational activities.
